Our findings expand on understanding newcomer persistence beyond traditional 2D environments, emphasizing how social dynamics influence the management of VR-specific issues like VR sickness during onboarding. Additionally, we highlight how successful newcomers overcome the lack of clear objectives in social VR by proactively constructing social meaning. We propose design suggestions to scaffold these successful integration pathways.

title: CMB constraints on dark matter-proton scattering: investigating prior-volume effects using profile likelihoods
authors: Maria C. Straight, Tanvi Karwal, José Luis Bernal, Kimberly K. Boddy
abstract: We present profile-likelihood constraints on velocity-independent dark matter-proton scattering, including cases in which only a fraction of dark matter has such non-gravitational interactions. Frequentist profile-likelihood techniques provide prior-independent constraints, circumventing prior-volume effects that we show arise in Bayesian constraints on this model. In the limit where the scattering cross section or the fraction of interacting dark matter approaches zero, the other interacting dark matter model parameters become unconstrained, causing the posterior distribution to favor that region of parameter space. Using Planck 2018 cosmic microwave background anisotropy data, we find a clear impact of prior-volume effects on the posteriors used to place constraints on dark matter scattering. Compared to the frequentist analysis, the Bayesian method consistently overestimates the constraints on the cross section. Given the potentially biased upper limits on models subject to prior-volume effects, such as this one, we recommend supplementing Bayesian constraints with frequentist statistics to better assess the impact of priors.

title: Hodge rigidity of Chern classes
authors: Yuxiang Liu, Artan Sheshmani, Shing-Tung Yau
abstract: In this paper, we study the homogeneous components of the Chern--Schwartz--MacPherson (CSM) classes of Schubert cells. We prove that, under suitable conditions, each such component is represented by an irreducible subvariety. In particular, our result extends Huh's result \cite{Huh} by relaxing the regularity assumption on log resolutions. As a consequence, the conclusion holds for all cominuscule Schubert cells of classical type and for a large family of exceptional cases. We also obtain analogous results for certain Schubert varieties in symplectic Grassmannians and flag varieties.

title: Giant Brillouin gain in frozen CS2 capillaries
authors: Simon Seiderer, Andreas Geilen, Luan N. Sliwa, Linqiao Gan, Xue Qi, Mario Chemnitz, Markus A. Schmidt, Birgit Stiller
abstract: Stimulated Brillouin-Mandelstam scattering offers exceptional capabilities for photonic signal processing, but current platforms demand performance trade-offs between long interaction lengths, high gain, low optical losses, and practical implementation. Here, we demonstrate a novel platform based on the reversible freezing of a carbon disulfide filled liquid-core optical fiber. This approach delivers a giant in-fiber Brillouin gain of 434 W-1m-1 with a linewidth of 24 MHz, while maintaining low propagation losses in a fully spliced architecture and providing the potential for meter-scale interaction lengths. Leveraging this gain, as a proof of principle, we realize an optoacoustic memory operating at sub-nanojoule pulse energies - more than two orders of magnitude lower than state-of-the-art implementations. This power reduction is universal for Brillouin-based fiber applications in general and will enable low-power photonic signal processing and neuromorphic computing, efficient microwave photonics and sensing, as well as in-fiber quantum optomechanics-based technologies.

title: A Minimum-Energy Control Approach for Redundant Mobile Manipulators in Physical Human-Robot Interaction Applications
authors: Davide Tebaldi, Niccolò Paradisi, Fabio Pini, Luigi Biagiotti
abstract: Research on mobile manipulation systems that physically interact with humans has expanded rapidly in recent years, opening the way to tasks which could not be performed using fixed-base manipulators. Within this context, developing suitable control methodologies is essential since mobile manipulators introduce additional degrees of freedom, making the design of control approaches more challenging and more prone to performance optimization. This paper proposes a control approach for a mobile manipulator, composed of a mobile base equipped with a robotic arm mounted on the top, with the objective of minimizing the overall kinetic energy stored in the whole-body mobile manipulator in physical human-robot interaction applications. The approach is experimentally tested with reference to a peg-in-hole task, and the results demonstrate that the proposed approach reduces the overall kinetic energy stored in the whole-body robotic system and improves the system performance compared with the benchmark method.

title: Carbon Footprint Efficiency Ratio: A Unified Indicator for Green Evaluation of Evolutionary Algorithms
authors: Mingyang Yu, Haorui Yang, Xinjian Wei, Shengwei Fu, Jing Xu, Jun Zhang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Emerging Topics in Computational Intelligence
abstract: Evolutionary algorithms (EAs) are widely adopted for black-box optimization, yet evaluation protocols remain largely accuracy-centric and often omit computational energy use, hindering carbon-aware deployment in green computing settings. We propose the Carbon Footprint Efficiency Ratio (CFER) to quantify optimization benefit per unit carbon emission and enable a unified assessment of carbon efficiency. To compute CFER without dedicated power-monitoring hardware, we develop a device-independent energy estimation model based on the Multimodal Energy Consumption Factor (MECF). Using a dataset constructed from 13 benchmark functions and 8 classical EAs, MECF is identified via outlier removal and Huber regression, achieving a mean estimation error of 1.80% and a maximum error of 2.51%. On the CEC2020 test suite, CFER-based comparisons reveal rankings that differ substantially from accuracy-only evaluations, highlighting algorithms that improve carbon efficiency with only marginal accuracy loss. Beyond metric-based assessment, we introduce a CFER-aware green optimization mechanism as an algorithm-agnostic plug-in control layer. Driven by real-time carbon estimation and marginal CFER feedback, it adaptively budgets computation through carbon-budget termination and population shrinking under low marginal carbon efficiency. Integrated into GA, DE, and PSO for uncrewed aerial vehicle (UAV) three-dimensional path planning, the resulting CFER-aware variants reduce CO<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub> emissions by 45.91%, 28.28%, and 59.14%, respectively, while increasing CFER by 78.59%, 32.45%, and 133.70%, respectively, yielding an improved performance–carbon trade-off with minor performance-score degradation. Overall, the proposed framework supports carbon-aware evaluation, algorithm selection, and adaptive computation budgeting for green AI systems.

title: Codeword-Based Auto-Correlation Receiver for Ultrasonic Time-Code-Indexed Modulation Systems
authors: Qianqian Wang, Baiqiang Long, Xinyue Pei, Yuankun Tang, Haoyue Qu, Xiangdong Jia
abstract: Ultrasonic index modulation can effectively improve the spectral efficiency and energy efficiency of intra-body communication systems. This letter proposes a codeword-based auto-correlation (C-AC) receiver for ultrasonic code-indexed modulation (UCIM) and ultrasonic time-code-indexed modulation (UTCIM) transmitters, employing Hadamard codewords. The core idea is to utilize the auto-correlation properties of the Hadamard codewords to demodulate the code index bits, where the demodulation of each code index bit is based on the sum of the dot products of every two sub-sequences, dispensing with precise synchronization and channel state information. The theoretical bit error rate (BER) is derived and verified by extensive Monte Carlo simulations over intra-body fading channels, revealing that the proposed non-coherent low-complexity C-AC receiver can achieve lower BER compared with the conventional transmitted-reference receiver for both the UCIM and UTCIM transmitters.

title: Progress in the Application of Optical Fiber in Quench Detection on CFETR CSMC
authors: Qicai Ni, Jincheng Jiang, Longgui Zheng, Yanlan Hu, Yu Chen, Yezheng Xiao, Huaichao Liu, Qing Yan, Jilin Zhang, Xinxin Zhu
abstract: The CFETR project is a key node in China's efforts to achieve nuclear fusion to solve the energy crisis. CSMC is a large-scale hybrid (Nb3Sn/NbTi) superconducting coil designed for the CFETR project's central solenoid magnet. It is used to obtain the scientific goals and physical characteristics of CFETR, laying a solid foundation for the future development of CFETR CS superconducting magnets. At present, the quench detection system of the superconducting magnet system is mainly based on the detection of terminal voltage changes, combined with the temperature data of the thermometer as an auxiliary means. Although this solution can meet the basic quench detection needs, it has significant defects in the quench warning and positioning functions, and the high-voltage penetrating thermometer can only be installed at the back of the insulator, resulting in a deviation between the temperature measurement data and the actual value. This paper selects the distributed optical fiber based on Rayleigh scattering and the fiber Bragg grating (FBG) temperature measurement technology as a countermeasure. This technology has the advantages of naturally avoiding electromagnetic interference, fast temperature response speed, and continuous detection of temperature changes in space. Seamless steel tubes are used to encapsulate distributed optical fibers to improve their mechanical properties. We arrange distributed optical fibers in the coil at the highest magnetic field and the helium tube at the thermal disturbance trigger point to detect the cooling of CSMC. The trend of the spectral offset obtained from the curve is consistent with that of the thermometer, with some deviations in details. The distributed optical fiber successfully captured a 3 K temperature fluctuation based on the 20 K cooling baseline at the 14th turn of the inner Nb3Sn coil (high-field region) around the 80 h mark, demonstrating its sensitivity to thermal anomalies near the warning threshold. For the ultra-low temperature range where CSMC works normally, the temperature rise of 3 K in the 20 K range serves as a high-confidence indicator for thermal runaway confirmation and fault localization, which complements the instantaneous but non-localized voltage-based detection. The distributed optical fiber responds to this more obviously, providing a reliable basis for optical fiber quench detection. In order to improve the response of the optical fiber to the temperature details around 20 K, we use a Bragg grating packaged with PEEK with higher sensitivity at low temperatures for corresponding supplementation.

title: Black-start Restoration Within the Data-Center-Integrated Power System Based on Distributed Optimization
authors: Shunqi Wang, Tao Ding, Yujie Ding, Yueyang Yang
abstract: With the development of modern economics, the dependence of socio-economic structure on electricity has grown increasingly pronounced. Consequently, the occurrence of large-scale power outages would result in substantial risks to public safety and economic losses. This study investigates the distributed black-start restoration strategy within the data center (DC) integrated power system. Recognizing the disparities in disaster response capabilities across various DCs, the study proposes a disaster restoration model of DCs accounting for differences in power supply levels. Furthermore, this study develops a linearized black-start restoration model of the power system in view of DC load adjustment. Lastly, this study presents a distributed optimization algorithm based on space exploration and unimodal region elimination for privacy protection between data center operators and the system operator. Simulation results verify the effectiveness of restoration coordination among generation sources, the grid, and loads. Besides, it is proven that the proposed distributed algorithm can effectively realize the maximal restored load on the premise of preserving the data privacy of each entity.

title: A Digital Compressed Sensing Framework with Optimized Measurement, Quantization, and Sparse Recovery for IoT-Enabled Wearable Edge ECG Health Monitoring Devices
authors: Sajeev K. Jose, Narendra Kumar Reddy Gangireddy
publish: IEEE Transactions on Instrumentation and Measurement
abstract: Wearable ECG monitoring devices, often limited by power constraints, require efficient methods to reduce energy consumption and extend battery life for continuous health tracking. This work proposes a digital compressed sensing approach for ECG (DCS-ECG), featuring a suitable sensing matrix, effective sparse signal recovery algorithms, and an optimized number of bits for quantization (BM). The framework is designed to achieve high compression ratios (CR) with minimal signal distortion and parameter extraction without the reconstruction of the original signal. The following five sensing matrices are examined: deterministic block binary diagonal (DBBD) matrix, random binary (RBin) matrix, random Bernoulli (RBen) matrix, random Gaussian (RG) matrix, and random sparse fixed binary (RSB) matrix. These are paired with a sparsifying matrix (ψ), a hybrid of identity matrix (I), discrete sine transform matrix (S), and discrete cosine transform matrix (C) (i.e. ψ = [I S C]). The following five sparse reconstruction algorithms are evaluated: iterative hard thresholding (IHT) algorithm, orthogonal matching pursuit (OMP) algorithm, smoothed ℓ0 (SL0) algorithm, ℓ1 least square (L1LS) algorithm, and subspace matching pursuit (SMP) algorithm. The proposed DCS-ECG system was tested on ECG signals from the MIT-BIH arrhythmia database. The configuration using the DBBD sensing matrix and OMP recovery algorithm with the sparsifying matrix achieved a low average percentage of root mean square difference (PRD) of 3.75 ± 1.43% at CR = 12.5%, which also offers fast reconstruction. For CR = 12.5% and BM = 8, the CS measurement generation on the Arduino Due consumes 2.02 mJ, while reconstruction on the Raspberry Pi requires 1.13 J. The wireless transmission energy is 1.2973 J. Overall, the proposed CS-based system achieves an 86.24% total energy saving compared to the without CS approach, demonstrating its suitability for low-power IoT-enabled wearable edge ECG health monitoring applications.

title: ICIIS 2026 Contributor Page

title: Automated Incident Management using Infrastructure-as-Code
authors: Niyazi Gökberk Gündüz, Florian Hofer, Eduardo Guerra, Nabil El Ioini, Claus Pahl
abstract: Enterprises that manage their cloud systems through Infrastructure-as-Code (IaC) often push dozens of changes every day. Human operators create a bottleneck in managing incidents in these rapidly changing systems: incident post-mortems reveal detection latencies of tens of minutes and manual recoveries that stretch into hours. An autonomous controller is presented that provides reliable incident self-healing in a Development and Operations (DevOps) environment. This self-healing controller architecture instantiates a common autonomic computing pattern. It provides end-to-end, rule-driven self-healing for an IaC pipeline without the need for specialized hardware or proprietary IT operation platforms. It contributes to handling infrastructure incidents in a reliable and automated fashion.

title: Instance-Conditioned Adaptation for Large-Scale Generalization of Neural Routing Solver
authors: Changliang Zhou, Xi Lin, Zhenkun Wang, Xialiang Tong, Mingxuan Yuan, Qingfu Zhang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Intelligent Transportation Systems
abstract: In modern intelligent transportation systems (ITS), particularly in freight transportation and logistics, real-time route planning is crucial. It presents unique challenges driven by high uncertainty in service requests, where the number of service customers can vary drastically, ranging from hundreds to thousands. Existing neural methods struggle to maintain performance under such significant variations, which severely limits their practical applicability. To address this crucial shortcoming, this work proposes a novel Instance-Conditioned Adaptation Model (ICAM) designed for better large-scale generalization. In particular, we design a simple yet efficient instance-conditioned adaptation function that adjusts the policy based on the specific geometry and density of the current traffic scenario to improve model adaptability with minimal computational overhead. Furthermore, we propose a powerful yet low-complexity instance-conditioned adaptation module to generate better solutions for instances across various scales. Extensive experiments on synthetic, benchmark, and real-world instances demonstrate that ICAM can consistently achieve promising generalization performance across four widely studied large-scale route planning scenarios. Notably, our proposed method delivers high-quality solutions with remarkably fast inference speed, providing a scalable and efficient solution for real-time intelligent transportation operations. Our code is available at <uri xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">https://github.com/CIAM-Group/ICAM</uri>

title: Flying Vehicle Detection Under Complex Conditions With RGB–Infrared Imagery: A Large-Scale Open-Source Suite and Benchmark Approach
authors: Xunkuai Zhou, Yijun Huang, Li Li, Jie Chen, Ben M. Chen
publish: IEEE Transactions on Intelligent Transportation Systems
abstract: While coordination among multiple flying vehicles improves aerial logistics efficiency, safe and orderly operation requires robust detection and collision-avoidance capabilities. These requirements apply to passenger aircraft as well as to urban traffic and maritime environments, including emerging underwater flying vehicles. However, existing detection methods often fail under challenging conditions such as low illumination or cluttered backgrounds. Their progress is further constrained by the lack of large-scale benchmarks and the high computational and memory costs required to achieve high accuracy, which limits their deployment in resource-constrained scenarios, such as air-to-air collision avoidance in aerial vehicles. To address this gap, we introduce FT55k, an open-source benchmark comprising over 55,000 annotated RGB and infrared images across diverse environments. We further provide baseline approaches tailored for platforms with different computational demands. Extensive experiments on FT55k and three public datasets demonstrate the superior accuracy and efficiency of our methods compared with state-of-the-art approaches. Notably, our approach is the first flying vehicle detection method with a computational cost below 0.5 BFLOPs, achieving real-time performance at 62.3 FPS on an edge-computing device. This work presents the first comprehensive benchmark for flying vehicle detection in complex environments, establishing a practical and scalable foundation for future research and deployment in intelligent transportation safety. Our datasets is publicly accessible at <uri xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">https://github.com/chriszxk/Flying-Vehicle-Detection</uri>

title: On the Importance of Tactile Sensing for Imitation Learning: A Case Study on Robotic Match Lighting_supp1-3677716.mp4
authors: Niklas Funk
abstract: The field of robotic manipulation has advanced significantly in recent years. At the sensing level, several novel tactile sensors have been developed, capable of providing accurate contact information. On a methodological level, learning from demonstrations has proven an efficient paradigm to obtain performant robotic manipulation policies. The combination of both holds the promise to extract crucial contact-related information from the demonstration data and actively exploit it during policy rollouts. However, this integration has so far been underexplored, most notably in dynamic, contact-rich manipulation tasks where precision and reactivity are essential. This work therefore proposes a multimodal, visuotactile imitation learning framework that integrates a modular transformer architecture with a flow-based generative model, enabling efficient learning of fast and dexterous manipulation policies. We evaluate our framework on the dynamic, contact-rich task of robotic match lighting - a task in which tactile feedback influences human manipulation performance. The experimental results highlight the effectiveness of our approach and show that adding tactile information improves policy performance, thereby underlining their combined potential for learning dynamic manipulation from few demonstrations.

title: Sampling Augmented Bilevel Trajectory Optimization for Constrained Quadrotor Flights_supp1-3677754.mp4
authors: Qiang Li
abstract: Planning smooth trajectories through a sequence of waypoints under nonconvex constraints is challenging due to the coupling between coefficient optimization and time allocation. Existing gradient-based spline trajectory optimization methods tend to be susceptible to local minima and poor initializations, or restrained by complicated gradient computations. We propose a sampling augmented bilevel optimization (SABO) approach that integrates gradient-based optimization with correlated spatio-temporal sampling for improved robustness and optimality. Through temporal normalization, the closed-form solution of coefficient optimization becomes an explicit function of segment durations, while the Hessian becomes linear in their powers, enabling analytic bilevel gradient computation without using finite differences or linearized constraints. Correlated mutations are subsequently performed around the gradient-induced solution to further explore the constrained spatio-temporal space, with sample projection and covariance matrix adaptation to guide sampling towards low-cost, feasible regions. Simulations show that SABO outperforms existing methods in terms of optimality and robustness. We validate SABO in flight experiments conducted on a quadrotor.

title: Efficient Cluster-Based Knowledge Distillation for Deep Face Recognition
authors: Xianwei Lv, Haibo Mi, Xin Niu, Wang Chen, Kun Wang, Chen Yu
abstract: Knowledge distillation has been widely used to improve the performance of small compact models for face recognition. However, selecting key knowledge and effectively transferring it from teacher to student remains a challenging problem. In this work, we propose an efficient Cluster-based Knowledge Distillation (CKD) dedicated to aligning the student model with the teacher model in terms of both sample relations and class centers. Specifically, CKD first determines the key sample relations based on the similarities between the sample features extracted by the teacher and their cluster centers generated by existing clustering algorithms. Then, CKD effectively transfers the knowledge of the above relations from the teacher to the student by designing a cluster-based relation distillation loss. Finally, CKD further improves the quality of the student's class centers by constructing a center loss between the above representative cluster centers and the student's class centers. We validate the proposed CKD on multiple face benchmarks. For example, CKD improves the baseline student performance from 91.95% to 94.20% on MegaFace and consistently outperforms recent competitive distillation methods on multiple benchmarks. These results demonstrate the effectiveness and superiority of CKD.

title: Optimal Experimental Design for Fuel Cell Model Parameter Identification
authors: Luis M. Winkler, Michael A. Danzer, Herbert Palm
abstract: Model-based development provides a systematic approach for investigating fuel cell (FC) and fuel cell system (FCS) operation, with particular emphasis on understanding reaction kinetics, transport phenomena, and aging mechanisms as functions of operating conditions. Characterizing these processes by physical models requires effective and efficient parameter identification, i.e., the derivation of precise parameter information with minimal experimental effort. Conventional strategies for parameter identification in FC models have so far suffered in particular from high time and resource requirements, leading to considerable bottlenecks in FC model development and system characterization. Therefore, this paper proposes a generic, systematic workflow for equally effective and efficient parameter identification in nonlinear, parametric models through structural and practical identifiability analysis based on the Fisher information matrix. The approach takes into account existing prior knowledge about parameter values, thereby enabling the calculation of optimal experimental designs. The methodology is universally applicable to differentiable parametric models. We demonstrate its practical applicability for a proton exchange membrane FC under realistic measurement conditions using a zero-dimensional model with six unknown parameters. Compared to conventional Latin Hypercube Sampling, the proposed approach achieves parameter identification with a 74% reduction in uncertainty or, conversely, saves 84% of the experimental effort required to achieve the same accuracy. The framework enables experimenters to conduct measurement campaigns that are both maximally effective and efficient. All methodological implementations, data, and demonstration results are made publicly available to facilitate reproducibility and community adoption.

title: What do I Know about AI Beyond Everyday Knowledge?
authors: Xingyao Xiao, Yihong Cheng
abstract: None

title: NPX: Automating Neuromorphic Processor Design from Spike-Based Learning to FPGA Prototyping
authors: Kyuseung Han, Hyeonguk Jang, Sukho Lee, Sung-Eun Kim, Kyudong Hwang, Jae-Jin Lee
publish: 2025 35th International Conference on Field-Programmable Logic and Applications (FPL)
abstract: Neuromorphic Processor eXpress (NPX) is a framework designed to facilitate the development of lightweight neuromorphic processors. To evaluate its efficacy, we conducted a case study involving the FPGA-based implementation of a traffic sign recognition system using an NPX-generated neuromorphic processor. The prototype integrates camera input and OLED output, successfully demonstrating full functionality. This case study confirms that NPX substantially streamlines the design and deployment of efficient neuromorphic processors for embedded artificial intelligence applications.

title: Benign vs. Malignant Lung Tumor Classification Using a CNN-SVM Hybrid Approach
authors: Swati Singh, Anupma Gupta, Gaurav Sharma, Ashish Kumar Singh, Jhulan Kumar, Deepak Banerjee, Manish Kumar Singla
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Emerging Trends in Computing and Communication (ETCOM)
abstract: This study develops a hybrid architecture of the Convolutional Neural Network and the Support Vector Machine model in the specific identification of lung cancer into the malignant groups. The sample size of the study dataset on lung CT, was 10,240 in total; 5,000 benign samples, and 5,240 malignant samples with the support ratios of 0.49 and 0.51 respectively. As can be seen in the proposed model, the proposed model can be effectively used to classify the dataset in question and the overall accuracy of both classes is up to 99 %. The model would demonstrate, regarding the benign category, the precision of 98.99, the recall of 98.000 and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$F 1$</tex> as 98.49 which implies that the model is effective at detecting non-cancer cases. It has attained a precision of 98.11, recall of 99.05 and F1 measure of 98.58 of the malignant class indicating that it is highly sensitive in terms of detecting cases of cancer. These findings indicate that the model is strong and efficient, which makes it an extremely efficient computer-aided diagnosis tool in the identification of lung cancer at an early and timely stage. Artificially balanced is available to the majority of real medical settings and improves the quality of diagnostic decisions and patient treatment.

title: Hardware Distortion Aware Precoding for ISAC Systems
authors: Murat Babek Salman, Emil Björnson, Özlem Tuğfe Demir
publish: 2025 59th Asilomar Conference on Signals, Systems, and Computers
abstract: The impact of hardware impairments on the spectral efficiency of communication systems is well studied, but their effect on sensing performance remains unexplored. In this paper, we analyze the influence of hardware impairments on integrated sensing and communication (ISAC) systems in cluttered environments. We derive the sensing signal-to-clutter- plus-noise ratio (SCNR) and show that hardware distortions significantly degrade sensing performance by enhancing clutter- induced noise, which masks target echoes. The isotropic nature of transmit distortion due to multiple stream transmission further complicates clutter suppression. To address this, we propose a distortion- and clutter-aware precoding strategy that minimizes the deviation from the communication-optimized precoder while improving sensing robustness. We also propose an alternative power allocation-based approach that reduces computational complexity. Numerical results confirm the effectiveness of the proposed approaches in overcoming hardware- and clutter- induced limitations, demonstrating significant performance gains over distortion-unaware designs.

title: Nuclear Power Project Construction Resource Allocation Model Based on Multi-Objective Optimization Algorithm
authors: Yuan Li, Songlin Wan
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Smart Innovations in Systems, Infrastructure, Mechanical, Power, AI and Computing Technologies (SISIMPACT)
abstract: In the construction of nuclear power projects, resource allocation is complex and limited. How to efficiently allocate construction resources while meeting multiple requirements such as construction period and safety has become an important problem that affects the smooth progress of the project. To this end, this paper proposes a nuclear power project construction resource allocation model based on a multi-objective optimization algorithm. Firstly, a multi-objective mathematical model of resource allocation is established, and objectives such as cost minimization, construction period shortening and resource utilization maximization are incorporated into the optimization framework. Secondly, a method based on an improved genetic algorithm (GA) is used to solve the multi-objective problem, so as to ensure that the model can find the optimal solution that takes into account the needs of all parties under multiple constraints. Finally, multiple rounds of iterations and parameter adjustments are used to improve the convergence efficiency of the algorithm and the quality of the solution. Experimental results show that the model based on the improved GA achieves a 15 % cost savings and shortens the construction period by 10.8 % compared to particle swarm optimization (PSO). In terms of human resources, the average resource utilization rate is increased to 76 %. The overall results verify the efficiency and practicability of this method in nuclear power engineering construction, and effectively solve the problem of resource allocation under multi-objective optimization.

title: Exploring Digital Logic Design through Quantumdot Cellular Automata Modelling and Analysis
authors: Shilpa K. C, Shwetha M, Pavithra G, Swapnil S. Ninawe
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Emerging Trends in Computing and Communication (ETCOM)
abstract: Quantum-dot Cellular Automata (QCA) represents a next-generation nanoscale paradigm that promises to transform digital circuit design by providing faster operation, higher density, and ultra-low power consumption, effectively overcoming the drawbacks of traditional CMOS technology. In this study, QCA-based multiplier and squaring circuits are designed and analyzed with emphasis on their applications in digital signal processing. Unlike CMOS logic systems, QCA performs computation using electron position configurations within quantum dots, eliminating conventional current-driven switching. The proposed circuits are implemented using binary adders, AND gates, and MUX-based RAM modules, and their performance is assessed based on area utilization, cell count, and energy consumption through QCADesigner simulations. The comparative findings reveal substantial gains in efficiency and scalability compared to CMOS implementations, establishing QCA as a strong candidate for future computing architectures

title: Integrating Deep Learning-Based Object Detection Into Assistive Systems for Dynamic Accessibility Challenges
authors: Shiplu Das, Saptarshi Mondal, Subhajyoti Halder
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Smart Innovations in Systems, Infrastructure, Mechanical, Power, AI and Computing Technologies (SISIMPACT)
abstract: In this research, we focus on the issue of detecting objects in environments with low lighting, aiming to enhance accessibility for people with visual impairments. Our approach involves a real-time AI solution using advanced deep learning techniques, particularly Generative Adversarial Networks (GAN), in combination with object detection models such as YOLO and SSD. Through the enhancement of low-light images, our system is designed to improve object visibility, thus facilitating safer navigation. The study details the architecture, training approach, and performance outcomes of the proposed model, demonstrating notable enhancements in object detection accuracy in difficult lighting conditions. This solution holds promise for enhancing public safety and accessibility, particularly in urban settings and emergency situations.

title: Automated Arthritis Detection from X-Ray Images Using Efficientnet B3 and Image Processing
authors: Prannov Shabari. N, Prarthana. K, Rahul Chiranjeevi. V
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Emerging Trends in Computing and Communication (ETCOM)
abstract: Deep learning algorithms have recently received substantial interest for detecting arthritis automatically in medical imaging context. The proposed research introduces a solid methodology to identify arthritis by applying deep learning algorithms to analyzed X-ray scans of bones. The processing method starts with an image preparation process consisting of histogram normalization and noise reduction alongside normalization along with segmentation which improves image quality. The EfficientNet-B3 model analyzes preprocessed images as its input because it represents a state-of-the-art CNN implementation that delivers high efficiency in medical image classification tasks. Using blocks with different convolutional kernel dimensions and resolutions the model learns complex representations of arthritis-pattern distribution in its deep feature extraction process. The feature maps proceed to a classification section that generates predictions for arthritis existence or non-existence. The classification process forms two output groups which include “Arthritis” and “No Arthritis.” The proposed model uses deep learning technology together with optimal image preprocessing capabilities to enhance arthritis detection accuracy and thus help doctors with early diagnosis and medical decision support. EfficientNet-B3 provides the perfect equilibrium between processing speed and diagnostic outcomes which leads to its suitability in medical equipment handling biological data in real time. The proposed detection method demonstrates its ability to accurately identify arthritic conditions compared to normal cases according to experimental findings. Experiments were performed on various datasets where the proposed method achieved Based on the experimental results proposed method achieved a significant amount of accuracy compared to state of art methods.

title: Dual-Path Attention-Guided E-Waste Classification using Swin Transformer and Graph Convolutional Networks
authors: Parvathi Sindhuja, Shaik Vaseem Akram, Syed Musthak Ahmed
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Recent Trends in Electrical, Electronics and Computing Technologies (ICRTEECT)
abstract: With the growing concern over electronic waste (ewaste) and its environmental impact, efficient and automated classification of e-waste components has become essential for sustainable recycling. In this paper, we propose a novel dual-path deep learning architecture that integrates the Swin Transformer and graph convolutional networks (GCNs) for accurate e-waste image classification. The first path leverages the Swin Transformer to extract hierarchical attention-based global features, while the second path employs GCNs to capture spatial and relational dependencies among e-waste components such as wires, chips, and connectors. A Cross-Attention Fusion Module is introduced to effectively merge features from both streams at the decision level. Extensive experiments conducted on a curated dataset of 1053 annotated e-waste images demonstrate the effectiveness of our approach, achieving a classification accuracy of 96.2% and outperforming conventional CNN and single-path models. The proposed architecture exhibits robustness against cluttered backgrounds and intra-class variations, offering a promising solution for intelligent and scalable e-waste management systems.

title: Email marketing campaign optimization using multi arm bandits
authors: Chirath Dasanayaka, Kanishka Dandeniya, Gimhani Samindika Dissanayake, Sudesh Chamara Jayathunge Bandara
publish: 2025 IEEE 19th International Conference on Industrial and Information Systems (ICIIS)
abstract: Firms using online marketing strategies regularly need to run experiments with multiple versions of their advertisements, as there is always uncertainty about which advertisement is more attractive and effective for profit maximization. There are a number of variables that need to be taken into account when running a marketing campaign, and the optimization of these variables will help to enhance the campaign’s performance. Unlike traditional advertising methods such as newspaper, billboards and television, digital media marketing techniques have the advantage of measurability of user engagement in real time. Being able to translate these data into insights then into actions will allow campaigners to refine the campaign's performance and maximize its success. The research presents the success of this data-driven email marketing campaign optimization approach with the aid of multi-armed bandits, which was able to maximize the click-through rate by 132% in comparison to the traditional A/B testing approach.

title: Multimodal Identification of Deepfake Media
authors: Medhavi Bajpai, Anuj Singh, Nisha Pal, Shubham Kumar
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Emerging Trends in Computing and Communication (ETCOM)
abstract: This paper introduces a comprehensive framework for deepfake detection based on multimodal analysis that integrates physiological signal evaluation with traditional visual artifact detection techniques. As the quality of synthetic media, particularly facial manipulations, continues to improve, maintaining digital content authenticity has become increasingly complex. The proposed methodology exploits both visual inconsistencies and physiological cues that artificial content often fails to replicate accurately. Experimental evaluation on benchmark datasets such as FaceForensics++ and the DeepFake Detection Challenge (DFDC) demonstrates that the proposed approach outperforms existing methods, especially in scenarios involving compressed media and diverse manipulation techniques. The findings confirm that combining multiple detection modalities significantly enhances reliability and robustness against advanced synthetic generation methods.

title: Parasitic antenna positioning for enhanced planar array performance
authors: Nitish Deshpande, Mohamed Akrout, Robert W. Heath
publish: 2025 59th Asilomar Conference on Signals, Systems, and Computers
abstract: Hybrid parasitic arrays are one approach to scale array aperture sizes with limited number of radio frequency (RF) chains and low power consumption. In this architecture, several active elements are connected to RF chains while parasitic elements are connected to digitally controlled reactances. Achieving good communication performance requires sufficient mutual coupling between active and parasitic elements. In this paper, we optimize the assignment of parasitic and active elements at the transmitter based on the signal-to-noise ratio (SNR) at the receiver. For a fixed fraction of parasitic antenna elements, this problem becomes a constrained assignment task, which is NP-hard due to its combinatorial nature. We employ a simulated annealing approach to find near-optimal antenna configurations that work well in a variety of channel conditions. The numerical results show how the optimized assignment patterns become beneficial for channels with stronger line-of-sight (LOS) component.

title: Green Cure- Detection and prediction of agricultural plant leaf diseases
authors: Nirmala Deve P, Gowri Priyaa A, Komathi K, Subhasiniy R C
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Recent Trends in Electrical, Electronics and Computing Technologies (ICRTEECT)
abstract: Machine learning is disrupting the manner in which the disease of plant leaves is determined by offering a faster, more precise, and efficient alternative to the manual checkup. This paper fills the gap of the necessity to use strong and generalized detection suggesting and applying a new Custom Deep Learning Model. We use a specialized Integrated Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) as a deep feature extractor and carefully test its functionality on a difficult benchmark. The CNN architecture developed with verifiable classification accuracy of 92.16 on the test set was trained and tested on the 29-class PlantVillage dataset (which covers 7 crops). To drive these performance levels to next-generation levels, we suggest a Hybrid Ensemble Model where these deep-learned features are fed to an ensemble of conventional classifiers, namely Support Vector machine (SVM) and Random Forest (RF) models, to reach the performance target of 94.0%. By allowing swift and accurate application of treatments early in the cycle and configured to operate with real-time deployment of edges and be integrated into an easy to use virtual assistant interface, this system assists in sustainable agriculture.

title: Quantile Randomized Kaczmarz with Time-Varying Noise in Signal
authors: Emeric Battaglia, Anna Ma
publish: 2025 59th Asilomar Conference on Signals, Systems, and Computers
abstract: When solving a linear system of equations, the signal vector may be corrupted by time-varying noise. When the corruption is sparse, quantile-based methods such as the quantile Randomized Kaczmarz method (qRK) can recover the underlying solution to the system. However, when the corruption is insufficiently sparse, convergence is only guaranteed up to an error horizon. In the setting of time-varying noise, current theory also suggests convergence up to an error horizon. However, if the noise decays over time, one would hope that qRK can recover the solution to the system. In this work, we present an improved error horizon bound and demonstrate that it improves the previously known bounds under mild constraints. We also demonstrate that, in the time-varying case, the horizon decays as well, and qRK converges to the unique solution of the system.

title: AutoHub: A Comprehensive Platform for Vehicle Management, Assistance, and Health Monitoring
authors: Sheela Chinchmalatpure, Rohit Rathod, Sakshi Rathod, Sanskar Raut, Anisha Sadanshiv
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Emerging Trends in Computing and Communication (ETCOM)
abstract: AutoHub brings together the fundamental automotive system to combine vehicle essentials with real-time support and intelligent analytics all in one scalable platform. The core system consists of five modules: a Car Wash Service Booking module with three fulfillment models supported with secure, online payments (pick-up, doorstep wash, and service-center appointment); a Vehicle Expense Manager, to provide vehicle expense management tailored to full tracking of fuel, maintenance, tolls, fines, and repairs; a QR-Code Document Manager to create a specific QR for vehicle compliance-related documents that provide fast, role-based verification of compliance documents with one-use secure viewing; an On-Call Mechanic Assistance module that provides nearby-mechanic lookup, and a broadcast emergency request with a five-minute acceptance window, and single use atomic backend assignment; and an OBD Telemetry and Analytics module based on an ELM327 OBD-II adapter with an ESP32 microcontroller and a NEO-6M GPS module to create dashboards in realtime, along with geofencing, error code interpretation, and AI driven alerts on driver behavior, fuel efficiency, and system health. The architecture brings together mobile and web clients, a cloud real-time layer (Firebase), back- end microservices, an MQTT-MongoDB time-series pipeline, and blockchain for tamper-proof service history. AutoHub demonstrates how a modular, extensible architecture can transform conventional service apps into a full-spectrum connected-vehicle platform for future mobility ecosystems by unifying operational ease, emergency responsiveness, and predictive intelligence.

title: Optimizing Oil Palm Plantation Monitoring Using YOLOv8 and YOLOv12: Detection, Counting, and Multiclass
authors: Dule Abera, Indra Hermawan, Riza Rumayanti Dewi, Harry Imantho
publish: 2025 IEEE Asia-Pacific Conference on Geoscience, Electronics and Remote Sensing Technology (AGERS)
abstract: Accurate identification, counting, and health assessment of oil palm trees are essential for sustainable plantation management and yield optimization. Traditional manual inventory methods are labor-intensive, timeconsuming, and impractical for large-scale operations. Leveraging highresolution aerial imagery, this study conducts the first head-tohead benchmark of YOLOv8 and the newly released YOLOv12 architectures for automated oil palm detection, counting, and multiclass classification. Three model variants Nano, Small, and Medium were evaluated to balance accuracy, computational efficiency, and deployment feasibility. The dataset comprises $\mathbf{3, 4 9 9}$ aerial images ($\mathbf{2, 8 0 8}$ training, 461 validations, 230 testing) annotated into four health categories: Healthy, Yellowing, Small, and Dead. Results demonstrate that all models achieved strong detection performance, with YOLOv12-Medium attaining mAP@0.5:0.95 = 0.978 and YOLOv8-Small achieving $\mathbf{F 1}=0.997$ and recall $=1.000$. In terms of efficiency, YOLOv8 models achieved up to $\mathbf{2 8 \%}$ faster inference and 35% shorter training time than YOLOv12 counterparts, confirming their suitability for real-time UAVbased applications. These findings highlight the complementary strengths of both architectures-YOLOv12 for peak accuracy and YOLOv8 for real-time efficiencyproviding a practical foundation for scalable, data-driven precision agriculture and intelligent plantation management.

title: Design and Analysis of CNTFET based INDEP 8T-Boosted Radiation Hardened SRAM Cell for Aerospace Applications
authors: Praveen R, Suryaprakash Tss, Kirubasankar K, R. M. Bommi
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Recent Trends in Electrical, Electronics and Computing Technologies (ICRTEECT)
abstract: In nanoscale SRAM design, issues related to cell stability, power dissipation, and delay are increasingly critical in the nanoscale SRAM design due to aggressive scaling of technology geometries. This paper presents a low-power and high-performance 8T SRAM cell design based on CNTFET technology for aerospace applications and the input-dependent (INDEP) technique. The proposed design addresses critical challenges in nanoscale SRAM, including high leakage currents, power dissipation, and stability issues. This architecture in comparison to conventional 6T and 12T SRAM cells brings notable performance gains. Superior stability is ensured by a read SNM of 402.5 mV, hold SNM of 402.2 mV, and write SNM of 423.6 mV. The design also shows faster operations, with a write time of 1.3 ns and read time of 1.8 ns, while maintaining energy efficiency with reduced read energy (18 aJ) and write energy (22 aJ). The static power consumption is also reduced to 0.7 µW with a considerable improvement compared with the traditional architectures. This work has set the basis for the 8T INDEP-CNTFET SRAM cell as an upcoming choice for next-generation energy-efficient memory applications in modern VLSI systems. Additionally, WSNM write access time is improved along with read access speed, which decreases and energy efficiency increases. These make the proposed 8T INDEP-CNTFET SRAM cell one of the promising solutions for future nanoscale memory applications.

title: Optimizing Prompt Engineering for Large Language Model-Based AI Systems
authors: Naga Sai Mrunal Vuppala, Kishore Subramanya Hebbar, Vishal Sharma, Vandana Roy
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Smart Innovations in Systems, Infrastructure, Mechanical, Power, AI and Computing Technologies (SISIMPACT)
abstract: The advent of large language models (LLMs) may have been a sudden increase in the use, which undoubtedly may intensify the hurry in promoting the precocious and accidental engineering of these models to enhance their accuracy, reliability, and ability to respond to specific contexts. With increased efficiency in different applications and reduced cheating with increased accuracy of performance, the AI systems will finally realise timely plans optimisation. In response to these problems, this study is devoted to the development of effective structure on the principles of automatic tuning prompts, reinforcement learning and passage embedding to the high-quality response as well as to the motivating stimulus to the effective cost of computing power. The integration of cognition together with flexible feedback chains make it possible to respond and understand various demands. There is also evaluation metrics scaling anticipated to evaluate the degree of timeliness-based optimization in terms of relevance, efficiency, and practicality. The strategy is also aimed at decreasing the consumption of energy in turning internationally as per the requirements of designing AI in a sustainable manner. The framework is also more adapt to and more responsive, in comparison with traditional methods, because of comparative validation. With these reactions seeming to suggest the feasibility of streamline the scenario of prompt engineering to redraw the human-AI engagement, and to enable the future smart infrastructures in the form of the LLC-based practices capable of working with the particularly elevated degree of transparency, scale-ability, and domain sustainability. The suggested system resulted in higher performance because it did higher than other methods where the highest degree of accuracy of 95% was through semantic clarity and the lowest at 93% through contextual accuracy.

title: Deep Learning for Multi-Tag Ambient Backscatter Communications
authors: Talha Akyildiz, Hessam Mahdavifar
publish: 2025 59th Asilomar Conference on Signals, Systems, and Computers
abstract: This paper presents a deep learning based prototype classifier for multi-tag detection in ambient backscatter communication (AmBC) systems. Classical detection approaches, e.g., multi hypothesis likelihood ratio test (LRT), are theoretically optimal yet presuppose accurate channel state information (CSI) for each tag. Acquiring precise CSI in AmBC is challenging due to weak backscatter signals, uncooperative ambient sources, and passive tag constraints. We address this by proposing a prototype based neural detection approach that learns robust feature embeddings from received signal statistics. By leveraging pilot symbols within each frame, our method forms prototypes of possible multi-tag states in an embedding space, and data symbols are then classified via nearest prototype matching, avoiding explicit channel estimation. For comparison, we also derive an optimal multi hypothesis LRT assuming perfect CSI and present an energy detector (ED) without CSI. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed solution approaches near optimal performance and significantly outperforms naive detection strategies without requiring CSI.

title: A Novel Hybrid Conical-Linear Laser Registration Method for Mixed Reality Navigation
authors: Ziyu Qi, Shiyu Zhang, Jiashu Zhang, Xiaolei Chen, Christopher Nimsky, Miriam H. A. Bopp
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and eXtended and Virtual Reality (AIxVR)
abstract: Mixed Reality Navigation (MRN) has been proposed as a low-cost and intuitive alternative to conventional navigation systems, yet its adoption remains limited by registration inaccuracy and strong user dependence. This work presents a Hybrid Conical-Linear Laser Registration (HCLR) method that combines a conical projection onto five coplanar fiducials with a single linear-laser constraint to recover all six degrees of freedom analytically. A systematic evaluation of 57 marker layouts identified three robust configurations, which were tested in simulated scenarios using clinical CT and MRI datasets from 19 patients. Under realistic noise conditions, HCLR achieved a mean target registration error (TRE) of 1.06 mm compared with 2.57 mm for standard fiducial-based registration, and nearly eliminated rotation errors. These findings demonstrate that HCLR can improve registration accuracy, reduce operator dependence, and provide a compact, hardware-light solution for MRN. With further physical and clinical validation, HCLR has the potential to advance cost-effective neurosurgical navigation.

title: AI-Based Framework for Signal Integrity Analysis and Design Optimization in High-Speed Systems
authors: Ravi Sekhar Kommuri
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Emerging Trends in Computing and Communication (ETCOM)
abstract: The paper presents a framework of artificial intelligence to be used to analyze signal integrity and optimize the design of high-speed systems. The suggested methodology uses hybrid neural networks in prediction of key SI parameters, and it is found to scale to 200 folds faster than standard electromagnetic simulations and still maintain accuracy at 2% of full wave accuracy. The framework combines to use of the reinforcement learning to optimize the placement of the components, convolutional neural networks to analyze the eye diagrams in real-time. It has been shown to be <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{3 5}$</tex> percent faster in crosstalk; <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{2 8}$</tex> percent faster in jitter and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{4 2}$</tex> percent faster in design convergence, when validated against PCIE 6.0 and DDR5 interfaces. The given AI-based solution enables the active SI avoidance regarding various high-speed standards, which makes it a potentially transformative solution to the next-generation electronic design.

title: SightBySound: Real-Time Sonification of Stereo Depth Maps Using Hilbert Curves for Assistive Navigation Supported by a Virtual Training Environment
authors: Milian Ingco, Sejong Yoon
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and eXtended and Virtual Reality (AIxVR)
abstract: Navigating unfamiliar indoor environments poses a significant challenge for people with visual impairments, especially in areas with unpredictable or overhead obstacles that cannot be detected by traditional aids such as canes or guide dogs. In this paper, we present a low-cost portable sensory substitution device (SSD) that enables users to perceive spatial depth using audio. Our method uses a novel real-time sonification technique based on the Hilbert curve, a space-filling curve that maps twodimensional depth images into a one-dimensional audio signal. Unlike prior approaches that sonify images through sequential scanning, our system represents entire scenes simultaneously by assigning frequencies and amplitudes to pixels based on position and depth. We show that this approach enables real-time audio rendering on resource-constrained hardware such as the Raspberry Pi 5 through an efficient multithreaded implementation. Our system fills a critical perception gap between obstacles at the waist and head level, offering a new form of spatial awareness to visually impaired users. In addition, we propose a virtual training environment to ease the steep learning curve of smart assistive devices. With a user study, we show that the virtual training environment may reduce the barriers to adoption. Our contributions lay the groundwork for more intuitive and efficient SSDs that can enhance independent navigation without costly hardware.

title: Aspect-Based Sentiment Analysis for Arabic Business Insights with Generative Language Models
authors: Asma Shayea, M.A. Mangoud
publish: 2025 International Conference on Innovation and Intelligence for Informatics, Computing, and Technologies (3ICT)
abstract: With the growth of online reviews across social media and e-commerce platforms, businesses face increasing difficulty in monitoring and leveraging customer feedback. Traditional sentiment analysis lacks the depth required for actionable insights. Research on Aspect-Based Sentiment Analysis (ABSA), with generative large language models (LLMs), and efficient fine-tuning for the Arabic language remains limited. This paper presents a scalable Arabic ABSA framework and introduces a practical approach to fine-tune LLMs such as T5, GPT, DeepSeek, and Allam using Low-Rank Adaptation (LoRA) and prompt-based optimization to achieve high accuracy on limited Arabic datasets with reduced computational cost. Among the evaluated models, GPT-4o-mini achieved the best performance in aspect extraction (F1-score 0.80), while Allam excelled in sentiment classification (F1-score 0.83). Finally, the paper demonstrates how these results enable businesses to extract actionable insights through a real-world analytics platform built upon the proposed framework.

title: Optimising Battery Energy Storage Systems for Wind Power: A Multi-Objective Approach Balancing Grid Stability and Battery Lifespan
authors: Subankan Balasubramaniam, Ahilan Kanagasundaram, Arulampalam Atputharajah, Kapila Bandara
publish: 2025 IEEE 19th International Conference on Industrial and Information Systems (ICIIS)
abstract: This paper proposes a novel multi-objective optimisation framework to balance grid stability and battery lifespan in wind power systems integrated with Battery Energy Storage Systems (BESS), using real-world data from the Thambapavani wind power plant. The framework introduces a trade-off parameter, alpha, to balance grid stability, measured as the sum of squared deviations from a constant output, against battery stress, quantified by charge/discharge activity, with state of charge (SOC) modelled considering efficiency. Uniquely, it integrates rain flow counting for accurate battery cycle analysis and lifespan estimation, addressing a gap in prior studies that often overlook degradation. The approach achieves stable power output by optimising alpha across a Pareto front and selecting an optimal trade-off via a weighted scoring mechanism while extending battery life to approximately 5.1 years for the optimal battery capacity at an alpha value of 0.3. This work’s novelty lies in combining multi-objective optimisation with rain flow counting and real-time SOC validation, offering a robust, data-driven tool for sustainable BESS management in renewable energy systems.

title: Accelerated Alternating Optimization of MIMO Transceivers
authors: Zilu Zhao, Fangqing. Xiao, Dirk Slock
publish: 2025 59th Asilomar Conference on Signals, Systems, and Computers
abstract: Various majorization approaches allow to transform a Weighted Sum Rate criterion into a cost function that is quadratic in Tx or in Rx and is simple to optimize in terms of other parameters. The standard strategy is then to apply alternating minimization to the majorizer. These alternating approaches converge fairly slowly. Another issue that they typically are guaranteed to converge, but to a local optimum. Deterministic annealing has been proposed to find the global optimum. To reduce convergence time, acceleration techniques can be introduced. In this paper we review existing acceleration methods. Two big families arise, either Nesterov acceleration or Successive Over-Relaxation. The question arises and remains open which approach would be best suited to alternating precoder design.

title: Smart Voting System with Face Recognition, OTP Verification, and National ID Integration: A Secure and Accessible Approach to Modern Elections
authors: T. Srilekha, Mamidala Sruthi, Srilatha Komakula
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Recent Trends in Electrical, Electronics and Computing Technologies (ICRTEECT)
abstract: The Smart Voting System is designed to increase the efficiency and security levels of elections through the in-corporation of facial recognition and biometric authentication. It allows voters to vote from anywhere or at a polling station without physical voter ID verification, thus reducing the chances of fraud. Deep learning algorithms will be used for accurate facial recognition and real-time validation against a centralised database. Dual authentication, combining Aadhar ID and facial recognition, ensures voter authenticity while protecting data. The system automates the voting process, minimizes manual intervention, and provide secure, transparent, and tamper-proof elections with real-time monitoring and fast result publication.

title: Smart Attendance Framework Using RFID and Fingerprint-Based Dual Authentication with Real-Time Email Notifications
authors: Vidya Sagar Potharaju, Nelli Anil Kumar, Nara Pranay, Naeikini Arun
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Recent Trends in Electrical, Electronics and Computing Technologies (ICRTEECT)
abstract: This paper proposes a Smart Attendance System that leverages RFID and Fingerprint Authentication through Dual-Factor Authentication to enhance reliability and security in educational institutions. Built using Raspberry Pi, the system addresses vulnerabilities in single-factor methods by verifying identity through both RFID tags and biometric data. It ensures accurate attendance tracking while preventing proxy entries. Upon successful verification, the system sends real-time SMS and Email Notifications to students and faculty, promoting transparency and accountability. Designed as a cost-effective and scalable Educational Technology solution, this framework strengthens institutional Security and simplifies attendance management through automation and real-time communication features.

title: Real-time On-device Inference for ECG Classification of Ventricular Dysfunction
authors: Antonio Mendoza, Cindy Nguyen, Joseph R. Cavallaro
publish: 2025 59th Asilomar Conference on Signals, Systems, and Computers
abstract: We present the development of a portable embedded system for real-time electrocardiogram (ECG) beat classification for ventricular dysfunction related arrhythmias and conditions. These conditions are clinically relevant for monitoring heart failure patients and possible Left Ventricular Assist Device (LVAD) need, as well as potentially enabling the monitoring of remodeling of the native heart after LVAD implantation. We create a dataset combining single-lead standard datasets from Physionet repository. The system processes single-lead ECG signals using a lightweight, quantized, hardware-accelerated model based on residual connections. We run the proof of concept in an ARM Cortex-M33-based SoC, measuring an inference time of 119 milliseconds with 0.95 mJ of energy consumption at 78 MHz, Flash usage of 709.6KB, and runtime RAM usage of 24.7 KB, with an average AUC of 0.9946 in the multiclass classification task. We validated the system end-to-end using a patient simulator and the MAX30003 sensor. The proposed system enables continuous monitoring without external computation, paving the way towards integration with LVAD systems to allow for patient monitoring.

title: Discovering Sparse Recovery Algorithms Using Neural Architecture Search
authors: Patrick Yubeaton, Sarthak Gupta, M. Salman Asif, Chinmay Hegde
publish: 2025 59th Asilomar Conference on Signals, Systems, and Computers
abstract: The design of novel algorithms for solving inverse problems in signal processing is an incredibly difficult, heuristic-driven, and time-consuming task. In this short paper, we the idea of automated algorithm discovery in the signal processing context through meta-learning tools such as Neural Architecture Search (NAS). Specifically, we examine the Iterative Shrinkage Thresholding Algorithm (ISTA) and its accelerated Fast ISTA (FISTA) variant as candidates for algorithm "rediscovery". We develop a meta-learning framework which is capable of rediscovering (several key elements of) the two aforementioned algorithms when given a search space of over 50,000 variables. We then show how our framework can apply to various data distributions and algorithms besides ISTA/FISTA.

title: Enhancing Intrusion Detection with Transformer Models: A Self-Attention Approach for Log-Based Cyber Threat Analysis
authors: Md. Amit Hasan Tonmoy, S.M. Mahabubul Alam, Muhammad Abul Kalam Azad
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Emerging Trends in Computing and Communication (ETCOM)
abstract: System logs are vital for detecting malicious activities but their quantity, complexity, and the continuously changing nature of attack patterns create considerable difficulties for conventional intrusion detection systems. To overcome these challenges, Transformer models utilizing self-attention processes have been studied for their capacity to analyze long-range temporal and semantic connections in diverse log data. This study employed fine-tuned CyberBERT and LogBERT frameworks on publicly accessible log datasets (HDFS, BGL, Thunderbird) and synthetic SIEM-generated traces that replicate real-world attack situations. Performance was evaluated against deep learning baselines (LSTM, GRU, CNN) and traditional anomaly detectors (Isolation Forest, One-Class SVM) employing assessment criteria including Accuracy, Precision, Recall, F1-score, AUC, False Positive/Negative Rates, and detection latency. The proposed SAIDS architecture obtains an F1 score of 95.6 and a PR-AUC of 98.1, exceeding both traditional and Transformer benchmarks. It enhances zero-day detection by up to +12 AUPRC points and decreases miss rates at 1% FPR by approximately 40% compared to LogBERT, while maintaining sub-20 ms latency and throughput above 50k events/s, proving it suitable for real-time SIEM implementation. The feasibility of deployment was confirmed by incorporating the optimal model into an ELK/Splunk pipeline, facilitating real-time inference using lightweight APIs. These findings confirm that self-attention-driven architectures are flexible, reliable, and effective solutions for log-based intrusion detection, with possible applications in multi-source surveillance and continuous learning inside SIEM systems.

title: Smart Home Technology Usage Among Aging and Midlife American Populations: A Systematic Literature Review
authors: Srinivas Thadakapally, Elyson De La Cruz, James O. Webb
publish: 2025 International Conference on Innovation and Intelligence for Informatics, Computing, and Technologies (3ICT)
abstract: The concept of Aging-in-Place is slowly gaining popularity among seniors due to the accelerating number of aging populations, paving the way for smart home technology (SHT) adoption, in enhancing independence, comfort, convenience, and alleviating the burden of care. The main objective of this systematic literature review is to synthesize insights on smart home technology adoption based on extant literature. The academic literature served as the basis of this systematic review, as retrieved from academic and research databases, including Google Scholar, PubMed, ScienceDirect, ACM Digital Library, SpringerLink, and IEEE Explore. Articles were selected from peer-reviewed journals (2020-2024) that focused on smart home technologies and their impact on older adults. The articles were reviewed to ensure their credibility and eligibility, and utilized to extract important data and findings. A total of 11 articles were included in the synthesis of the systematic review after the selection phases, data extraction, review, and assessments. The review highlighted important knowledge and insights on the adoption of smart home devices among older American citizens. The findings revealed that SHTs are important tools in alleviating the burden of care among older populations, providing benefits such as enhanced convenience, independence, safety, and remote health monitoring. It also highlighted the nascent stages of SHT research focused on aging populations.

title: Optimization of Waveform and Wavefront for Sub-Terahertz Communications Systems
authors: Sota Uchimura, Josep Miquel Jornet, Koji Ishibashi
publish: 2025 59th Asilomar Conference on Signals, Systems, and Computers
abstract: This paper investigates beamforming techniques for sub-terahertz communications, which play a vital role in the optimization of the waveform and wavefront. Specifically, to overcome frequency-selective fading and severe signal attenuation while maintaining low peak-to-average power ratio (PAPR), a joint design of an equalizer and beamformers for single-carrier (SC) transmissions is proposed. In addition, beamforming designs with unique wavefronts are introduced to exploit near-field effects in performance improvement. The theoretical and numerical analyses confirm that the proposed SC transmissions achieve lower bit error rate (BER) than orthogonal frequency division multiplexing (OFDM) without sacrificing PAPR performance, and that adjusting wavefronts according to communication conditions is required to optimize communication performance.

title: Fuzzy Technique for Active Power Flow Control in Grid-Connected Converters in Unbalanced Environments
authors: Pradyumna Kumar Dhal, Vempalle Rafi, Shaik Hussain Vali, Sadhu Radha Krishna, Chandra Shekhar M, Vanam Satyanarayana
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Recent Trends in Electrical, Electronics and Computing Technologies (ICRTEECT)
abstract: This study controls watt power flow in grid-tied converters in an imbalanced situation using SVPWM. This study proposes zero-sequence adjusted voltage support for converter-interfaced devices to improve voltage suppression. Its purpose is to reliably keep the 3-ph voltages at the link point within the given welfare limits. The suggested method accounts for the zero-sequence component and active power injection in its compensation. The suggested vss may be used in resistive distribution systems, unlike previous approaches. However, the contribution provided by this effort is small. In the second piece of work, we suggest merging zcvs with the zero-capacitance voltage source and the finite watt power oscillation. Sustaining the alternating current host grid in the face of highly unbalanced faults while reducing oscillation (lapo) to the voltage source with zero capacitance (zcvs). This capability allows the ac host grid to be supported in the face of severely unbalanced faults while restricting oscillation to a user-specified value. Finally, a formula for the maximum active power delivery (mapd) of the zcvs to the alternating current grid has been created. Using the fuzzy method and complementary approaches, chosen simulation test scenarios are employed to assess the successful outcomes of the proposed support system.

title: Power-Aware Streaming Scan Network Implementation for Improved Test Coverage and IR Drop Mitigation
authors: Praveenkumar Jayakumar, Sudhagar G
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Recent Trends in Electrical, Electronics and Computing Technologies (ICRTEECT)
abstract: As integrated circuits (ICs) continue to scale in complexity and density, ensuring efficient and reliable testing becomes increasingly critical. This dissertation presents a comprehensive approach to enhancing scan-based testing methodologies through the implementation of the Streaming Scan Network (SSN). The proposed work focuses on improving test coverage and efficiency while addressing key challenges such as IR drop during the capture phase. To mitigate these issues, pipeline stages are strategically introduced at the On-Chip Controller (OCC) level, thereby reducing power-related anomalies and improving signal integrity during high-speed testing.The study also explores scalable SSN architectures that support design-for-testability (DFT) in large and hierarchical designs. By leveraging industry-standard tools such as Tessent Shell, the SSN framework is implemented, simulated, and optimized to ensure robust functionality. The methodology includes detailed verification and debugging processes to identify and resolve scan chain anomalies, ensuring accurate fault detection and diagnosis.Through architectural enhancements and tool-driven optimization, this work demonstrates significant improvements in test reliability, power efficiency, and scalability. The proposed SSN-based testing framework offers a practical and effective solution for modern IC verification, contributing to faster design closure and higher manufacturing yield.

title: Toward algorithmic workflows for passive correlation-based radar imaging
authors: Julien Chaput, Secil Dogan, James Wright, Mohammad Al-Khaldi, Joel Johnson, Emre Ertin, Jeremiah Lightner, Morris Cohen, David Hysell
publish: 2025 59th Asilomar Conference on Signals, Systems, and Computers
abstract: Passive correlation-based methods have revolutionized seismic imaging and temporal monitoring over the last several decades by implementing blind use of the complete source environment. Under the support of DARPA’s Defense Application of Innovative Remote Sensing program, we here present an initial study aimed at practical interpretation of array-based passive correlation radar methods with an emphasis on geometric untangling of range-Doppler images. Both isotropic and azimuthally biased source distributions are considered, and we describe through both real and synthetic examples how target information may be recovered in each scenario. We present results from single closely spaced linear arrays and show that interpretability, correlation function convergence, and an understanding of the source environment can lead to usable information. This precludes the need for the transmitter location and waveform modeling involved in classic passive radar approaches, and opens the possibility of using distributed receiver networks to broadly image and monitor environmental and anthropogenic targets.

title: Performance and Power Quality Improvement of Standalone PV Systems: An Approach Using CNN-MPPT and Output Filtering
authors: Riddhi Maratha, Sanju Tanwar, Seema Agrawal
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Emerging Trends in Computing and Communication (ETCOM)
abstract: This paper evaluates the performance of a standalone photovoltaic (PV) system that uses a convolutional neural network (CNN) for Maximum Power Point Tracking (MPPT) control. The PV array is modeled in MATLAB/Simulink (2023b) and is linked to a DC-DC boost converter. This converter is managed by the CNN-MPPT algorithm to maximize power extraction in changing irradiance and temperature conditions. A standard Pulse Width Modulation (PWM)-controlled inverter interfaces the DC power to an AC load. The system performance is analyzed in terms of voltage and current waveforms, output power, and Total Harmonic Distortion (THD). The system's performance looks at voltage and current waveforms, output power, and Total Harmonic Distortion (THD). Simulation results show how well the CNN-MPPT controller improves power quality and keeps the standalone PV system running smoothly, following the IEEE 5191992 standards.

title: AIrtist: VR Framework for Interactive Engagement via Stroke-Based Neural Rendering
authors: Edward Scott Carvalho Johnson, Arthur Galdino Dangoni, Bianca Mirtes Araújo Miranda, Pedro Elias Engelberg Silva Borges, Diogo Fernandes, Arlindo Rodrigues Galvão Filho
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and eXtended and Virtual Reality (AIxVR)
abstract: The preservation and dissemination of cultural heritage in modern society rely heavily on the accessibility of artistic knowledge; however, traditional museum experiences often remain passive, limiting visitors to visual appreciation without offering insight into the procedural complexities of artistic creation. Static exhibitions fail to capture the temporal evolution and decision-making processes required to construct a masterpiece. To address this gap, we propose “AIrtist,” an immersive Virtual Reality (VR) framework designed to bridge art appreciation and interactive engagement. Our system leverages the Meta Oculus Quest 3 to immerse users in a reconstruction of an artist's studio, specifically focusing on the works of Vincent van Gogh. The methodology integrates a modular pipeline: a theoretical learning phase driven by Large Language Models (LLMs) and ElevenLabs Text-to-Speech (TTS) for contextual narration, and a practical gamified painting phase grounded in Deep Reinforcement Learning (DRL). We utilize the “Learning to Paint” model to decompose images into stroke sequences. To translate these algorithmic outputs into human-interpretable guides, we developed a novel stroke analysis engine that employs Principal Component Analysis (PCA) and connected component labeling on differential frame masks to extract geometric stroke vectors. Qualitative results demonstrate that our pipeline successfully decomposes texture-rich paintings into coherent, interactive steps, allowing users to experience the construction of complex artistic styles within the virtual environment.

title: Towards Augmented Reality Authoring Tools That Ensure Cognitive Accessibility of Instructions
authors: Valentin Knoben, Jonas Blattgerste, Thies Pfeiffer, Björn Hein, Christian Wurl
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and eXtended and Virtual Reality (AIxVR)
