Furthermore, a Gradient-Aware Metric Function interprets gradients as directional vectors to adaptively refine sample–prototype relations, thereby overcoming the limitations of conventional scalar distance metrics. Extensive experiments on multiple cross-domain benchmarks demonstrate that GRASP-Net achieves superior accuracy and robustness compared with state-of-the-art methods, particularly in scenarios with limited labeled samples and large domain shifts.

title: Low Noise and High Resolution Micro-thrust Measurement Under Heavy Loads for Space-borne Gravitational Wave Detection
authors: Jiabin Wang, Jianfei Long, Mingshan Wu, Jiawen Xu, Ning Guo, Detian Li
publish: IEEE Transactions on Instrumentation and Measurement
abstract: The Taiji program is a space-borne gravitational wave detection project conducted in heliocentric orbits, utilizing micro-thrusters for drag-free control to maintain satellites in an ultra-quiet and ultra-stable state. High-precision and wide-band ground evaluation of the thrusters is crucial. As the mass of micro-propulsion systems increases, this imposes requirements for low thrust-to-weight ratios and low noise on micro-thrust measurement devices. This paper develops a flexible inverted pendulum-based micro-thrust measurement device capable of handling heavy loads. With an experimental mass of 8 kg, by adjusting the center of mass to coincide with the center of the flexible element, the system’s elastic restoring force is provided solely by the flexible element, ultimately achieving a minimum resolution of 0.05 μN for the sub-micro-Newton thrust measurement system. The background noise is better than 0.1 μN/Hz−1/2 in the 0.1 mHz to 1 Hz range, Partial frequency point dynamic thrust inversion tests were also conducted, providing a strong ground testing foundation for the engineering implementation of the Taiji program.

title: A Multi-Component Sparse Near-Field Scanning with Peak-and-Trough Searching
authors: Di Wang, Xing-Chang Wei, Si-Yao Tang, Yu Tian, Ming-Jie Pang, Richard Xian-Ke Gao
publish: IEEE Transactions on Instrumentation and Measurement
abstract: In order to improve the efficiency of traditional near-field scanning measurement, a multi-component sparse near-field sampling method is introduced in this article. The proposed method features three strategies: equal-height searching, random distribution, and peak-and-trough searching. This method is capable of effectively detecting both peaks and troughs of near-field distribution patterns, ensuring sufficient accuracy to characterize multiple components of electromagnetic field. Two simulation examples are employed in efficiency validation of the proposed method. Subsequently, the proposed method is coded into a practical near-field scanning measurement system for evaluation using three real-world cases. Compared to the traditional full scan method, the proposed method reduces the scanning time to 26.97% for a wireless router, 25.37% for a WIFI module, and 44.67% for a camera module. Furthermore, the relative errors for all electromagnetic field components of the three devices under test remain below 7%, certifying the effectiveness and robustness of the proposed method.

title: SyndromeCode: Feedback-Assisted Concatenated Coding based on Transformer
authors: Jonggyu Jang, Hongjae Nam, Vishrant Tripathi, David J. Love
publish: 2025 59th Asilomar Conference on Signals, Systems, and Computers
abstract: Deep learning-based feedback coding has demonstrated superior performance over classical linear counterparts under specific channel conditions by learning robust non-linear mappings. To achieve better reliability, these neural codes are typically deployed as inner codes within a concatenated architecture, wrapped around an outer linear error-correcting codes (ECC). However, existing data-driven architectures typically treat the feedback coding as a black-box optimization, done independently of the selection of the outer ECC. Unfortunately, this means the data-driven architecture is not optimized to exploit the algebraic structure of the outer code, resulting in an inability to consider the underlying outer code structure when they are concatenated. To bridge this gap, we propose SyndromeCode, a novel feedback coding architecture that explicitly incorporates the outer code structure via syndrome inputs. Instead of relying solely on raw received signals, SyndromeCode leverages a soft syndrome vector—a differentiable indicator of parity violations derived from the outer code structure—and feeds it directly into the Transformer-based encoder and decoder. Furthermore, we demonstrate the versatility of this approach in multiple-input multiple-output (MIMO) multi-stream scenarios, as well as different spatial noise correlations. Our extensive results demonstrate that SyndromeCode outperforms the baselines by validating the power of injecting domain knowledge into deep learning-based feedback coding architecture.

title: Modeling a Pupillary Response on Reaching Movement in the Virtual Environment
authors: Sachika Takemori, Juri Nakagawa, Shintaro Nakatani
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and eXtended and Virtual Reality (AIxVR)
abstract: This study proposes a model to quantitatively describe the relationship between the pupillary response during reaching movements and the dynamic characteristics of visual targets, using a system identification approach as a proxy for locus coeruleus - norepinephrine (LC-NE) system activity to enable its real-time assessment during motor tasks. Participants performed a motor adaptation task using a robot manipulator integrated with virtual reality (VR). The estimated model confirmed system stability and revealed a delay of approximately 1.0 s in the onset of the response. This prolonged delay is interpreted to reflect the total cognitive-motor time, including LC-NE modulation. For the relationship between movement magnitude and maximum pupillary dilation, the gain derived from the FIR model was shown to be relatively close to the gain obtained by applying the least squares method to the experimental data, with a relative difference of approximately 25%. This result suggests that, despite the known nonlinearity of the pupillary response, the linear model approach can capture the principal trend of the system's dynamic characteristics. The proposed model provides a novel framework for real-time quantitative evaluation of LC-NE system activation during motor learning and is expected to contribute to the design of optimized rehabilitation protocols.

title: Recent Regulatory Actions in 6 GHz
authors: Monisha Ghosh
abstract: There have been a number of recent developments, worldwide, in the 6 GHz band (5.925 – 7.125 GHz) since the first regulatory decision almost six years ago by the U.S. Federal Communications Commission (FCC) that permitted unlicensed use of the band for the first time. On April 24, 2020, the FCC issued the First Report and Order (R&O) and Further Notice of Proposed Rulemaking (FNPRM) for the 6 GHz band, permitting unlicensed use on a shared basis <xref ref-type="bibr" rid="ref1" xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">[1]</xref>. A past column in October 2023 <xref ref-type="bibr" rid="ref2" xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">[2]</xref> described the incumbents in the band and how regulations had evolved in various parts of the world to support emerging use cases. Recently, there have been a number of significant developments worldwide in developing new regulations in the band, which will be the subject of this column. These recent developments lead to new research problems related to spectrum sharing in 6 GHz.

title: Active Learning for Joint AoA and CSI Acquisition in mmWave Systems
authors: Chi-Shiang Gau, Tara Javidi
publish: 2025 59th Asilomar Conference on Signals, Systems, and Computers
abstract: Millimeter-wave (mmWave) communication systems require accurate initial beam alignment to overcome high path loss and fully leverage its directional transmission. Joint estimation of the channel and angle of arrival (AoA) must respect hardware limitations, most notably the random phase distortions in analog phase arrays. In this paper, we propose a two-stage beam alignment strategy that operates without prior channel knowledge and relies solely on magnitude measurements. In the first stage, a coarse angular scan ensures that the true AoA is captured within one of the scanning beams, enabling a reliable estimate of the channel gain. The second stage refines the AoA estimate through a fine-grained active search algorithm guided by the marginal posterior probability. Simulation results show that our method outperforms existing approaches in scenarios with unknown CSI, achieving superior beamforming accuracy and channel estimation.

title: Automatic Wire-Harness Color Sequence Detector
authors: Indiwara Nanayakkara, Dehan Jayawickrama, Mervyn Parakrama B. Ekanayake
publish: 2025 IEEE 19th International Conference on Industrial and Information Systems (ICIIS)
abstract: Wire harness inspection process remains a labor-intensive process prone to errors in the modern Electronics Manufacturing Services (EMS) industry. This paper introduces a semiautomated machine vision system capable of verifying correct wire positioning, correctness of the connector polarity and correctness of color sequences for both linear and circular wire harness configurations. Five industrial standard CMOS cameras are integrated into a modularized mechanical framework in the physical structure of the solution and a HSV and RGB color domain value comparison based color sequence classifier is used in the operation. For each harness batch, a user can train the system using at least five reference samples; the trained file is stored and reused for similar harness types. The Solution is deployed at GPV Lanka Pvt. Ltd. (Fig. 2) and the system achieved 100% detection accuracy and reduced inspection time by 44% compared to manual methods. Additional features include user management, adjustable lighting, session data storage, and secure login. Results of this product usage in the real world situation demonstrate that this approach delivers reliable and efficient inspection capabilities. [1]–[3].

title: Multilingual Conversational AI Chatbot for Mental Health: A Scalable Solution for Personalized and Empathetic Support
authors: Suparna Das, Kamil Reza Khondakar, Hirak Mazumdar, Likhita Varma Penumathsa, Aryasree Adivishnu
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Smart Innovations in Systems, Infrastructure, Mechanical, Power, AI and Computing Technologies (SISIMPACT)
abstract: The increasing demand for accessible mental health support highlights the need for scalable and inclusive solutions. This study presents a multilingual conversational AI chatbot designed to provide personalized and empathetic mental health assistance. Leveraging Natural Language Processing (NLP), Machine Learning (ML), and the Gemini API, the chatbot can understand user sentiment, detect emotional cues, and deliver contextually appropriate responses across multiple languages. Integrating Cognitive Behavioral Therapy (CBT) principles, it offers therapeutic interventions such as mood tracking, coping strategies, and emotional guidance. The chatbot's architecture ensures user privacy through encrypted communication and enables scalability for large user bases. Experimental results demonstrate its ability to engage users effectively, identify emotional states accurately, and provide supportive, human-like interactions. This system contributes to making mental health care more accessible, inclusive, and responsive, marking a step forward in AI-driven psychological support and early mental health intervention.

title: Google Cloud Implementation of Deep Learning Models for Crop Health Classification
authors: Tushar Mishra, Shivansh Tiwari, Sarthak Yadav, Vinod Kumar
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Emerging Trends in Computing and Communication (ETCOM)
abstract: This work presents an integrated framework for deploying and optimizing deep learning models for crop health classification that makes use of Google Cloud's computational infrastructure. The designed platform gets its input from the high-resolution agricultural images taken by drone and IoT sensors and processes them using Google Cloud Storage and TensorFlow APIs. Various convolutional neural network (CNN) architectures, namely ResNet50, InceptionV3, and EfficientNet, were implemented and trained on the Google Cloud AI Platform to determine the health condition of crops and to distinguish between healthy, stressed, and diseased categories. The models were scored based on a set of criteria including accuracy, precision, recall, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{F 1}$</tex>-score, and inference latency. It was found that EfficientNet not only achieved the highest classification accuracy of 96.8% but was also able to significantly reduce the computational cost and training time. Moreover, the use of Google Cloud's scalable services made it possible to perform data handling in an efficient manner, thus allowing for model deployment to be done at a faster pace and for classification to be carried out in real-time over a large area of agricultural monitoring.

title: Code-Constrained Inverse Design of Concrete Mixes with Deep Generative Models and Realism-Enhanced Dataset Schema
authors: Swajit Singh Goud, Rahul Shrivastava, Trasha Bansal
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Smart Innovations in Systems, Infrastructure, Mechanical, Power, AI and Computing Technologies (SISIMPACT)
abstract: This paper presents a code-constrained inverse concrete mix design framework that integrates ensemble learning, optimization, and deep generative modeling to produce realistic and compliant mixes tailored to specified strength and workability targets. A critical enhancement of the framework lies in its dataset schema: beyond mix proportions, each record includes essential material descriptors–specific g ravities (SG), fineness modulus (FM), aggregate gradation, absorption, moisture contents, and measured slump. These fields ensure volumetric consistency, enable water–cement corrections, and capture rheological effects critical to transferability across local aggregate sources. The framework operates through two complementary tracks: (i) an ensemble–optimizer pipeline combining a weighted regressor ensemble with genetic and Bayesian search for accurate but narrow solutions; and (ii) a conditional variational autoencoder (CVAE) conditioned on target strength, slump, and exposure, which generates diverse candidates filtered for compliance with IS 456:2000 and IS 10262:2019. Evaluation results demonstrate that the dual-track framework achieves low prediction error (RMSE <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$=2.0 \text{MPa}$</tex> for compressive strength, 9 mm for slump), <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$>93 \%$</tex> compliance, and reduced cement utilization compared to IS 10262 baselines. Inclusion of SG, FM, and absorption/moisture corrections improved generalization and slump prediction by <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$>15 \%$</tex> relative to proportion-only baselines. The hybrid framework thus delivers robust, scalable, and sustainable mix recommendations, ensuring practical transferability and compliance across regions with differing aggregate characteristics. Code-Constrained Inverse Design of Concrete Mixes with Deep Generative Models and Realism-Enhanced Dataset Schema

title: Towards Accelerated Healthcare Federated System Through Heterogeneous Accelerators
authors: Giuseppe Sorrentino, Davide Conficconi
publish: 2025 35th International Conference on Field-Programmable Logic and Applications (FPL)
abstract: Federated Learning (FL) enables collaborative model training across multiple hosts without exposing sensitive data. Yet, privacy-preserving is critical, introducing a nonnegligible overhead. Furthermore, while GPUs perfectly suit AI workloads, FPGAs outperform them in networking and cryptographic tasks as Fully Homomorphic Encryption (FHE), widely employed in FL to safeguard privacy. In this context, applications like Image Registration (IR), requiring different computeintensive steps, each suitable for a different architecture, even worsen the hardware dichotomy. Thus, this research explores innovative federated network structures to reduce privacy risks while assigning roles to each host, guaranteeing complete hardware acceleration per each compute-intensive task by partitioning each step on different hardware layers of modern heterogeneous platforms as Neural Processing Unitss (NPUs), combining AI Engines (AIEs) and GPUs, Versal VCK5000, combining FPGA and AIEs, and multi-platform peer-to-peer systems.

title: Ensemble Methods for Short-Term Solar Power Forecasting in Sri Lanka
authors: Aatham Lebbe Nizam, Piyumi Madhushika, Chamath Amarasinghe, Gihan Amarasinghe
publish: 2025 IEEE 19th International Conference on Industrial and Information Systems (ICIIS)
abstract: Accurate forecasting of solar power generation is crucial for island countries like Sri Lanka, particularly when large-scale solar farms are integrated into the power grid. Significant variations in solar power, resulting from the intermittent nature of solar irradiance, impact the reliability and stability of the power grid. This work reports a novel approach for predicting short-term solar power generation using ensemble models. Historical solar power generation data were acquired from the Buruthakanda solar park in Hambantota, Sri Lanka. Different weather parameter-based machine learning algorithms, such as Artificial Neural Network (ANN), Random Forest Regression, Support Vector Regression, and time series regression models such as Long Short Term Memory (LSTM), 1-Dimensional Convolutional Neural Network (1D-CNN), and LSTM-CNN, are utilized for forecasting solar power generation. The ANN and LSTM predictions had the lowest Root Mean Square Error (RMSE) values, 0.0429 and 0.0489, respectively. Several ensemble models were developed using ANN and LSTM algorithms, and it was found that the (ANN+LSTM)+(Extreme Gradient Boosting) ensemble has the lowest RMSE of 0.04, which is 7.25% and 22.25% lower than single ANN and LSTM models. The R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> score of the ensemble model is approximately 0.975, demonstrating the reliability of the model. Additionally, the impact of Numerical Weather Prediction (NWP) errors on the accuracy of machine learning algorithms is investigated. The RMSE increases by nearly 17.5% when NWP errors are incorporated into the ensemble models.

title: Electrophysiologic Biomarkers for Detection of Neurodegenerative Diseases
authors: K.G. Harindi, D.N.T. Lawrencehewa, S.L. Nandasiri, R.D. Ranaweera, V. Weerasinghe, T.L. Dassanayake
publish: 2025 IEEE 19th International Conference on Industrial and Information Systems (ICIIS)
abstract: Electroencephalography (EEG) offers a noninvasive and cost-effective avenue for detecting early biomarkers of neurodegenerative diseases such as Alzheimer’s disease (AD) and Frontotemporal Dementia (FTD). We present a hybrid framework that integrates Graph Convolutional Neural Networks (GCNNs) with Random Forest classification to analyze brain connectivity patterns and differentiate disease states. The features used for classification were derived from the values of connectivity matrices, constructed using Phase Locking Value (PLV) and coherence measures, with alpha and theta frequency bands identified as the most discriminative for classification. To reduce noise and improve mapping of new raw EEG data points to the disease spectrum, a K-Nearest Neighbors (KNN)–based smoothing algorithm was employed. This approach enables robust differentiation between AD, FTD, and healthy controls (HC), while maintaining interpretability—a critical requirement for clinical translation. Our findings highlight the potential of EEG connectivity features combined with graph-based and interpretable machine learning methods as a pathway toward low-cost, clinically applicable diagnostic tools.

title: Enhancing Edge Computing Efficiency Through Intelligent Gateway Resource Management
authors: Saurabh Kapoor, Arishya Begum, Swati, Nupur Tyagi, Katragadda Anuhya, Nyangam Rime
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Smart Innovations in Systems, Infrastructure, Mechanical, Power, AI and Computing Technologies (SISIMPACT)
abstract: The processing speed of Internet of Things (IoT) applications rises significantly because of edge computing implementation. The existing conventional systems experience difficulties due to data loss together with functionality vulnerabilities and resource control inefficiencies. This research develops LUCR architecture with IP functionality to enhance edge server resource optimization and collaborative management under LUCR architecture with IP functionality. The researchers use Aggregative Sub-Quadratic Continual Cauchy Neural Network (ASCC-NN) to perform efficient resource distribution among Edge Service Providers (ESPs). The proposed system incorporates PBLBMO as an energy efficiency enhancement scheme which improves overall performance through reduced latency and enhanced throughput. Intelligent edge resource management techniques prove essential for optimizing real-time IoT applications because of their importance according to the study results. The presented research establishes powerful infrastructure for effective data processing that enhances system performance and security features in edge computing networks. Experimental testing indicates that the LUCR-EIGA-JO framework surpasses contemporary frameworks because it boosts operational performance and decreases energy usage and delivers better QoS metrics. The proposed model achieves 99% accuracy, with a 50% reduction in transmission delay and a 12% decrease in energy use relative to existing methods.

title: Adaptive GAN Architecture with Constrained Latent Embedding for Unsupervised Video Anomaly Detection
authors: Takveer Singh, P. Chitra, Saswati Roy Chel, Mani Ramakrishnan, Rakesh Kumar Yadav, T. Manoj Kumar
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Smart Innovations in Systems, Infrastructure, Mechanical, Power, AI and Computing Technologies (SISIMPACT)
abstract: In order to do unsupervised video anomaly identification, we provide CVAD-GAN, an adaptive GAN based framework that incorporates a latent embedding technique that is subject to restrictions. The model consists of Generator that is grounded on an encoder-decoder and a Discriminator, which are adversarially trained to discriminate amongst normal and anomalous video frames. To enhance the reconstruction faithfulness and discrimination of anomalies, the latent space is regularised with Kullback-Leibler(KL) divergence, which imposes a Gaussian prior. To enhance generalizability and robustness, input frames are adjusted using white Gaussian noise. The Generator is tuned to reconstruction of normal frames, and anomalies cause distortion because they were not seen in advance. Skip connections and dilated convolutions allow preserving spatial features, enlarging the receptive field and quality of reconstruction. The outcomes of the Discriminator and the pixel-wise restoration errors are used to compute anomalous scores, and the optimum threshold is found via the search for grids. Experiments on dataset UCSD Peds1 show that CVAD-GAN outperforms the current methods in relations of accuracy and speed of performance. The framework is compact and can be executed in resource-limited devices in real-time surveillance purposes.

title: Maximizing Resource Utilization for Stencil Computing
authors: Pedro Henrique Rosso, Guido Araujo
publish: 2025 35th International Conference on Field-Programmable Logic and Applications (FPL)
abstract: Developing FPGA applications, either using RTL or HLS, requires knowledge about the platform on which the application will be deployed. The availability of resources changes according to each FPGA model. To deal with this, the components of applications may be implemented using different primitives. For example, Floating-Point operations can be implemented using Flip-Flops and Look-up Tables, or with the addition of DSP units, the same happens for other components, such as FIFOs. During development, the implementation of the components is often chosen and used for the rest of the design. Using Integer Programming, this work explores how different floating-point implementations can be mixed to achieve performance optimization in a stencil application. Furthermore, it discusses how the same idea can be used for other components and applications.

title: FedAS-TSAD: Two-Stage Active Detection Against Data Poisoning in PFL
authors: Honglin Liu, Jie Li
publish: 2025 International Conference on Big Data and Data Mining (BDDM)
abstract: FedAS is an excellent solution for addressing Non-IID (Independent and Identically Distributed) data and client inconsistency in Federated Learning (FL). However, when facing data poisoning attacks from malicious clients, although this method adopts a passive downweighting defense mechanism, it fails to truly distinguish between malicious clients and low-quality clients. In scenarios with a high proportion of malicious clients, FedAS is at risk of failure. Thus, this paper proposes FedAS-TSAD based on tFIM (trace of Fisher Information Matrix) and PA (Federated Parameter-Alignment) parameters. Experiments on the public Cifar-100 dataset demonstrate that the attack resistance of FedAS-TSAD has improved by 4.69 % to 7.45 % compared with the original FedAS under different scenarios.

title: Bridging Linguistic Gap: A Deep Learning Approach to Tulu-Kannada Translation
authors: Spoorthi, Sudarshan K, Greeshma K, Rutika P Gowda, Rashmi K P, Prashanth Kannadaguli
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Emerging Trends in Computing and Communication (ETCOM)
abstract: The rapid advancements in natural language processing and deep learning have enabled the development of translation systems for widely spoken languages. However, low-resource languages such as Tulu remain underrepresented in computational linguistics. This paper presents a deep learning–based framework for Tulu-Kannada character-level translation. The proposed system utilizes a curated dataset of handwritten Tulu characters, comprising 458 folders of scanned and preprocessed images. Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) are employed to recognize Tulu characters, which are then mapped to their corresponding Kannada script. Experimental evaluation demonstrates promising accuracy in character recognition and translation, highlighting the potential of the system for educational and linguistic applications. The study contributes to the preservation of the Tulu language while addressing the linguistic gap between Tulu and Kannada speakers.

title: Reinforcement Learning for Robust Explosive Demolition: Overcoming Collapse Failures through Adaptive Decision Making
authors: Marios Tsaousidis, Theofanis Kalampokas, Anastasia Moutafidou, Eleni Vrochidou, George A. Papakostas
publish: 2025 International Conference on Innovation and Intelligence for Informatics, Computing, and Technologies (3ICT)
abstract: To ensure cost-effective, safe and efficient demolition of a building, careful planning of the process, as well as simulations to ensure safe collapse, are necessary. The work deals with the planning and simulation of building demolition under controlled explosion using Reinforcement Learning (RL) in a Unity simulation environment. RL offers a powerful framework for adaptive decision-making in dynamic and uncertain environments. In the realm of controlled building demolition using explosives, where precision, safety, and reliability are paramount, traditional methods fall short. The aim of this work is to define the optimal placement locations and amount of explosive material for the building to follow the desired demolition plan. Implementation includes the design of a building in Unity with realistic properties and dimensions, so that the strength and behavior of the materials during the explosion could be efficiently simulated. RL is used to determine the optimal location and quantity of explosives, while the proposed designed approach allows for the visualization of the simulation results, i.e., the progressive collapse of the building throughout its total demolition.

title: Artificial Intelligence in Phishing Detection: Enhancing Cyber security
authors: Punyaban Patel, Kilari Ram Priya, Shaheena Fatima, Ravi Kumar Chandu, S. Vasanthi, J.Jebathangam
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Recent Trends in Electrical, Electronics and Computing Technologies (ICRTEECT)
abstract: Phishing attacks remain among the most regular cybersecurity threats since they exploit human weaknesses to penetrate systems unauthorized. Security procedures that exist today are unable to identify advanced cyberattacks effectively which leads to a requirement for updated protection measures. The combination of machine learning with deep learning and natural language processing methods within artificial intelligence creates an effective tool for detecting phishing threats. Algorithms based on artificial intelligence enable the analysis of large datasets for detecting anomalous email patterns and identifying dangerous URLs while recognizing unhonest website characteristics at a level of high precision. This research investigates how artificial intelligence (AI) detects phishing through its superior capability to identify threats in real time while improving accuracy by learning from experience and establishing fewer incorrect alerts. The text addresses main obstacles like adversarial attacks, data privacy challenges along with the requirement of continuous model maintenance. The incorporation of AI-based phishing detection systems into existing cyber security frameworks leads to defense improvements that lower both data breach possibilities and financial losses for organizations.

title: Self-Supervised Learning for Network Traffic Analysis in 5G Environments
authors: Roopalatha Mangalseth Budda, Nandan Sharma, Nasmin Jiwani, Ketan Gupta, K. Suganyadevi
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Emerging Trends in Computing and Communication (ETCOM)
abstract: The extensive deployment of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{5 G}$</tex> networks facilitates more complicated issues on traffic identification, like high-speed data transmission, dynamic and diverse traffic patterns, and evolving security threats. Traditional supervised learning solutions suffer from a paucity of labeled data to input and the necessity for real-time adjustability. However, selfsupervised learning (SSL) has emerged as a beneficial alternative that utilizes unlabeled traffic data to generate strong representations that do not require manual annotations. This paper examines the SSL approaches for <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{5 G}$</tex> traffic analysis that cover anomaly detection, traffic classification, and intrusion prevention. These are then used for pre-training models on large-scale unlabeled traffic datasets with contrastive learning or reconstruction-based objectives and enable the approach to acquire valuable features that could improve downstream tasks. Their main novelties comprise adaptive pretext tasks optimized for 5 G peculiarities (e.g., slicing-aware embeddings), and lightweight edge-friendly architectures. Results on popular realworld 5G datasets show that SSL increases detection accuracy by <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 5 - 2 0 \%}$</tex> over supervised baselines in low-labeling scenarios, achieving sub-millisecond latency for real-time processing. It is also robust to new attack vectors and network conditions (high generalization). This research enhances computational efficiency for large-scale deployments by optimizing models for 5G edge nodes. The experiments further indicate that delays in secure streaming can significantly disrupt the functioning of autonomous network managers in 5 G, highlighting the importance of scalability, adaptability, and efficiency in nextgeneration traffic analysis.

title: AI for Finance: Hybrid Learning Models for Stock Prediction
authors: Padusuri SaiBabu, Maridu Bhargavi, Vemula Jayasri, Ganpisetty Pravallika, Uzma Afzal
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Emerging Trends in Computing and Communication (ETCOM)
abstract: Stock price prediction remains one of the most challenging problems in financial analytics due to market volatility, nonlinear dependencies, and sensitivity to external economic factors. Accurate stock forecasting is essential for investors and financial institutions to make informed decisions, reduce risk, and maximize returns. Traditional econometric approaches, such as ARIMA, struggle to model the nonlinear and dynamic behavior of stock data, leading to increased interest in machine learning (ML) and deep learning (DL) techniques. This paper presents a hybrid framework that integrates Support Vector Regression (SVR), Random Forest Regression (RFR), and Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) networks for stock price prediction. The methodology includes rigorous data preprocessing, feature extraction using technical indicators such as Moving Averages, RSI, MACD, Bollinger Bands, and OBV, followed by normalization for model stability. SVR and RFR serve as classical regression baselines, while LSTM captures temporal dependencies. An ensemble layer combines their predictions through weighted averaging optimized using validation performance. Experiments conducted using five years of S&P 500 daily historical data show that the LSTM model achieved an RMSE of 2.15 and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{R}^{\mathbf{2}}$</tex> of 0.91, outperforming the standalone ML models. The hybrid ensemble achieved further improvements with an RMSE of 1.92 and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{R}^{\mathbf{2}}$</tex> of 0.93, proving that hybridization enhances both robustness and predictive accuracy. The results confirm that deep learning effectively captures sequential dependencies, while ensemble methods provide resilience and generalization. Future work will incorporate sentiment analysis, macroeconomic indicators, and explainable AI (XAI) to improve interpretability and real-world adoption.

title: An Efficient Neural Network by Safe and Secured IoT for Smart Device Protection Using Lightweight AI and Edge Security
authors: L Swathi, G Anja Reddy, M Naresh, V Sravani Kumari, S.Theetchenya, B.Booba
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Recent Trends in Electrical, Electronics and Computing Technologies (ICRTEECT)
abstract: The rapid expansion of smart gadgets and the Internet of Things (IoT) has created important privacy and security issues that call for sophisticated protective systems. Many times, conventional security measures depend on cloud-based processing, which might cause more latency, high computational expenses, and possible data vulnerabilities. This work offers an effective neural network-based security system using lightweight artificial intelligence (AI) and edge security to protect IoT ecosystems, hence overcoming these constraints. While reducing resource use, the suggested paradigm improves device authentication, anomaly detection, and real-time threat detection. Processing security risks at the edge helps the system to rely less on cloud infrastructure, hence guaranteeing quicker responses and better data privacy. Experimental tests show how well the model reduces cyber risks, protects smart devices, and maximizes resource use. This work helps to provide strong, low-power, smart security systems specifically designed for IoT uses.

title: AI Copilots for Machine Learning Pipelines: A Comparative Study on Code Quality and Model Performance
authors: Nikos Flamourtzoglou, Eleni Vrochidou, George A. Papakostas
publish: 2025 International Conference on Innovation and Intelligence for Informatics, Computing, and Technologies (3ICT)
abstract: As Artificial Intelligence (AI) continues to reshape, among others, the field of software development, its integration into coding workflows opens up both opportunities and critical questions. The rise of AI-powered coding assistants has introduced a paradigm shift in the way developers approximate programming tasks. This work aims to investigate the efficacy and quality of AI-powered coding in the development of end-to-end machine learning pipelines. More specifically, a comparative study of Amazon Q, GitHub Copilot, Windsurf and Gemini is conducted, by developing two machine learning pipelines, for customer churn prediction and Titanic survival prediction. Each assistant was prompted with the same instructions towards a fair evaluation, and their outputs were assessed using static and dynamic code analysis tools, such as Pylint, Radon, SonarQube and MLflow. The evaluation is focused on code quality, model performance, feature importance, explainability and fairness. Our findings provide practical insights into the coding assistants' strengths and limitations, offering guidance for developers and researchers for the selection of the appropriate tool for machine learning tasks.

title: Autoencoder–LSTM and Graph-Theory Hybrid Framework for Secure Clustering and Lifetime Maximization in WSNs
authors: Yuvaraj Durasiamy, Rangu Shirisha, Md. Chan Basha, M. Sugumaran, A. Kaliappan, Ananthi M.
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Emerging Trends in Computing and Communication (ETCOM)
abstract: Smart sensors that are smaller, cheaper, and more energy efficient have been made possible by the fast evolution of MEMS technology, which has greatly increased the importance of WSN. Structured as networks of interconnected nodes, these sensors track a variety of environmental variables and relay that information wirelessly to a main BS. But problems like secure clustering and limited network lifetimes still exist. Secure Clustering and Lifetime Maximisation is a suggested framework that aims to tackle these concerns. To improve the quality of sensed data, the framework uses data preparation techniques. To make clustering and routing more efficient, it uses a graph-based learning methodology. A Graph-AE-LSTM is trained with graph sequences generated using random walks, breadth-first search, and shortest path algorithms to obtain continuous vector representations of network topologies. Through the use of the Lifetime Extension Factor, these representations make it possible to aggregate data in an efficient and safe manner, all while increasing the lifetime of nodes. The Graph-AE-LSTM model accomplished a remarkable 95.31% accuracy in comparison and classification tasks, according to the experimental data. The SCLM framework establishes a solid groundwork for smart and sustainable WSNs by improving their performance in three key areas: secure communication, balanced energy consumption, and extended network operation.

title: Real Time Air Quality Monitoring and Classification using IoT and Machine Learning
authors: Rajendran V.G, Thalaimalaichamy M, Akshaya K, Padmavathi R
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Recent Trends in Electrical, Electronics and Computing Technologies (ICRTEECT)
abstract: Air pollution remains a critical challenge impacting public health and environmental quality worldwide. This project proposes a low-cost, real-time air quality monitoring system that integrates Internet of Things (IoT) technology with machine learning algorithms for effective pollution assessment. The system utilizes the MQ135 gas sensor and DHT11 temperature-humidity sensor, interfaced with an ESP8266 Wi-Fi module for wireless data transmission to the ThingSpeak cloud platform. Collected environmental data is processed using supervised machine learning models—Decision Tree and Support Vector Machine (SVM)—to classify air quality into predefined categories. Experimental results indicate that the Decision Tree model achieves 100% accuracy in classification, outperforming SVM. The developed system demonstrates a scalable and practical solution for intelligent air quality monitoring, offering valuable insights for real-time environmental analysis and decision-making.

title: Posterior Cramér–Rao Bounds on Localization and Mapping Errors in Distributed MIMO SLAM
authors: Benjamin J. B. Deutschmann, Xuhong Li, Florian Meyer, Erik Leitinger
publish: 2025 59th Asilomar Conference on Signals, Systems, and Computers
abstract: Radio-frequency simultaneous localization and mapping (RF-SLAM) methods jointly infer the position of mobile transmitters and receivers in wireless networks, together with a geometric map of the propagation environment. An inferred map of specular surfaces can be used to exploit non-line-of-sight components of the multipath channel to increase robustness, bypass obstructions, and improve overall communication and positioning performance. While performance bounds for user location are well established, the literature lacks performance bounds for map information. This paper derives the mapping error bound (MEB), i.e., the posterior Cramér–Rao lower bound on the position and orientation of specular surfaces, for RF-SLAM. In particular, we consider a very general scenario with single- and double-bounce reflections, as well as distributed anchors. We demonstrate numerically that a state-of-the-art RF-SLAM algorithm asymptotically approaches this MEB. The bounds assess not only the localization (position and orientation) but also the mapping performance of RF-SLAM algorithms in terms of global features.

title: Machine Learning Approaches for Airfare Prediction and Demand Forecasting
authors: Talla Prashanthi, Rupesh Mishra
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Recent Trends in Electrical, Electronics and Computing Technologies (ICRTEECT)
abstract: The nature of the airline industry presents some challenges in predicting airfare and forecasting passenger’s demand. This explores machine learning approaches that develops accurate data-driven models. Historical flight data, pricing trends, seasonal patterns, external factors and various supervised learning algorithms are used to analyze and model airfare fluctuations. These forecasting techniques are used to predict passenger demand across different routes and periods. The proposed model supports airline operators in optimizing pricing strategies and passengers in Decision-making. Experimental results as shown that these methods make Predictions more accurate and more consistent. This helps airlines operate better and provide better services to the customers.

title: A Conditional GAN Approach for Artwork Restoration Using Paired Image-to-Image Translation
authors: N Prabhakaran, Neeraj Das, D. Jebakumar Immanuel, Rahul Thakur, Jeberson Retna Raj, Y.N. Raja
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Smart Innovations in Systems, Infrastructure, Mechanical, Power, AI and Computing Technologies (SISIMPACT)
abstract: The proposed research is a conditional Generative Adversarial Network (cGAN) based framework that restores degraded artwork images through a paired image-toimage translation manner. The extent of the proposed architecture lies in using a U-Net-based generator with two attention blocks and a channel transformer amongst skip connections to maintain local and global contextual details in addition to the excellent fusion of multiscale features. The spatial and channel attention modules enhance capability of the network in capturing inter-spatial and inter-channel relations and the channel modernizer helps to bridge the semantic gap between the encoder and decoder layers. An image authenticity discriminator is implemented in a patch wise manner. Training of the model employs a multiloss function comprising adversarial loss, Perceptual loss (Smooth L1) and SSIM loss, which allows improved perceptual superiority and structural constancy of the restored images. Subensive experiments on three benchmarking datasets namely, the 'Art images,' 'Best artwork of all time, and offline information set prove that the suggested approach produces better performance with SSIM up to 0.95, MSE up to 0.31, and PSNR up to 53.92 than the existing state-of-the-art methods.

title: Magnetorheological Dampers in Semi-Active Suspension Systems: Enhancing Vehicle Safety in Automotive Engineering
authors: Ranjit Vasant Rajale, C Goud, M P Nagarkar
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Recent Trends in Electrical, Electronics and Computing Technologies (ICRTEECT)
abstract: The automotive suspension systems that have been updated over the years have made vehicle performance, comfort and safety better than before. Due to their ability to adoption based on driving conditions based on changing roads, at least in real time, semi-active systems based on magnetorheological (MR) dampers have become a feasible alternative in a spectrum of recent suspension systems. The MR dampers provide real-time control of high and medium restraining power with minimal energy consumption and high reliability, due to the ability to vary the rheological properties of MR fluids in response to the effect of a regulated magnetic field. This paper presents a discussion of the application of MR dampers in a semi-active suspension system and how it provides effect to the safety of vehicles. A quarter-car was trained as a method in MATLAB/Simulink where the active, passive and semi-active suspension methods are tested under different road excitation condition. The performance variables are evaluated including: road-holding capacity, handling stability and the ride comfort. The outcomes of simulations are then compared to experimental values of a prototype MR damper and drive the simulation models towards validation in a hardware-in-the-loop (HIL) test bench. MR dampers are therefore particularly effective in regard to vibration reduction; MF is capable of increasing passenger comfort and stability compared to the traditional systems. These characteristics of this technology are compelling enough to improve the safety of crashed vehicles and bridge the gap between passive and another aspect of full active suspensions, yet there remain challenges in affordability and heat sensitivity.

title: Efficient Cell-free Wireless Access and Fronthaul Networks
authors: Ramprasad Raghunath, Bile Peng, Eduard A. Jorswieck
publish: 2025 59th Asilomar Conference on Signals, Systems, and Computers
abstract: Resource allocation in wireless networks, particularly cell free massive MIMO (CF mMIMO) systems, presents fundamental optimization challenges that often require solving computationally demanding programming problems. Machine learning (ML) approaches offer a low complexity policy to solve these optimization problems. Graph neural network (GNN), which can effectively exploit the underlying network topology, have emerged as powerful tools for learning effective allocation policies. However, GNN operations can induce significant front-haul traffic between access points (APs) and the central processing unit, creating a critical bottleneck in scalable cell-free deployments. This work investigates the impact of edge pruning—a technique designed to reduce GNN computational complexity and, crucially, mitigate front-haul communication overhead on resource allocation performance. We comprehensively evaluate the performance of pruned GNNs on the energy efficiency (EE), exploring the fundamental trade-offs between GNN sparsity, computational efficiency, front-haul load reduction, and network performance. Our results demonstrate that substantial front-haul overhead mitigation can be achieved through strategic pruning with negligible degradation in allocation performance, thereby enabling the deployment of lightweight, front-haul-aware GNN-based resource allocation solutions that are essential for practical distributed cell-free system implementations.

title: Enhanced Algal Bloom Detection Using Three Novel Geometry-Based Spectral Indices in a Hybrid CNN-CatBoost Ensemble
authors: Manoranjan Dash, Sabita Misra, Atul Dash
publish: 2025 IEEE Asia-Pacific Conference on Geoscience, Electronics and Remote Sensing Technology (AGERS)
abstract: Accurate estimation of chlorophyll-a (Chl-a) concentration from multispectral satellite data is critical for early detection and monitoring of harmful algal blooms (HABs). Conventional indices such as the Normalized Difference Chlorophyll Index (NDCI) and Normalized Difference Vegetation Index (NDVI) often exhibit reduced sensitivity in optically complex inland and coastal waters. In this study, we propose an ensemble framework integrating a Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) and CatBoost to predict in-situ Chl-a concentrations from Sentinel-2 surface reflectance data provided by the GLORIA dataset. Three novel spectral indices-Angles@(650,704) capturing curvature characteristics of the red-region reflectance profile, and Polyfit Residual Sum (PRS) quantifying the deviation of observed spectra from fitted polynomial baselines-were developed and incorporated along with standard spectral bands and ancillary features in an ensemble model of CNN and CatBoost. Comparative and fair evaluation against seven existing indices (e.g., NDCI and NDVI) demonstrated that the proposed indices (Angles@(650, 704) and PRS) achieved higher accuracy in terms of $\mathbf{R}^{2}$ and RMSE. The inclusion of these proposed indices also showed a clear improvement in terms of MAE, CI(Confidence Interval) and bias across multiple water types. The results highlight the effectiveness of combining physically interpretable indices with deep and boosted learning architectures, paving the way for more robust satellite-based Chl-a retrieval and early HAB warning systems.

title: Optimizing Artificial Neural Networks with PSO and WIPSO for Solar Power Yield Prediction
authors: Atul Kumar, Harsh Sharma, Vivek Kumar
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Recent Trends in Electrical, Electronics and Computing Technologies (ICRTEECT)
abstract: The research studies the forecasting abilities of Artificial Neural Network (ANN) daily inverter yields and cumulative energy output in solar power facilities through Weighted Improved particle swarm optimization (WIPSO) and particle swarm optimization (PSO). Scientific data was collected during 30 days across two solar power facilities using inverter power generation measurements alongside sensor-collected environmental data placed strategically throughout each facility. The study combines PSO and WIPSO under ANN architecture to increase the prediction accuracy for solar energy output, so operational decision-making and long-term solar system planning can improve. Results show that ANN optimization through PSO generated R-squared between 72% to 88% and WIPSO optimized ANN achieved R-squared rates between 68% to 88% both demonstrating effective prediction capabilities for AC power output across most inverters.

title: Channel Charting for Efficient Precoding in Cell-Free Massive MIMO
authors: Hussein Abbass, Luc Le Magoarou, Joumana Farah, Matthieu Crussière
publish: 2025 59th Asilomar Conference on Signals, Systems, and Computers
abstract: Cell-free massive MIMO (CF-mMIMO) systems promise enhanced spectral efficiency and uniform service by deploying numerous distributed access points (APs) that jointly serve users. However, centralized precoding, which offers optimal performance by leveraging global channel state information (CSI), imposes significant fronthaul overhead due to the extensive CSI exchange between APs and the central processing unit (CPU). In contrast, distributed precoding reduces fronthaul demands, but undergoes performance degradation due to the lack of coordination between APs. This paper introduces a novel framework that strikes a balance between these two scenarios by employing channel charting (CC) as a lightweight CSI compression mechanism at each AP. CC maps high-dimensional CSI into a low-dimensional pseudo-location space, capturing essential channel characteristics. These pseudo-locations are transmitted to the CPU with minimal overhead, where advanced machine learning models are employed to infer effective downlink precoding vectors, thereby approximating centralized performance while reducing fronthaul signaling. Extensive simulations on realistic synthetic datasets demonstrate that our approach achieves very promising results. This work highlights the potential of integrating CC and machine learning to enhance scalability and efficiency in CF-mMIMO networks.

title: A Review of Energy Storage Technologies for Load Frequency Control in Islanded Microgrids
authors: Rudaraiah Yanaganti, Chandan Kumar Shiva, Prakash Kamisetti
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Recent Trends in Electrical, Electronics and Computing Technologies (ICRTEECT)
abstract: Although the use of island microgrids has caused operational problems, this is still an important research topic because these systems generally use renewable energy sources. Complex load frequency control technologies are in high demand because of their low system inertia and variable power output. The ability of energy storage systems (ESS) to quickly correct generation-demand imbalances makes them essential components for lowering frequency deviations. In this critical review, we evaluate the main technologies for energy storage devices, such as battery energy storage devices, flywheel energy storage devices, supercapacitors, and hybrid systems, based on their islanded operational capacities, control strategies, and dynamic response characteristics. The scalability, efficiency, and life cycle performance of the system are described in a comparative presentation using technical and economic perspectives. The forthcoming conversation will include data-driven control trends and hybrid storage scheme coordination strategies for the same. The findings of this study show that ESS can be incorporated into independent microgrids to produce reliable and adaptable load frequency control systems.

title: Traffic Congestion Prediction Using Vehicle Trajectories from Drones and Combined Graph Attention and Temporal Convolutional Network
authors: Hana Gharrad, Nesrine Ouled Abdallah
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Unmanned Vehicle Systems-Oman (UVS)
abstract: Congestion prediction is an important and challenging task in intelligent transportation systems. This task should effectively capture the spatio-temporal dependencies in road networks because traffic congestion can propagate spatially over time. With recent advancements in computer vision, the use of Unmanned Aerial Vehicles (UAVs) has gained increasing attention as a tool for traffic data collection. In this work, we evaluate the performance of a Temporal Convolutional Network (TCN) per edge versus a Temporal Graph Neural Network for predicting the congestion ratio of road segments in a road network. The modeling of the graph structure was extracted from open vehicle trajectories data recorded using a swarm of drones. Two different models were implemented and compared for the task of congestion prediction per edge aiming to capture structural correlations between edges, spatial effects, and temporal traffic patterns.

title: Hardware-in-the-Loop Simulation of a Robust Controller to Damp Low-Frequency Oscillations in Power Systems
authors: Nawanjana Y. Ranathunga, Vimukthi R. Liyanage, D. Prasad Wadduwage, Lahiru L. Premasiri
publish: 2025 IEEE 19th International Conference on Industrial and Information Systems (ICIIS)
abstract: Poorly damped low-frequency oscillations in multi-area power systems (0.1 Hz - 2 Hz) can create significant challenges to its security level. A properly-tuned robust damping controller can mitigate these adverse effects by improving the damping of such oscillations to acceptable values while ensuring the small signal stability of the power systems. Real-time simulations, performed using the Real Time Digital Simulator (RTDS), provide a state-of-the-art approach for testing and validating the performances of these damping controllers under realistic operating conditions. This paper presents the design and implementation of a robust damping controller using the RTDS facility via Hardware-in-the-loop (HIL) approach. The implementation of the damping controller for 2-area test system using C2000 microcontrollers by Texas Instruments demonstrates its near-real-time, robust operation under different operating conditions.

title: Large-scale Unlabeled Compressed Sensing via Bilinear Matrix Recovery
authors: Mohamed Akrout, Amine Mezghani, Faouzi Bellili
publish: 2025 59th Asilomar Conference on Signals, Systems, and Computers
abstract: This paper introduces an algorithmic solution to a broader class of unlabeled sensing problems with multiple measurement vectors (MMV). The goal is to recover an unknown structured signal matrix, X, from its noisy linear observation matrix, Y , whose rows are further randomly shuffled by an unknown permutation matrix U. A new Bayes-optimal unlabeled compressed sensing (UCS) recovery algorithm developed from the bilinear approximate message passing (Bi-VAMP) framework using non-separable and coupled priors on the rows and columns of the permutation matrix U. Standard unlabeled sensing boils down to a special case of the proposed UCS framework, which assumes neither a partially shuffled signal matrix X nor a small-size permutation matrix U. Numerical results demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed UCS algorithm and its advantage over state-of-the-art approaches in various applications.

title: Autonomous Detection of DDoS Attacks in IoT Networks Using Machine Learning
authors: Praveen Kumar G, Kalaiarasan T, Priyadharshini V, Vidhya Nagavel, Parameswaran Ramesh, Bhuvaneswari P.T. V
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Smart Innovations in Systems, Infrastructure, Mechanical, Power, AI and Computing Technologies (SISIMPACT)
abstract: The exponential growth of the Internetof Things (IoT) has introduced new security challenges, particularly Distributed Denial of Service (DDoS) attacks that can severely disrupt interconnected systems. The prediction and removal of these attacks has become essential for enhancing the security and reliability of next generation smart networks. Existing researches have primarily focused on static, rule based and signature-based detection mechanisms. While effective to some extent, these traditional methods often struggle with adapting to sophisticated and evolving attack patterns that characterize modern DDoS threats. Moreover, the high false-positive rates and inability to operate efficiently in real-time limit their practical application in dynamic IoT networks. These limitations highlight the need to rethink traditional methods by developing intelligent, adaptive, and scalable detection frameworks, motivating the use of machine learning techniques for improved performance. In this study multiple learning approaches are evaluated using benchmark IoT-specific d ataset, C ICIDS2017. S upervised learning models including Decision tree (DT), Random Forest (RF) and Naïve Bayes are employed to classify known DDoS patterns. Unsupervised learning models such as K-means Clustering and Isolation tree are implemented for anomaly detection without prior label information. Furthermore, reinforcement learning techniques like Q-learning and PPO are introduced to enable autonomous learning and real-time decision-making under uncertainty. All models are analyzed based on precision, recall, F1-score, training time, and adaptability to new threats. The results of the approaches are visualized and compared to identify the model with the highest accuracy, with the Random Forest algorithm modified to further improve its performance.

title: Multi-Stage Pipeline for Audio Source Separation and Parallel Excitement Analysis in Boxing Highlight Generation
authors: D.M.L.A. Dissanayake, L. Ranathunga
publish: 2025 IEEE 19th International Conference on Industrial and Information Systems (ICIIS)
abstract: Automatic highlight generation in sports broadcasting is a significant challenge, with prior research often limited by coarse audio segmentation techniques that fail to handle overlapping sources and discard valuable information from crowd reactions. This paper introduces a novel, multi-stage pipeline that performs true audio source separation and subsequent parallel excitement analysis. The proposed system first employs a Support Vector Machine (SVM) "Triage Classifier," achieving 93% accuracy, to efficiently categorize audio segments based on a rich feature vector. For mixed segments, a custom mask-based separation algorithm, utilizing a second specialized SVM trained on granular spectrogram bin features, computationally unmixes the concurrent commentator and crowd audio. Finally, two separate Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs), which achieved over 92% validation accuracy, analyze the clean audio tracks to generate synchronized excitement timelines. This approach addresses a key research gap by treating the crowd as a primary signal and successfully separating overlapping sources, providing a robust and effective method for automatically identifying key events in sports broadcasts.

title: Augmented Reality Visual Retrieval for Object Detection and Corpus-Guided Content Generation
authors: Alireza Taheritajar, Jieqiong Zhao, Jason Orlosky
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and eXtended and Virtual Reality (AIxVR)
abstract: Object detection models have recently become lightweight enough for deployment on resource-constrained devices such as smartphones and Augmented Reality (AR) headsets. Techniques such as few-shot, zero-shot, and incremental learning have moved toward lower training times and faster inference for detection models, but most frameworks still require substantial training or retraining to dynamically add new object classes in real-time for practical use with AR. In this paper, we present a novel AR-integrated object detection system that leverages Visual Retrieval Augmented Generation (ARVRag for short), eliminating much of the lengthy and expensive training processes associated with traditional object detection models. By vectorizing a smaller set of training images and conducting a local similarity search, we achieve enough accuracy for practical use with AR in significantly less time than state-of-the-art models like YOLO. Moreover, we have built this process into support systems for maintenance and industrial operations. End-users can select an object using a 2 D image capture interface that automatically retrieves semantically similar items from a local corpus (database), and the resulting detection provides metadataenhanced object-specific responses from a large language model (LLM). This work demonstrates a practical and scalable pathway for LLM-supported object detection in AR applications, especially for manufacturing and repair.

title: Deep Learning for Early Detection of Diabetic Retinopathy Using Retinal Fundus Images
authors: Shwetha Nayak, Mounesh Arkachari, Shreeya G R, Nandini K A
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Emerging Trends in Computing and Communication (ETCOM)
abstract: Diabetic Retinopathy (DR) remains a leading cause of preventable blindness worldwide, affecting approximately one-third of diabetic patients. Early detection through retinal fundus imaging is crucial for preventing vision loss, but manual screening presents challenges in scalability and inter-observer variability. This paper presents a novel hybrid deep learning architecture that integrates ResNet50 for hierarchical feature extraction with Capsule Networks for precise spatial relationship modeling. The proposed model addresses critical limitations of conventional Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs), particularly their inability to preserve spatial hierarchies and partwhole relationships essential for accurate DR severity grading. Our approach leverages ResNet50's powerful feature extraction capabilities to identify intricate retinal patterns, while Capsule Networks capture spatial dependencies through dynamic routing and vector outputs. Experimental results on retinal fundus images demonstrate superior performance compared to standalone CNN architectures, with significant improvements in detecting subtle pathological features such as microaneurysms, exudates, and hemorrhages. The hybrid model achieved an overall accuracy of 97.2 in five-class DR classification, showcasing strong potential as an automated screening tool for clinical deployment.

title: Geo-demographic Determinants of Brand Choice: An Analysis of Urban and Rural Consumers in Telangana
authors: Padma. A, N. Suman Kumar, D. Srinivas, Ramesh G
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Recent Trends in Electrical, Electronics and Computing Technologies (ICRTEECT)
abstract: This research shows how consumers in the city and rural areas in Telangana, India accept COSMETIC products based on brand attributes and geo-demographic factors. As people become more concerned with skincare and health care, the choice of the brand has become one of the major factors of purchasing behaviour. This study examines the nature of variables (brand consciousness, brand name and brand loyalty) in consumer decision making. Based on the descriptive research design and the primary data obtained with 678 participants with the help of a structured questionnaire, the study uses SPSS to prove the existence of specific hypotheses by conducting the statistical test. The results indicate that age, gender, income, marital status and education is a demographic variable that significantly affects consumer build and brand loyalty in Young Generation 15 to 25 age group. The findings are used to emphasize the role of branding strategy in market positioning and building long-term customer relationships in various consumer segments. The findings do not delve into the contribution of lifestyle, personality values psychographic variables in consumer-brand relations. This study can be expanded in future, through incorporation of psychographic profiling into geo-demographics to create a complete description of the consumer behaviour in terms of brand selection.

title: Sparse Error Correction for Robust State Estimation: Vulnerability and Robustification
authors: Razeel Hassan Ibrahim Rasheed, Jinsub Kim
publish: 2025 59th Asilomar Conference on Signals, Systems, and Computers
abstract: Robust linear state estimation in the presence of adversarial sensor data falsification is considered. In the literature, the basis pursuit approaches have been proposed to nullify the impact of the falsified measurements on the state estimate by leveraging the sparsity assumption that only a small fraction of measurements are subject to falsification. It is first demonstrated that adversaries can design a successful sparse attack, which can mislead the basis pursuit approach while requiring falsification of only a small fraction of sensor measurements. To address the discovered vulnerability, a robust sparse error correction approach is developed by integrating randomized weighting into the basis pursuit approach. The proposed approach is experimentally evaluated using random measurement matrices as well as power system DC state estimation for IEEE 14-bus test case. Experimental evaluations show that the proposed approach offers much enhanced resilience against adversarial data falsification compared to the existing approaches.

title: Secure Session Management in MERN Stack Applications Using Token Rotation and Refresh Strategies
authors: Suchith Goud Arella, Pranav Malaraju
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Recent Trends in Electrical, Electronics and Computing Technologies (ICRTEECT)
abstract: The rapid adoption of the MERN (MongoDB, Express.js, React, Node.js) stack in the development of scalable single-page applications (SPAs) has increased the demand for efficient and secured mechanisms of session management. State-of-the-art token-based authentication systems, although highly efficient in operation, make applications prone to token replay attack, session hijack attack, and concurrency-related risk of token refreshment acquisition. Token-rotation-based and sender-constraining and refresh-mechanism-dependent secure session management schemes are discussed in this experiment to improve the security profile of MERN applications without affecting usability for the end-user. By rotation of refresh tokens, creation of reuse detection mechanisms, and taking advantage of backend-for-frontend (BFF) design artifacts, the approach presented in the paper damps the risk of stateless JSON Web Tokens (JWTs) and bearer token attack. Experimental analyses confirm that token rotation with centralized session management reduces token replay risk by more than a factor of 80 without affecting the maximum level of application performance and is a usable and scalable reference source for software practitioners based on existing IETF standards and OWASP-best-practice guidelines and a basis for future work in advanced revocation mechanisms and concurrency-safety-driven session management in distributed systems.

title: Drone Surveillance via Coordinated Beam Sweeping in MIMO-ISAC Networks
authors: Palatip Jopanya, Diana P. M. Osorio, Erik G. Larsson
publish: 2025 59th Asilomar Conference on Signals, Systems, and Computers
abstract: This paper introduces a scheme for drone surveillance coordinated with the fifth generation (5G) synchronization signal block (SSB) cell-search procedure to simultaneously detect low-altitude drones within a volumetric surveillance grid. Herein, we consider a multistatic configuration where multiple access points (APs) collaboratively illuminate the volume while independently transmitting SSB broadcast signals. Both tasks are performed through a beam sweeping. In the proposed scheme, coordinated APs send sensing beams toward a grid of voxels within the volumetric surveillance region simultaneously with the 5G SSB burst. To prevent interference between communication and sensing signals, we propose a precoder design that guarantees orthogonality of the sensing beam and the SSB in order to maximize the sensing signal-to-interference-plus-noise ratio (SINR) while ensuring a specified SINR for users, as well as minimizing the impact of the direct link. The results demonstrate that the proposed precoder outperforms the non-coordinated precoder and is minimally affected by variations in drone altitude.

title: Enhanced Multi-model Online Conformal Prediction
authors: Erfan Hajihashemi, Yanning Shen
publish: 2025 59th Asilomar Conference on Signals, Systems, and Computers
abstract: Conformal prediction is a framework for uncertainty quantification that constructs prediction sets for previously unseen data, guaranteeing coverage of the true label with a specified probability. However, the efficiency of these prediction sets, measured by their size, depends on the choice of the underlying learning model. Relying on a single fixed model may lead to suboptimal performance in online environments, as a single model may not consistently perform well across all time steps. To mitigate this, prior work has explored selecting a model from a set of candidates. However, this approach becomes computationally expensive as the number of candidate models increases. Moreover, poorly performing models in the set may also hinder the effectiveness. To tackle this challenge, this work develops a novel multi-model online conformal prediction algorithm that reduces computational complexity and improves prediction efficiency. At each time step, a bipartite graph is generated to identify a subset of effective models, from which a model is selected to construct the prediction set. Experiments demonstrate that our method outperforms existing multi-model conformal prediction techniques in terms of both prediction set size and computational efficiency.

title: Deep infant brain segmentation from multi-contrast MRI
authors: Malte Hoffmann, Lilla Zöllei, Adrian V. Dalca
publish: 2025 59th Asilomar Conference on Signals, Systems, and Computers
abstract: Segmentation of magnetic resonance images (MRI) facilitates analysis of human brain development by delineating anatomical structures. However, in infants and young children, accurate segmentation is challenging due to development and imaging constraints. Pediatric brain MRI is notoriously difficult to acquire, with inconsistent availability of imaging modalities, substantial non-head anatomy in the field of view, and frequent motion artifacts. This has led to specialized segmentation models that are often limited to specific image types or narrow age groups, or that are fragile for more variable images such as those acquired clinically. We address this method fragmentation with BabySeg, a deep learning brain segmentation framework for infants and young children that supports diverse MRI protocols, including repeat scans and image types unavailable during training. Our approach builds on recent domain randomization techniques, which synthesize training images far beyond realistic bounds to promote dataset shift invariance. We also describe a mechanism that enables models to flexibly pool and interact features from any number of input scans. We demonstrate state-of-the-art performance that matches or exceeds the accuracy of several existing methods for various age cohorts and input configurations using a single model, in a fraction of the runtime required by many existing tools.

title: Tuning Database Using Domain Knowledge and Performance Bottlenecks-Based Knob Search
authors: Zhongwei Yue, Dandan Lu, Siyuan Liu, Pengwei Ma, Chunyu Jiang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Big Data and Data Mining (BDDM)
abstract: Numerous database tuning tools aim to optimize performance by tuning configuration parameters. For practical adoption, a tool must have two key characteristics: efficiency and interpretability. Efficiency involves achieving high performance quickly, while interpretability ensures DBAs fully understand the tuning process. A lack of understanding can undermine trust and hinder adoption. However, existing tools often fail to meet both needs. To address this, we present XTuner, a new tuning system consisting of two components. The first component, the rule-based sub-tuner, extracts domain knowledge to initialize parameter values and assists in the parameter tuning of the second component. the second component, the search-based sub-tuner, iteratively selects and refines knob values based on performance bottlenecks and domain knowledge. We applied XTuner to two popular open-source database systems with three workloads. Experimental results show XTuner outperforms alternatives in efficiency, and we demonstrate its interpretability with a detailed example.

title: IEEE Recommended Practice for the Installation, Operation, Maintenance, Testing, and Replacement of Lithium-ion Batteries for Stationary Applications
authors: N/A
abstract: Recommended practices for system design, storage, installation, ventilation, instrumentation, operation, maintenance, capacity testing, and replacement of Li-ion batteries are provided in this document. While the principles covered in this document apply to all stationary standby and cycling applications, some of them may be excessive for smaller systems, such as those often found in residential installations. Guidance on compliance with safety standards, as well as best practices for worker safety during installation, maintenance, and testing is also provided.
