Across both embedding types and domains, we consistently observe low <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{R}$</tex> statistics and non-significant <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{p}$</tex>-values, indicating no significant regional bias in judgment semantics. Our results corroborate the hypothesis that Indian High Courts enforce law equally, and we further establish the utility of embedding-based statistical testing in legal AI systems.

title: Exploring Geo-demographic and Digital Drivers of Consumer Behavior in Telangana: Urban and Rural Perspectives on Brand Choice
authors: Padma. A, N. Suman Kumar, D. Srinivas, Ramesh G
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Recent Trends in Electrical, Electronics and Computing Technologies (ICRTEECT)
abstract: This paper will examine the relationship between consumer preference on fast-moving consumer goods (FMCG) products in both urban and rural areas in Telangana, India, and the brand attributes and geo-demographic variables. This has been as the consumers are taking health maintenance more into consideration in their consumer behavior, and brand choice has become a factor of significant influence in the consumer buying behavior. This study examines the influence of variables, brand consciousness, brand loyalty, price, and emotional connection on consumer choices. The study uses SPSS to support the testing of appropriate hypotheses based on statistical results in relation to the applied qualitative and quantitative research method design, and primary data gathered using Likert 5point scale structured questionnaire through 230 respondents. Results indicate that demographic factor, which includes age, gender, income, marital status, and education, plays a significant part on the decision of consumers to buy products or services. These findings demonstrate the significance of brand attributes and the effect they have on consumer behavior and creation of branding strategies to create customer relationships with various consumer segments.

title: Identifying Sat Resilient Blocks Through LUT Switching Analysis for Breaking Compound Logic Locking Schemes
authors: Binbin Wang, Xinmu Wang, Xinyu Zhang, Shibo Tang, Huisi Zhou, Wei Hu
publish: 2025 35th International Conference on Field-Programmable Logic and Applications (FPL)
abstract: Logic locking is an effective approach for protecting integrated circuits against security threats such as Intellectual Property (IP) piracy and malicious design modifications. To defeat SAT attacks, state-of-the-art locking schemes, which are typically constructed using point-function that remains constant when the correct key is applied, while producing a wrong output only under a specific input pattern if an incorrect key is provided. Although this significantly increases the effort required for SAT attack, it introduces a new vulnerability that can be exploited to discover SAT-resilient blocks. Our observation is that pointfunction can lead to control signals, which exhibit significantly lower switching probability and yield clues for identifying anti-SAT cones. This work aims to reveal such clues at the level of FPGA netlist, where switching behavior characteristics can be quantified using the initialization vectors of Look-up-Tables (LUTs). We leverage the switching behavior measurements of LUTs to pinpoint signals with extremely low switching activity to locate control signals associated with SAT-resilient blocks. By forcing the identified control signals to a constant value, the protective function of the SAT-resilient blocks can be neutralized. We validate our attack method on locked circuits with anti-SAT enhancement. Experimental results on six compound logic locking schemes yield an average attack success rate of 99.6% and 100% key recovery rate for each neutralized circuit.

title: AR-Based Spatial Figure Learning with Tangible Vertex Markers on a Wireframe Cube
authors: Kei Tsukahara, Nobuyuki Umezu
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and eXtended and Virtual Reality (AIxVR)
abstract: We present a learning-support system that integrates physical three-dimensional models with augmented reality (AR) to facilitate the acquisition of spatial-figure concepts. The system is implemented on an iPad, which functions as both input and output device. Following instructions displayed on the screen, users mentally construct the spatial figure that satisfies a displayed problem and then place vertex markers along the edges of a physical cube model. The iPad's rear camera tracks these markers, and the system superimposes the user-defined spatial figure onto the model in real-time AR, enabling the learner to examine the figure from multiple viewpoints. While interacting with the system, data-including responses, completion times, and pre- and post-task questionnaire scores-are recorded. These data are subsequently analyzed to evaluate learning outcomes and to inform iterative refinement of instructional content and exhibit design. An evaluation experiment was conducted at a university festival. Nineteen participants completed questionnaires assessing ease of use, perceived effectiveness for learning spatial figures, and interest. The results indicated generally positive evaluations. Although the system did not yield immediate, statistically significant learning gains-likely due to operational difficulty and the inherent challenge of the material-the integration of AR with tangible models appears promising for stimulating learner engagement in the comprehension of spatial figures.

title: Static Analysis of Micro Functionally Graded Porous Plate
authors: Rahul Kumar, Nikhil M. Kulkarni, Ashok Magar, Sakshi Barskar
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Smart Innovations in Systems, Infrastructure, Mechanical, Power, AI and Computing Technologies (SISIMPACT)
abstract: The present paper reveals the characteristics of porous functionally graded micro plate for static analysis. Material properties gradation based on power law along thickness direction of the plate is taken. Uniform porosity distribution, which degrade the material properties along thickness direction, has been considered for the present investigation. First order shear deformation theory using Finite Element Method based model has been used to develop MATLAB program for present work. First the program is verified by comparing the present results with published one and then further parametric study has been performed.

title: Parkinson's Disease Prediction Using Random Forest
authors: Nisha M, R Archana Reddy, Sonali Agrawal, Sarika Pal, R. Chinnaiyan, Ravi Kumar
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Smart Innovations in Systems, Infrastructure, Mechanical, Power, AI and Computing Technologies (SISIMPACT)
abstract: Parkinson's disease (PD) is a progressive neurological disorder that impairs motor skills and speech, underscoring the importance of early and accurate diagnosis for effective treatment. This study presents a machine learning driven diagnostic framework that employs the Random Forest algorithm to identify PD using voice-based biomarkers. A Genetic Algorithm (GA) is utilized for optimal feature selection, while Bayesian Optimization is applied for fine-tuning hyperparameters to maximize model performance. The proposed approach attains a classification accuracy of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{9 4 \%}$</tex> and an ROC-AUC score of 97 %, indicating strong diagnostic reliability. Comparative evaluations reveal that conventional models, including Random Forest and SVM, typically achieve accuracy between 93.84 % and 96.61 %, whereas the integration of GA and Bayesian Optimization significantly improves both precision and robustness. These outcomes underscore the promise of AIpowered, non-invasive voice analysis for early PD detection. Future work will focus on exploring deep learning architectures, longitudinal speech datasets, and multimodal biomarkers to further enhance diagnostic accuracy and generalizability.

title: Cluster Parameterization and Modeling from Noisy Channel Measurements and Their Application to Sub-THz D2D Channels
authors: Zihang Cheng, Naveed A. Abbasi, Jorge Gomez-Ponce, Gary Xu, Won-Suk Choi, Jianzhong Charlie Zhang, Andreas F. Molisch
publish: 2025 59th Asilomar Conference on Signals, Systems, and Computers
abstract: In upcoming sixth-generation (6G) communications, sub-terahertz (sub-THz) bands will be employed to meet data rate requirements. For designing systems operating in that band, accurate modeling of the propagation channels, and in particular the multipath propagation, is vital. Measurements show that multipath components (MPCs) occur in clusters; incorporation of cluster structure is beneficial to make models more realistic as well as simpler. In this paper, we present a methodology for extracting cluster statistics from channel measurements that are noisy (and thus necessitate thresholding for noise suppression) and/or have limited dynamic range; this affects the relationships between intra-cluster decay constants and intra-cluster RMS spreads, as well as the observed number of clusters. To address this, we analytically derive the deviations between actual cluster parameters and their observable statistics, followed by validation through simulations. By solving the corresponding inverse problem using the derived expressions and the dynamic range, more realistic cluster parameters are retrieved. Moreover, the retrieved cluster parameters enable the extrapolation of channel characteristics across varying dynamic range conditions. The methodology is applicable at all frequency bands, and we demonstrate the impact of this modeling on the parameterization of ultra-wideband, double-directionally resolved sub-THz measurements of device-to-device (D2D) propagation in outdoor urban environments.

title: Transformer-Based Adverse Event Detection in Oncology Clinical Trials: A Text Mining Approach
authors: Ajinkya M. Chavan, Sarika Nitin Zaware
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Recent Trends in Electrical, Electronics and Computing Technologies (ICRTEECT)
abstract: The detection of Adverse Events (AE) is important in oncology clinical trials to provide patient safety, regulatory compliance and determination of drug efficacy. Nevertheless, a substantial portion of the data concerning AEs and the eligibility criteria is presented in unstructured clinical reports, which means that extracting and classifying it automatically is not a trivial task. To overcome this challenge, in the current paper, we present a two-phase transformer-based natural language processing (NLP) framework to handle this challenge using BioBERT-a biomedical domain-specific transformer model. In Phase 1, we address the eligibility classification task by fine-tuning BioBERT to differentiate between eligible and ineligible textual eligibility criteria in cancer clinical trial protocols. The model demonstrated a classification rate of more than 99% with high F1 and AUC rates. Phase 2 We will use a weak supervision approach to automatically annotate AE mentions based on a domain-specific dictionary and negation processing. Such annotations are subsequently trained on a BioBERT-based AE detection classifier. The final model showed an almost perfect F1-score of 0.996 and an AUC of 1.00, which means that it has an outstanding ability to generalize to different clinical sentence structures. To prove our findings, we provide an extended visual analysis, consisting of ROC and precision-recall curves, the distribution of model confidence, confusion matrices and the plot of the frequency of keywords used in AE. Our results highlight the effectiveness and stability of the usage of the transformer-based models to extract the eligibility and detect AE.

title: Probabilistic Wildfire Susceptibility from Remote Sensing Using Random Forests and SHAP
authors: Udaya Bhasker Cheerala, Varun Teja Chirukuri, Venkata Akhil Kumar Gummadi, Jintu Moni Bhuyan, Praveen Damacharla
publish: 2025 IEEE Asia-Pacific Conference on Geoscience, Electronics and Remote Sensing Technology (AGERS)
abstract: Wildfires pose a significant global threat to ecosystems worldwide, with California experiencing recurring fires due to various factors, including climate, topographical features, vegetation patterns, and human activities. The aim of this study is to develop a comprehensive wildfire risk map for California by applying the random forest (RF) algorithm, augmented with Explainable Artificial Intelligence (XAI) through Shapley Additive exPlanations (SHAP), to interpret model predictions. Model performance was assessed using both spatial and temporal validation strategies. The RF model demonstrated strong predictive performance, achieving near-perfect discrimination for grasslands (AUC =0.996) and forests (AUC = 0.997). Spatial cross-validation revealed moderate transferability, yielding ROC-AUC values of 0.6155 for forests and 0.5416 for grasslands. In contrast, temporal split validation showed enhanced generalization, especially for forests (ROC-AUC = 0.6615, PR-AUC =0.8423). SHAP-based XAI analysis identified key ecosystem-specific drivers: soil organic carbon, tree cover, and Normalized Difference Vegetation Index (NDVI) emerged as the most influential in forests, whereas Land Surface Temperature (LST), elevation, and vegetation health indices were dominant in grasslands. District-level classification revealed that Central Valley and Northern Buttes districts had the highest concentration of high-risk grasslands, while Northern Buttes and North Coast Redwoods dominated forested high-risk areas. This RF-SHAP framework offers a robust, comprehensible, and adaptable method for assessing wildfire risks, enabling informed decisions and creating targeted strategies to mitigate dangers.

title: AI-Powered Smart Product Recommendation and Price Prediction System for Electronics
authors: Deepan S B, Ashwin Karthik S M, Priya S B
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Emerging Trends in Computing and Communication (ETCOM)
abstract: The fast growth of the electronic appliances especially mobile phones and laptops has made consumers have too many options to choose. Although recommendation systems can give recommendations on a particular product based on their preferences, features, they seldom look at the economic aspect of the product belonging, including resale value and possible repair costs. The current study introduces a combined intelligent system, which fills this gap with a combination of personalized product recommendation and predictive cost analytics. The system uses machine learning algorithms trained with web-scraped content of various e-commerce websites to come up with customized recommendations depending on user tastes, specifications and market dynamics. It also incorporates the Gemini API to give real-time estimates of resale price and repair costs based on the condition of the devices and market dynamic. Combining recommendation intelligence and financial foresight, the system allows consumers to make better purchasing decisions and know the economic perspective on the long-term economic impact of their buying behavior. The effectiveness of the proposed system is proven by the results of the experiment which show that the system provides correct recommendations and effective forecasts of cost which illustrates its potential as a multifunctional decisionsupport system within the consumer electronics sphere.

title: A Hybrid Security Model: Leveraging Blockchain and Machine Learning Models for Safe Transactions
authors: S Raghavendra, Shaik Jasmine, Deepika Adepu, Naga Swaroopa, Sangeethapriya R, V.Sumalatha
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Recent Trends in Electrical, Electronics and Computing Technologies (ICRTEECT)
abstract: Digital transactions happen more frequently so security measures and fraud prevention along with data protection need to be at the highest level possible. Modern security controls struggle to address emerging cyber threats because of their inflexible operation. This document outlines an integrated blockchain and machine learning model design for developing flexible and extensive protection measures for secure online transactions. Through blockchain mechanism organizations achieve decentralization alongside immutability and transparency which helps prevent risks connected to unauthorized access and data manipulation. The security benefits of ML models include instant fraud detection while they identify anomalies and predict threats so systems can take active measures against cyber threats. This research examines different ML methods beginning with supervised learning and proceeding to anomaly detection and deep learning so as to boost transaction surveillance and automation security. Besides the paper offers suggestions for smooth implementation of strategies to address scalability issues and computational performance problems alongside integration challenges. The hybrid method delivers improved transaction security through enhanced protection and simultaneously produces reduced false positive incidents and speeds up cyber threat response times. This research unites blockchain's trustless system with ML's intellectual ability to develop a modern security solution for financial deals and digital asset protection and e-commerce transactions.

title: Smart Reconstruction: AI-Driven Image Super-Resolution via CNN
authors: Nihar Shetty S, Ankitaa Pupneja, M J Akshaya, Anisha, Siddharth Kumar
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Emerging Trends in Computing and Communication (ETCOM)
abstract: The demand for high-resolution (HR) digital imagery is ever-increasing across various domains, yet images are often limited to a low-resolution (LR) format due to hardware or bandwidth constraints. While state-of-the-art deep learning models achieve remarkable reconstruction quality, their immense computational cost often limits their practical deployment. This paper introduces EffiSR-Net, a computationally efficient Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) designed to address this performance-efficiency trade-off. Our approach involves modifying the established ESRGAN architecture by reducing its network depth from 23 to 10 residual blocks, thereby creating a lightweight variant. We conduct a systematic performance analysis, comparing EffiSRNet against its parent model, ESRGAN, the Transformerbased SwinIR, and a traditional bicubic baseline. Quantitative evaluation demonstrates that EffiSR-Net achieves a significant reduction in model parameters and inference latency—nearly halving the size and doubling the speed of ESRGAN—while maintaining high-fidelity image reconstruction with only a marginal decrease in PSNR and SSIM scores. The results validate EffiSR-Net as a compelling alternative for applications where computational resources are limited, offering a practical balance between speed and quality for real-world superresolution tasks.

title: Comparison of CNN and SVM Models for Automated Seizure Detection via EEG Signals
authors: Dhruv Aggarwal, Sachin Choudhary, Aarchi, Mahadev Ajagalla
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Emerging Trends in Computing and Communication (ETCOM)
abstract: A popular neurophysiological technique for tracking electrical activity in the brain, electroencephalography (EEG) is especially useful for assessing epileptic seizures. The need for automated methods is highlighted by the fact that traditional methods that depend on manual feature extraction frequently miss subtle but significant signal characteristics. In this work, seizure events are classified using a pyramidal-dimensional Convolutional Neural Network (CNN). Accurate detection of epileptic seizures is difficult because they can happen at any age, are very unpredictable, and may be influenced by genetic factors. Pre-ictal, ictal, and post-ictal are the three stages into which epilepsy is typically classified clinically. The suggested CNNbased framework provides a dependable framework for realtime seizure monitoring and diagnostic assistance by not only predicting seizure occurrences but also differentiating between various categories of brain activity. The proposed integrated CNN architecture achieves an average accuracy of approximately 95% during training and 75% during testing. Furthermore, comparative analysis between the two-label CNN model and the two-label Support Vector Machine (SVM) model indicates that the CNN model exhibits superior classification performance. This improvement can be attributed to the inherent capability of CNNs to model complex non-linear relationships, whereas SVMs require carefully designed kernels to capture such non-linearities.

title: Data-Driven Methods in Simultaneous EEG-fPET-fMRI: Opportunities and Challenges
authors: Penghui Du, Jingyuan E. Chen
publish: 2025 59th Asilomar Conference on Signals, Systems, and Computers
abstract: Recent advances in human dynamic functional imaging now allow simultaneous tracking of electrophysiological activity (EEG), hemodynamic responses (fMRI), and metabolic fluctuations using a novel functional [<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">18</sup>F]-fluorodeoxyglucose PET technique (fPET-FDG). This tri-modal approach yields an exceptionally rich dataset that captures complementary physiological processes across distinct spatial and temporal scales. This conference paper addresses the analytical challenges and opportunities posed by such datasets, with a particular focus on time-resolved fusion methods capable of probing the intricate interplay among neuronal, hemodynamic, and metabolic dynamics. We first outline the specific signal characteristics and experimental design considerations for tri-modal EEG-fPET-fMRI acquisition. We then discuss existing and emerging approaches for time-resolved data integration and highlight methodological considerations for leveraging versatile, data-driven techniques to advance holistic interpretations of brain function.

title: Heterogeneous IoT Traffic in Data Center Networks: Challenges and Future Directions
authors: Ze Zhang, Chongxiang Zhang, Shaohan Wu, Liye Zhu, Qian Dong, Limin Yu, Gordon Owusu Boateng, Junjie Zhang, Long Li
publish: 2025 International Conference on Big Data and Data Mining (BDDM)
abstract: With the increasing demand for reliable computing power and large-scale storage, the Internet of Things (IoT) has been rapidly expanding, interconnecting a vast number of heterogeneous devices and enabling data-intensive applications. Data Center Networks (DCNs) play a critical role in processing massive volumes of sensor-generated data while ensuring high throughput and low latency. However, IoT traffic often combines continuous high-volume flows from devices such as cameras with short and bursty transmissions from low-power sensors. This heterogeneity often results in congestion, packet loss, and elevated latency within DCNs, thereby undermining overall network efficiency. Currently, most DCNs rely on the conventional TCP transport protocol, which is ill-suited to manage such diverse traffic dynamics and frequently exacerbates performance issues. This paper reviews existing research on IoT traffic characteristics, summarizes key performance requirements, and examines the limitations of traditional transport protocols. Finally, it discusses emerging approaches and outlines future directions for optimizing IoT traffic management in DCNs.

title: Privacy-Preserving Identity Management using Blockchain in Cybersecurity Systems
authors: Sudha P, Raghu Narayana Reddy, N. Balasundaram, Lovish Dhingra, Raman Verma, B. Sathiyaprasad
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Smart Innovations in Systems, Infrastructure, Mechanical, Power, AI and Computing Technologies (SISIMPACT)
abstract: The proposed privacy-preserving identity management framework in this research is the combination of blockchain technology, zero-knowledge proofs, and adaptive sharding, which improves security, scalability and regulatory compliance and cybersecurity system. In contrast to current blockchain-based identity mechanisms, the presented mechanism involves encryption of identity attributes and distributes them on dynamic permissioned shards with the ability to selectively disclose identity information without leaking sensitive information. Comparison to Sovrin SSI, uPort and Civic show off impressive results, with a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$33-63$</tex> percent increase in the transaction rate, 32 percent reduction in the duration of verification and 27 percent less time to generate the proofs. Compliance is achieved by reducing the storage overhead during which the immutability is preserved with chameleon hash-based redaction. There is a huge probability of leakage of privacy and efficiency of the consensus is 97 percent, so identity operations are sound and resistant to tampering. The architecture even makes it possible to have safe cross-domain authentication within heterogeneous environment. These results reassure that the framework will provide secure, efficient, and privacy-compliant identity management and thus will be very useful in enterprise, government, and IoT-based cybersecurity environments.

title: Few-Shot Learning for Domain-Specific Intent Detection in Task-Oriented Dialog Systems
authors: Prateeksha, Nigora Norqobilova, Ollabergan Allaberganov, Odilbek Matsapayev, Izzatbek Nafasov, Mukhammad Khabibullaev
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Recent Trends in Electrical, Electronics and Computing Technologies (ICRTEECT)
abstract: The success of task-oriented dialog systems depends on the effective intent detection. Nevertheless, annotated data are not easily found in novel or specialized fields, and this is a difficult task. This paper examines how few-shot learning can be used to speed up domain-specific intent detection and aims to enable dialog systems to adapt quickly and use few-shot labeled examples of tasks at low costs. In this direction we present a meta-learning model based on prototypical networks which is trained on a small number of annotated utterances per intent. It uses contextual and semantic representations to make someone else understand the intents that were already understood in the source domains that have adequate training data to a new domain that have very few training examples. Increased adaptability and reduced overfitting The method relies on contextualized language embeddings and fine-tuning to ensure more adaptability. Our widespread benchmark dataset experiments and domain-specific experiments demonstrate that our approach can be significantly more effective than other supervised and transfer learning baselines and can be used with severe data constraints. The experiments carried out show that few-shot learning does not only decrease the time of developing a dialog system but can also be used to obtain high intent classification accuracy (including rare intents). This paper shows that meta-learning is effective in achieving the objectives of scalable, flexible and efficient deployment of intent detection modules in future domain-adaptive task-oriented dialogue systems.

title: Identifying Spam and Fake Accounts in Social Platforms
authors: Kundur Nikitha, Chikati Aravind Kumar, N.Satyavathi
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Recent Trends in Electrical, Electronics and Computing Technologies (ICRTEECT)
abstract: With the exponential growth of online social networks (OSNs), platforms have become increasingly vulnerable to malicious activities such as spamming and the creation of fake user accounts. These threats compromise user privacy, degrade user experience, and undermine trust in digital platforms. This paper presents a web-based application developed using Java Server Pages (JSP) to detect spammers and identify fake user accounts in real-time within online social networks. The system integrates a hybrid approach combining behaviour-based analysis and content-based filtering techniques to analyse user activities, post frequency, friend request patterns, and message content. Supervised machine learning algorithms are employed to classify user profiles based on extracted features such as activity ratio, URL frequency, and sentiment polarity. The model is trained and validated using a benchmark social network dataset, achieving promising results in terms of accuracy, precision, and recall. This work contributes towards enhancing the security and reliability of OSNs by providing an efficient tool for early detection and mitigation of suspicious behaviours.

title: Intelligent Malicious Access Identification in IoT Networks Using Optimized LightGBM
authors: Sumukh Pandey, S. Kannimuthu, Sachin Mittal, Anusudha T A, Saba Inamdar, Brindha N
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Smart Innovations in Systems, Infrastructure, Mechanical, Power, AI and Computing Technologies (SISIMPACT)
abstract: IoT devices have made networks more intelligent, but they have also made attacks such as DoS, DDoS, spoofing and privacy invasion more common. The study suggests using a Light Gradient Boosting Machine (LGBM) to form an effective intrusion detection framework and set optimal hyperparameters. Thanks to Grid Search (GS) and Randomized Search (RS), the model's parameters are improved, so it handles high-dimensional, imbalanced sets well. Improving these hyperparameters such as the learning rate, type of boosting, values for regularization and feature importance, allows the proposed method to make more accurate predictions. Training the LGBM model with optimization on the benchmark datasets found that it performs better in F1-score, precision, recall and AUC when compared to classical and ensemble ML models. Using both RS and GS for parameter tuning improves performance, makes things less complicated and cuts down the time needed for training. It means the model is adaptable to new IoT conditions and provides an effective way to detect many types of cyber intimidations accurately.

title: Deep Survival Analysis for Field Failure Prediction With Reporting Delays and Uncertain Retirement
authors: Rong Nie, Lijun Zhou, Zhili Jiang, Hongmei Li, Yuejun Li, Ling Wu
publish: 2025 International Conference on Big Data and Data Mining (BDDM)
abstract: Predicting field failures is central to industrial reliability planning but is complicated by left truncation (monitoring starts after installation), right censoring at a data-freeze point (DFP), discrete recording to a time unit (TU), unknown retirement of unfailed units, and non-negligible reporting delays. Parametric lifetime models (e.g., Weibull) are popular yet constrain the hazard shape. We propose a deep, discrete-time survival framework that: (i) replaces fixed parametric failure-time assumptions with a neural hazard; (ii) encodes, in a single discrete-time log-likelihood, interval-recorded failures (nearest-TU), a known reporting-delay PMF, the observation window (monitoring start to DFP) via an explicit indicator, and a competing retirement process via a domain-informed prior; and (iii) produces point forecasts and prediction intervals for future reports using Poisson-Binomial aggregation that combines process and parameter uncertainty. On an industrial appliance dataset, the proposed model learns a markedly decreasing early-life hazard and forecasts near-zero additional reports for the remaining fleet, whereas a Weibull baseline-under the same retirement prior-forecasts mid-teens reports. We provide batch-level calibration, uncertainty quantification, and an architectural sensitivity analysis.

title: Maximize PSNR of MRI Image Reconstruction and De-Noising using Neural Network
authors: Shashthee Bisen, Ram Pratap Singh
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Smart Innovations in Systems, Infrastructure, Mechanical, Power, AI and Computing Technologies (SISIMPACT)
abstract: MRI is a critical medical imaging modality, but it often suffers from noise and artifacts due to acquisition constraints and environmental factors. This study focuses on enhancing MRI image reconstruction and de-noising using deep learning techniques, with a primary goal of maximizing the PSNR. The proposed auto-encoder is a deep CNN based architecture designed for image reconstruction and denoising. It follows an encoder-bottleneck-decoder structure to extract essential features while preserving spatial details. A hybrid approach combining residual learning, perceptual loss, and adversarial training is proposed to effectively remove noise and artifacts. The model is trained and evaluated on publicly available Brain Tumor and X-ray datasets with simulated and real-world noise conditions. Experimental results demonstrate a significant improvement in PSNR, Loss, MSE, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{R}^{2}$</tex> and RMSE compared to conventional filtering and model-based approaches. This research contributes to the advancement of AI-driven MRI enhancement, facilitating better diagnostic accuracy and clinical applications.

title: Dynamic Load Balancer Integration for Multi-Cluster Kubernetes Environments in Bare-Metal Deployments
authors: Venkata Madhu Prateek Reddy Kambala
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Emerging Trends in Computing and Communication (ETCOM)
abstract: With the rapid adoption of cloud computing platforms like Amazon Web Services (AWS), Google Cloud Platform (GCP), and Microsoft Azure, the demand for scalable computing resources has surged. Kubernetes has emerged as a vital orchestration tool for managing dynamic, cloud-native applications across multiple nodes, ensuring efficient scaling and failover mechanisms. However, in bare-metal environments, the challenge of providing a dynamic, adaptable load-balancing solution for Kubernetes clusters remains. This work explores the development of a custom load balancer integration for multi-cluster Kubernetes environments in bare-metal deployments, focusing on the gaps in current load-balancing solutions for such setups. By leveraging Kubernetes' capabilities, the proposed solution enables automated provisioning, health checks, and failover, optimizing resource allocation and ensuring high availability. This paper also presents the Kubermatic Kubernetes Platform and the KubeLB project as key components in addressing these challenges while also detailing the architecture, implementation, and future prospects of this load-balancing solution in large-scale Kubernetes infrastructures.

title: Meta-Learning for Few-Shot Adaptation in Robotic Control Tasks
authors: Saksham Mittal, Samariddin Makhmudov, Sarvarbek Matniyozov, Barno Matchanova, Asadbek Sobirov, Temur Eshchanov
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Recent Trends in Electrical, Electronics and Computing Technologies (ICRTEECT)
abstract: Meta-learning is emerging as a powerful paradigm to allow robots to learn new tasks quickly and with little data, to address an obstacle in robot control. This paper investigates few-shot adaptation methods such as MAML, Reptile, and prototypical network that leverage experience to promote performance on a wide range of manipulation, locomotion, and grasping tasks. By observing a collection (distribution) of simulated or real-world tasks, meta-learning algorithms adapt initialization parameters or latent features such that fast fine-tuning (typically across 5-10 trials) is possible for new tasks. Key contributions include memory-augmented neural networks for contextual memory, gradient-based optimization for fast parameter updates, and hierarchical meta-policies for exploiting multi-task scale. Get this Paper Challenges remain in the sim-to-real transfer setting, in which dynamics mismatches and sensory noise lead to poor performance; the paper further investigates domain randomization and latent space alignment. We measure metrics such as adaptation time, task success rate, and sample efficiency over benchmark environments (e.g., Meta-World, RoboSuite). It is found that meta-learning reduces the requirement for data by 70-80% compared to traditional reinforcement learning and achieves robustness against kinematic variations and environmental distractors. Future work may consider fusing physics-informed priors and the hybrid meta-learning architecture, containing meta-learning and imitation learning. This work highlights the promise of meta-learning in enabling democratized robotic deployment across unstructured settings from warehouses to homes, where task diversity and data scarcity are becoming central limitations.

title: RetinaScope: Real-Time Retina Disease Classification Using VGG16 with Explainable AI and Collaborative Doctor-Patient Hub
authors: A. Deepak Kumar, Jayaharini J, Jerlin J
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Emerging Trends in Computing and Communication (ETCOM)
abstract: This paper presents RetinaScope, an AI-driven platform designed for the early detection of retinal conditions, specifically cataract, diabetic retinopathy, and glaucoma. The system's architecture combines a VGG16-based classification model with an Explainable AI (XAI) component, Grad-CAM, to ensure prediction interpretability. Furthermore, it features a real-time doctor-patient hub, built on a React frontend and FastAPI backend, which facilitates remote image uploads, synchronous reviews, and collaborative diagnostics. RetinaScope achieves high classification accuracy and emphasizes transparent, interpretable predictions, offering a scalable and reliable solution for patient-centric, AI-assisted ophthalmology.

title: Developing an Advanced Cross layer Distributed Model for Enhanced Attack Detection Model
authors: Smriti Singhatia, Madhavi Dhingra
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Recent Trends in Electrical, Electronics and Computing Technologies (ICRTEECT)
abstract: The increasing interconnectivity of digital ecosystems such as the Internet of Things (IoT), Industry 5.0, and smart grids has led to a surge in sophisticated, distributed, and multi-vector cyber-attacks. Conventional intrusion detection systems (IDS), constrained by single-layer analysis and centralized architectures, are inadequate in addressing these evolving security challenges. This study presents a novel Advanced Cross-Layer Distributed Intrusion Detection Model (ACLD-IDM) that integrates federated learning with an attention-based deep learning mechanism to enable real-time, privacy-preserving, and cooperative threat detection. The proposed model introduces a cross-layer feature fusion strategy that correlates traffic data across the physical, network, and application layers, thereby uncovering hidden attack patterns that traditional single-layer systems often overlook. The ACLD-IDM employs hybrid artificial intelligence (AI) techniques, including recurrent neural networks (RNNs), long short-term memory (LSTM) networks, graph neural networks (GNNs), and Transformer architectures, to enhance detection accuracy, scalability, and resilience in heterogeneous and resource-constrained environments. Evaluation on benchmark datasets—NSL-KDD, CICIDS2017, and UNSW-NB15—demonstrates the model’s superior adaptability to zero-day and multi-vector attacks while maintaining strong privacy preservation through decentralized federated learning. The novelty of this research lies in its fusion of cross-layer analytics, decentralized intelligence, and attention-based adaptive learning, establishing a robust foundation for next-generation, intelligent, and self-sustaining cybersecurity frameworks capable of defending complex digital infrastructures against emerging threats

title: AI-Driven Breast Cancer Detection: An Empirical Analysis of Deep Learning and Machine Learning Techniques
authors: Vibhor Gupta, Amit Kumar Bindal, Amit Kumar
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Smart Innovations in Systems, Infrastructure, Mechanical, Power, AI and Computing Technologies (SISIMPACT)
abstract: Breast cancer is regarded as one of the most prevalent and perilous diseases affecting women globally. A fast and accurate diagnosis is crucial for improving patient survival. Mammography, biopsy, and pathologist interpretation are essential for breast cancer diagnosis; nevertheless, they are fraught with deficiencies such as human error, exorbitant costs, and limited accessibility. This research presents deep learning and machine learning techniques for automated breast cancer screening to overcome these challenges. Models including CNN, ResNet50, U-Net, Random Forest, and SVM were evaluated using critical datasets such as WDBC, BreakHis, and BCDR-D01. The empirical analysis demonstrates that the deep learning architecture ResNet50 outperformed others, achieving an accuracy of 0.97, an F1 score of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{0. 9 6}$</tex>, and an AUC of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{0. 9 8}$</tex>.

title: Modified Transformers are Provably In-context MAP Estimators over Block Fading Channels
authors: Mohamed Ghada Waeel Hamdy, Bhaskar Rao, Piya Pal
publish: 2025 59th Asilomar Conference on Signals, Systems, and Computers
abstract: Motivated by recent advances in in-context learning demonstrated by transformers, we propose a transformer-based decoder that frames detection as a few-shot inference problem, leveraging past pilot symbols as contextual information without requiring retraining, knowledge of the channel statistics, or explicit channel estimation. Unlike prior work, we demonstrate that, by employing a new attention mechanism based on a novel nonlinearity, the transformer asymptotically converges, as the number of pilots increases, to the context-aware (i.e., with perfect channel state information) maximum a posteriori probability estimate that minimizes the bit error rate, without assuming a fixed noise power.We provide both analytical justification and empirical validation for this result. To support practical deployment, we further introduce a mixture-of-experts transformer architecture tailored to high-order constellations, in which individual attention heads specialize over constant-norm subsets. This design outperforms existing architectures. Overall, this study offers new insight into the in-context learning capabilities of transformers by applying them to a well-studied detection problem.

title: Adaptive AI ECC Hybrid Burst Error Correction using Deep Learning for Next Generation Communication and Memory Devices
authors: Shiv Prasad Lingampally, Chakradhar Adupa, Gyanendra Kumar Maurya
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Recent Trends in Electrical, Electronics and Computing Technologies (ICRTEECT)
abstract: Burst errors—extended runs of consecutive bit corruptions—pose a fundamental challenge for next-generation systems such as 6G wireless networks and QLC NAND flash memories. Conventional error correction codes (ECC), such as LDPC and BCH, offer strong protection against random noise but deteriorate markedly when faced with structured burst patterns. Conversely, AI-based detectors can localize burst-prone regions but lack mathematically grounded correction. To bridge this gap, we introduce a hybrid error-control framework that couples deep learning models (CNNs, LSTMs, and Transformers) for burst-region detection with LDPC decoding to ensure reliable correction. Evaluation was carried out using synthetic datasets together with real-world traces covering wireless, optical, and memory use cases. Experimental evaluations confirm consistent gains in bit error rate (BER), burst coverage ratio (BCR), and decoding latency, outperforming both ECC-only and AI-only baselines. Beyond performance gains, the design emphasizes explainability, scalability, and hardware readiness, thereby positioning it as a strong candidate for real-time deployment in future communication networks and high-density storage systems.

title: Empowering Language Understanding Through Advanced Language Models and NLP Innovations
authors: C. Shyamala Kumari, S. Vijayaselvarani, Vuriti Sai Pranay, Rajneesh Pandey, T. Ranjitha
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Recent Trends in Electrical, Electronics and Computing Technologies (ICRTEECT)
abstract: The development of Tamil Question Answering (QA) systems faces significant challenges due to the language’s low-resource status and language complexity. In this work, we specifically address these challenges by implementing multilingual contrastive training and code-switching strategies to improve model performance on Tamil QA tasks. Code switching-where speakers alternate between two or more languages within a conversation or sentence. In our approach, code-switching is applied primarily in two ways: as a data augmentation technique and to better handle real-world two language Tamil-English QA scenarios. Leveraging powerful models like LLMs, Transformers, and BERT can greatly enhance Tamil language processing, enabling applications such as machine translation, subtitle generation. A critical part of our approach involves assembling and utilizing carefully curated datasets, including Parallel corpora that map Tamil to other languages, high-quality transcribed speech data, and digitized obtained through OCR pipelines form the backbone for such efforts. These datasets form the foundation for model training and evaluation. Beyond the data itself, Tamil’s rich morphology—with words carrying multiple layers of grammatical meaning—and its broad spectrum of dialects, from formal centami to regional colloquial varieties, complicate model development. To address these hurdles, researchers increasingly turn to cross-lingual transfer learning, synthetic data generation, and semi-supervised approaches to improve model generalization despite limited native-language resources. This work explores these challenges and opportunities, focusing on sourcing datasets, leveraging existing models, and applying effective techniques to develop a reliable Tamil QA system.

title: Non-Stationary Causal Learning via Hierarchical Modeling
authors: Daniel Waxman, Petar M. Djurić
publish: 2025 59th Asilomar Conference on Signals, Systems, and Computers
abstract: Causal discovery in time-varying environments requires methods that accommodate changes in the underlying causal mechanisms while retaining computational tractability. Classical approaches impose a stationarity assumption that forces all observations to share a single directed acyclic graph (DAG), an assumption that fails in many real systems. Modern differentiable causal discovery frameworks address the static problem through continuous optimization but provide limited support for non-stationary settings. This paper introduces a hierarchical framework for non-stationary causal discovery based on an unconstrained parameterization of DAGs. A stochastic-process prior on the sequence of latent potentials restricts the temporal evolution of the graphs and permits smooth, abrupt, or locally adaptive structural changes. Random-walk, Gaussian-process, and switching-state priors arise as special cases. The resulting formulation produces a fully differentiable objective that incorporates data fit, sparsity, and temporal structure. Experiments under linear structural equation models show that appropriate temporal regularization improves the recovery of time-varying causal relationships relative to stationary estimators. The proposed framework, therefore, provides a flexible and computationally efficient approach to causal identification in non-stationary time series.

title: Connection Tracking at 400 Gbps
authors: David Vodák, Oliver Gurka, Jiří Matoušek, Daniel Kondys
publish: 2025 35th International Conference on Field-Programmable Logic and Applications (FPL)
abstract: Many critical applications, such as network monitoring, firewall, or VPN acceleration, are based on connection tracking. This process aggregates packets into network flows and tracks the state of each active flow. It is a challenging task to implement connection tracking for a high-speed network link. It needs to be accelerated in hardware (because of high packet rates) and also utilize external memory (because of high active flow counts). However, the latency of external memory access can seriously impact the performance of connection tracking if not handled properly. This demo showcases our implementation of a high-speed connection tracking table (CTT) that targets FPGA SmartNICs. It supports 400 Gbps throughput and up to 512 M of active flows. We also demonstrate the integration of our FPGA firmware with IPFIXProbe, an open-source monitoring software developed by CESNET.

title: Towards Transparent Diagnostics: Explainable AI Across Biomedical Signals
authors: Ali Mohammad Alqudah, Zahra Moussavi
publish: 2025 International Conference on Innovation and Intelligence for Informatics, Computing, and Technologies (3ICT)
abstract: This paper delves into the pivotal role of Explainable Artificial Intelligence (XAI) within the realm of biomedical signal analysis. As AI systems become increasingly integrated into clinical workflows, the imperative for transparency and interpretability in their decision-making processes has become paramount. This work explores the foundational principles of XAI, its diverse applications across various biomedical signals, including tracheal breathing sounds, and the profound clinical advantages derived from its implementation. We emphasize how XAI can significantly enhance diagnostic accuracy, foster trust among healthcare professionals, and facilitate more informed patient management by providing clear insights into AI-driven predictions. Furthermore, the paper addresses the inherent challenges and outlines future trajectories for XAI in this rapidly evolving and critical domain.

title: Routino: Accelerating FPGA Routing Through Efficient Memory Representation
authors: Davide Nicolini, Corrado De Sio, Eleonora Vacca, Luca Sterpone
publish: 2025 35th International Conference on Field-Programmable Logic and Applications (FPL)
abstract: The rapid increase in the complexity of Field-Programmable Gate Arrays (FPGAs) is significantly impacting the efficiency of the design implementation flow. In particular, the routing process presents challenges in achieving computational efficiency and reducing time-to-solution due to the increasing on-chip resources and device complexity. This work introduces a novel router that leverages optimized data structures and memory access patterns to minimize memory consumption. Experimental results prove how the proposed approach can significantly reduce time-to-solution, identifying memory consumption as a barrier to achieving scalability and proposing solutions based on FPGA modular architecture to face it, achieving an average memory usage reduction of about 90 % and an average decrease of routing time of 40 %.

title: Advancing Bayesian Inference with Quantile-Based Likelihoods
authors: Imran Ali Mohammed
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Emerging Trends in Computing and Communication (ETCOM)
abstract: This paper extends Bayesian inference capabilities to accommodate probability distributions defined through their quantile functions. We present an indirect likelihood method for Bayesian models where sampling distributions lack an explicit cumulative form. The equivalence of this quantile-based approach to conventional density-defined likelihoods is demonstrated. Practical considerations for the numerical inversion of quantile functions, including ensuring their validity using polynomial approximations, are addressed, with implementation guidance provided for common statistical platforms. Additionally, an indirect prior definition is proposed, offering new avenues for robust Bayesian analysis in complex distributional settings.

title: Characterizing Seizure Dynamics Using Spectral Graph Inference for Seizure Onset Zone Localization
authors: Aila Teimouri, Ali Azizpour, Santiago Segarra, Nitin Tandon, Behnaam Aazhang
publish: 2025 59th Asilomar Conference on Signals, Systems, and Computers
abstract: Accurate localization of the seizure onset zone (SOZ), the brain regions where seizures originate, is critical for the success of resective and neuromodulation therapies in patients with focal drug-resistant epilepsy. Traditional SOZ localization by clinicians is subjective, time-consuming, and may overlook the rapidly evolving network interactions that characterize the initiation and propagation of seizures. Moreover, most automated network-based approaches rely on static or pairwise connectivity metrics such as correlation or coherence, which fail to capture higher-order and time-varying network structure essential for identifying the epileptogenic network. In this work, we introduce a dynamic graph topology inference framework for SOZ localization that models neural activity as the output of a diffusion process on a latent, time-varying brain network. By directly estimating a sequence of graphs from intracranial EEG (iEEG) recordings and extracting spatiotemporal graph-based features, our method provides an interpretable representation of how the network structure evolves around the seizure onset. We apply our framework to iEEG recordings from the publicly available HUP dataset and assess its clinical utility by evaluating the SOZ prediction performance. Across seven patients, our method outperforms several existing graph inference baselines in SOZ detection, achieving the highest sensitivity, specificity, and AUC.

title: President's Message
authors: N/A
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Emerging Trends in Computing and Communication (ETCOM)

title: Design of a Digital Twin System for a Biped Robot for Prediction of Foot-terrain Contact Forces and its Validation using a Force-sensing Foot_supp2-3677302.pdf
authors: Vyankatesh Ashtekar
abstract: This paper presents a digital twin system that facilitates robot operation and combined prediction of state and dynamics of a biped robot with imperfections such as parasitic compliance. The key contribution lies in utilising proprioceptive sensor feedback together with a contact-aware forward-dynamics (shadow) simulation, augmented by a contact-implicit inverse-dynamics controller—assuming MuJoCo's rigid-body contact formulation—to improve the replication of the robot's posture and dynamics. The speed and accuracy of the developed method is demonstrated through physical experiments on a small biped robot standing on flat, steps or inclined ground. The predicted contact forces are validated using the feedback of a force-sensing robot foot via the concept of zero-tilting moment point. For the first time, a systematic design of a robot foot using force-sensing resistors is presented to achieve repeatability, linearity, and sensitivity to small loads.

title: Proceedings of the IEEE Publication Information
authors: N/A

title: Non-orthogonal Coding Against Information Leakage in Image Semantic Communication Systems
authors: Linlin Xiao, Buxiang Sheng, Zhiqiang Feng, Peisong Zhang, Donghong Cai, Zahid Khan
abstract: This paper focuses on the information leakage and secure encoding transmission in semantic communication systems. A two-path images feature-coupling coding transmission is proposed to improve the transmission efficiency and bandwidth utilization while ensuring the restoration quality of each image. In particular, the designed encoder first extracts the feature of each-path image and then performs superimposed encoding in the feature space. In order to separately restore the two images at the decoder without successive interference cancellation or other traditional multi-user estimation, we add the device vectors to train the encoder and decoder. Unlike embedding the device vectors into each image, we embed the device vectors in the feature space instead. Furthermore, we construct an inversion attack network based on generative adversarial networks, which can simulate a potential eavesdropper and use the received transmission signal to recover the original image. Therefore, we can evaluate the system’s defense capabilities against model inversion attacks. Moreover, we use Huawei Ascend Atlas 200I DK A2 development board to conduct hardware simulation of legal user communication with proposed feature-coupling coding and the interception of information by eavesdroppers. Experimental results show that the proposed feature-coupling coding has better Peak Signal-to-Noise Ratio performance compared to the data–coupling coding and effectively resists information leakage among different noise level.

title: Simultaneous Self-Localization and Base Station Localization with Resonant Beam
authors: Guangkun Zhang, Wen Fang, Mingliang Xiong, Qingwen Liu, Mengyuan Xu, Yunfeng Bai, Mingqing Liu, Siyuan Du
abstract: High-precision positioning in GPS-denied environments is a challenging yet critical technology. Resonant beam positioning (RBP) utilizes a resonant beam with properties such as energy focusing, self-establishment, self-alignment, and passive operation, offering a promising solution for this task. However, traditional RBP algorithms require a fixed number of resonant beam base stations, which can be costly to expand coverage. To address this limitation, we propose a distributed resonant beam positioning (DRBP) system that simultaneously estimates the base station and mobile target (MT) positions. Firstly, the MT receives resonant beam samples to locate the base station in the limited field of view (FoV) region. Subsequently, it estimates its own position by tracking the apparent motion of these newly localized base stations. During moving, the DRBP system facilitates self-positioning on the MT side, enabling dynamic expansion of both the number of base stations and the coverage area. Numerical results demonstrate that DRBP achieves a positioning root mean square error (RMSE) of 0.1 m and a rotation RMSE of 2<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$^\circ$</tex-math></inline-formula>, validating the system's high accuracy.

title: Dual Time-Frequency Feature Fusion Framework for Incipient ITSC Fault Diagnosis in Induction Motors
authors: Jinhan Zhou, Xilian Wang, Junfeng Gui, Jianfeng Hong, Huabin Wen
publish: IEEE Transactions on Instrumentation and Measurement
abstract: Existing time-frequency feature analysis based on deep-learning framework for induction motor (IMs) fault diagnosis predominantly focus on single amplitude spectrum, overlooking the potential of phase spectrum and their fusion. This study proposes a dual time-frequency fusion diagnostic method for incipient inter-turn short-circuit (ITSC) to address this gap. The method uniquely integrates features from the time and dual frequency domain (both amplitude and phase spectrum) of vibration signals. Firstly, the feasibility of utilizing dual frequency domain of vibration signals for detecting ITSC is validated through theoretical derivation. Subsequently, a multi-channel cross-attention mechanism is designed to extract and fuse time-frequency features. An adaptive gated dual-spectral fusion strategy is then employed to dynamically weight and integrate the amplitude and phase spectral features, capturing comprehensive fault signatures. Experimental investigations and data acquisition were conducted on induction motors with ITSC to evaluate this model under multiple fault scenarios. The method is demonstrated that it achieves a fault diagnosis accuracy of 99.4% even under complex operating conditions when the short-circuit degree is as low as 0.4%. The results confirm that incorporating phase information with proposed structure significantly enhances diagnostic precision and fault severity discrimination, showcasing the model's potential for reliable detection of incipient faults.

title: ICIIS 2026 Contributor Page
authors: N/A
publish: 2025 IEEE 19th International Conference on Industrial and Information Systems (ICIIS)

title: AI-Assisted Electronic Digital Twin for Capacitive Pressure Sensors Controlling Robotic Gripping
authors: Alessandro Massaro, Nicola Epicoco
abstract: The paper focuses on the analysis of the Artificial Intelligence (AI) classification of the pressure forces to control the gripping of a robotic hand. The goal is to structure an Electronic Digital Twin (EDT) model of capacitance sensors to classify robotic finger movements. The model simulates a robotic application of two fingers gripper (Figure (a)) by means of a circuit model based on the capacitive effect principle. The proposed approach is able to classify and apply a control signal using an AI engine trained by circuit simulations (Figure (b)) providing the theoretical hand responses to pressure forces. The AI analysis is initially performed to select the best algorithm for the classification of the gripping and its control (Figure (c)) by processing a dataset of an electronic glove characterized by different time series signals of capacitance sensors. Compared with other AI supervised algorithms, such as Random Forest (RF) and Artificial Neural Network (ANN), the Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) exhibits the best performance for complex capacitive sensor systems. The CNN-EDT model is then successively applied to a system of two coupled capacitive sensors (Figure (d)) by proving the possibility to process even more complex robotic control systems, such as an electronic glove. The EDT is constructed by using LTSpice and KNIME open-source tools.

title: VATS: Visual–Audio Multitask Transformer With Specialty Audio Encoder for Multimodal Deepfake Detection in CPSS
authors: Ruiqi Zha, Zhichao Lian, Qianmu Li
publish: IEEE Transactions on Computational Social Systems
abstract: Detecting multimodal deepfakes has become a pressing concern due to the rising sophistication of generative techniques capable of creating highly convincing visual-speech synchronized deepfakes. As these manipulated contents proliferate on social media, remote education, smart meetings, smart homes, and virtual reality (VR), their increasing realism significantly impacts individuals, underscoring the urgent need for effective detection methods to curb their deceptive spread. Several research efforts have aimed to detect visual–audio deepfakes by examining inter-modal correlation and inter-class consistency. However, optimal detection results have yet to be achieved. Studies have indicated that detectors learn forged characteristics specific to particular generative methods and hide the real class in the visual modality, leading to a bias towards forgery distributions and degrading detection performance. Since audio modality data is a 2-D signal, we consider this bias in the audio modality to be more serious than in the visual modality, a conclusion we have verified through experiments. Therefore, an audio encoder that learns a universal audio distribution is indispensable for effective audio modality deepfake detection. In this work, we propose a new visual–audio deepfake detection method visual–audio multitask transformer with specialty audio encoder (VATS), designed to learn a more robust and comprehensive representation, thereby mitigating overfitting. The structure includes unimodal encoders for preliminary encoding and a dual-stream structure based on a multimodal framework for multilevel interaction and precise detection. To prevent the audio encoder from focusing excessively on specific identities or generator textures, we employ a pretrained speech encoder in the audio stream, while utilizing a linear projection encoder in the visual stream to focus on the fine-grained features of each video frame rather than the semantic information of the graphic. Our method comprises three key components: the fast temporal multimodal alignment and embedding module (FTAE), the modality interaction block (MIB), and the interpretable manipulated location module (IML). Experimental results on FakeAVCeleb demonstrate that our proposed method outperforms previous works in multimodal deepfake detection.

title: A Novel Data-Driven Framework Based on K-NN and PCA: Evolutionary Calibration of Game Parameters in Multi-Agent Systems
authors: Xin Wu, Kai Zou, Jianyi Luo
publish: 2025 International Conference on Big Data and Data Mining (BDDM)
abstract: The validity of conventional game models frequently finds itself constrained by their reliance on theoretical assumptions for parameters, which are devoid of empirical validation. In order to address this fundamental limitation, this paper puts forward a data-driven framework that employs an innovative and robust integration of the K-Nearest Neighbours (K-NN) algorithm and Principal Component Analysis (PCA), with a view to achieving empirical parameter calibration. The parameter estimation problem is transformed into a multiple regression task, which involves predicting payoff matrix parameters for a four-agent evolutionary game. These parameters are based on feature similarity between target scenarios and historical cases. A comprehensive data set was constructed, comprising 120 real-world event records across 28 dimensions. PCA facilitates the reduction of dimensionality and enhancement of features, thereby ensuring computational efficiency while concomitantly mitigating multicollinearity. The core framework utilises a distance-weighted K-nearest neighbour (KNN) regression model in order to calibrate cost, payoff, and incentive parameters. It is posited that parameters derived through data mining processes are to be integrated into replicator dynamic equations, with the aim of simulating strategic interactions among finitely rational agents. The efficacy of the proposed model has been substantiated by a substantial body of numerical simulations. In comparison to conventional parameterised models, the proposed model has been shown to demonstrate a 45% reduction in oscillation amplitude and an 18% enhancement in convergence rate. In addition, a sensitivity analysis of the data-derived parameters has exposed critical system thresholds and counterintuitive optimal policy ratios. These are conclusions that are unattainable through theoretical deductions alone. The central tenet of the present research lies in the establishment of a rigorous, transparent, and reproducible methodological framework. The employment of fundamental data mining techniques has been demonstrated to bridge the gap between abstract game theory and empirical reality. This enhancement of the predictive capability and practical value of evolutionary game models has been shown to facilitate more efficient analysis of complex multi-agent systems.

title: Enhancing Financial Protection using Advanced Fraud Detection Methods
authors: Krishnaveni V, V Sumalatha, Mridul Dixit, Ajay Kuamr Sahu, Deep Biswas, Jolly Sneh Kapoor
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Smart Innovations in Systems, Infrastructure, Mechanical, Power, AI and Computing Technologies (SISIMPACT)
abstract: Financial fraud continues to pose a significant threat to financial institutions and individual consumers alike. Traditional rule-based systems struggle to keep pace with the evolving nature of fraudulent activities. This paper explores the potential of Artificial Neural Networks (ANNs) with a specific focus on binary classification for identifying fraudulent transactions in real-time. We propose a novel approach utilizing a powerful ANN architecture trained on historical labeled data, enabling effective and efficient detection of anomalous patterns indicative of fraud. Furthermore, the paper introduces the development of a comprehensive and user-friendly dashboard designed to seamlessly integrate with the ANN model. This interactive dashboard empowers stakeholders with real-time visualization of transaction activity, allowing for dynamic monitoring and management of flagged transactions. By combining the strengths of an advanced ANN model and a user-centric dashboard, this research proposes a comprehensive solution aimed at enhancing the accuracy and efficiency of financial fraud detection, ultimately safeguarding financial institutions and consumers from potential losses. This research signifies a valuable contribution to the field of financial fraud detection by demonstrating the effectiveness of ANNs coupled with an insightful dashboard, offering a more robust and comprehensive approach to combatting financial crime.

title: Blue Elixir - Real Time Water Purity Assessment and Purification Recommendation System using Isolation Tree Model and Sensors
authors: Shanmughapriya M, Uma Saras A, Sandhya A, Srivarthini P
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Recent Trends in Electrical, Electronics and Computing Technologies (ICRTEECT)
abstract: Water is the next big market in the world today! It is indeed a challenging task to provide good quality water for the purpose of drinking. The main objective of the proposed work is to build a customized device that can monitor the water purity at real time. The aim of this work is to design an artificially intelligent model to measure the water quality based on Total Dissolved Solids (TDS). Ion Selective Electrode is used with eight different membranes for measuring the total dissolved solids in water. The Isolation Tree Model (ITM) is built by using the customized dataset collected from the national water quality monitoring council website. The Isolation tree model is trained with the customized dataset, the data collected from the real time sample using electrodes is fed to the model via a web application, the model classifies the water quality into three groups a) For Drinking - The collected water is well within the limits in terms of TDS as recommended by monitoring council b) For Domestic use - the collected water can be used for domestic use like washing and cleaning c) Needs purification - the water collected is not suitable for use and methods of purification is recommended based on the measures of ions and chemicals. The isolation tree model's classification accuracy is 97%. The purification recommendations are based on the water council’s guidelines.

title: Resource Orchestration of Natural Language Processing and Artificial Intelligence of Things in Resilient Supply Chain Management
authors: Anubrata Dhar Chaudhuri
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Smart Innovations in Systems, Infrastructure, Mechanical, Power, AI and Computing Technologies (SISIMPACT)
abstract: This study investigates how integrating Natural Language Processing (NLP) with Artificial Intelligence of Things (AIoT) technologies enhances supply chain resilience (SCR) and performance (SCP). While existing literature predominantly examines NLP in customer analytics contexts, this research addresses a critical gap by exploring NLP-AIoT synergies for supply chain optimization under disruption conditions. Grounded in Resource Orchestration Theory (ROT), the proposed framework conceptualizes NLP adoption as a resource-bundling mechanism where perceived usefulness of NLP-enabled AIoT (PUAIoT) and NLP-enabled supply chain management (PUSCM) drive technology implementation. The model further examines how leadership support moderates the relationship between NLP adoption and resilience outcomes. Empirical validation comes from 1,302 supply chain professionals across 24 manufacturing and logistics firms in North America and Asia. Partial Least Squares Structural Equation Modeling (PLS-SEM) analysis confirms that PUAIoT <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(\beta=0.24, \mathrm{p}<0.01)$</tex> and PUSCM (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\beta=0.31, \mathrm{p}<0.001$</tex>) significantly drive NLP adoption, which subsequently improves SCR <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(\beta=0.33, \mathrm{p}<0.001)$</tex> and SCP (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\beta=0.29, \mathrm{p}<0.001$</tex>). Leadership support amplifies the NLP-SCR relationship (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\boldsymbol{\beta}=\mathbf{0. 2 1}, \mathbf{p}<\mathbf{0. 0 1}$</tex>), while SCR mediates 68 % of NLP's impact on performance outcomes. The model explains 71 % of SCP variance, offering theoretical advances in digital supply chain management and practical frameworks for disruption mitigation.

title: Comparative Study of Deep Learning Models for Diabetic Retinopathy Classification Using Fundus Images
authors: Arshaq Ibrahim, Tilani Gunawardena
publish: 2025 IEEE 19th International Conference on Industrial and Information Systems (ICIIS)
abstract: Diabetic Retinopathy (DR) is a major cause of vision loss globally, necessitating early and accurate diagnosis. This paper evaluates the effectiveness of various deep learning models such as InceptionV3, DenseNet121, DenseNet169, DenseNet201, and ResNet50 in classifying DR stages using retinal fundus images and the efficacy of a custom CNN binary classification model for DR detection. Two publicly available datasets, APTOS 2019 and DR Detection 2015, were used. The study explores the impact of dataset balance, volume, and hyperparameter optimization on model performance. InceptionV3 and DenseNet201 emerged as top performers among the pretrained models used for multi-class classification, with InceptionV3 achieving 75% accuracy on APTOS and DenseNet201 matching it. Meanwhile, the custom CNN binary classification model achieved an accuracy of 91.45%. The findings demonstrate the feasibility of using transfer learning for automated DR stage screening and the efficacy of Convolutional Neural Networks in identifying Diabetic Retinopathy while highlighting the significance of dataset quality and class balance.

title: A Comparative Survey of V2V Communication Technologies: DSRC, Wi-Fi, LoRa and VLC
authors: Sudha G, Raghav G S, Saranya S, Yugenthar A P, Aakash P, Rajalakshmi B
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Recent Trends in Electrical, Electronics and Computing Technologies (ICRTEECT)
abstract: The proposed methodology introduces a new way to improve Vehicle-to-Vehicle (V2V) communication by comparing and contrasting numerous inferred approaches from the existing technologies on the subject. This proposed study includes the solution provided using the LoRa, DSRC, Wi-Fi model of communication, and VLC with their advantages and limitations they hold in the respective areas. They each individually hold different aspects of advantages in various areas, including efficient communication, reliability, and latency rates. Overall, this article presents a comprehensive solution for vehicle communication and traffic analysis, leveraging the advantages of various technologies. By facilitating data driven traffic control and smooth communication, the suggested system might significantly improve the efficiency and safety of current transportation networks.

title: Monotonic Learning: Guaranteed Improvement in Neural Network Training
authors: Sa'eed Serwan Abdulsattar, Fayzeh Abdulkareem Jaber, Hani Al-Balasmeh, Rahmeh Abdulkareem Jaber, Ahmad AbdulKareem Jaber, Nour Abdulkareem Jaber
publish: 2025 International Conference on Innovation and Intelligence for Informatics, Computing, and Technologies (3ICT)
abstract: This paper presents Monotonic Neural Training, a novel approach to neural network optimization that eliminates performance regressions while improving model quality. Unlike conventional methods that accept all parameter updates, our method enforces strict improvement criteria, ensuring each step advances validation performance. Traditional training often suffers from unpredictable dynamics, with sudden deteriorations requiring constant monitoring and manual intervention. Monotonic Neural Training addresses this via an “improve-or-reject” mechanism: updates are only committed if they demonstrate measurable improvement. The approach treats optimization as a quality-controlled process rather than blind descent. Candidate updates are validated, and failing updates trigger automatic retries with varied batch sampling and intelligent perturbations, forming a self-correcting training loop that guarantees monotonic progress. Experimental results on both regression and classification tasks demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed monotonic training framework. On the diabetes regression benchmark, the method achieved a mean coefficient of determination (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$R^{2}$</tex>) of 0.569 compared to 0.450 for conventional training, reflecting a 26 % improvement. On the breast cancer classification task, it reached an accuracy of 0.991 versus 0.947, yielding a 4.6 % gain. While the approach requires greater computational effort due to its selective update process—accepting less than 1 % of candidate steps—it ensures strictly non-degrading performance and stable, deterministic convergence across all runs. Beyond performance gains, Monotonic Neural Training navigates complex loss landscapes autonomously, adapting to training difficulties without human supervision. Even steps requiring over thousands of retries ultimately succeed, demonstrating that patient, qualityfocused optimization outperforms rapid but unstable approaches. Ultimately, the fastest path to superior models is not accelerating updates but ensuring that every update genuinely improves performance.

title: Design and Analysis of a Power-Efficient 4-Bit ALU with XNOR Logic for Enhanced Performance
authors: K. Padmavathi, Venkatarao Tirumalasetty, K.Swetha, D.Prathyusha, B.Kaveri, M.Jhansi, K.Sridevi, A. Jayasree
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Recent Trends in Electrical, Electronics and Computing Technologies (ICRTEECT)
abstract: This paper focuses on designing a 4-bit ALU that prioritizes low power consumption and high speed of operation by utilizing XNOR logic. The ALU supports a range of arithmetic functions—such as addition, subtraction, pass-through, and two’s complement—as well as logic operations including AND, OR, XOR, and XNOR. Since the full adder is a fundamental component of the ALU, optimizing its power usage significantly affects the overall power efficiency. The designed ALU exhibits a power consumption of 2.26 mW, a propagation delay of 1.58 ns, and a power-delay product (PDP) of 3.5210 pJ. By adopting an XNOR-based full adder design, the system achieved a 50% reduction in power consumption compared to the traditional Gate Diffusion Input (GDI) full adder. The entire design was simulated using the Cadence Virtuoso platform at the 90 nm technology node, operating at a supply voltage of 1.8 V.

title: Fostering Engagement Through a Latency-Optimized LLM-Based Dialogue System for Multimodal ECA Responses
