The study suggests a comprehensive theory of data property rights through the principle of adaptive legal framework, global collaborators, and online defence to the steady the search, the rights of person and the public eudaemonia.

title: Dynamic Pricing Scheduling and Mobile Charging Scheme for Electric Vehicles Based on Cloud and Fog Computing
authors: Cui Gu, Ke Gu
publish: 2025 International Conference on Big Data and Data Mining (BDDM)
abstract: Recently, there has been a widespread adoption of electric vehicles (EVs), aiming to fully supplant vehicles powered by internal combustion engines. However, the availability of electrical resources at charging stations and parking areas is limited, so an optimized charging scheme needs to be developed. In this paper, leveraging a cloud-fog computing architecture, we propose a dynamic pricing scheduling and mobile charging scheme for electric vehicles. In our scheme, we first develop a cloud-fog computing-based system model that assists EV users in selecting appropriate charging points and provides a foundation for unified scheduling. Second, we propose a real-time dynamic pricing scheme driven by time-of-use tariffs and congestion at charging points, which can avoid the situation where some charging points are too congested while others are empty. Third, we propose an enhanced scheduling and routing algorithm designed to minimize both time and cost expenses. This algorithm leverages fuzzy logic control and Dijkstra's method to address the complexities of scheduling and routing challenges effectively. Finally, we also propose a standby program for users whose power is too low to reach a charging point by arranging for them to be charged by a mobile charging truck. Our scheme achieves the dual objectives of minimized user cost and maximized charge cycles, as evidenced by experimental results.

title: Customized Power Grid Solutions for Mitigating IR Drop and Noise in Advanced Process Nodes
authors: Praveenkumar Jayakumar, Sudhagar G
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Recent Trends in Electrical, Electronics and Computing Technologies (ICRTEECT)
abstract: As semiconductor technology advances into lower nodes such as 7nm and below, power integrity and power consumption have become critical concerns in digital VLSI design. One of the key contributors to power inefficiencies and reliability issues in these nodes is the degradation of the Power Grid due to increased current densities and limited routing resources. This paper presents the importance of Power Grid optimization as a fundamental step toward achieving power-efficient designs in lower technology nodes. It highlights the significant role of Power Grid enhancement during the Floor planning stage, where early architectural decisions can influence the performance and integrity of the entire power delivery network (PDN). By incorporating intelligent power strap planning, early IR-drop and electromigration (EM) analysis, and layout-aware power grid synthesis, it is possible to reduce dynamic and static power losses while improving voltage stability across the chip. The proposed methodology demonstrates measurable improvements in power delivery efficiency and design reliability, making it a crucial consideration in next-generation system-on-chip (SoC) implementations. This can be achieved by understanding the routing resources available, complexity of the design and its requirements. Power Grid improvement, shielding of the clock nets to mitigate noise and signoff checks are explored in this work.

title: Simultaneous Detection of Anomalous Nodes and Edges in Graphs
authors: Abdullah Karaaslanli, Selin Aviyente, Panagiotis A. Traganitis
publish: 2025 59th Asilomar Conference on Signals, Systems, and Computers
abstract: Graph anomaly detection is an important research problem that aims to identify structures in a graph that deviate significantly from the rest of the graph. Existing work proposed various approaches to detect anomalous nodes, edges or sub-graphs using tools from machine learning and statistical signal processing. Nevertheless, the majority of existing methods are limited to detecting only a single type of anomalous object, that is, they consider detection of node, edge, or subgraph anomalies as separate tasks. Real world graphs can simultaneously include multiple types of anomalous objects, motivating the development of new algorithms that can perform joint detection. This work proposes an algorithm that aims to simultaneously identify node and edge anomalies. In particular, we consider graphs whose topology includes anomalous edges and whose nodal attributes are contaminated with anomalous labels. The proposed approach uses an autoencoder to find node embeddings that are informative for identification of both types of anomalies. A sequential ensemble learning process is also put forth to train the autoencoder to reduce the effect of anomalies on training. Preliminary results showcase the potential of this novel approach.

title: Co-Design of Electric Drives and Mechanical Transmission for Enhanced Efficiency in Electric Vehicles
authors: Kbvsr Subrahmanyam, B.Satish Kumar, O. Rajeshwari, B.Nagi Reddy
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Recent Trends in Electrical, Electronics and Computing Technologies (ICRTEECT)
abstract: The powertrain of an electric vehicle (EV) emphasizes the urgency of the electric vehicles to improve on efficiency, long range, and overall performance. Design of electric drives and mechanical transmissions has traditionally been carried out in segregated ways and often lead to less-than-optimal use of energy and higher operational losses. In this paper, the authors have met this challenge by coming up with a co-design framework that integrates the design of both electric drives and mechanical transmission system in EVs simultaneously. Based on the simulations using elaborate simulations created in MATLAB/ Simulink, the paper simulates the interaction of the motor properties, gear ratios, and the vehicle dynamics on typical road driving cycles. An optimized combination of the desirable torque-speed properties, energy consumption, and thermal limitations is gained by means of multi-objective optimization strategy. Findings indicate that the co-design approach results in the system efficiency being significantly improved and overall energy losses can be as low as 15 percent in comparison to the general practical sequential design methodologies. In addition, the optimized torque-speed characteristics and gear ratios will lead to an improvement in drive performance even though the downsizing of battery packs can be potentially performed without range loss. The results demonstrate the great value of combining electric drive and transmission design flows with great insights that can be used by manufacturers and engineers who want to create the next-generation of EV powertrain. The suggested strategy preconditions more environmentally friendly, affordable, and efficient electric vehicles.

title: Optimizing Type 2 Diabetes Management Using Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning
authors: Buthaina Gady, Long Jun, Chen Yuedan
publish: 2025 International Conference on Big Data and Data Mining (BDDM)
abstract: Managing type 2 diabetes requires a lifelong, ongoing approach involving interventions in various areas such as medication, nutrition, and physical activity. Effectively monitoring and coordinating these factors presents a complex challenge. While reinforcement learning has made significant strides in type 2 diabetes management, its application has generally been limited to one aspect of management. In this study, we proposed multi-agent reinforcement learning (MARL) framework using the collaborative QMIX algorithm to address all three areas (medication, nutrition, and exercise decisions) simultaneously, providing personalized recommendations for each within a healthcare setting with patient level reward functions, to achieve optimal glucose control. To test this approach, we used two algorithms, QMIX and MADDPG, and conducted a comparative analysis. The results indicated that the QMIX algorithm outperformed the MADDPG algorithm. The QMIX algorithm's ablation study provided a more in depth evaluation of the impact of its individual components. The results underscore QMIX's potential in managing type 2 diabetes and providing comprehensive, multidimensional recommendations.

title: An Edge-Cloud Collaborative System for AI-Enabled Project Portfolio Management with Cyber Threat Detection
authors: Sai Krishna Thota, Priyanka R. Raval, Kiran Onapakala, Jaswant Singh Taur, Amit Jain, S. Balamuralitharan
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Smart Innovations in Systems, Infrastructure, Mechanical, Power, AI and Computing Technologies (SISIMPACT)
abstract: With the new terrain of digital project governance, adding intelligent decision-making from proactive cybersecurity has become a necessity. The presented study introduces a derivation of a new Edge-Cloud Collaborative System as EC-P2M, which can be used to improve Project Portfolio Management (PPM) by using analytics and real-time threat identification with AI. The system is underpinned with a local monitoring of the project-execution metrics based on edge computing and global optimization and coordination based on cloud infrastructure. Light-weight neural models implemented on the edge predict the presence of an anomalous performance in order to trigger the subsequent orchestration actions. The orchestrator, located on the cloud, uses Multi-Criteria Reinforcement Learning to dynamically re-prioritize the projects based on the resource and risk constraints. At the same time, an Adaptive Threat Shield on the base of Graph Neural Network (GNN) maintains consistent monitoring of cyberspace within the infrastructure and implements Zero Trust measures by identifying anomalous behaviour. Roles of federated learning provide the privacy of data, and blockchain-supported logs offer indelible audit records. Such an architecture enables data integrity, scalability and resilience in distributed environments. As shown by experimental simulations, there is a better sense of project orientation with regards to strategic objectives, a short response to appearing threats, and better resources usage. The EC-P2M system takes the bar of smart, secure, and flexible project portfolio management to a different level, and it helps overcome the twofold challenge of business efficiency and cyber resilience of AI-enabled enterprise systems. The suggested Edge-Cloud Collaborative approach attained an overall accuracy of 94.1%, surpassing Heuristic Rule-Based Scheduling <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{ (8 2. 3 \%) }$</tex>, Static Cloud-Centric Orchestration (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{8 5. 6 \%}$</tex>), Deep QLearning Scheduler (89.7%), Federated Learning with Reinforcement Learning <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(91.2 \%)$</tex>, and Edge-Only Secure Task Engine (88.5%).

title: Smart Connected Healthcare System by the Role of IoT and Wearable Sensors in Patient Monitoring
authors: Nagarjuna Tanikonda, Shahbaz Khan, B Venkataramanaiah, Suman Mishra, B.Thiyaneswaran, K.Kasturi
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Recent Trends in Electrical, Electronics and Computing Technologies (ICRTEECT)
abstract: Smart, linked healthcare systems are made possible by the fast developments in the Internet of Things (IoT), which have greatly changed healthcare. Wearable sensors combined with IoT technology let continuous real-time tracking of important physiological indicators including heart rate, blood pressure, oxygen saturation, glucose levels, and body temperature. By means of early disease identification, remote monitoring, and prompt medical interventions, these systems improve patient care and lower hospital visits, hence increasing healthcare efficiency. By sending real-time health data to cloud-based systems, IoT-driven healthcare solutions improve data-driven decision-making and let healthcare personnel evaluate patient status from a distance. Widespread adoption will depend on issues including data privacy, cyber security threats, interoperability, and energy efficiency even with all the advantages. This article investigates how IoT and wearable sensors fit into contemporary healthcare, hence affecting real-time patient monitoring, healthcare access, and general patient outcomes.

title: Efficient Methods for Modeling Shift-Varying Operators
authors: Nebiyou Yismaw, Chinmay Hegde, M. Salman Asif
publish: 2025 59th Asilomar Conference on Signals, Systems, and Computers
abstract: Many imaging systems require accurate characterization of their forward operators for reliable reconstruction. While shift-invariant systems admit efficient convolutional representations, many practical imaging systems are shift-varying and cannot be captured by classical convolution models. We propose the Shift-Varying Neural Operator, an efficient and expressive architecture for learning spatially varying linear operators directly from measurements. Our method builds on existing factorizations of spatially varying convolutions and expresses them in a learnable architecture. Each layer implements a spatially adaptive transformation constructed from a low-rank factorization of modulated convolutional bases. Our experiments show that our proposed method accurately recovers spatially varying point spread functions (PSFs) and learns interpretable operators. Furthermore, we show that the learned forward operator can be integrated into existing iterative inverse problem solvers.

title: Evaluating AutoML Frameworks in the Context of Autism Diagnosis: A Comparative Analysis
authors: Karpagam C, Mahadevi M, Manivasagam G
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Smart Innovations in Systems, Infrastructure, Mechanical, Power, AI and Computing Technologies (SISIMPACT)
abstract: Automated Machine Learning (AutoML) is a transformative paradigm that aims to leverage access to machine learning by automating the model development process. Traditional machine learning workflow requires domain expertise and substantial manual effort in tasks such as data preprocessing, feature selection, model selection, hyperparameter tuning and validation. AutoML frameworks simplify these processes in various sectors, especially in the field where limited expert knowledge or computational resources is required. AutoML enables more efficient and effective model generation than the existing machine learning approach. In the context of health informatics and particularly in autism spectrum disorder (ASD) detection, AutoML offers a promising solution for accelerating the development of diagnostic models. By automating the optimization of model performance, AutoML discovers robust predictive patterns in complex and heterogeneous data types. This paper evaluates a comparative analysis of AutoML approaches on the autism questionnaire dataset. The analysis and results generated provide valuable insights into their applicability, efficiency and generalizability in supporting early and accurate autism diagnosis. The AutoML approaches evaluated are AutoGluon, H2O, PyCaret and FLAML.

title: Drivers of E-Recruitment Adoption in Indonesia's Automated Businesses
authors: Rini Kurnia Sari, Aisyah Qanita, Marita Salandanan, Baghas Budi Wicaksono
publish: 2025 International Conference on Innovation and Intelligence for Informatics, Computing, and Technologies (3ICT)
abstract: Digital transformation in various fields has driven the implementation of information technology, including in the employee recruitment process. E-recruitment, as part of the human resource information system (HRIS), is increasingly used due to its efficiency in reaching candidates widely. In Indonesia, around 55% of companies have adopted HRIS to manage the recruitment process by 2023. However, although the implementation of e-recruitment continues to increase, challenges remain, especially in building user trust and reducing stress when using digital platforms. This study aims to analyze the factors that influence behavioral intentions in adopting erecruitment in automation-based businesses in Indonesia. Using the Technology Acceptance Model (TAM) approach, this study explores the role of Perceived Effectiveness (PU), Perceived Ease of Use (PEU), Vividness (VIV), and Perceived Internet Stress (PIS) on user intentions in using e-recruitment. Data were collected from 204 respondents and analyzed using the Structural Equation Modeling-Partial Least Squares (SEMPLS) method. The results showed that PEU had the greatest influence on user intention, followed by PU, indicating that the easier and more useful a system is, the higher the likelihood of users adopting it. Vividness and PIS factors also have an influence, although their impacts are smaller. The implications of this study highlight the importance of a user-friendly interface, attractive system design, and technical support features to reduce user stress in encouraging the adoption of erecruitment in Indonesia.

title: Real-Time Adaptive Camera-Attack Detection and Correction for Autonomous Vehicle Platoons in Cyber-Physical Systems via a MADRL Approach
authors: Mohamed El Jbari, Atika Revinq, Mohamed Moussaoui, Felipe A. P. de Figueiredo, Omar Bouazizi, Chaimae Azroumahli
publish: 2025 International Conference on Innovation and Intelligence for Informatics, Computing, and Technologies (3ICT)
abstract: In autonomous vehicle platooning, members of the platoon not only use their own sensor data for making driving decisions. They also rely on data shared by other members of the platoon. This research proposes a novel framework for real-time detection and correction of camera-based attacks on platoonbased autonomous vehicles within Cyber-Physical Systems (CPS) using a Multi-Agent Deep Reinforcement Learning (MADRL) approach. Camera systems, critical for perception in autonomous vehicles, are vulnerable to adversarial attacks such as image manipulation, occlusion, or sensor jamming, which can disrupt platoon coordination and safety. The proposed system leverages MADRL to enable adaptive, decentralized decision-making among vehicles, ensuring robust attack detection and mitigation while maintaining platoon stability. By integrating real-time camera sensor data fusion, anomaly detection, and corrective action policies, the framework enhances resilience against cyber-attacks. Experimental results demonstrate improved detection accuracy, reduced response time, and enhanced platoon performance under various attack scenarios compared to traditional methods.

title: A High-Performance and Resource-Efficient FPGA-Based Multi-Object Tracking System Using Event Cameras
authors: Wei Xiong, Jianfan Zhang, Xingzhe Zhu, Jiacheng Cao, Jian Wang, Jinmei Lai
publish: 2025 35th International Conference on Field-Programmable Logic and Applications (FPL)
abstract: As emerging visual sensors, event cameras are gaining attention for their excellent temporal resolution, low power consumption and extremely high dynamic range. Some studies have explored Field Programmable Gate Array (FPGA)-based systems for edge event-driven Multi-Object Tracking (MOT) tasks, which aim to achieve high real-time performance and tracking accuracy with strict resource and power constraints. Among these FPGA-based systems, the per-event processing systems can effectively utilize event cameras' high temporal resolution and asynchronous nature. To address the performance and resource overhead issues in per-event processing systems, this paper presents the Boundary-Movement-based Multi-Object Tracking (BMMOT) event-driven system for high-performance and resource-efficient MOT tasks while keeping reasonable power consumption and accuracy. BMMOT employs a straightforward boundary-movement-based tracking mechanism for tracker design. Additionally, a tracker lifecycle management strategy and a tracker merging strategy, based on concise motion trend criteria, are adopted to ensure the system functions properly. Effective BMMOT circuit designs are also proposed. The efficiency of BMMOT is validated by multiple academic and real-world datasets captured by different cameras. BMMOT implemented on an AMD ZYNQ FPGA has an event processing latency of 19.6-<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$137.2 ~\text{ns} /$</tex> event and can process up to 51 million events per second with a power consumption of less than 2.71 W. BMMOT keeps good accuracy <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(52.1 \%-79.4 \%)$</tex> for the datasets. Under a similar tracking system configuration, higher performance is obtained while almost all block RAMs, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$16.4-41.0 \%$</tex> look-up tables, and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$64.5-78.7 \%$</tex> flip-flops can be saved compared to existing work.

title: AI-Driven Resume Analysis: Ensemble-Based Skill Extraction and Semantic Job-Candidate Matching
authors: Nandan P Vernekar, Sanath P Suvarna, Kawshik A, S Pranava
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Emerging Trends in Computing and Communication (ETCOM)
abstract: Traditional Applicant Tracking Systems rely on rigid keyword matching that fails to understand semantic context, resulting in qualified candidates being overlooked. Large Language Model-based solutions offer better semantic understanding but suffer from high computational costs, and inconsistent outputs due to lack of domain-specific training for technical skill extraction. We propose an AI-Powered Platform using a custom-trained Named Entity Recognition model with an ensemble extraction pipeline that combines entity recognition, fuzzy matching, TF-IDF analysis, and semantic embeddings. Our locally-deployed system achieves 99.72 % accuracy and 81.48% precision in skill extraction across <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{4, 0 0 0}$</tex> resumes with 1.084 seconds average processing time, ensuring both data privacy and cost efficiency. The platform generates intelligent job-candidate compatibility scores, provides personalized Career Path recommendations, and includes an AI-powered Mock Interview System with real-time feedback. This solution eliminates manual screening overhead while overcoming the limitations of existing ATS and LLM-based recruitment approaches.

title: Decoding Risky Decision-Making from Nonlinear Neural Latent Dynamics
authors: Angela Zhou, Logan Peters, Karen A. Moxon
publish: 2025 59th Asilomar Conference on Signals, Systems, and Computers
abstract: Decision-making is a complex cognitive process often disrupted in neuropsychiatric disorders, making it essential to understand the underlying neural dynamics of deliberation. To investigate, we examined high frequency activity (70–200 Hz) recorded from intracranial EEG (iEEG) across multiple brain regions as human subjects performed a dual-option neuroeconomic decision-making task involving risk-taking. In this task, subjects had to choose between a safe bet for a fixed reward or to gamble for a potentially higher reward or no reward. We applied DFINE, a nonlinear dynamical systems model, to learn low-dimensional latent representations of neural activity. The model was first trained in an unsupervised manner, optimizing neural reconstruction and k-step-ahead prediction objectives to produce a set of latent variables. After training, the latent variables were used as inputs to both linear and nonlinear classifiers to classify trials as either safe or gamble trials. Next, we modified the DFINE model to include training of the classifier during the DFINE model training in a supervised manner. We compared 5 different modes of behavioral supervision: an artificial sigmoid trajectory, a simple mean-pool perceptron classifier, a flattened latent feature perceptron, a Gated Recurrent Unit (GRU) and a Temporal Convolutional Network (TCN).

title: Evaluating the Reliability of Natural Ground Control Points and Check Points Compared with Artificial Targets for UAV Photogrammetry in Urban Areas
authors: Kittipong Prapakan, Chayanon Penyalai, Jurawan Nontapon, Siwa Kaewplang
publish: 2025 IEEE Asia-Pacific Conference on Geoscience, Electronics and Remote Sensing Technology (AGERS)
abstract: Improving UAV photogrammetric accuracy in urban areas is essential for civil engineering, planning, and public-safety applications, yet deploying artificial ground control points can be difficult in constrained environments. This study evaluates whether natural ground control points and natural check points can reliably replace artificial targets across eight UAV flight configurations combining RTK and non-RTK positioning. Independent checkpoints and RMSE-based metrics at the 95% confidence level were used for accuracy assessment. Results show that while RTK improves horizontal accuracy, non-RTK flights with well-distributed control points achieve comparable sub-five-centimeters performance. Uncontrolled flights produced $\sim \mathbf{2} \mathbf{m}$ vertical error, whereas configurations using artificial or natural control achieved mapping-grade vertical accuracy of $\mathbf{0. 0 4 - 0. 0 9 ~ m}$. Carefully selected natural features matched or exceeded the accuracy of artificial targets while reducing field effort. These findings demonstrate that NGCP/NCP workflows offer a practical and reliable alternative for high-precision UAV mapping in urban environments.

title: Real-Time Fall Detection and Alerting System Using NodeMCU and MPU6050 with Threshold-Based Analysis
authors: Santosh Kumar Chaudhary, Pradeep Kumar, Kothawala Yogishwar Sai, Pendam Tanvi, V. Sai Bharath
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Recent Trends in Electrical, Electronics and Computing Technologies (ICRTEECT)
abstract: Falls are a major cause of serious injuries (to the head and/or neck) in older adults. This paper describes a real-time fall detection and protection system that can reduce or eliminate severe head and/or neck injuries through the deployment of a neck-mounted airbag, which will deploy when a fall is detected. The system is built on an Arduino platform, using an MPU6050 inertial sensor as the basis of its fall detection algorithm, which includes a lightweight three-stage threshold-based approach to monitor motion and detect fall events. If a fall is detected, the solenoid valve activates to release compressed CO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf> gas, filling the airbag within seconds after the event. The key to this design is converting passive fall detection to active protection against serious injury, with an emphasis on protecting the vulnerable upper body areas that have not received sufficient attention from existing products. In contrast to existing systems using machine learning or cameras to detect falls, our design is inexpensive, does not invade the owner's privacy, and is computationally efficient; therefore, it has great potential for use in the home and within elder care facilities. Experimental results demonstrate a reliable detection rate, an average response time of 2.1 seconds, and minimal false alarms in diverse test scenarios. This work contributes a practical and innovative solution that combines accurate sensing with immediate protective action to enhance elderly safety.

title: A Unified Testing Framework for ML-Based Fraud Detection in Healthcare Claims
authors: Gayathri Surianarayanan, Srikanth Singireddy, Abinaya Mettupatti Sivagnanam
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Emerging Trends in Computing and Communication (ETCOM)
abstract: The role of Machine Learning (ML) models has grown substantially in the detection of fraud and abuse in healthcare claims data. However, the lack of a unified testing and validation framework complicates the problems of reliability, transparency, fairness, and regulatory compliance. This paper develops a Unified Testing Framework (UTF) designed specifically for validating ML-based fraud detection systems in healthcare claims processing. The framework includes modules for model performance evaluation, integrity of data validation, adversarial robustness, explainability, and compliance verification. Using a hybrid approach consisting of statistical testing, adversarial simulation, and explainable AI (XAI) metrics, we develop a rigorous methodology by which to assess whether a model is sufficiently mature to be deployed in real-world payer environments. Experimental results from simulated healthcare claims datasets show that the UTF improves accuracy by 8-12% while reducing bias and false positives compared to conventional ad hoc validation methods. The proposed framework is well aligned with industry standards such as HIPAA, GDPR, and the CMS program integrity guidelines for ethical deployment of AI.

title: Transformer and Recurrent Neural Network-based Spatio-temporal Modeling of Mangrove Vegetation Health Based NDVI and MVI Features
authors: Jonathan Marcelino, HosheaSophia Tañedo, Ronnie Concepcion, Edwin Sybingco, Giolo Rei Mababangloob
publish: 2025 IEEE Asia-Pacific Conference on Geoscience, Electronics and Remote Sensing Technology (AGERS)
abstract: This study explores transformer-based modeling for spatiotemporal analysis of mangrove vegetation health in the Mandaue Mangrove Eco-Park, Cebu City. Sentinel-2 multispectral images from 2017 to 2025 were used to derive the Normalized Difference Vegetation Index (NDVI) and Mangrove Vegetation Index (MVI) as indicators of canopy vigor and ecosystem condition. Four deep learning models were implemented and compared: Temporal Fusion Transformer (TFT), TabTransformer, Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM), and Gated Recurrent Unit (GRU). Traditional spatiotemporal models such as LSTM and GRU often face challenges in capturing long-range temporal dependencies, managing multivariate inputs, and providing interpretability, which limit their accuracy in complex environmental datasets. Transformer-based architectures, particularly the TFT, overcome these limitations through attention and gating mechanisms that enhance feature selection, interpret variable importance, and support multi-horizon forecasting. The models were trained using data from 2017 to 2023 and evaluated on data from 2024 to 2025 for both forecasting and vegetation-health classification. Model performance was assessed using MAE, RMSE, $\mathbf{R}^{\mathbf{2}}$, Accuracy, Precision, Recall, F1-Score, and Cohen’s Kappa. Results showed that the TFT provided the highest NDVI forecasting accuracy, while the GRU achieved the best MVI prediction and classification performance. Both models effectively identified Low, Normal, and High vegetation-health categories, demonstrating their ability to capture temporal variations in mangrove canopy dynamics. The findings highlight the potential of transformer and recurrent architectures as reliable tools for mangrove monitoring and recommend exploring additional vegetation indices and transformer variants in future research.

title: AI Driven Speech-Based Asthma Detection using Feature Optimization for Early Diagnosis
authors: Md.Asim Iqbal, Dr.K.Devarajan, Syed Musthak Ahmed, M. Sadanandam
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Recent Trends in Electrical, Electronics and Computing Technologies (ICRTEECT)
abstract: Asthma is a common respiratory disorder affecting individuals across various age groups, often going undetected until symptoms become severe. Traditional diagnostic methods are time-consuming and require clinical intervention, which limits the possibility of real-time, early diagnosis. To address this issue, speech-based analysis has gained attention as a non-invasive and efficient diagnostic tool, leveraging the impact of asthma on voice characteristics. This work presents a real-time asthma detection and classification model using speech signals, integrating an AI-driven model. The proposed model uses an improved social spider optimization combined with genetic algorithm (ISSO-G) for optimal feature extraction, feature optimization, followed by a convolutional neural network (CNN) for accurate classification of asthmatic and non-asthmatic conditions. The model is trained and validated using the COSWARA dataset, which contains respiratory-induced voice samples across different health categories. The ESSO-G algorithm ensures effective selection of discriminative speech features, while CNN enables robust classification performance. Experimental results show that the proposed ESSO-G-CNN model delivers superior detection accuracy, sensitivity, and specificity compared to existing state-of-the-art techniques, demonstrating its potential for early-stage asthma diagnosis through speech in real-time environments.

title: Design and Development of Cost-Effective Unmanned Aerial Vehicle for Crop Field Monitoring
authors: R.S.W. Madanayaka, C.V. Kumarasiri, Chathurika S. Silva
publish: 2025 IEEE 19th International Conference on Industrial and Information Systems (ICIIS)
abstract: This paper presents the development of a low-cost unmanned aerial vehicle (UAV) designed specifically for field-based agricultural monitoring. The UAV platform incorporates a custom-designed flight controller, offering an affordable alternative to commercial autopilot systems. Essential sensors, including an inertial measurement unit, barometric pressure sensor, magnetometer and GPS module are integrated to achieve flight stabilization using a PID control algorithm. The flight controller was engineered using in-house PCB fabrication techniques to maximize cost reduction and modularity. Supporting modules include a remote-control system and 3D-printed components for structural and environmental protection. The system was tested under field conditions for stability, power efficiency and responsiveness. The results demonstrate that the custom-built, cost-effective UAV is a viable tool for agricultural remote sensing. Its utility is validated by successfully processing captured RGB imagery to generate standard Vegetation Indices for crop analysis, enabling more accessible and scalable monitoring approaches.

title: Block-sparsity Through Bayesian Hyper-parameter Learning for Generalized Wideband DOA Estimation
authors: Hitesh D. Khunti, Bhaskar D. Rao
publish: 2025 59th Asilomar Conference on Signals, Systems, and Computers
abstract: This work presents a Bayesian framework for generalized wideband Direction-of-Arrival (DOA) estimation that simultaneously exploits block sparsity in spatial and spectral domains to resolve closely spaced sources with unknown bandwidths and spatial spreads. We uniquely extend the well-known Sparse Bayesian Learning (SBL) algorithm for wideband DOA estimation. A key contribution is the introduction of a flexible regularization framework on the hyperparameter space for enforcing spatial and spectral block sparsity which supports a broad class of penalty functions. This allows the algorithm to dynamically adapt to sources of varying bandwidths and spatial spreads. Furthermore, we utilized MM and ADMM techniques to decompose the large-scale MAP optimization into a collection of simple and highly parallelized scalar optimization problems while efficiently handling both smooth and non-smooth regularizers. Numerical simulations demonstrate significant improvement in resolution and accuracy over established methods.

title: Modeling Willingness to Pay with Cultural Integrity Signals in AI Mediated Markets
authors: Lall Apoorva, Soma Mukhareejee, Jyoti, Gule Ganesh, Dabi Das Mukherjee
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Recent Trends in Electrical, Electronics and Computing Technologies (ICRTEECT)
abstract: This study investigates how Artificial Intelligence (AI) is transforming digital communication and consumer behavior, with a focus on marketing, advertising, and visual communication. By integrating personalization, predictive analytics, dynamic content generation, chatbots, and immersive technologies such as AR/VR and the metaverse, AI reshapes how consumers interact with brands. Empirical results show measurable improvements: click-through rates rose by 41%, conversions by 31%, average order value by 12%, and repeat purchases by 5 percentage points, while return rates dropped by 1.8 points. Similarly, recommender systems across Netflix, Amazon, and Booking demonstrated uplift in engagement and transactions ranging from 14.9% to 42.9%. Survey evidence from 300 Indian participants revealed that 50% were highly familiar with AI, 70% supported AI-generated art, and 65% endorsed its creative benefits. Collectively, these findings highlight AI’s capacity to deliver efficiency, personalization, and emotional resonance, while also raising ethical challenges of privacy, bias, and fairness in global digital ecosystems.

title: Hybrid Machine Learning Approaches for Agronomic Price Prediction Using Multi-Source Data
authors: Shanmugam Sundaramurthy, Aditya Raj Singh, Shail Shah, C Jothi Kumar
publish: 2025 International Conference on Innovation and Intelligence for Informatics, Computing, and Technologies (3ICT)
abstract: In the agricultural sector, farmers can greatly benefit from accurate price forecasting, which enables wellinformed decision making. However, forecasting agronomic commodity prices presents significant challenges, as prices depend on multiple factors including historical trends, weather patterns, economic indicators, and satellite imagery. This paper proposes a hybrid machine learning framework that uses ensemble learning to integrate specialized models, each trained on a specific data modality. Ridge regression models economic trends, LightGBM captures weather-related impacts, and LSTM networks identify temporal patterns in historical prices. A CatBoost meta-learner combines these predictions through decision-level fusion. Experiments on multi-source Indian agricultural datasets demonstrate that this hybrid approach outperforms traditional single-model methods, achieving superior performance across RMSE, MAE, and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{R}^{2}$</tex> metrics. The results confirm that the multi-model ensemble is more effective and robust, successfully accounting for the multidimensional factors influencing agricultural commodity prices.

title: Ride-Hailing Trip Distribution and Its Relationship with Road Network Density and Public Transport Accessibility in Jakarta using Gravity Model
authors: Roland Febrian, Shierly Anastastya Lie, Miranda Setiawati, Edy Irwansyah
publish: 2025 IEEE Asia-Pacific Conference on Geoscience, Electronics and Remote Sensing Technology (AGERS)
abstract: This research investigates how road network concentration, accessibility to public transportation, and the distribution of ride-hailing trips are spatially interconnected within Jakarta’s urban villages. While gravity models are widely used in transportation studies, this study introduces a novel integration of road network density, ride-hailing trip distribution, and public transport accessibility within a single analytical framework. The enhanced gravity model incorporates factors such as population size, road density, and multimodal accessibility to better capture spatial mobility dynamics in a developing megacity context. The results indicate that neither population size nor road availability alone explains ride-hailing mobility patterns. Correlation analysis reveals moderately positive relationships between road concentration and trip interactions, while unique patterns emerge in densely populated but low-mobility areas, as well as in transit-oriented zones showing high interaction despite limited road infrastructure. These findings highlight the significance of integrating infrastructure, accessibility, and land use considerations to support balanced and sustainable urban mobility planning in Jakarta.

title: Intrusion Response System Using Reinforcement Learning TD3 Algorithm
authors: Dharani Kanta Roy, Hemanta Kumar Kalita
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Emerging Trends in Computing and Communication (ETCOM)
abstract: The increasing complexity of cyberattacks requires intelligent intrusion response systems that provide adaptive defense. This paper introduces a novel Intrusion Response System (IRS) that utilizes the Twin Delayed Deep Deterministic Policy Gradient (TD3) algorithm, an advanced reinforcement learning technique suited for continuous action spaces. Unlike traditional intrusion detection methods that focus solely on identifying threats, our system proactively learns optimal defense strategies to minimize damage and further recovery. In modeling the cybersecurity environment as a Markov Decision Process (MDP), a hierarchical state representation is employed that captures multilevel network contexts, enabling a detailed understanding of emerging threats. To improve decision-making, integrate a Feature-wise Additive Attention mechanism that highlights the most critical features in the network state, enabling the TD3 agent to focus on relevant information when selecting responses. The response is optimized by training the TD3 agent's attention and a multi-objective reward function, enabling the system to dynamically learn adaptive strategies that minimize damage, reduce false alarms, and accelerate recovery. The system is implemented in MATLAB using cybersecurity dataset UNSW-NB15, which includes a range of single-stage and multi-stage attack scenarios. TD3 reinforcement learning agent, attention mechanisms, and a multi-objective reward function to minimize damage, speed up recovery, and maintain system stability, thereby responding intelligently to confirmed threats.

title: Multi-Modal Sensor Fusion for Proactive Blockage Prediction in mmWave Vehicular Networks
authors: Ahmad M. Nazar, Abdulkadir Celik, Mohamed Y. Selim, Asmaa Abdallah, Daji Qiao, Ahmed M. Eltawil
publish: 2025 59th Asilomar Conference on Signals, Systems, and Computers
abstract: Vehicular communication systems operating in the millimeter wave (mmWave) band are highly susceptible to signal blockage from dynamic obstacles such as vehicles, pedestrians, and infrastructure. To address this challenge, we propose a proactive blockage prediction framework that utilizes multimodal sensing, including camera, GPS, LiDAR, and radar inputs in an infrastructure-to-vehicle (I2V) setting. This approach uses modality-specific deep learning models to process each sensor stream independently and fuses their outputs using a softmax-weighted ensemble strategy based on validation performance. Our evaluations, for up to 1.5s in advance, show that the camera-only model achieves the best standalone trade-off with an F1-score of 97.1% and an inference time of 89.8ms. A camera+radar configuration further improves accuracy to 97.2% F1 at 95.7ms. Our results display the effectiveness and efficiency of multi-modal sensing for mmWave blockage prediction and provide a pathway for proactive wireless communication in dynamic environments.

title: Multivariate EMD for EEG-Based Characterization of Neurodegenerative Diseases
authors: Ethan Zhu, Marjan Nassajpour, Behnaz Ghoraani
publish: 2025 59th Asilomar Conference on Signals, Systems, and Computers
abstract: Accurate and early detection of neurodegenerative diseases using electroencephalography (EEG) remains a challenging yet vital task in clinical neuroscience. This paper presents a data-driven framework for EEG signal decomposition and feature extraction using Multivariate Empirical Mode Decomposition (MEMD), aiming to enhance classification and spatial analysis of EEG signals in patients with neurodegenerative disorders. EEG recordings from healthy controls (HC), Alzheimer’s disease (AD), and frontotemporal dementia (FTD) subjects were processed through both MEMD and conventional Empirical Mode Decomposition (EMD). A comprehensive set of temporal and spectral features was extracted from intrinsic mode functions (IMFs), and statistically significant features were used to train support vector machines (SVM), Random Forest, and XGBoost classifiers. Our results demonstrate that MEMD yields superior classification accuracy when compared to EMD, achieving the best results with XGBoost for AD vs. HC (0.83 vs. 0.71) and AD vs. FTD (0.76 vs. 0.70), and with SVM for FTD vs. HC (0.77 vs. 0.70). These findings highlight the potential of MEMD as a robust EEG processing tool for characterizing neural changes associated with neurodegenerative diseases.

title: Directional Modulation: Advancing Electromagnetic Signal Processing for Secure Communications
authors: G. Bacci, G. Galgani, R. Guidi, M. Luise, V. Pellegrini, L. Sanguinetti
publish: 2025 59th Asilomar Conference on Signals, Systems, and Computers
abstract: Improving security features of wireless communications for both civil and military applications has been a hot topic since the early days of radio science. One effective method is through physical-layer (PHY) encryption, that can be achieved through the application of advanced electromagnetic signal processing techniques at both transmitting and receiving link-ends by using antenna arrays, possibly in a disjoint manner. Among others, directional modulation (DM) can be regarded as a promising technique, which uses antenna arrays as spatial encryption systems, to provide information confidentiality in the transmit direction, already at the PHY. Still, as securing communications extends beyond countering eavesdropping by also comprising integrity, availability and reliability guarantees for the transmitted information, it turns out that PHY approaches can help at the receive link-end with mitigation of such attacks as jamming, meaconing, and spoofing. This can be achieved through antenna arrays with dynamic phase shifting, and can enhance communication, radar, and navigation applications. While there exists a consistent literature for the transmit-side, to the best of our knowledge similar solutions at the receiver side are not available. In this paper we describe a DM-based approach to design an add-on antenna array that partitions the surrounding space into intelligible and interdicted zones. Within the latter, any harmful signal originating from potential attackers is rejected by taking advantage of traditional beamforming techniques, such as null-steering, while also building upon them via the application of the space-dependent distortion processes associated with DM.

title: Natural Language Processing for Healthcare Text Mining and Clinical Decision Support
authors: Abhinav Mishra, Sakshi Sobti, M. Gowri, P.S. Joan Kingsly, T Pushpalatha, N. Kalidas
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Smart Innovations in Systems, Infrastructure, Mechanical, Power, AI and Computing Technologies (SISIMPACT)
abstract: Natural Language Processing (NLP) has developed a powerful technique of obtaining valuable knowledge out of the enormous volume of unstructured textual information produced within healthcare systems in clinical notes, discharged summaries, medical literature, etc. This paper discusses some of the advanced NLP text mining methods in healthcare with a view to supporting accurate and expedient clinical decision making. The suggested framework combines the models of domain-specific languages, semantics and named entity recognition to detect key medical concepts, discover patterns, and obtain contextual relationships. Using the ontologies and medical knowledge bases enables the system to maximize information search and facilitate evidence-based medical decision-making. The strategy will ensure the minimization of diagnosis mistakes, enhance treatment planning, and help healthcare specialists control the patient care process in a more efficient manner. Experimental evaluation of the NLP-based text mining value, promise, in optimizing workflows, individualization of care, and proactive interventions is evidence of how intelligent, data-driven, scalable, and saleable healthcare solutions can be achieved.

title: Analytic Comparison of Super-Resolution Artificial Intelligence Models: UAE Case Study for Upscaling Digital Elevation Models
authors: Ahmed Ammar Kuba, Mohammed Ahmed, Qassim Nasir, Mohammed Kamil, Manar Abu Talib
publish: 2025 IEEE Asia-Pacific Conference on Geoscience, Electronics and Remote Sensing Technology (AGERS)
abstract: Access to high-resolution terrain data, which is important for a wide range of geospatial applications, remains limited in the gulf region. Estimating high-resolution Digital Elevation Models (DEMs) from low-resolution maps can be a viable approach to help address this problem. This research study investigates the use of deep learning-based super-resolution techniques to enhance low-resolution DEMs in the context of the United Arab Emirates (UAE). We evaluate four artificial intelligence upscaling models for recovering high-resolution elevation details from UAE DEMs. The models are trained on a dataset consisting of 97 high- and low-resolution DEM pairs, with the high-resolution maps having a resolution of 1 meter, and 10 meters for the low-resolution maps. The models are evaluated using metrics like PSNR, RMSE, and MAE. VDSR4DEM achieved the highest accuracy with a PSNR of 69.0 dB, RMSE of 1.482 meters, and MAE of 0.864 meters, outperforming D-SRGAN (PSNR 41.9, RMSE 1.752, MAE 1.257), SRCNN (PSNR 54.14, RMSE 6.78, MAE 5.73), and Real-ESRGAN (PSNR 30.0, RMSE 7.03, MAE 5.92). VDSR can upscale DEMs without image conversion, minimizing artifacts and preserving terrain integrity. These findings demonstrate the potential of AI super-resolution to generate high-quality elevation data for geospatial analysis in arid and urban regions. Potential future directions include training more advanced super-resolution models on UAE DEMs to improve accuracy, using 3D visualization tools for better analysis and planning, and integrating upscaled UAE DEMs into flood modeling and hydrological simulations.

title: Geospatial Modeling of House Price Using MGWRSAR: A Case Study in DKI Jakarta
authors: Yenro P Sagala, Sutikno, Achmad Choiruddin
publish: 2025 IEEE Asia-Pacific Conference on Geoscience, Electronics and Remote Sensing Technology (AGERS)
abstract: The dynamics of house prices in Jakarta are influenced by both spatial dependence and spatial heterogeneity. Conventional models, including the Spatial Autoregressive (SAR) and Geographically Weighted Regression (GWR), are only capable of partial capture of these effects, which often result in model misspecification. The present study employs the Mixed Geographically Weighted Regression-Spatial Autoregressive (MGWR-SAR) model to concurrently address the spatial dependence and heterogeneity in house price determinants. We used a dataset of houses listed in 2024, incorporating house and location characteristics. The construction of spatial dependence weight matrix was based on semi-variogram analysis, and optimal kernel bandwidths were selected using cross-validation method. The model is estimated using The Spatial Two-Stage Least Square (S2SLS) approach to address endogeneity in spatial lag component. The findings of the study indicate that the land and building area are the most significant price determinants, with spatial variation in their influence across Jakarta’s subregions. Jakarta Pusat exhibits stronger spatial spillovers, while Southern district demonstrates heightened sensitivity to building area. The MGWR-SAR model effectively eliminates residual spatial autocorrelation and captures location-specific variation, offering enhanced explanatory power for urban housing market. These findings underscore the importance of incorporating spatial structure in housing price modeling for more targeted policies and valuation strategies.

title: Armed Unmanned Ground Vehicle Using Virtual Reality
authors: Swathi K, Nutana Shetty, Akshatha N M, Shrinidhi Alva
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Recent Trends in Electrical, Electronics and Computing Technologies (ICRTEECT)
abstract: The design and deployment of an Armed Virtual Reality-Based Unmanned Ground Vehicle (AVR-UGV) are examined in this technical study. In a variety of applications, such as military operations, long-distance transportation, hazardous environment investigation, and surveillance, AVR-UGVs provide notable advantages over human operation. This study combines a gyroscope sensor with a VR headset to record head motions, which are subsequently sent to a UGV microcontroller. After processing this data, the microcontroller rotates a camera in three axes using servo motors, giving the VR headset real-time video input. The operator can experience and operate the UGV in a virtual reality setting thanks to this configuration. The UGV also has a robotic arm that can move like a human hand, allowing for remote object manipulation.

title: Deep Learning-Driven Joint Beamforming and Channel Tracking via Sparse Bayesian Filtering for Massive MIMO
authors: Xichun Cheng, Risto Wichman
publish: 2025 59th Asilomar Conference on Signals, Systems, and Computers
abstract: The temporal variation of massive multiple-input multiple-output (MIMO) channels poses significant challenges for accurate channel estimation and effective beamforming design. To address this problem, we propose an end-to-end deep learning scheme for channel tracking with beamforming optimization. The proposed scheme integrates a convolutional neural network (CNN)-based beamformer fed by the sparse channel estimates to align with the dominant directions, together with an adaptive channel estimator using the online variational sparse Bayesian filter (OVSBF) algorithm, which recursively updates the channel information at each time slot by leveraging temporal correlations. Numerical results demonstrate that the proposed approach achieves superior beam alignment and significantly outperforms other beamforming benchmarks.

title: Performance Analysis of RIS-Aided Synchronized Systems
authors: Hadi Hariri, Nazih Salhab
publish: 2025 International Conference on Innovation and Intelligence for Informatics, Computing, and Technologies (3ICT)
abstract: The uncontrollable nature of radio wave propagation poses a significant challenge to wireless network performance. As a promising solution, Reconfigurable Intelligent Surfaces (RIS) can intelligently reconfigure the propagation environment by shaping and directing radio waves. In this paper, we investigate an RIS-assisted uplink communication system in which a user communicates with a base station (BS) via an RIS. We propose a mathematical model for the Signal-to-Noise Ratio (SNR), outage probability, and data throughput under Nakagamim fading, with a specific focus on the critical impact of phase synchronization. Then we formulate our optimization problem with the aim of maximizing the achievable rate. Our results demonstrate that synchronized RIS phase shifts lead to a notable improvement in received SNR and a significant reduction in outage probability. Specifically, an interestingly low outage probability is achievable at a transmit power of 15 dBm with phase synchronization, highlighting the substantial gains over both non-synchronized RIS or conventional communication without using an RIS. Our analysis of these three scenarios—with synchronized RIS, non-synchronized RIS, and without RIS confirms the pivotal role of RIS phase synchronization for optimizing performance.

title: Target Induced Angle Grid Regularized Estimation for Ghost Identification in Automotive Radar
authors: Junho Kweon, Vishal Monga
publish: 2025 59th Asilomar Conference on Signals, Systems, and Computers
abstract: This study presents a novel algorithm for identifying ghost targets in automotive radar by estimating complex valued signal strength across a two-dimensional angle grid defined by direction-of-arrival (DOA) and direction-of-departure (DOD). In real-world driving environments, radar signals often undergo multipath propagation due to reflections from surfaces such as guardrails. These indirect paths can produce ghost targets—false detections that appear at incorrect locations—posing challenges to autonomous navigation. A recent method, the Multi-Path Iterative Adaptive Approach (MP-IAA), addresses this by jointly estimating the DOA/DOD angle grid, identifying mismatches as indicators of ghost targets. However, its effectiveness declines in low signal-to-noise ratio (SNR) settings. To enhance robustness, we introduce a physics-inspired regularizer that captures structural patterns inherent to multipath propagation. This regularizer is incorporated into the estimation cost, forming a new loss function that guides our proposed algorithm, TIGRE (Target-Induced angle-Grid Regularized Estimation). TIGRE iteratively minimizes this regularized loss and we show that our proposed regularizer asymptotically enforces l<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">0</inf> sparsity on the DOA/DOD grid. Numerical experiments demonstrate that the proposed method substantially enhances the quality of angle-grid estimation across various multipath scenarios, particularly in low SNR environments, providing a more reliable basis for subsequent ghost target identification.

title: Robust Training of Low-Parameter Models for Active Speaker Detection from Digital Videos
authors: Sebastian Janampa, Bonnie Morales Ugarte, Constantinos S. Pattichis, Marios S. Pattichis
publish: 2025 59th Asilomar Conference on Signals, Systems, and Computers
abstract: Active speaker detection (ASD) uses visual and audio information from raw videos to determine which speakers are talking in each frame. The majority of models for ASD have been large-parameter models. Recently, Light-ASD and LR-ASD achieved a reduction of 41X in the number of required parameters while also achieving competitive performance. Yet, during training, Light-ASD and LR-ASD can often suffer from vanishing gradients. In this paper, we modify the original architecture to avoid the vanishing gradient problem while reducing the required number of parameters and FLOPs. In out-of-distribution testing, our proposed model surpasses all competitors while using fewer parameters and FLOPs.

title: Optimization of Coalition Update Mechanisms for Multi-Agent Systems
authors: Jie Zhang, Fengyuan Yang, Yingqi Liao
publish: 2025 International Conference on Big Data and Data Mining (BDDM)
abstract: Multi-Agent Systems (MAS) achieve complex task solving through cooperation among individual agents and have been widely applied in swarm intelligence, cooperative control, and distributed decision-making. As an important organizational form of multi-agent cooperation, the coalition structure plays a critical role in determining overall system performance and cooperative efficiency, and its dynamic update mechanism directly affects system effectiveness. However, existing coalition update methods generally rely on static rules or local rewarddriven strategies, making it difficult to balance global cooperative benefits and system stability in complex dynamic environments. To address this issue, this paper proposes an optimization method for coalition update mechanisms in multi-agent systems. The proposed method constructs a coalition utility function and introduces individual contribution evaluation and structural evolution constraints to achieve adaptive optimization of coalition structures while ensuring system stability. Furthermore, by combining game-theoretic principles with heuristic search strategies, an efficient coalition update strategy is designed to reduce computational complexity and improve convergence performance. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed method outperforms comparative approaches in terms of cooperative reward, system stability, and convergence speed, validating its effectiveness and robustness in multi-agent cooperative tasks.

title: Sentinel-5P Image and HYSPLIT Modeling for Analysis The Distribution of Sulfur Dioxide and The Impact on Acute Respiratory Infection in Surabaya City
authors: Shafa Nabila Prawitasari, Bangun Muljo Sukojo
publish: 2025 IEEE Asia-Pacific Conference on Geoscience, Electronics and Remote Sensing Technology (AGERS)
abstract: Urbanization in Surabaya has led to increased air pollution from vehicle emissions and industrial activities, with significant impacts on public health and air quality. Sulfur Dioxide (SO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>) emissions are one of the factors contributing to the high incidence of Acute Respiratory Infections (ARI). Integration of Sentinel-5P with HYSPLIT modeling is needed to fill data gaps and enhance the accuracy, analysis and efficiency. Sentinel-5P SO2 for periods 2020-2023 were processed using the Google Earth Engine platform and HYSPLIT model was used to simulate the transport and dispersion of pollutants in the atmosphere. The results indicate that SO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf> concentrations in Surabaya show fluctuations each year. However, ISPU in Surabaya City is in the good category between 2020-2023. Correlation between SO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf> and ARI shows a weak relationship between 0,091 and 0,2524. Spatial analysis shows a stronger relationship in each subdistrict with an $\mathbf{R}^{\mathbf{2}}$ value of 0.008 to 0,795. The simulation from HYSPLIT shows the particle trajectory and SO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf> concentration distribution, indicating a spread toward the north to northeast with a range up to 50 km and pollution sources near the surface, such as factories and textile industries. This research shows the spatial and temporal distribution of SO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf> and potential impacts on ARI. Therefore, this research is expected to be considered in the reformulation of air pollution control and prevention.

title: Pyramid Broad Learning System for Object and Scene Point Cloud Segmentation
authors: Yifei Tian, Yan Fu
publish: 2025 International Conference on Big Data and Data Mining (BDDM)
abstract: Point clouds, known for their high precision and spatial richness, play a crucial role in 3D scene understanding and object-level analysis. However, their unordered and irregular structure presents significant challenges for direct feature extraction and segmentation using traditional models. To overcome these limitations, this paper proposes a novel variant of the broad learning system, termed the pyramid broad network (PyramidBLS), designed specifically for efficient and accurate 3D point cloud segmentation. The proposed model incorporates a multi-scale feature mapping layer to capture detailed point-wise geometric features. These features are enhanced in the nonlinear enhancement layer by projecting them into a high-dimensional space, enabling better representation. Furthermore, a pyramid structure integrates local contextual information at multiple receptive field scales with varying neighborhood sizes. By concatenating outputs from the feature, enhancement, and pyramid layers, the architecture forms a broad and shallow network where all units directly contribute to the final segmentation output. To enhance adaptability and scalability, PyramidBLS supports horizontal expansion by adding more enhancement nodes and vertical stacking by deploying multiple broad networks in series. The model is evaluated on two benchmark datasets: ShapeNet for object part segmentation and S3DIS for indoor scene semantic segmentation. Experimental results demonstrate that PyramidBLS achieves superior performance compared to existing broad learning algorithms and machine learning models, highlighting its effectiveness in complex point cloud segmentation tasks.

title: Convergence Assurance for Probabilistic AI: Rank-Aware, Tail-Sensitive Diagnostics for Multi-Chain Inference
authors: Keval Barvaliya
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Emerging Trends in Computing and Communication (ETCOM)
abstract: Reliable uncertainty in modern AI systems hinges on robust Bayesian inference, yet standard convergence checks can miss scale and heavy-tail pathologies in multi-chain samplers; this paper introduces a rank-aware convergence suite that fuses rank-normalized and folded <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\widehat{R}$</tex> with bulk/tail effective sample sizes and rank-based visual diagnostics to detect location, scale, and tail mismatches across chains in probabilistic pipelines. The methods provide actionable thresholds and quantile-focused Monte Carlo error estimates, and are validated via controlled simulations and canonical case studies, showing improved sensitivity over traditional diagnostics while remaining practical for large-scale probabilistic programming used in AI.

title: Scenario-Based Liquefaction Potential Analysis Using Geotechnical Data and Electrical Resistivity Tomography: A Case Study in Caraga State University, Butuan City, Philippines
authors: Rutchen A. Alibangbang, Hizle Jeyhne J. Busque, Olayvar Yvonne T, Daniel Jose L. Buhay, Nichole Anthony D. Pacle
publish: 2025 IEEE Asia-Pacific Conference on Geoscience, Electronics and Remote Sensing Technology (AGERS)
abstract: Butuan City, Philippines, is prone to seismic events due to its proximity to numerous earthquake generators, resulting in a range of hazards such as liquefaction. This study investigates the liquefaction potential in Caraga State University - Main Campus by analyzing the PF: Surigao and PF: Esperanza segments, an unnamed fault west of Butuan City, and the Philippine Trench. The maximum magnitudes Mw 7.4, Mw 7.1, Mw 6.5, and Mw 8.1, respectively, along with the minimum recorded Mw 5.1, which triggered liquefaction in the Philippines, were considered. A deterministic approach was employed to calculate ground attenuation values. Electrical resistivity tomography (ERT) profiles identified areas prone to liquefaction using resistivity meters, and borehole data were used to calculate the liquefaction potential index (LPI). The calculated LPI varies with the earthquake magnitude and PGA, ranging from high (5-15 LPI) to very high potential (>15 LPI) based on maximum magnitudes and low to no liquefaction based on the smallest magnitude. These findings underscore the need for effective mitigation strategies to reduce the potential damage caused by liquefaction in the study area.

title: Spectral regularization for adversarially-robust representation learning
authors: Sheng Yang, Jacob A. Zavatone-Veth, Cengiz Pehlevan
publish: 2025 59th Asilomar Conference on Signals, Systems, and Computers
abstract: The vulnerability of neural network classifiers to adversarial attacks is a major obstacle to their deployment in safety-critical applications. Regularization of network parameters during training can be used to improve adversarial robustness and generalization performance. Usually, the network is regularized end-to-end, with parameters at all layers affected by regularization. However, in settings where learning representations is key, such as self-supervised learning (SSL), layers after the feature representation will be discarded when performing inference. For these models, regularizing up to the feature space is more suitable. To this end, we propose a new spectral regularizer for representation learning that encourages black-box adversarial robustness in downstream classification tasks. In supervised classification settings, we show empirically that this method is more effective in boosting test accuracy and robustness than previously-proposed methods that regularize all layers of the network. We then show that this method improves the adversarial robustness of classifiers using representations learned with self-supervised training or transferred from another classification task. In all, our work begins to unveil how representational structure affects adversarial robustness.

title: VR Panorama Inpainting: A Feasibility Study of High-Resolution View Synthesis for Virtual Reality
authors: Lillian Eagan, Nam Nguyen, Angela Victoria Chen, Theresa Zhu, Seth Maxwell Johnson, Pranav Reddy Dumpa, Benjamin Jonson Geil, Jonathan Ventura, Stefanie Zollmann
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and eXtended and Virtual Reality (AIxVR)
abstract: In this work, we propose a machine learning-based pipeline for generating a 3D-colored mesh representation of a scene from a single-input panorama. Our approach allows us to create a fully immersive Virtual Reality (VR) experiences. Unlike neural rendering approaches that are constrained by lowresolution inputs and offline processing, we focus on high-quality resolution inputs (4 K) and real-time rendering on VR headsets by estimating a depth map and creating a layered depth image (LDI) representation. We iteratively fill unseen regions behind objects through a cutting and inpainting process, after which the LDI is converted into an optimized, texture-mapped 3D mesh, resulting in a compact and efficient representation. We assess the feasibility of this approach through a quantitative evaluation with a user study in VR, focusing on the practicality, visual quality, and perceived presence of the generated scenes.

title: Charting Channels in the Presence of RIS
authors: Mohamed El Mehdi Makhlouf, Yamil Vindas, Anil Kumar, Maxime Guillaud, Marco Di Renzo
publish: 2025 59th Asilomar Conference on Signals, Systems, and Computers
abstract: This work explores the extension of channel charting, a technique for constructing low-dimensional representations of channel state information (CSI) through self-supervised learning, to wireless environments including reconfigurable intelligent surfaces (RIS). Channel charting has shown promise in pseudo-position based radio resource management applications by capturing spatial relationships between transmitters and receivers. We investigate the challenges and opportunities that RISs present to channel charting, particularly focusing on how RIS reconfiguration affects the learned low-dimensional representations. We focus on a contrastive learning framework with triplet loss to derive a channel chart through distance learning, and incorporate the RIS configuration information into the learning process, aiming to develop channel charts that are robust to changes in the RIS configuration. We also explore the potential for leveraging channel charts to inform RIS configuration, enabling applications where the channel chart is used to determine optimal RIS settings from a given codebook.

title: How Linguistic Variation Shapes Hallucination Risk in Large Language Models: A Psychometric Perspective

title: Cooperative H ∞ Consensus Control with Non-convex Constraints and Parameter Uncertainties
authors: Hongmei Zhang, Xue Li
abstract: This paper investigates the distributed H<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">∞</sub> consensus problem for an undirected network of first-order and second-order multi-agent systems, under the simultaneous influence of external disturbances, parameter uncertainties, and non-convex control input constraints. To address the nonlinearity caused by the non-convex constraints, a scaling factor approach is employed, enabling the transformation of the original systems into their equivalent reduced-order forms. Based on this transformation, a tailored Lyapunov function is constructed, and sufficient conditions are derived to ensure that all agents achieve consensus with a desired H<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">∞</sub> performance index while satisfying corresponding non-convex input constraints. Finally, a numerical simulation is presented to verify the effectiveness of the proposed theoretical results.

title: IEEE Transactions on Emerging Topics in Computational Intelligence Information for Authors
