title: Adaptive Fault-tolerant Control for Actuator Failure of Flexible Satellites
authors: Dong Zhao, Mengfei Li, Wenjing Ren, Darong Huang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Aerospace and Electronic Systems
abstract: In this paper, when actuator failure occurs, an adaptive fault-tolerant control (AFTC) strategy is proposed to solve the problem of attitude tracking control and vibration suppression in a flexible satellite system described by an ODE–PDE interconnected system. The moment of inertia matrix of the system and which actuator fails and when the failure occurs are unknown. To solve these problems, an adaptive control strategy is designed based on uncertainty parametrization and symmetric positive definite matrix–diagonal matrix–unit upper triangular matrix (SDU) factorization. The modified Lyapunov method and the assumed mode principle are used to prove that attitude tracking and vibration suppression can be realized. Finally, the simulation results show that AFTC strategy can be realized and is effective.

title: Generalized Reliability Assessment and Redundant Routing for Safety-Critical Traffic in Time-Sensitive Networking
authors: Zhen Liao, Feng He, Xueqian Tang, Ershuai Li
publish: IEEE Transactions on Aerospace and Electronic Systems
abstract: In safety-critical domains such as avionics and industrial automation, the transmission of network traffic may be affected by transient failures that result in data errors. Time-Sensitive Networking (TSN) enhances traffic reliability through mechanisms like Frame Replication and Elimination for Reliability (FRER), which enables traffic to be multipath through the network by providing link redundancy. However, FRER does not provide a method for assessing reliability and redundant routing. In existing reliability assessment studies of traffic, the assumptions of redundant paths are based on combinations of multiple disjoint end-to-end paths, which is not universal considering the diversity of network topologies and the flexibility of redundant routing. Furthermore, reliability is not only related to redundant routing, real-time performance also plays a crucial role since its delay at nodes and links will directly affect failure probability. Most of the previous works focus on time-triggered (TT) flows, whose real-time results can be obtained directly through a predefined time window, while audio video bridging (AVB) flows need to be introduced into real-time analysis method to realize overall assessment of reliability. In this regard, first, this paper proposes a generalized TSN transient failure reliability assessment method, which can be used for arbitrary redundant routes to give generalized reliability assessment results. Then, we introduce a Network Calculus based model to analyze the AVB flow's real-time performance and its impact on reliability. On this basis, we also propose a redundant routing method for flows and conduct an example analysis, and the results prove that the method can guarantee the reliability of traffic under a variety of network topologies, demonstrating its effectiveness and applicability.

title: SMDFusion: Multimodal Sensor Fusion for Auto Driving Perception Under Mixed-Traffic Scenarios
authors: Ting Qu, Dewang Liu, Jinghua Zhao, Hong Chen
publish: IEEE Transactions on Intelligent Transportation Systems
abstract: Autonomous driving relies on accurate and reliable perception particularly in mixed traffic scenarios. In such complex environments including vehicles, pedestrians, barriers, and significant occlusions occur, degrading the quality of information collected by LiDAR and cameras. Therefore, current multimodal sensor fusion frameworks face difficulties in capturing long-range dependencies in point clouds and integrating information from sensors of different quality, which leads to inaccurate localization and object misclassification. To address these issues, we propose SMDFusion, a novel multimodal framework that effectively fuses LiDAR features, image frequency features, and depth information. Specifically, a Sparse-Mamba (SMM) module is introduced to capture long-range dependencies in sparse voxel features via Hilbert curve serialization and state-space modeling; a Depth-guided Frequency Enhancement (DFE) module leverages wavelet decomposition to refine image feature representations; and a Dynamic Gated Multimodal Fusion (DGMF) module adaptively models spatial, channel, and modality interactions for robust fusion. In addition, a distribution classification loss is designed to optimize the decision boundaries and mitigate misclassification among visually similar categories. Experiments on the large-scale nuScenes benchmark demonstrate that SMDFusion achieves 74.6% NDS and 71.9% mAP on the test set without test-time augmentation or model ensemble, while ablation studies further validate the contribution of each component.

title: Towards simplicity and practicality: A novel framework and guidance for robotic table tennis applications_supp1-3677709.mp4
authors: Qitong Guo
abstract: Although many impressive advances have been reported in the table tennis robots field using reinforcement learning method, challenges related to policy complexity and adaptability continue to hinder large-scale deployment and practical applications. In this work, building upon extensive prior studies, we propose several techniques and integrate them into a unified framework that reduces the difficulty of training and deployment while enhancing the human player enjoyment. Specifically, a recursively nested design is introduced in the hierarchical decision-making system, which fully separates high-level decision-making from low-level execution, eliminating the need to train multiple agents at the execution layer. The multi-objective problem is also studied by introducing a tolerance-based weight modulation mechanism, which can balance the landing accuracy with other objectives and adjust the playing strategy to meet diverse goals. Comprehensive experiments demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed framework and techniques. Notably, the robot achieved up to 18 consecutive rallies with human player, which, to the best of our knowledge, is the longest rally attained with a fixed-base collaborative robot arm. The proposed methods can be readily extended to other racket-sport robots or similar tasks. Our project website: <uri xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">https://guoqitong.github.io/ttrobot-hrl/</uri>.

title: A Phase Switching Fractional Output Divider with Split-DTC based Nonlinear Calibration
authors: Xin Hong, Ang Hu, Shengxiang Liu, Dongsheng Liu, Deng Luo, Xuecheng Zou, Ke Sun
publish: IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems II: Express Briefs
abstract: This brief presents a fractional output divider (FOD) utilizing phase-switching and split-DTC technique. The split-DTC based background calibration technique is used to mitigate the gain mismatch and nonlinearity of the DTC chain. By using the phase switching technique, the divider division step is reduced from 1 to 0.25. Moreover, the DTC dynamic range is decreased to 1/4 quadrature signal period, leading to the optimized noise performance and power consumption of the DTC chain. The prototype chip was designed and fabricated using 40nm CMOS process, occupying a core area of 0.055mm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> and consuming 5.4mW at a 1.2V supply. The fractional spur is reduced from -44.01dBc to -74.48dBc when the calibration technique turning on and the worst spur level across the fractional-N division range is -70dBc.

title: DSM: Constructing a Diverse Semantic Map for 3D Visual Grounding
authors: Qinghongbing Xie, Zijian Liang, Fuhao Li, Long Zeng
abstract: Effective scene representation is critical for the visual grounding ability of reasoning. However, existing 3D Visual Grounding methods either only focus on geometric and visual cues, or like traditional 3D scene graphs, lack the multi-dimensional attributes needed for complex reasoning. To bridge this gap, we introduce a novel scene representation framework, Diverse Semantic Map (DSM), that enriches robust geometric map with a spectrum of VLM-derived semantics, including appearance, physical, and affordance attributes. The DSM is first constructed online by fusing multi-view observations within a temporal sliding window, creating a comprehensive map of scene knowledge. Building on this foundation, we propose a new grounding paradigm, DSM-Grounding, that shifts grounding from free-form queries to a structured reasoning process over the semantic-rich map for VLM. Extensive evaluations validate our approach's superiority, improving accuracy and interpretability. DSM-Grounding achieves a state-of-the-art 59.06% overall accuracy of IoU@0.5, surpassing others by 10% on ScanRefer. In semantic segmentation, our DSM attains a 67.93% F-mIoU, outperforming all baselines in Replica. Furthermore, successful deployment on physical robots for complex navigation and grasping tasks confirms the framework's practical utility in real-world scenarios.

title: Semantically Guided Counterfactual Model for Multiclass Anomaly Detection
authors: Yuzhong Zhong, Ming Lei, Xuke Zhong, Bin Guo, Songyi Dian
abstract: Reconstruction-based anomaly detection is appealing for industrial inspection because it reconstructs anomaly-free references and produces interpretable pixel-level residual maps. However, in multiclass settings, it often suffers from, first, identity mapping, where abnormal regions are overreconstructed and residuals vanish, and second, cross-category feature entanglement, which introduces artifacts and weakens localization. Residual scoring is further biased because it mixes true defect discrepancies with model-induced reconstruction errors, causing false alarms and confusing explanations. We propose a semantically guided counterfactual model (SGCAD) for multiclass anomaly detection and localization. SGCAD applies a masking intervention to a candidate region, reconstructs the intervened input, and contrasts factual and intervened residuals to estimate reconstruction bias, yielding a debiased anomaly map by treating consistent differences as anomalies. To stabilize reconstruction, we introduce a dual-branch bottleneck with latent constraints to mitigate identity mapping while preserving normal details, and a hierarchical adaptive prompt to reduce cross-category confusion during decoding without class priors. Extensive experiments on four public benchmarks demonstrate that while SGCAD maintains competitive image-level detection performance, it achieves superior state-of-the-art results in pixel-level localization. By effectively mitigating bias-induced false positives, SGCAD offers a more precise and reliable solution for practical industrial inspection.

title: POMACS V10, N1, March 2026 Editorial

title: Real-Time Utility Assessment of Privacy-Anonymized Data: A Cross-Domain Framework for Smart Cities
authors: Zeinab Shahbazi, Sadiqa Jafari, Zahra Shahbazi, Magnus Johnsson
abstract: Smart cities collect lots of data from sources like traffic systems and sensors. This data is vital for improving services such as transport management, public safety, energy saving, and citizen involvement. However, keeping people’s privacy secure by making data anonymous can make the data less useful. Anonymization methods include mix-zones, pseudonymization, and data masking. Existing systems often evaluate the usefulness of data after it’s processed, but usually can not adjust to changes in real-time or work well across different fields. It also does not typically consider fairness for individuals or integrate well with privacy-preserving data methods on devices, which are crucial for city services needing quick responses. This paper presents a new system to assess anonymized data in smart cities in real-time. The system includes four main components: 1. A real-time tool that continuously monitors how anonymization affects data quality across various areas. 2. A utility model that adjusts usefulness scores based on the needs of different smart city services. 3. A module that detects and addresses unfair differences in data usefulness caused by anonymization. 4. A data combination layer that operates well on devices, allowing on-site decision-making while complying with privacy regulations like GDPR. The framework is tested with multiple real-world transportation datasets, anonymized in different ways, and applied to tasks like traffic prediction, anomaly detection, and location-based services. The results demonstrate that the framework provides accurate data usefulness estimation with minimal computing effort, and it offers fair, situation-specific trade-offs in usefulness in real-time. This work connects privacy and usefulness, offering a scalable solution for responsibly using anonymized data in smart city environments. The framework is validated on both transportation and smart energy datasets, demonstrating consistent improvements in privacy–utility–fairness balance and confirming its generality across smart-city domains.

title: A High-Precision Reconstruction Method for Angular Velocity of High-speed Rotation Aircraft
authors: Tao Zheng, Yan Zhao, Xiang Wang, Jie Li, Jun Liu
abstract: In order to address the problem of low measurement accuracy of the angular velocity of the high-speed rotation aircrafts, in this article, an angular velocity reconstruction method (AVRM) based on microelectromechanical systems gyroscopes is proposed. To improve the measurement accuracy, three innovations are proposed. First, a partial strapdown inertial measurement system (PSIMS) which able to isolate aircraft high-speed rotation motion is designed, and enables the combination of small-range gyroscopes and optical encoders to measure large-amplitude angular velocities. Second, a composite range dual inertial measurement units configuration scheme is proposed to acquire angular velocity under the influence of precession. Finally, an AVRM based on BP neural network is constructed to improve measurement accuracy. The PSIMS is processed, and the comparative experiments are performed. The experimental results show that the PSIMS can isolate more than 90% of the aircraft's angular velocity. Also, the angular velocity measurement accuracy of the PSIMS with the AVRM is four times than the accuracy of the PSIMS without the AVRM.

title: Toward Safer Overtaking Maneuvers: A Simulation-Based Study on Accident Mitigation in Automated Vehicles
authors: Sandy Bolufé, Brian Contreras, Karel Toledo
abstract: Automated vehicles (AVs) require proactive safety systems to safely operate in diverse driving scenarios. The complexity and challenges of high-risk driving maneuvers, such as complete overtaking, have led the research community to make significant efforts toward developing reliable operating modes for overtaking maneuver systems. These efforts aim to better understand the benefits and limitations of intrinsic operating modes in complete overtaking to take AVs to the next level. This paper delves into the challenges of complete overtaking maneuvers by developing and evaluating an overtaking system for AVs. Specifically, a vision and radar-based automated overtaking system is designed, which utilizes data from cameras and radars to track surrounding vehicles, evaluate overtaking feasibility based on overtaking time (OT) and time-to-collision (TTC), and decide the driving behavior of the ego vehicle. A modular architecture is developed encompassing perception, sensor fusion and tracking, collision detection and decision-making, overtaking planning, and motion control. Extensive simulations are conducted under varying conditions, utilizing a specialized framework consisting of RoadRunner, Automated Driving Toolbox, and MATLAB/Simulink, to assess the system’s ability to avoid potential collisions and enhance maneuver reliability. The findings highlight the significance of the operating mode in accident mitigation and contribute to the broader goal of achieving safe automated driving.

title: A Privacy-preserving and Explainable Method for Diabetic Retinopathy Classification and Localisation
authors: Giovanni Ciaramella, Mario Cesarelli, Fabio Martinelli, Patrizia Agnello, Marta Petyx, Antonella Santone, Francesco Mercaldo
abstract: Diabetic Retinopathy is a leading cause of vision impairment, requiring early and accurate detection to prevent severe complications. Over the years, researchers have proposed several Deep Learning-based methods for detecting this disease. Nonetheless, these proposals have shown promise in DR classification; challenges such as data privacy concerns and the lack of explainability hinder their adoption in real-world clinical settings. For these reasons, this paper proposes a method for explainable diabetic retinopathy classification that exploits Federated Machine Learning to address data privacy and model interpretability simultaneously. Specifically, in this study, we utilized a publicly available dataset comprising 3,662 retinal images, representing both patients with Diabetic Retinopathy and healthy controls. Moreover, we allocated samples across 20 clients using non-Independent and Identically Distributed (non-IID) data to represent a real-world scenario better. To increase the privacy and security of the model, we utilized the zeroing norm aggregator, which mitigates the risk of gradient leakage attacks by suppressing sensitive gradient information. Furthermore, we trained several models using two custom versions of two architectures, such as Standard CNN and MobileNet Small, and a grid search algorithm to identify the optimal hyperparameters. At the end of the training, validation, and test phases, the best model identified reached an accuracy of 0.813 using the Custom MobileNet demonstrating robustness in a non-IID federated setup. We then applied three Class Activation Mapping (such as: Grad-CAM, Grad-CAM++, and Score-CAM) to provide visual explanations of model decisions. The proposed approach aims to enhance the adoption of artificial intelligence in medical imaging by improving data privacy and prediction explainability, thereby ensuring compliance with privacy regulations and fostering trust in deep learning-based diagnoses.

title: Optimized Design of a Novel Permanent Magnet System for the Force Standard Machine Traceable to the Planck Constant
authors: Na Wang, Jinxin Xu, Yuanjie Chen, Keyi Zhu, Changxin Gong, Cheng Lai, Jiong Ding, Dongfang Hu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Instrumentation and Measurement
abstract: Following the redefinition of the kilogram based on the Planck constant ℎ at the 26th CGPM in 2018, electromagnetic force standards traceable to ℎ can be realized using Kibble/joule balances. Based on this principle, a force standard machine with a capacity of 100 kN is proposed. This paper focuses on the design and optimization of the permanent magnet system, which is a key component of the proposed force standard machine. A permanent magnet system capable of generating a standard electromagnetic force of up to 5 kN is designed using a quantitative optimization strategy based on minimizing the power dissipation per unit volume of the suspended coil. A magnetic field compensation scheme is introduced to improve field uniformity and reduce edge effects in the air gap. The optimized magnet system provides a radial magnetic flux density of 0.8 T with an extended uniform region of 120 mm and a relative field deviation of 8×10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">-4</sup>. In addition, a dual-air-gap configuration inspired by NPL-type designs is adopted to suppress current-induced effects. The thermal behavior of the suspended coil is analyzed, including the influence of coil heating and the resulting upward airflow. The current-induced effect is found to be on the order of 10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">-5</sup>, while the effect of upward airflow is on the order of 10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">-7</sup>, both relative to the maximum electromagnetic force.

title: IEEE Standard for Wireless Sensor Data of Power Transmission and Transformation Equipment
authors: N/A
abstract: Data unit format for use by Internet of Things (IoT) power sensors is specified in this standard. The exchange of service data, control data, and fragmented data via the IEC 61850/IEC 60870-104 or Message Queuing Telemetry Transport (MQTT) protocol is described by the data format.

title: GPU-Accelerated Syndrome Decoding for Quantum LDPC Codes below the 63 μs Latency Threshold
authors: Oscar Ferraz, Bruno Coutinho, Gabriel Falcao, Marco Gomes, Francisco A. Monteiro, Vitor Silva
publish: 2025 59th Asilomar Conference on Signals, Systems, and Computers
abstract: This paper presents a GPU-accelerated decoder for quantum low-density parity-check (QLDPC) codes that achieves sub-63 μs latency, below the surface code decoder’s real-time threshold demonstrated on Google’s Willow quantum processor. While surface codes have demonstrated below-threshold performance, the encoding rates approach zero as code distances increase, posing challenges for scalability. Recently proposed QLDPC codes, such as those by Panteleev and Kalachev, offer constant-rate encoding and asymptotic goodness but introduce higher decoding complexity. To address such limitation, this work presents a parallelized belief propagation decoder leveraging syndrome information on commodity GPU hardware. Parallelism was exploited to maximize performance within the limits of target latency, allowing decoding latencies under 26 μs for [[784, 24, 24]] codes and as low as 16.9 μs for smaller codes, meeting the tight timing constraints of superconducting qubit cycles. These results show that real-time, scalable decoding of asymptotically good quantum codes is achievable using widely available commodity hardware, advancing the feasibility of fault-tolerant quantum computation beyond surface codes.

title: A Unified Framework for Text Classification: Evaluating Feature Extraction Techniques and Classifiers from BoW to BERT
authors: Savitha Ramesh, Devarashetty Nandini, Dhanya Sri Kokku, Mandadapu Pooja
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Emerging Trends in Computing and Communication (ETCOM)
abstract: Text classification is one of the applications in the field of Natural Language Processing (NLP) and it can incorporate applications such as spam detection, topic classification, etc. In this paper, we have developed a comprehensive and unified framework to help measure and evaluate the performance of classical, deep learning and transformer-based approaches in text classification. We analyze and compare the different feature extraction methods—BoW, TF-IDF and BERT—with classification approaches, such as Naive Bayes, LSTM and finetuned BERT. We evaluate representation methods, classifiers and investigate how they interact with classifiers through thorough testing on benchmark datasets, highlighting some important trade-offs in terms of accuracy, efficiency, and computational cost. This research is meant to support researchers and endusers in making informed approaches to solving a variety of text classification problems using the methods presented in this paper.

title: Evaluating IoT-Enabled Waste Management Readiness in Kenyan Urban Slums: A Systematic Review Towards Achieving SDG 6 and 11
authors: Ogeto Treza Nyanchama, Reny Nadlifatin, Apol Pribadi Subriadi
publish: 2025 International Conference on Innovation and Intelligence for Informatics, Computing, and Technologies (3ICT)
abstract: This study examines the potential of Internet of Things (IoT) technologies to improve waste management in Kenya's informal settlements. Despite the promise of smart bins, RFID, and low-power networks, their implementation in areas like Kibera and Mathare often falls short of addressing local challenges. Reporting followed the PRISMA 2020 guideline and checklist to ensure transparency and completeness of the systematic literature review of 36 peer-reviewed studies from 2019 to 2025. This research reveals key barriers such as unreliable infrastructure, digital exclusion, and policy gaps that hinder sustainable adoption. Addressing a gap in existing literature, the study proposes a holistic evaluation framework integrating technical readiness, community trust, and policy alignment to assess the feasibility of IoT systems in slum contexts. This is done while borrowing from the TAM and DOI theories. The approach aims to help policymakers, technologists, and urban planners design context-sensitive, inclusive solutions that advance Sustainable Development Goals 6 and 11. Although Kenya serves as our main focus, we triangulate our findings using similar data from Sub-Saharan Africa and other low- and middle-income contexts to improve generalizability and underscore contextspecific variations.

title: Digital Transformation in Auditing: Key Drivers of Information Technology Adoption by Government External Auditors
authors: Anita Carolina, Nur Aini Rakhmawati, Bambang Setiawan
publish: 2025 International Conference on Innovation and Intelligence for Informatics, Computing, and Technologies (3ICT)
abstract: This study investigates the impact of information technology competency, accounting information system complexity, organizational support, and external pressure on information technology adoption in auditing, drawing on the concepts of task-technology fit theory. A survey of external government auditors in Indonesia was conducted. Partial least squares structural equation modeling was employed to investigate the associations among the variables. The findings revealed that information technology competency, accounting information system complexity, organizational support, and external pressure influence technology adoption. This study extends the task-technology fit theory by highlighting the internal and external factors of technology adoption in a high-risk, regulated profession. Practical recommendations are provided to help organizations enhance their information technology adoption mechanisms, thereby improving the audit process's efficacy and efficiency, which ultimately results in a higher-quality audit report.

title: Social Media Signal Analysis for Predicting Influencer Impact on E-commerce Consumer Behavior
authors: Kiran Kakade, Reena Mahapatra Lenka, Mangesh Manohar Sanap, Priyadarshini Daga, Shilpa C. Shinde, Shailesh Kediya
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Recent Trends in Electrical, Electronics and Computing Technologies (ICRTEECT)
abstract: The great quantity of social networking sites has had a significant impact on behaviour, particularly with relation to online buying. One of the most crucial elements influencing consumers' purchases now is social media celebrity presence. They may convert people's opinions and boost revenue. This study investigates how social media signal analysis may be used to project influencer behaviour on online shopping. Important indicators of an influencer's efficacy include social media cues like likes, shares, comments, and interactions amongst followers. Using data analytics and machine learning, this research investigates how much influencers really matter and how one may use these indicators to predict what people would purchase. This research looks for relationships between social media signals and activities like clicks, sales, and purchase intentions by means of predictive modelling. We also examine other types of media, including advertising postings, reviews, and unboxing videos, to see how these influence behaviour. The research findings provide us with insightful knowledge on how businesses may cooperate with powerful individuals to make their marketing initiatives more successful and better target the appropriate target clients.

title: Environmental Remote Sensing with Passive Ambient Noise Radar
authors: Michael Lexa, Ethan S. Miller, Phillip M. Corbell, Stephen R. Kaeppler, Yi-San Lai, Wesley C. Leong, Charles A. Mohr
publish: 2025 59th Asilomar Conference on Signals, Systems, and Computers
abstract: The high-frequency (HF; 3–30 MHz) band enables beyond line-of-sight communications and surveillance via ionospheric skywave and oceanic surface wave propagation modes. Passive ambient noise radar (PANR) is a new HF surveillance paradigm that exploits ambient noise fields regularly present in the HF spectrum. This paper derives a generalized likelihood ratio detector for PANR. The detector is based on the predicted phenomenon that the presence of targets alter spatiotemporal correlations measured by two (or more) geographically separated sensors. We propose an SNR-like quantity called the "scattered-plus-direct to direct-noise ratio" (SDDNR) as the driving quantity in detection performance. We also present two examples and show preliminary evidence that some of the model assumptions hold in a real HF radar system.

title: Reversible Data Hiding in Hyperspectral Images Using Pixel-Frequency-Based Band Selection with Minimal Overflow and Without Preprocessing
authors: Sai Pragna Koritala, Kavya Gudiseva, Sai Naren Polavarapu, Bhavya Sri Vangapandu, V. M. Manikandan
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Emerging Trends in Computing and Communication (ETCOM)
abstract: This paper proposes a reversible data hiding (RDH) technique designed for hyperspectral images (HSI) in ENVI format, enabling lossless embedding of binary data while preserving spectral integrity. The hyperspectral dataset used in this study was acquired using a Specim IQ camera, covering a spectral range of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$400-1000$</tex> nanometers, providing high-resolution imagery across 204 contiguous spectral bands. The method identifies an optimal band for embedding by selecting one with minimal overflow risk and maximum pixel repetition. In our study, Band 186 with pixel value 246 was chosen, allowing precise control during embedding. Binary data is embedded by incrementing the frequent pixel (246) to 247 for bit ‘ 1 ’ and leaving it unchanged for bit ‘ 0 ’. To prevent pixel collision, values above 246 are preshifted by +1. The extraction process restores both the embedded data and original image by traversing the band in raster order and reversing the modifications. Experimental validation includes pixel-level matrix comparisons, difference heatmaps, and 3D visualizations, confirming complete recovery of both data and image. The approach demonstrates 100% reversibility and zero distortion, making it ideal for secure and sensitive applications in remote sensing and surveillance.

title: Design and Deployment of GSM-Based IoT System for Real-Time Monitoring of Water Quality in Rural Areas
authors: Seven Siren, Rothna Pec, Sreynou Chin, Chhengiy Mao, Angkeaprek Yonchem, Channareth Srun, Chivon Choeung, Khov Veng Chhay, Sokheang Eang
publish: 2025 IEEE Asia-Pacific Conference on Geoscience, Electronics and Remote Sensing Technology (AGERS)
abstract: The Tonle Sap Lake and the Mekong River are the main water sources in Cambodia. Recently, industrial activities have led to growth in Cambodia’s economy. The dumping of factory waste and garbage into water bodies causes water pollution. This paper introduces Water Quality Monitoring Based on Cellular Communication, integrated with the Internet of Things (IoT), which provides real-time data on water quality over long distances, and enhancing water management. The system incorporates cellular communication and crucial parameters such as potential of Hydrogen (pH), Total Dissolved Solids (TDS), Turbidity, and Temperature. These parameters are combined in each node, which inspects the water quality and transmits the information via the Global System for Mobile Communications (GSM) system to the main system. The data are sent from Main to Amazon Web Services (AWS) to be stored and allow users to access AWS to request information. Thus, users obtain the data through smartphone applications, providing easy access to the information.

title: Reliable Connectivity Optimization in Wireless Networks with Dependent Link Failures
authors: Zheng Ge, Lara Jüschke, Lars Wolf, Eduard A. Jorswieck
publish: 2025 59th Asilomar Conference on Signals, Systems, and Computers
abstract: Resilient network design is a mandatory feature for future wireless network infrastructure. When links in mesh networks fail, multi-connectivity and multi-path transmissions can establish the required end-to-end availability. In this work, we investigate a general wireless network graph with dependent link failures. We consider both packet duplication and load balancing strategies, as well as resource allocation across various network topologies with different levels of multi-connectivity. Our approach is based on analytical lower and upper bounds on network connectivity to compute the overall reliability, complemented by numerical simulations to illustrate the results. By modeling the entire resilience cycle from the event, through survival, to the recovery phase, different tradeoffs are characterized: redundancy versus efficiency, dependencies, data rates, and network topologies. Finally, we present a method for identifying the optimal link configuration to achieve the desired network performance.

title: Automotive 4D Imaging MIMO Radar Online Channel Imbalance Estimation via Bilinear NLMS
authors: Esmaeil Kavousi Ghafi, Matthias Wagner, Alexander Melzer, Mario Huemer
publish: 2025 59th Asilomar Conference on Signals, Systems, and Computers
abstract: In this work, a channel imbalance estimation method for automotive 4D imaging MIMO radars is proposed. The imbalances are estimated iteratively using a bilinear normalized least mean squares (BLNLMS) algorithm. Our method features low computational complexity, and does not require any prior knowledge of the targets in the environment. This is because it jointly estimates the targets’ parameters and imbalances in the signal processing chain of the radar. In addition, our method is not restricted to specific scenarios. In contrast, the majority of works in the literature are limited to the evaluation of measurements from a single target in the angular spectrum. Due to these advantages, our approach is highly qualified for a fast and reliable online channel calibration and/or fault detection tasks. We will demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed method with the help of simulation results.

title: Implementation of the YOLOv11 and MobileNetV3 in a Real-Time System for Detecting and Estimating the Gender and Age of Visitors
authors: Muhammad Farid Hakim, Faisal Rahutomo, Joko Hariyono
publish: 2025 International Conference on Innovation and Intelligence for Informatics, Computing, and Technologies (3ICT)
abstract: Understanding visitor demographics in real-time is crucial for optimizing services and strategic decision-making in various sectors. However, existing data collection methods are often manual, inefficient, and limited in scope. This study proposes a fully automated system for real-time visitor detection and demographic analysis (gender and age) by leveraging existing CCTV infrastructure. The system employs a two-stage deep learning pipeline integrating YOLOv11 for efficient detection of visitors and faces, followed by a fine-tuned MobileNetV3-Small model trained on the FairFace dataset for simultaneous gender classification and age group estimation. The YOLOv11n-face model achieved an mAP@50-95 of 0.55 for face detection, while the MobileNetV3 model reached 86.6% accuracy for gender classification and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{5 5. 8 \%}$</tex> for age estimation. Integrated system tests demonstrated real-time operation at <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\sim$</tex> 6.5 FPS on limited hardware, with a counting error rate of 9.52 % due to occlusion. These results validate the feasibility and efficiency of combining YOLOv11 and MobileNetV3 for realtime visitor demographic analytics.

title: A Study on Deep Fake Detection and Content Suspension Mechanisms from Creation to Control
authors: Sreekanth Rallapalli, Jeeru Sowmya, Dileep M R
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Recent Trends in Electrical, Electronics and Computing Technologies (ICRTEECT)
abstract: In today's digital world, deepfake tech has turned into a big headache. This type of AI creates fake real-looking text, sound, video, or pictures that can fool or misdirect people. This research looks at various ways to spot deepfakes how well they work, and steps to reduce their impact. It digs into forensic methods, AI-powered detection tools, and cutting-edge machine learning systems.Deepfakes are a real problem for privacy, democracy, and how much we can trust what we see and hear. They mess around with video and sound using smart tech like Generative Adversarial Networks (GANs). Trying to spot these fakes gets tougher as the tricks to make them get more advanced. So, we need to make better tools to catch them and keep online info legit. This study also shows that it’s super important for governments, businesses that make tech, and schools to work together. They need to spread the word to everyone, improve the technology, and set up rules to block those deepfakes from fooling us all.

title: Analysis on intelligent DevOps by using AI-Powered automation for cloud application management
authors: G Divya, Kasarla Priyanka, L Chandra Sekhar Reddy, Shaik Munawar, M.Mohammed Ibrahim, Balaji Kannan
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Recent Trends in Electrical, Electronics and Computing Technologies (ICRTEECT)
abstract: The combination of greater cloud computing pace and DevOps adoption allowed software deployment and monitoring as well as scaling to undergo automated enhancement. The current DevOps approaches using human intervention rules during automation process cause management inefficiencies in complex cloud-native systems. The research explores the integration of Machine Learning with Artificial Intelligence features into DevOps methodologies to develop autonomous healing capabilities through combining predictive diagnostic information with automated problem-fixing systems. When AI technologies are integrated under the AIOps framework companies acquire automatic anomaly detection tools and predictive resources management features and system failure assessment tools which improve their CI and CD deployment processes. The implementation of artificial intelligence-based monitoring systems enables organizations to receive genuine performance data and security protection functionality and cloud application scaling features. Automated correction systems initiated through programming helped identify sources of problems while reducing operational costs and system uptime losses.A DevOps platform created through AI automation enables deployment management and monitoring in addition to incident resolution as cloud-native functions. The analysis presents relevant research data and experimental evidence to prove how deployment operations benefit from AI and system reliability increases alongside the reduction of operational costs. Administrative roles experience substantial performance improvement through DevOps automation that employs AI because the system removes manual work and generates consistent reliable operations. The results indicate that cloud-native application control will utilize AI-driven DevOps that permits autonomous management along with self-adaptive operation of cloud platforms.

title: Sub-Problem Reduction Using Hypervolume Difference for Decomposition-Based Many-Objective Optimization
authors: Makoto Ohki
publish: 2025 International Conference on Innovation and Intelligence for Informatics, Computing, and Technologies (3ICT)
abstract: This paper proposes novel algorithms to solve a many-objective optimization problem (MaOP) by decomposing MaOP into two- or three- objective optimization problems (MOPs) and reducing the number of these sub-problems. First, all combinations of two or three objectives are enumerated from all objectives, and each objective subset is defined as a subproblem. Next, for an external non-dominated solution set, each sub-problem performs the non-dominated selection and obtains a sub-problem non-dominated solution set. The conventional method employs the size of the sub-problem non-dominated solution set as the contribution of the sub-problem, repeatedly select a sub-problem with great contribution in descending order of the contribution based on the set covering model until when the non-dominated solution set corresponding to the selected sub-problems sufficiently covers the original non-dominated solution set. When certain non-dominated solutions are unevenly distributed in the objective space, those concentrated solutions are associated with specific sub-problems, and the sub-problems are necessarily not ranked properly. In other words, the domain of the objective space covered by the surviving sub-problems that are not removed loses diversity. In order to reduce as many sub-problems as possible while maintaining as much diversity as possible in the objective space, this paper proposes to employ the sum of hypervolume differences (HVDs) of the non-dominated solutions of each sub-problem as the sub-problem contribution. Furthermore, the following two termination conditions are prepared, which are the coverage rate of the non-dominated solution set of the selected sub-problems as in the conventional method and the HVD rate of the selected sub-problems. The proposed algorithm, the conventional method, and NSGA-III, which is considered effective for solving MaOPs, are applied to test problems to verify their effectiveness.

title: Knowledge-Augmented Multimodal Reasoning for Automated Bridge Crack Diagnosis
authors: Yang Fang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Big Data and Data Mining (BDDM)
abstract: Current Vision-Language Models (VLMs) in structural health monitoring often suffer from hallucinations and superficial reasoning. To address this, a professionalgrade framework integrating Multimodal Retrieval-Augmented Generation (MRAG) with few-shot Multimodal Chain-of-Thought (MCoT) is proposed. Built on a domainenhanced Qwen2.5-VL backbone, the system utilizes MRAG to retrieve expert exemplars, effectively anchoring the model's latent space. Subsequently, MCoT calibrates diagnostics through a rigorous four-stage sequence: Visual Deciphering, Analogy Mapping, Deductive Synthesis, and Adaptive Remediation. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed configuration achieves a composite score of 91.0, outperforming the zero-shot baseline by 25.4 points. This approach effectively mitigates logical gaps, offering a robust, auditable solution for automated bridge maintenance.

title: Mapping Seagrass Cover Percentage and Estimation of Aboveground Carbon Stock using PlanetScope SuperDove Imagery and Random Forest Algorithm in Bontang, East Kalimantan
authors: Diki Akhyar Amanatulloh
publish: 2025 IEEE Asia-Pacific Conference on Geoscience, Electronics and Remote Sensing Technology (AGERS)
abstract: Seagrass meadows are a vital component of blue carbon ecosystems, playing a significant role in carbon sequestration and storage, and contributing effectively to climate change mitigation. However, the sustainability of seagrass ecosystems is increasingly threatened by climate-induced degradation and anthropogenic disturbances. Therefore, spatial inventory of seagrass meadows including aboveground carbon stock (AGCseagrass) estimation is essential for conservation and climate action. High-resolution remote sensing imagery from PlanetScope SuperDove, integrated with field survey data, offers an optimal approach for mapping benthic habitats, seagrass percentage cover (PCv), and AGCseagrass. This method is particularly effective and efficient for covering extensive areas with diverse terrain conditions. The aim of this research is to map the biophysical properties of seagrass meadows including benthic habitats, seagrass percentage cover, and aboveground carbon stock using PlanetScope SuperDove imagery with a spatial resolution of 3 meters. The Random Forest algorithm was employed for both classification and regression, integrating remote sensing data with field observations. The results indicate the presence of five benthic habitat classification classes, with an overall accuracy (OA) of 96.44% and a kappa accuracy of 95.16%. For seagrass percent cover modeling, the Random Forest algorithm yielded an $\mathbf{R}^{\mathbf{2}}$ of $\mathbf{0. 6 6}$ ($\mathbf{R M S E}=\mathbf{2 1. 0 9}$), and an average seagrass percent cover of 67.06%. The estimated total aboveground carbon stock (AGCseagrass) in Bontang reached 984,915 tons C, with an average value of $274.05 \mathrm{~g} \mathrm{C} / \mathrm{m}^{2}$, calculated across a spatial extent of $32.35 \mathrm{~km}^{2}$. The AGC modeling also resulted in an $\mathbf{R}^{2}$ of 0.66 (RMSE = 9.25).

title: DEFA: Design Space Exploration for FPGA Overlay Accelerators Through Frequency Prediction and Bayesian Optimization
authors: Qilong Zhu, Yunfei Dai, Shiyan Bi, Huizhen Kuang, Dylan Wang, Wenbo Yin, Lingli Wang
publish: 2025 35th International Conference on Field-Programmable Logic and Applications (FPL)
abstract: In edge AI inference, FPGAs demonstrate superiority in performance-area balance. FPGA Overlay Accelerators (FOAs) are programmable accelerators implemented on FPGAs, typically highly parameterized to enable flexible hardware realization. These parameters, varying across a wide design space, have a significant impact on performance and require efficient Design Space Exploration (DSE). However, current frameworks struggle to accurately predict performance metrics like maximum frequency and fail to fully explore the design space, limiting DSE's effectiveness. In this paper, we propose a DSE framework for FOA (DEFA) based on Bayesian optimization, providing more effective and comprehensive DSE. To address complex parameter interdependencies in FOA, a dependency-aware design space modeling approach (DAM) is proposed. This approach applies fine-grained pruning to the parameter space while addressing dependency constraints. Based on this pruned parameter space, we develop a custom regression predictor (CREP) for maximum frequency using LightGBM, significantly enhancing performance estimation accuracy. Furthermore, the search efficiency is improved through enhanced Latin hypercube sampling and the Tree-Structured Parzen Estimator. We use the proposed framework to optimize an FOA template, Intel FPGA AI Suite. The Pearson correlation coefficient of CREP's predictions regarding the maximum frequency of accelerator instances achieves 0.87. In the throughput optimization experiment, the proposed DSE framework improves 30.16 % compared to the architecture optimization functionality provided by Intel FPGA AI Suite across the given 10 benchmarks on average. In the areathroughput trade-off optimization experiment, compared with FPGA AI Suite, the proposed DSE framework improves 5.01 % in frequency, 18.48 % in throughput and 21.60 % in area.

title: AI-Driven Predictive Maintenance for Industrial Robotics Using IoT Sensor Fusion
authors: Kapil Rajput, Anvar Absamatov, Shakir Yusupov, Odilbek Matsapayev, Etibor Sariyeva, Mukhammad Khabibullaev
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Recent Trends in Electrical, Electronics and Computing Technologies (ICRTEECT)
abstract: Further predictive maintenance of industrial robotics based on advanced AI with sensor data fusion with IoT is a mere game changer towards reducing downtimes and enhancing efficiency in smart factories to produce goods. After analyzing data about various sensors, vibration, temperature and current sensors, as well as with the help of machine learning algorithms, such as deep neural net and random forests, the framework deduces liabilities and equipment degradations prior to failure occurrences. Edge or cloud computing will manage the real-time data stream of IoT-based robots and be used to monitor them and make decisions. Some of the techniques that enhance predictive models include time-series analysis, anomaly detection, and RUL estimation. The analysis of historical data is a necessity. Create digital twins, which can be considered as a reflection of a connected system, in which it is possible to contemplate and experiment with the scenario to validate it. Handling high-dimensional, noisy sensor data, low latency inference and being able to generalize across a diverse array of robotic systems are major challenges. More recent advances are federated learning to support privacy-aware collaboration between factories, and explainable AI (XAI) to support interpretable fault diagnostics. The addition of 5G and edge AI will also make the real-time feedback faster. This kind of paradigm is a major reduction in the cost of maintenance and extends the life of robotic assets, as a part of the Industry 4.0 objectives. The future research directions include the development of hybrid physics-informed AI models, label-efficient training, and scalable deployment schemes. Lastly, AI predictive maintenance with IoT support will revolutionize the world of industrial robots with predictive and data-driven reliability management.

title: Energy-Aware Big Data Analytics for Sustainable Computing
authors: Abdul Hadi, Rahul Kumar Konduru, Geetha Krishna Sangam, Sai Raghavendra Varanasi, Sesha Sai Sravanthi Valiveti, Uday Kumar Reddy Gangula
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Emerging Trends in Computing and Communication (ETCOM)
abstract: The dynamic increase in data-driven applications and massive cloud deployments has only aggravated the energy demand of digital infrastructures, and it has become a strong challenge to sustainability. The Energy-Aware Big Data Analytics has become a radical concept that will incorporate energy optimization methods with smart data processing to attain eco-friendly computation. This paper introduces a holistic framework, which makes use of the energy-adaptive scheduling, workload aware resource provisioning and machine learningbased power prediction models to reduce energy overheads at the expense of high analytical performance. The suggested architecture incorporates the heterogeneous big data platforms, such as edge, fog, and cloud systems, with the help of dynamic voltage and frequency scaling (DVFS), renewable energy forecasting, and carbon-conscious workload orchestration. Experimental analysis shows reduction in energy use, as much as 35 percent, increases in processing throughput, and huge saving of costs without affecting service level guarantees. The proposed approach stands out as superior in terms of sustainability goals when the comparison is made with the state-of-the-art approaches. The work provides a scalable, smart and green computing infrastructures solution that is environmentally responsible.

title: Coordinated Decentralized Resource Optimization for Cell-Free ISAC Systems
authors: Mehdi Zafari, Rang Liu, A. Lee Swindlehurst
publish: 2025 59th Asilomar Conference on Signals, Systems, and Computers
abstract: Integrated Sensing and Communication (ISAC) is emerging as a key enabler for 6 G wireless networks, allowing the joint use of spectrum and infrastructure for both communication and sensing. While prior ISAC solutions have addressed resource optimization, including power allocation, beamforming, and waveform design, they often rely on centralized architectures with full network knowledge, limiting their scalability in distributed systems. In this paper, we propose two coordinated decentralized optimization algorithms for beamforming and power allocation tailored to cell-free ISAC networks. The first algorithm employs locally designed fixed beamformers at access points (APs), combined with a centralized power allocation scheme computed at a central server (CS). The second algorithm jointly optimizes beamforming and power control through a fully decentralized consensus ADMM framework. Both approaches rely on local information at APs and limited coordination with the CS. Simulation results obtained using our proposed Python-based simulation framework evaluate their fronthaul overhead and system-level performance, demonstrating their practicality for scalable ISAC deployment in decentralized, cell-free architectures.

title: Designing a Verbal Instruction-Based Medication Adherence System for Individuals with Memory Impairment
authors: Yume Masuya, Monica Perusquía-Hernández, Kiyoshi Kiyokawa, Alexander Marquardt
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and eXtended and Virtual Reality (AIxVR)
abstract: Medication management can be challenging for people with memory impairments, especially when dosing instructions must be remembered and applied throughout the day. To address these challenges, this work investigates the design requirements and technical feasibility of using natural speech input to extract the timing information required for medication reminder support from verbal instructions. This approach aims to minimize the need for manual interaction and provide more accessible reminder-based assistance. To understand user expectations and requirements for such a system, we conducted an online survey <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(N=44$</tex>). Among other insights, survey responses emphasized the importance of effective reminder support, which informed our decision to focus the prototype on the temporal dimension of medication assistance. We developed a proof-ofconcept processing pipeline that transcribes spoken Japanese medication instructions and uses a schema-constrained Large Language Model to generate structured temporal medication events suitable for generating time-based reminders. A preliminary validation showed that text-based inputs were extracted reliably, while speech inputs also achieved high accuracy, with most remaining errors attributable to medication name misrecognition during transcription. These findings demonstrate the technical feasibility of converting spoken medication instructions into reliable reminder schedules, establishing a foundation for future context-aware assistance on wearable and mobile devices.

title: Machine Learning Approaches to Predict Thermal Efficiency of Semi-Cylindrical Solar Still with Thermal Energy Storage Materials
authors: Ashutosh Singh Raghubanshi, Kshitij Yugbodh, Ekta Jain, Geetanjli Khambra, Rahul Agrawal, Chandra Kumar Pardhi
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Smart Innovations in Systems, Infrastructure, Mechanical, Power, AI and Computing Technologies (SISIMPACT)
abstract: Worldwide demand of drinking water has continuous increased due to fast growing populations and serious concern to deliver for all peoples. Also, the availability of fresh drinking water in remote and coastal area has fewer amounts compare to the requirement for the human being, animals and other application. However, the solar still has been the feasible and sustainable solution for potable water. The present investigation, involves the measurement of thermal efficiency of modified semi cylindrical solar still experimental setup including different energy storage materials such as MSSS1 (Modified semi cylindrical solar still including tray), MSSS2 (Modified semi cylindrical solar still with tray, and gravels), and MSSS3 (Modified semi cylindrical solar still including tray, and PCM). Also predict the thermal efficiency using machine learning approaches such as Multi linear Regression (MLR), AdaBoost Regressor (ABR), and Random Forest Regressor (RFR). The model was developed using the Jupyter notebook web application provided by the anaconda computing package in python programming. However, the model performance was calculated via <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{R}^{2}$</tex> (coefficient of determination), MSE (Mean square error), RMSE (Root mean square error), and MAE (mean absolute error). Moreover, the maximum energy and exergy recorded for MSSS3 is 67.13% and 19.2 respectively. Also, ABR machine learning model indicates best performance for predicting the thermal efficiency.

title: Research on Voxel-Accelerated Iterative Algorithm for Point Cloud Surface Projection
authors: Mingyu Xu, Chengzhi Su, Enguo Wang, Yunqi Yang, Yunlong Lian, Xingxing Yang, Xiaohua Wu
publish: 2025 International Conference on Big Data and Data Mining (BDDM)
abstract: Point cloud surface projection is a key technology for 3D modeling and reconstruction, but traditional methods suffer from low efficiency in large-scale data processing and insufficient accuracy for complex scenes. This paper proposes a voxel-accelerated iterative algorithm for point cloud surface projection: First, a voxel grid is constructed, combined with local PCA to extract geometric features, and 27-neighborhood expansion is used to improve the stability of sparse point voxels. A 3D DDA algorithm is then designed to locate candidate projection regions, combined with a fast iteration mechanism and a non-convergence compensation strategy. Tests on flat plate, tube, and free-form surface models show that the algorithm achieves an average speedup of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{4}-\mathbf{6}$</tex> times compared to the traditional minimum distance method, and reduces the average projection error by <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$38.7 {\%}-58.3 {\%}$</tex>, validating its advantages in efficiency and accuracy.

title: ORBGRAND: An SDR Study
authors: Akshaya Bali, Zeynep Ece Kizilates, Arslan Riaz, Muriel Medard, Ken R. Duffy, Rabia Tugce Yazicigil
publish: 2025 59th Asilomar Conference on Signals, Systems, and Computers
abstract: Error correction schemes based on the Guessing Random Additive Noise Decoding (GRAND) framework have demonstrated strong performance in simulated environments, offering code-agnostic operation and near-optimal decoding across a wide range of codes. However, their performance under realworld wireless channel conditions has not yet been experimentally characterized. This work presents the first over-the-air evaluation of GRAND-based universal decoders, including hard-decision GRAND, soft-decision ORBGRAND, and correlation-aware ORBGRAND-AI. Block error rate (BLER) measurements collected using a software-defined radio testbed show gains of approximately 1 dB for GRAND, 4 dB for ORBGRAND, and an additional 0.5 dB improvement for ORBGRAND-AI relative to a CRC-only reference baseline. These results confirm the practical effectiveness of noise-centric universal decoding and demonstrate its potential for deployment in future energy-efficient wireless communication systems.

title: Beam Selection in MIMO-ISAC Systems: A Greedy Approach
authors: Subin Shin, Jeonghun Park
publish: 2025 59th Asilomar Conference on Signals, Systems, and Computers
abstract: In integrated sensing and communication (ISAC) systems, antenna (or beam) selection plays a vital role in reducing hardware cost, power consumption, and computational complexity, especially in large-scale multiple-input multiple-output (MIMO) deployments. However, designing an effective beam selection strategy is challenging due to the mismatch in performance metrics between communication and sensing—namely, mutual information (MI) versus beam-pattern mean squared error (MSE) or the Cramér-Rao lower bound (CRLB). To overcome this, we propose a low-complexity greedy beam selection framework maximizing a unified MI-based performance metric applicable to both functions. By decomposing the total MI into per-beam contributions, we introduce the greedy cofactor-based selection (GCS) algorithm, which iteratively selects the beam that provides the lowest contribution and eliminates it. Simulation results show that GCS achieves near-optimal performance with significantly lower complexity than exhaustive search, demonstrating its practical effectiveness for beamspace MIMO-ISAC systems.

title: High Resolution Analysis of Accident Risk in Nganjuk Using Spatio-Temporal Point Process on a Linear Network
authors: Ahmad Syarif Nur Wahid, Achmad Choiruddin, Yuri Aditya Rahmad Ramadhan, Sutikno
publish: 2025 IEEE Asia-Pacific Conference on Geoscience, Electronics and Remote Sensing Technology (AGERS)
abstract: The study examines traffic accident risks in Nganjuk Regency, Indonesia, performing a spatio-temporal point process on a linear network. Contrary to conventional models that presume accidents occur in a two-dimensional space, this approach incorporates the actual road network configuration and temporal dynamics of incidents. First-order intensity was estimated non-parametrically using a kernel-based approach to identify accident-prone segments and temporal fluctuations in risk over time. Spatial analysis revealed hotspots along arterial roads and around intersections, while temporal analysis indicated peaks during periods of high mobility, such as national holidays. Secondorder analysis employing the inhomogeneous K-function and pair correlation function (PCF) indicated significant clustering within 200 meters of accident locations and recurrence within approximately one month after the previous incident. The results emphasize the presence of heterogeneous and localized risk patterns across space and time, suggesting that safety interventions should be directed to the short road segments surrounding accident sites, approximately 200 meters from the crash point, and to the critical one-month post-accident period which represents a high-risk area and time. A range of pragmatic measures can be implemented, including the implementation of speed-calming strategies, the optimization of traffic signal timing at intersections, and the intensification of monitoring and enforcement. These measures are designed to ensure the effective and targeted management of road safety.

title: TradeSync: An AI-Powered Web Platform for Real-Time Stock Analysis and Prediction
authors: Kirtan Khare, Radha Gohil, Vandana Joshi
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Recent Trends in Electrical, Electronics and Computing Technologies (ICRTEECT)
abstract: The stock market plays a vital role in the global economy but remains highly volatile due to various factors such as investor sentiment, economic indicators, and geopolitical events. Recent advances in deep learning have enabled the modeling of complex, nonlinear, and temporal patterns in financial data, improving prediction accuracy. This paper introduces TradeSync, an intelligent, real-time stock analysis and prediction platform that integrates a hybrid CNN-LSTM deep learning model with real-time data from financial APIs. The system offers features such as stock prediction, trading bot, portfolio tracking, and market news aggregation, all accessible through a unified, user-friendly interface. Extensive experiments on both Indian and U.S. stocks demonstrate the model’s effectiveness, achieving high R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> scores and low MAE and RMSE values. TradeSync bridges the gap between academic research and real-world application by combining accurate predictive modeling with practical usability, making it a valuable tool for investors, analysts, and automated trading systems.

title: Enhanced Supervised Machine Learning Classification Framework for Intelligent Loan Approval Prediction in Banking Systems
authors: Neelesh Dandotiya, Kirti Jain, Prashant Kumar Shrivastava
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Smart Innovations in Systems, Infrastructure, Mechanical, Power, AI and Computing Technologies (SISIMPACT)
abstract: The exponential growth in loan applications necessitates intelligent automated systems for credit assessment in banking. This paper presents an Enhanced Supervised Machine Learning Classification Framework that integrates XGBoost, Gradient Boosting, and CatBoost algorithms for loan approval prediction. Our methodology employs comprehensive data preprocessing including SMOTE for dataset balancing and Min-Max normalization for feature scaling. Experimental evaluation on real-world loan datasets demonstrates superior performance with CatBoost achieving 88.16% accuracy, 88.37% precision, and 88.37 % F1-score, outperforming traditional baseline methods (Random Forest: 73%, Gaussian Naive Bayes: 80%, KNN: 73%). The proposed framework addresses critical challenges in automated lending decisions while maintaining computational efficiency suitable for real-time banking applications. Results indicate significant improvement in loan approval workflow optimization and default risk minimization.

title: Biometric based User Authentication using Behavioral Analytics through Mouse Tracking and Deep Learning
authors: Parthiban Krishnamoorty, Samithran Ramesh, Pranjay Seksaria
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Smart Innovations in Systems, Infrastructure, Mechanical, Power, AI and Computing Technologies (SISIMPACT)
abstract: Recently, there has been a major increasing prevalence in the number of authentication fraud attacks. These attacks have led to huge financial losses and widespread data leaks. The most widely used authentication methods are Password and Multi factor Authentication, which can be easily deciphered using Keylogging, Phishing and Credential stuffing. Hence, there has been a drive in need of a more robust authentication approach. Behavioral biometrics, specifically Mouse dynamics provides a strong, non-obstructive and hardware based unconstrained approach. In this research paper, we proposed a DL-based methodology applying ConvNeXt-Tiny to classify user behavior from the Balabit Mouse Dynamics Dataset. The mouse movement is transformed into visual representations, which are then processed using DL models to differentiate between legal and illegal users. Our Execution demonstrates that ConvNeXt-Tiny produces high accuracy, utilizing its optimized architecture for feature extraction. The proposed approach achieves superior robust authentication, making it a viable answer for real world problems. Additionally, we used the primary features and technique of the model using Gradient-weighted Class Activation Mapping [Grad-Cam].

title: A Sustainable and Cost-effective Approach for Real-Time Monitoring of Smart Plants in Indoor Environmental Conditions
authors: Chaitra S Kumar, Anubhav Kumar Pandey, Deekshitha S Nayak, Sumana S
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Recent Trends in Electrical, Electronics and Computing Technologies (ICRTEECT)
abstract: The proposed system describes the need to use smart plants to monitor the internal environment. Intelligent plants are inexpensive and easy ways to monitor your internal environment. These plants are integrated into sensors such as soil moisture, temperature, moisture, and CO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf> sensors to monitor the internal environment without the use of complex techniques. These systems monitor the amount of CO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf> absorbed from surrounding areas and distribute data to existing total CO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>. This is very important to maintain good air quality and positive energy in the environment. Intelligent plants can be equipped with sensors or linked to IoT (Internet of Things) networks to monitor and assess CO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf> concentrations in the air. These plants may have sensors embedded in leaves or pots so that air quality data can be transmitted to interconnected systems. It is also an economic and potential alternative to traditional air quality monitoring systems and hence is accessible to reach more people, particularly in urban areas. Furthermore, smart plants contribute to the development of green infrastructure, promoting healthier, greener living conditions and more familiarity with nature by promoting SDG 11.

title: Brinjal Leaf Disease Detection with Region-Based Adaptive Kuwahara Filter and Optimized ResNet-50 Model
authors: T. Anupama Thankam, Sivarajan S, S. Maheswari, S. Parthiban, A. T. R. Krishna Priya, D. Karthikeyan
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Emerging Trends in Computing and Communication (ETCOM)
abstract: Agricultural professionals frequently face a range of diseases that impact vegetables, fruits, cereals, and commercial crops. Brinjal leaves are susceptible to various diseases that significantly impact crop health. Diagnosing these diseases is labour-intensive, with early symptoms often only visible at microscopic levels, making accurate diagnosis challenging. The present paper uses a novel approach to detect and classify diseased brinjal leaves using a Black Widow Optimized (BWO) ResNet-50 technique. Initially, the input leaf images undergo preprocessing with an adaptive Kuwahara filter to reduce noise and enhance image quality. Following this, the watershed segmentation technique is employed to isolate the diseased regions of the leaves. Essential features such as texture, colour and structure are then extracted using the Discrete Fourier Transform (DFT), which facilitates effective classification. Python software is used for the simulation results which shows a better accuracy of 94% and the specificity value of 0.92. This outperforms traditional methods, underscoring the efficacy of the proposed approach in diagnosing leaf diseases accurately.

title: ATAPP: Architecture and Technology Aware Power Predictor for Unseen FPGAS
authors: Zhigang Wei, Aman Arora, Emily Shriver, Lizy K. John
publish: 2025 35th International Conference on Field-Programmable Logic and Applications (FPL)
abstract: Machine Learning has been successfully adopted to estimate power consumption for FPGA designs using features from the early stages of High-Level synthesis. However, existing ML-based power models work only for the same FPGA the model is trained on, and do not generalize well for unseen or futuristic FPGAs due to the lack of consideration of architectural and technology features, which are highly relevant to the power consumption. In addition, state-of-the-art analytical models take hours to synthesize and simulate hardware designs to perform accurate power analysis. It leads to a long turnaround time and inefficiency for researchers who want to explore the effects of the FPGA architecture on power. In order to tackle the problem, we proposed ATAPP, a novel Graph Neural Network-based power model where both design switching activities and architecture features are encoded to make power prediction. The features of the FPGA architecture are extracted with RapidWright at the tile level of the layout, and the embeddings are generated using positional encoding. The design features are composed of switching activities at the intermediate representation operator level and the data flow of the design in a graph. With the two representations, ATAPP is able to predict the average dynamic power with a new design overlay on an unseen FPGA. Our experiments show that ATAPP demonstrates an average error of 13.09 % with unseen FPGAs and designs, while the best prior produces more than 40 % error and XPE produces more than 200 %.

title: Simulation of Electric Field in Transmission Line in Complex Scenarios Based on Finite Difference Time Domain Method
authors: Wei Feng, Xiaochao Li, Xinglong Liu, Rui Li, Guohua Wei, Zhipeng Wang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Big Data and Data Mining (BDDM)
abstract: With the development of power systems toward larger capacity, longer transmission distances, and ultra-high voltages, the electromagnetic environment issues of transmission lines in complex scenarios have become increasingly prominent. To accurately evaluate the electric field distribution around transmission lines under such conditions, this paper establishes a three-dimensional integrated simulation model of the transmission lines, the ground, and the surrounding environment based on the Finite Difference Time Domain (FDTD) method, enabling time-domain electric field simulations for various typical complex scenarios. First, in a scenario with a uniform air medium and level ground, the alternating electric field around a 500 kV three-phase transmission line is numerically simulated to validate the rationality of the FDTD algorithm and parameter selection. Subsequently, the spatial distribution of the electric field is analyzed in more complex scenarios where transmission lines cross hills and valleys. The research demonstrates that the FDTD method can intuitively depict the propagation and distortion of electromagnetic waves in complex environments, providing valuable references for route planning, environmental impact assessment, and coordinated layout with other infrastructure under complex terrain conditions.

title: Paper Tit Revolutionizing Vehicle Manoeuvrability: A Comprehensive Study on Innovative Electronics-Enabled Four-Wheel Steering Systems For Zero Turning Radius
authors: B. Venkatesh, Y Suresh, J.Chinna Babu, P. Chandra Reddy, B. Hari Babu, Shaik Karimullah
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Recent Trends in Electrical, Electronics and Computing Technologies (ICRTEECT)
abstract: This article aimed to build and install a revolutionary steer-by-wire four-wheel steering (4WS) system to improve the turning capabilities of vehicles operating in extremely confined environments. Conventional steering systems usually function poorly in these environments, demanding innovative solutions. This study also uses numerical simulations, electronics and mechanical design, and control system theory to undertake a detailed evaluation of various four-wheel steering setups. A PID-based electronics control system was developed and tested in MATLAB to study the dynamic steering response to ensure system stability and responsiveness over a wide range of operating conditions. Also, this work provides the structural and electronics performance of the steering linkage information under applied stress. It shows a significant improvement in the turning radius and stability. The proposed technology offers a realistic solution for improving manoeuvrability, safety, and energy economy in various driving circumstances while aligning with current automobile and electronics trends.

title: Advances in Machine Learning and Deep Learning for Early Disease Detection in Healthcare
authors: Vijay Singh, Halima Sadia
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Recent Trends in Electrical, Electronics and Computing Technologies (ICRTEECT)
abstract: Artificial intelligence (AI) is reshaping the landscape of early disease detection by leveraging multimodal data, advanced deep learning architectures, and privacy-preserving frameworks. This review consolidates developments from 2023–2025, emphasizing foundation models, multimodal large language models (MLLMs), self-supervised learning (SSL), and federated learning (FL). Results across cardiovascular, oncological, neurodegenerative, infectious, and renal/metabolic domains demonstrate that foundation models such as MedSAM and MedCLIP reduce annotation costs by over 40%, while SSL-pretrained ECG classifiers improve arrhythmia detection accuracy by 12–15% compared to supervised baselines. MLLMs, including Med-PaLM and BioGPT-4, have achieved zero-shot diagnostic reasoning with consistency scores exceeding 80% on external validation datasets. FL deployments have enabled secure multi-hospital collaborations, improving cardiovascular risk prediction AUC by 0.07–0.10 over siloed models. Despite these advances, challenges remain in fairness, calibration, interpretability, and sustainability. Future directions highlight hybrid FL– foundation approaches, test-time adaptation, causal discovery, and digital twin integration as pathways toward globally scalable and equitable AI systems. This synthesis underscores that AI in early detection is moving from experimental prototypes to deployment-ready frameworks that balance accuracy, privacy, and clinical utility.

title: An AI – Based Approach For Diabetic Retinopathy Detection And Severity Classification Using GAN And CNN
authors: A. Pradeep Kumar, Anne Anuhya, Sai Pradeep Reddy, Kotte Venkat Sai, Avula Gopal
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Recent Trends in Electrical, Electronics and Computing Technologies (ICRTEECT)
abstract: Automated diabetic retinopathy (DR) detection, screening, and diagnosis is crucial for preventing vision impairment and assisting ophthalmologists in mass screening programs. One condition that complicates diabetic retinopathy is that it develops and causes permanent damage of the retina and in untreated cases, leads to permanent blindness. The disease shows changes mainly in the retina, such as microaneurysms, hemorrhages and neovascularization. Early diagnosis of DR is very important for timely intervention. Recent strides in the automated system show potential in minimizing subjective interpretation and relieving screening burdens off clinicians. The Hybrid GAN- CNN Approach for diabetic retinopathy diagnostics proposes a methodology built around Generative Adversarial Networks and Convolutional Neural Networks, enhance the standard of retinal images and precise classification of DR severity. The suggested method tackles class imbalance in DR diagnostics via training the GANs using the adaptive hyper parameter setting. This approach aims at providing better accuracy of benchmark retinal imaging datasets in hopes of releasing the model from previous forecasts’ constraints. It aims to help clinicians in identifying vascular changes by improving image clarity through GAN enhanced preprocessing.

title: Drone-based Estimation of Chlorophyll-a in Reservoirs: A Cubist-based Ensemble Model
authors: Soheil Ghasemnezhad, Mohammad Reza Nikoo, Mahmoud Mashal, Malik Al-Wardy, Navid Nasiri
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Unmanned Vehicle Systems-Oman (UVS)
abstract: Water quality monitoring is crucial for optimal reservoir operation and management. chlorophyll-a is a vital water quality parameter, as it plays a key role in the eutrophication of lakes and reservoirs. In this research, drone imagery and machine learning models were used to estimate chlorophyll-a concentrations in the Wadi Dayqah reservoir in Oman. Six bands from the drone’s camera were used to evaluate three vegetation indices and two band ratios. The minimum-Redundancy-Maximum-Relevance (mRMR) algorithm was utilized to identify the most influential features for chlorophyll-a estimation.
