Hence, we propose a Web Application for detecting the disease of the crops by uploading a real-time leaf(s) images which is computed using Convolutional Neural Network(CNN) with image processing and process the results such as diseases classification, symptoms, modern remedies available and how to use mechanisms will be educated on their own languages and displayed to the farmers which make them to give an optimized solutions, creates an organized pathway for the cultivators to develop their livelihood. On the experimental results, we obtained 97 percent test accuracy gives a valuable decision support tool. This line of approach helps in empowering the Farmers for their life called as "Agri Lifeline".".

title: AI-Driven System for Symptom-Based Disease Prediction and Review-Informed Medicine Recommendation
authors: Afsa Mailmuri, Manohar Madgi, Vishwanath P Baligar
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Emerging Trends in Computing and Communication (ETCOM)
abstract: The use of Artificial Intelligence (AI) in different fields is a major potential for improvement in healthcare, especially in diagnostic processes therapeutic suggestions, and pharmaceutical discovery and development. Overcoming the difficulties in receiving timely medical advice, avoiding inappropriate self-medication, and aiding healthcare workers in lowincome settings requires data-driven and creative solutions. This research presents a Novel AI-powered system aimed at predicting diseases based on user symptoms provided by users dynamically and recommending suitable medications, this is designed to enhance healthcare decision-making. The system starts by taking symptoms from the users to predict the possible disease using a Random forest classification model and label encoder, which has been trained on the Disease dataset, with disease labels managed by the Disease label encoder. After disease prediction, it also filters a comprehensive medicine based on the predicted disease and enriches these recommendations with metadata and sentiment analysis based on user reviews(Excellent, Average, and Poor review percentages). The system then uses a scoring mechanism based on reviews and sentiment to identify and recommend a curated list of the top 4 drugs from the filtered set, Providing the users with prioritized options and critical information like composition and manufacturer, empowering them to consider alternatives based on factors like composition allergies or personal preference. The Analysis showed that the model can predict diseases correctly with 100% accuracy. Further, the system correctly filters and ranks suitable drugs, thus providing an evidence-based model for Disease prediction and correct medicine recommendation. The system has the potential to be a decision support system for healthcare professionals, prevent dangerous self-medication, and act as a helpful preliminary tool in areas with control access to medical professionals, with a final outcome of better patient treatment.

title: Day-Ahead Market Clearing Model for HVDC-Interconnected Multi-Area Systems with Renewable Energy
authors: Junlei Liu, Feng Li, Xinmiao Liu, Chong Tang, Xun Lu, Yuanyuan Lou, Chu Jin
publish: 2025 3rd Power Electronics and Power System Conference (PEPSC)
abstract: This paper investigates the day-ahead market clearing problem for a multi-area power system interconnected via high-voltage direct current (HVDC) tielines. A unified bidding framework is proposed in which thermal units and wind farms jointly participate in the energy market. To capture the uncertainty of wind generation, multiple wind power scenarios are generated using Latin Hypercube Sampling and then reduced via a backward scenario reduction method. A security-constrained unit commitment (SCUC) model is developed for clearing thermal and wind bids across regions, followed by a security-constrained economic dispatch (SCED) model for dispatch refinement and locational marginal price (LMP) calculation. Case studies on a modified two-area 12-bus system demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed model in enabling wind integration, supporting inter-area coordination through HVDC links, and estimating the upper and lower bounds of nodal LMPs under uncertainty.

title: A Scalable MVDR Beamforming Algorithm that is Linear in the number of Antennas
authors: Sanjaya Herath, Armin Gerami, Kevin Wagner, Ramani Duraiswami, Christopher A. Metzler
publish: 2025 59th Asilomar Conference on Signals, Systems, and Computers
abstract: The Minimum Variance Distortionless Response (MVDR) beamforming technique is widely applied in array systems to mitigate interference. However, applying MVDR to large arrays is computationally challenging; its computational complexity scales cubically with the number of antenna elements. In this paper, we introduce a scalable MVDR beamforming method tailored for massive arrays. Our approach, which is specific to scenarios where the signal of interest is below the noise floor (e.g., GPS), leverages the Sherman-Morrison formula, low-rank Singular Value Decomposition (SVD) approximations, and algebraic manipulation. Using our approach, we reduce the computational complexity from cubic to linear in the number of antennas. We evaluate the proposed method through simulations, comparing its computational efficiency and beamforming accuracy with the conventional MVDR approach. Our method significantly reduces the computational load while maintaining high beamforming accuracy for large-scale arrays. This solution holds promise for real-time applications of MVDR beamforming in fields like radar, sonar, and wireless communications, where massive antenna arrays are proliferating.

title: Decision-Theoretic Learning of Human Perception in Uncertain Environments
authors: Mohammad Alali, Tian Lan, Seyede Fatemeh Ghoreishi, Mahdi Imani
publish: 2025 59th Asilomar Conference on Signals, Systems, and Computers
abstract: Understanding how humans perceive and act in partially known environments is crucial for developing collaborative and assistive systems. In this paper, we introduce a data-driven framework to infer latent perceptual models of humans by representing their movement behavior as a Partially Observable Markov Decision Process (POMDP). We model each possible environment configuration using a discrete set of latent variables and compute the posterior distribution over these configurations based on observed human trajectories. Our approach decomposes the observation likelihood into two components: (I) a prediction model, which is the likelihood of observations given past actions and states, (II) a behavioral model, which is the likelihood of actions given past observations. We model (I) using LSTM networks trained to predict future observations, and we model (II) using a Boltzmann policy based on Q-values learned with Deep Recurrent Q-Networks (DRQN). This allows us to incrementally update our posterior beliefs about the environment in a fully data-driven manner. We validate our method in a simulated rescue maze environment with partially observable and uncertain regions. Extensive experiments show that our framework significantly outperforms the baselines. Our results demonstrate that jointly modeling action and perception yields a powerful tool for understanding human decision-making under uncertainty.

title: Prediction of Cybersickness Symptoms in Virtual Reality Using Prototypical Networks
authors: Anjan Kumar Dev, John Quarles, Maryam Tabar
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and eXtended and Virtual Reality (AIxVR)
abstract: Cybersickness could negatively impact user comfort, immersion and the long-term adoption of virtual reality technologies. Past literature suggests the benefits of accurate cybersickness prediction for early interventions; however, the labeled data scarcity poses significant challenges to the development of an accurate predictive machine learning approach in this problem domain. In particular, traditional fully-supervised methods require large labeled datasets, which are expensive and timeconsuming to collect. This, in turn, could have significant adverse effects on the effectiveness of such fully-supervised approaches in this application domain. Accordingly, this study investigates a few-shot learning approach based on prototypical networks to predict cybersickness symptoms (introduced in the Simulator Sickness Questionnaire) under the label scarcity challenge, and compares its performance with a fully-supervised single-task learning approach. Our empirical evaluation shows that, on average, the best-performing Prototypical Network achieves a mean AUC of 0.54 across all symptoms, compared to 0.45 for the singletask baseline. In particular, on several symptoms, the Prototypical Network outperforms the baseline by more than 0.2 in terms of AUC. However, some exceptions have also been observed with respect to the superiority of Prototypical Networks to the baseline on certain symptoms. Therefore, these results suggest that fewshot Prototypical Networks deliver average improvements in cybersickness symptom prediction while highlighting a need for further research to consistently achieve a high accuracy across all symptoms.

title: Prompt Fairness: Sub-group Disparities in LLMs
authors: Meiyu Zhong, Noel Teku, Ravi Tandon
publish: 2025 59th Asilomar Conference on Signals, Systems, and Computers
abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs), though shown to be effective in many applications, can vary significantly in their response quality. In this paper, we investigate this problem of prompt fairness: specifically, the phrasing of a prompt by different users/styles, despite the same question being asked in principle, may elicit different responses from an LLM. To quantify this disparity, we propose to use information-theoretic metrics that can capture two dimensions of bias: subgroup sensitivity—the variability of responses within a subgroup—and cross-group consistency—the variability of responses across subgroups. Our analysis reveals that certain subgroups exhibit both higher internal variability and greater divergence from others.Our empirical analysis reveals that certain demographic subgroups experience both higher internal variability and greater divergence from others, indicating structural inequities in model behavior. To mitigate these disparities, we propose practical interventions, including majority voting across multiple generations and prompt neutralization, which together improve response stability and enhance fairness across user populations. In the experiments, we observe clear prompt-sensitivity disparities across demographic subgroups: before mitigation, cross-group divergence values reach 0.28 and typically fall in the 0.14–0.22 range. After applying our neutralization and multi-generation strategy, these divergences consistently decrease, with the largest gap reduced to 0.22 and many distances falling to 0.17 or below, indicating more stable and consistent outputs across subgroups.

title: Optimized Sensor Scheduling in Wireless Sensor Networks Using ML-Powered Predictive Models
authors: P.S. Joan Kingsly, Chandan Chavadi, N. Kalidas, Manish Nagpal, Rajeev Sharma, G.Sabeena Gnanaselvi
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Smart Innovations in Systems, Infrastructure, Mechanical, Power, AI and Computing Technologies (SISIMPACT)
abstract: In this paper, a new structure of optimized sensor scheduling within the Wireless Sensor Networks (WSNs) using predictive modeling that is enabled by machine learning will be introduced. Compared to other traditional duty-cycling or fixed scheduling, the suggested model integrates the study of spatio-temporal data correlation and self-adaptive learning, in the prediction of the sensing requirement. The system dynamically identifies the best set of active sensors to set to avoid redundant measurements and cover 100 percent of the environment and/or network traffic patterns by forecasting environment behavior and network traffic patterns. This predictive model can show the mixture of temporal sequence learning and anomaly - sensorial tuning to adapt to the changes in sensing in real time. Moreover, a multi-objective cost function is used to mix energy consumption, latency, and sensing accuracy so that it can be deployed in on a scalable basis in heterogeneous WSNs. The given proactive, intelligence-facilitated scheduling solution has the benefit of extending network lifetime, minimizing communication overhead, and increasing reactiveness of decision-making, thus well-suited to mission-critical and resource-constrained IoT application.

title: ECO-WOA: An Energy and Cost Optimized Whale Optimization Algorithm for Wireless Sensor Networks
authors: Victor Das, Ira Nath, Avishek Banerjee
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Smart Innovations in Systems, Infrastructure, Mechanical, Power, AI and Computing Technologies (SISIMPACT)
abstract: Energy efficiency and cost reduction are critical challenges in the design and operation of wireless sensor networks (WSNs). This paper proposes ECO-WOA, an enhanced Whale Optimization Algorithm tailored for simultaneous minimization of energy consumption and operational cost in WSNs. By integrating a balanced exploration-exploitation strategy with a localized search mechanism, ECO-WOA effectively navigates the complex optimization landscape to identify optimal sensor configurations. Experimental evaluations demonstrate that ECO-WOA significantly outperforms baseline approaches in reducing total energy expenditure and deployment costs while maintaining network performance. The algorithm's convergence behavior and solution quality are validated through extensive simulations, showcasing its potential as a robust and computationally efficient optimization tool for resource-constrained wireless systems. Future extensions of ECO-WOA may incorporate additional constraints and objectives, further enhancing its applicability in practical WSN deployments.

title: Review on POS Tagging in Hinglish Code-Mixed – Rule Based and Statistical Sequence Models
authors: Surinder Pal Singh, Vinod Kumar, Prabhjot Kaur, Seema Verma, Gopal Ghosh, Vishal Gupta
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Smart Innovations in Systems, Infrastructure, Mechanical, Power, AI and Computing Technologies (SISIMPACT)
abstract: The hybrid language that combines Hindi and English Hinglish is becoming more common on the social networks and poses a lot of difficulties to Part-of-Speech (POS) tagging. The non-formal character of the language, irregularity of syntax and constant interweaving between Hindi and English offer significant challenges to traditional tagging methods. The paper will compare in-depth both established rule-based and statistical models as HMM, CRF, SVM, and MaxEnt with state-of-the-art neural network architectures, among them BiLSTM, BiLSTM-CRF, CNN-augmented and GRU-based attention networks. We can compare the performance of these models to the actual code-mixed datasets and evaluate them in terms of accuracy, precision, recall and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{F 1}$</tex>-score. These findings show that conventional methods have the advantage of the feature crafting care, but it is the neural models with the exception of BiLSTM-CRF and CNN-BiLSTM that prove more robust in the wake of noisy, code-mixed input. Those results indicate the significance of detailed contextual modeling and character-level representations in improving the performance of POS tagging systems with regard to challenging, multilingual social media text.

title: Agentic Retrieval-Augmented Generation for On-Premise Infrastructure: Enhancing Security and Performance for Enterprise Knowledge Management
authors: Aarya Sawant, Kahan Bhatt, Shantil Khadatkar, Masooda Modak
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Emerging Trends in Computing and Communication (ETCOM)
abstract: In the digital age, enterprises deal with copious amounts of data. A large amount of this data is unstructured, which poses big challenges when it comes to knowledge management. The primary problems associated with the usage of cloud based Large Language Models (LLMs), are the security and privacy risks [1], which are a major barrier to its adoption in regulated industries [2]. This paper presents a complete blueprint for an agentic Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG) system this is designed for secure, on-premise enterprise knowledge management. The architecture that has been proposed by us, addresses the possible data sovereignty concerns, by ensuring that all of the data processing remains confined within local infrastructure. The system's methodology is divided into two core components. That is a document processing framework, which uses open source tools to ingest, chunk, and embed the various internal document types into a local ChromaDB vector store. Along with it, is an innovative multiagent RAG pipeline, which is orchestrated by an intelligent Agent Orchestrator. The Orchestrator is used to dynamically handle complex queries. This agentic approach allows the system to carry out advanced capabilities like multistep planning, tool use, and a Validate & Refine loop. This not only enhances accuracy, but also its reliability beyond the traditional single pass RAG systems. The system employs open-source as well as lightweight LLMs that are deployed on-premises such as Phi-4-Mini, Phi-3-Mini, and LLaMA 3.2. Such LLMs provide a compromise between good quality performance and cost-effective computation that is functional in the consumer level hardware and in the high end enterprise hardware. The system performance is quantitatively validated with the help of the RAGAS framework [3] that quantifies in a variety of metrics such as relevance of the answer, faithfulness and precision of context as a means of ensuring that there is factual correctness and that the output is reliable. This proves that effective and robust enterprise knowledge management is an effective and secure solution that can be achieved with the help of on-premise agentic RAG architecture. Which mitigates the effect of potential privacy risks, but also opens up the capacity of enterprises to a significant degree in terms of internal functions.

title: Autonomous AI Agents for Offensive Security: Redefining Penetration Testing and Remediation
authors: Suryaprakash Nalluri, Kaza Goutham Kumar, Nadiya Sri Nadikudhiti, Hemalatha Kandagiri
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Recent Trends in Electrical, Electronics and Computing Technologies (ICRTEECT)
abstract: The AI-Driven Paradigm Shift in Offensive Security. The integration of Artificial Intelligence (AI) into cybersecurity operations marks a fundamental shift, particularly in the domain of offensive security and penetration testing. Autonomous AI agents, powered by advanced machine learning and Large Language Models (LLMs), are now capable of executing the full spectrum of offensive operations: from reconnaissance and vulnerability enumeration to exploitation and post-exploitation. These agents operate with minimal human oversight, enabling rapid, adaptive, and context-aware decision-making across dynamically shifting environments.Such a transformation speeds the penetrating testing process that previously required weeks and now takes only hours but is vastly more in-depth and thorough as well. Since ephemeral workloads (such as on the cloud, or as reached by CI/CD pipelines), and distributed attack surfaces are multiplying, the resiliency and speed of AI-based systems will be essential. Even human roles will change accordingly, shifting the operating KPIs of ensuring correct manual execution to strategic decision-making to risk, ethics, and action, such as the Mean Time to Remediate (MTTR) and to Respond (MTTR).In this age of an average data breach cost of more than 4.45 million dollars, AI is shifting not only to a strategic advantage but also to a minimum requirement. Companies which do not automate AI in offensive security may be left surpassed by opponents who already have access to automated power. The ones that survive will restructure the future of thunderous, adaptive cyber defense.

title: Design of Slotted Trapezoidal Patch Antenna for Wideband Applications
authors: Radha Sharma, Rajesh Kumar Vishwakarma, Shweta Srivastava
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Smart Innovations in Systems, Infrastructure, Mechanical, Power, AI and Computing Technologies (SISIMPACT)
abstract: An apple-shaped slotted trapezoidal patch microstrip antenna featuring a defected ground and an apple-shaped metallic element is proposed for X-band applications. The antenna structure is designed in three steps, utilizing a low-cost FR-4 substrate with a height of 1.6 mm. Firstly, an inset fed trapezoidal-shaped patch resonator is designed for the 5.3 GHz band, with its bandwidth enhanced by introducing a defected ground. In the second step, an apple-shaped slot is cut in the middle of the trapezoidal patch antenna with a defected ground to extend the current path. Finally, in the third step, the variation of the apple-shaped metallic element from the center of the feed line is implemented, allowing for an increase in impedance bandwidth while reducing the antenna's size and space occupancy. Simulation, physical fabrication, and device testing were conducted to demonstrate the feasibility of the proposed antenna.

title: Spectral Distortions of Signals Induced by Nonlinear Sensor Motion: Acceleration, Jolt, and Relativistic Effects
authors: Bryce M. Barclay, Alex Mahalov
publish: 2025 59th Asilomar Conference on Signals, Systems, and Computers
abstract: The frontier of electromagnetic applications in the aerial environment includes communication with and detection of a variety of high-speed highly-maneuverable objects such as low-earth-orbit satellites, drones, supersonic and hypersonic vehicles. In this work, we assess the impact of the high speed, acceleration, and jolt of such objects on the spectrum of signals observed by them. Observer acceleration and jolt induce dynamic Doppler effects manifested in spectral broadening, nonlinear chirps and Airy-type oscillations in the amplitude spectrum. Additionally, the 3-D geometry of the observer path produces nonlinear phase distortions in the signal. Our assessment incorporates the effects of relativity, noise, and movement in 3-D space. We compare signal chirping induced by acceleration in both classical and relativistic frameworks.

title: Person Pose Estimation with WIFI and Eultimodal Feature Fusion
authors: Yuanzhi Ye, Junyuan Zheng, Yi Luo, Qiang Han
publish: 2025 International Conference on Big Data and Data Mining (BDDM)
abstract: To address the challenges in human pose estimation based on channel state information (CSI), such as insufficient features, underutilized data potential, and inaccurate key point localization in sequential frames, this paper proposes a novel solution: First, CSI is collected in Monitor mode, which captures 90 more subcarriers than AP mode, providing richer information for feature extraction; second, amplitude (reflecting the spatial distribution of signal strength) and phase (indicating multipath propagation characteristics) information are fused to construct amplitude representations of CSI signals, compensating for the limitations of using only one type of information; third, a neural network model is designed that equal-length CSI frames via a sliding window, builds a denoising feature extraction network based on phase and fused amplitude, and employs gated recurrent units (GRUs) to capture inter-frame temporal features, generating CSI feature matrices that are converted into intermediate feature maps. In addition, a multi-constraint convolutional neural network module is used to restrict the range of key point generation, establishing the correlation between CSI data variations and human key points. Experiments demonstrate that this method effectively improves the accuracy and robustness of key point localization.

title: AI Mobile Health Applications: Real-Time Monitoring and Support
authors: Tharun Anand Reddy Sure, Akshay Mittal
publish: 2025 International Conference on Innovation and Intelligence for Informatics, Computing, and Technologies (3ICT)
abstract: Mobile health (mHealth) applications are increasingly incorporating artificial intelligence (AI) to enhance healthcare delivery on smartphones and tablets. This comprehensive survey examines AI-enabled mHealth across four domains: personalized health monitoring, diagnostic decision support, mental health coaching, and clinical triage. This research demonstrates that AI achieves significant improvements in predictive analytics accuracy (15–30% over traditional methods), personalized intervention effectiveness, and healthcare accessibility in underserved regions. Four detailed case studies reveal concrete impacts: an insulin dose calculator achieving 90% food recognition accuracy, skin cancer detection matching dermatologist-level performance (87% agreement), cough analysis tools detecting pediatric pneumonia with 87% accuracy, and AI triage chatbots providing clinically appropriate recommendations comparable to human physicians. Technical analysis contrasts on-device frameworks (TensorFlow Lite, Core ML) with cloud-based models, highlighting privacy–performance trade-offs. Critical challenges persist: 78% of users report privacy concerns, 23% of diagnostic systems exhibit algorithmic bias, and 68% app abandonment rates within 30 days, alongside evolving regulatory frameworks. This research identifies emerging solutions including federated learning, TinyML for edge computing, explainable AI for clinical trust, and large language model–powered conversational agents that address current limitations and enable next-generation intelligent mHealth systems.

title: Automated Rorschach Test for Personality and Mental Health
authors: Akshay Chaudhary, Avaneesh Iyer, Nishanth Kata, Kumravell Natarajan, Arathi Boyanapalli
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Recent Trends in Electrical, Electronics and Computing Technologies (ICRTEECT)
abstract: The Rorschach inkblot test has long been used in psychological assessments to examine how individuals interpret ambiguous stimuli, offering insights into their cognitive and emotional functioning. Traditionally, this test is administered by clinicians in a controlled environment, with responses evaluated subjectively based on the clinician's expertise. However, manual administration and analysis can be time-consuming, costly, and subject to variability between practitioners. Additionally, the traditional method limits scalability, accessibility, and the ability to track progress over time efficiently. This paper presents the design and development of an online Rorschach inkblot test model to address these limitations. The proposed system automates the delivery of inkblot stimuli, allowing users to take the test remotely at their convenience. The application uses NLP and machine learning to analyze responses, offering standardized interpretations and reducing subjectivity. Additionally, the model is designed to help reveal key insights about a participant's state of mind, identifying traits such as whether an individual may be experiencing a depressed state, or if they possess a healthy psychological condition. It also helps uncover deeper personality traits, providing valuable information for both clinical assessments and self-reflection. The platform enhances accessibility by offering a user-friendly interface that allows clinicians and researchers to reach larger and more diverse populations. The online model can also be integrated with tracking features, enabling long-term analysis of users' psychological profiles. Data privacy and security are prioritized, ensuring that all participant information remains confidential and compliant with ethical standards. This modernized approach increases efficiency, scalability, and consistency in administering the test, broadens its application, enabling the detection of mental health conditions and personality traits.

title: Towards Instruction-Controlled In-Pipeline GEMM Acceleration in a Dual-Issue RISC-V Core for Edge Applications
authors: Ahmad Othman, Darmen Ilyas, Ahmed Kamaleldin, Diana Göhringer
publish: 2025 35th International Conference on Field-Programmable Logic and Applications (FPL)
abstract: GEMM (General Matrix Multiplication) is a fundamental operation in deep learning (DL), serving as the key computing kernel for neural network layers, such as convolutional and fully connected layers. As the deployment of DL models expands beyond high-performance computing to resource-constrained embedded systems, there is a growing demand for efficient GEMM implementations that can meet strict power budgets and real-time processing requirements. This paper introduces a dual-issue in-order RISC-V core with an inpipeline GEMM accelerator based on the VeeR EH1 core [13], eliminating control signals and data transfer overhead inherent to coprocessors and standalone accelerators. By integrating GEMM execution directly into the pipeline, the design leverages core utilization and enables compiler-time preloading of operands, streamlining memory access and reducing dynamic branching, thereby minimizing branch mispredictions. Further, pipelined load/store operations mitigate memory access stalls and cache misses, thereby enhancing the pipeline throughput. Speedup, measured using Verilator simulation, demonstrates an average of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\sim 5 \times$</tex> over the reference VeeR EH1 baseline core. By integrating GEMM acceleration within the pipeline, this work bridges the gap between domain-specific accelerators and general-purpose cores, delivering scalable computing performance.

title: Dataset and UAV Propagation Channel Modeling for LoRa in the 860 MHz ISM Band
authors: Joachim Tapparel, Andreas Burg
publish: 2025 59th Asilomar Conference on Signals, Systems, and Computers
abstract: LoRa is one of the most widely used low-power wide- area network technology for the Internet of Things. To achieve long-range communication with low power consumption at a low cost, LoRa uses a chirp spread spectrum modulation and transmits in the sub-GHz unlicensed industrial, scientific, and medical (ISM) frequency bands. Due to the rapid densification of IoT networks, it is crucial to obtain tailored channel models to evaluate the performance of LoRa networks. While channel models for cellular technologies have been investigated extensively, specific characteristics of LoRa transmissions operating at long range with a rather small (~ 250 kHz) bandwidth require dedicated measurement campaigns and modeling efforts. In this work, we leverage an SDR-based testbed to gather and publish a dataset of LoRa frames transmitted in a campus environment. The dataset includes IQ samples of the received frames at multiple locations and allows for the evaluation of channel variations with high time resolution. Using the gathered data, we derive empirical propagation channel models for LoRa that include receiver correlation over distance for three scenarios: unmanned aerial vehicle (UAV) line-of-sight (LoS), UAV non-LoS, and pedestrian non-LoS. Furthermore, the dataset is annotated with synchronization information, enabling the evaluation of receiver algorithms using experimental data.

title: Task-driven Heterophilic Graph Structure Learning
authors: Ayushman Raghuvanshi, Gonzalo Mateos, Sundeep Prabhakar Chepuri
publish: 2025 59th Asilomar Conference on Signals, Systems, and Computers
abstract: Graph neural networks (GNNs) often struggle to learn discriminative node representations for heterophilic graphs, where connected nodes tend to have dissimilar labels and feature similarity provides weak structural cues. We propose frequency-guided graph structure learning (FgGSL), an end-to-end graph inference framework that jointly learns homophilic and heterophilic graph structures along with a spectral encoder. FgGSL employs a learnable, symmetric, feature-driven masking function to infer said complementary graphs, which are processed using pre-designed low- and high-pass graph filter banks. A label-based structural loss explicitly promotes the recovery of homophilic and heterophilic edges, enabling task-driven graph structure learning. We derive stability bounds for the structural loss and establish robustness guarantees for the filter banks under graph perturbations. Experiments on six heterophilic benchmarks demonstrate that FgGSL consistently outperforms state-of-the-art GNNs and graph rewiring methods, highlighting the benefits of combining frequency information with supervised topology inference.

title: Automated Detection of Abnormal Fungal Infections in Spinach Leaves Using Image Processing and Machine Learning
authors: D. Ferlin Deva Shahila, Nalini. N, Valantina Stephen, D. Lilly Evangelin, Berakhah. F. Stanley, Yeswanth Hanumanthu
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Recent Trends in Electrical, Electronics and Computing Technologies (ICRTEECT)
abstract: Fungal diseases in spinach crops lead to substantial losses in yield and crop quality which threatens food security and sustainable agriculture. This paper proposes an automated system that can detect and classify abnormal fungal infections within spinach leaves at an early stage. Using image processing and machine learning techniques, the model can detect, diagnose, and classify six conditions which are; Anthracnose, Cercospora Leaf Spot, Cladosporium Leaf Spots, Downy Mildew, Stemphylium Leaf Spot, and Healthy leaves by considering a large variety of hand-crafted and learned deep features. The approach will incorporate Explainable AI (XAI) approaches such as Grad-CAM and LIME, visualizing the important regions that contribute to the model's decisions to improve interpretability. Different features will be extracted from the leaf images that are texture, shape, morphology, statistical, and deep features, then the classifiers will be trained against Random Forest, SVM and deep CNNs. The proposed model also yields promising results across evaluation metrics such as accuracy, F1-score, recall, and loss, demonstrating the potential for scalable, trustable disease diagnosis in smart farming contexts.

title: Design of Low-Power High-Performance Digital Circuits Based on Carbon Nanotube Technology
authors: Imran Ullah Khan, Yusra Siddiqui, Nupur Mittal
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Recent Trends in Electrical, Electronics and Computing Technologies (ICRTEECT)
abstract: As the need for faster and more energy-efficient electronic devices continues to grow, this study takes a dive into cutting-edge approaches for designing low-power, high-speed digital circuits using carbon nanotube technology. The growing demand for faster and more energy-efficient digital systems is pushing the boundaries of traditional design approaches and materials. Among the most promising alternatives, carbon nanotubes stand out for their extraordinary electrical characteristics, offering a convincing path toward low-power, high-speed digital circuit design. This research investigates into the design and realization of digital circuits built on carbon nanotube field-effect transistors (CNTFETs), achieving notable gains in both speed and energy efficiency. Through the development of innovative circuit architectures and targeted optimization techniques, we demonstrate how CNTFETs can be harnessed for superior performance with significantly reduced power consumption. Experimental results authenticate this direction, highlighting substantial decreases in energy dissipation without compromising speed. This study further undertakes a thorough evaluation of the reliability and scalability of CNTFET-based digital circuits, underlining their strong potential as a foundation for next-generation semiconductor technologies. By capitalizing on the exceptional electrical characteristics of carbon nanotubes, this work marks a significant stride in advancing low-power, high-speed digital design. A thorough comparative analysis is also presented, measuring a range of emerging technologies composed to replace conventional CMOS. The findings reinforce the superior behavior of CNTFETs over traditional MOSFETs, chiefly in terms of transfer and drain characteristics. Notably, the research introduces a 32×32 SRAM array implemented with CNTFETs, incorporating row and column decoders, SRAM cells, sense amplifiers, and write driver circuits. This architecture delivers considerable performance improvements, achieving reductions of 98.41% in write delay, 97.15% in read delay, and 62% in total power dissipation when compared to CMOS-based counterparts. These results decisively establish CNTFET-based memory arrays as a compelling and efficient alternative to traditional CMOS technologies.

title: Resilient Channel Charting Under Varying Radio Link Availability
authors: Jonas Pirkl, Jonathan Ott, Maximilian Stahlke, George Yammine, Tobias Feigl, Christopher Mutschler
publish: 2025 59th Asilomar Conference on Signals, Systems, and Computers
abstract: Channel charting (CC) has become a key technology for RF-based localization, enabling unsupervised radio fingerprinting, even in non line of sight scenarios, with a minimum of reference position labels. However, most CC models assume fixed-size inputs, such as a constant number of antennas or channel measurements. In practical systems, antennas may fail, signals may be blocked, or antenna sets may change during handovers, making fixed-input architectures fragile. Existing radio-fingerprinting approaches address this by training separate models for each antenna configuration, but the resulting training effort scales prohibitively with the array size.We propose Adaptive Positioning (AdaPos), a CC architecture that natively handles variable numbers of channel measurements. AdaPos combines convolutional feature extraction with a transformer-based encoder using learnable antenna identifiers and self-attention to fuse arbitrary subsets of CSI inputs. Experiments on two public real-world datasets (SISO and MIMO) show that AdaPos maintains state-of-the-art accuracy under missing-antenna conditions and replaces roughly 57 configuration-specific models with a single unified model. With AdaPos and our novel training strategies, we provide resilience to both individual antenna failures and full-array outages.

title: Spectrum Occupancy Assessment of the UHF TV Band in the University of the Philippines Los Baños
authors: Annie Liza C. Pintor, Gerald P. Arada
publish: 2025 International Conference on Innovation and Intelligence for Informatics, Computing, and Technologies (3ICT)
abstract: As a solution to spectrum scarcity, TV white space is being explored since the very high frequency (VHF) and the ultra high frequency (UHF) TV Broadcast Bands remain underutilized in many regions. The first step towards the utilization of TV white space is identification of available frequencies or channels that could be deployed for different applications. This study investigates TV white space in six locations in the University of the Philippines Los Baños using a handheld spectrum analyzer connected to a rubber duck UHF antenna and a laptop containing the spectrum analyzer software to identify the noise threshold. Determination of the noise threshold in each location is based on the 80% method suggested by Recommendation ITU-R SM 1753. The highest and lowest noise threshold values are −84.5 dBm and −100.9 dBm. Parameters evaluated from the noise threshold include total occupied channels (TOC), total available channels (TAC), and spectrum resource occupancy (SRO). Results show that in all locations, the UHF TV band is underutilized, with a maximum and minimum SRO of 39.47% and 23.68% respectively.

title: Real-Time Causal Pattern Discovery in Structure Fires: A Cloud-Based AI Framework for Smart Emergency Response
authors: Swarnamouli Majumdar, Lorant Andras Szolga, Abhishek Bhardwaj, Sélim Gawad
publish: 2025 International Conference on Innovation and Intelligence for Informatics, Computing, and Technologies (3ICT)
abstract: Structure fires pose significant risks to human life and infrastructure, yet real-time analytical capabilities for fire incident data remain limited. This paper presents a novel cloudbased artificial intelligence framework that enables interpretable, data-driven pattern discovery from large-scale fire incident records in near real time. Unlike prior studies that focus primarily on predictive accuracy or post-event analytics, this work emphasizes causal understanding and operational integration. The framework combines unsupervised clustering and association rule mining with a scalable Amazon Web Services (AWS)native deployment. Measurable contributions include identifying geotemporal clusters of structure-fire causes across United States regions, quantifying rule-based relationships between ignition sources and contributing factors, and demonstrating a reduction in data-to-decision latency through automated daily dashboard updates. Results from the 2024 U.S. Structure Fire Incident dataset show that the system effectively transforms raw National Fire Incident Reporting System (NFIRS) records into proactive, explainable insights-offering a benchmark for real-time, AIenabled fire intelligence in smart cities.

title: Quantum Machine Learning for Smart Grid Load Balancing
authors: Guan Hong, A Fong
publish: 2025 3rd Power Electronics and Power System Conference (PEPSC)
abstract: The modernization of power grids, transitioning into smart grids, demands innovative technologies to manage and optimize energy distribution effectively. As power systems continue to evolve into smart grids with distributed and dynamic architectures, efficient load balancing becomes critical. Classical methods often prove inadequate in handling the complexity and variability of smart grid data. Increasing popularity of renewable energy sources adds another layer of complexity. The integration of renewable energy sources and increasing demand volatility pose significant challenges for smart grid load balancing. Traditional machine learning (ML) methods, while promising, face limitations in real-time optimization and scalability. Quantum Machine Learning (QML), which leverages quantum computing principles like superposition and entanglement, offers a promising frontier for optimizing energy distribution and load balancing by enhancing established ML algorithms. This paper surveys and compares current research on the application of QML to smart grid load balancing, exploring quantum neural networks (QNNs), quantum support vector machines (QSVM), as well as quantum optimization algorithms like Quantum Approximate Optimization Algorithm (QAOA), to improve various aspects of load-balancing efficiency in smart grids. By encoding classical grid data into quantum states, QML models exploit superposition and entanglement to process high-dimensional datasets faster than classical methods.

title: Developing and Evaluating a Miniaturized Augmented Reality Navigation Study Quality Instrument (MARNSQI) for Image-Guided Surgery: A Work-in-Progress Protocol
authors: Ziyu Qi, Christopher Nimsky, Miriam H. A. Bopp
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and eXtended and Virtual Reality (AIxVR)
abstract: Miniaturized augmented reality (AR) surgical navigation—i.e., cost-sensitive intraoperative guidance using projectors, smartphones/tablets, or head-mounted displays—has grown rapidly, yet research quality is assessed inconsistently and the drivers of study quality remain unclear. This work-in-progress protocol presents the Miniaturized Augmented Reality Navigation Study Quality Instrument (MARNSQI), a structured, risk-of-bias-style checklist designed to appraise the internal validity of AR surgical navigation studies across the full translational spectrum. MARNSQI will be developed and iteratively refined from high-quality evidence and expert input (Phase 1), psychometrically evaluated in a cross-sectional sample of 200 MARN studies published in 2020-2021 across 40 peer-reviewed journals (Phase 2; internal consistency, inter-/intra-rater reliability, and construct/criterion validity), and finalized via modified Delphi consensus (Phase 3). The expected outcome is a validated and reusable instrument that enables more comparable quality appraisal and supports more rigorous evidence generation for costsensitive AR surgical navigation.

title: An Integrated Augmented Reality Support System for Evacuation and RescueOperations in Disaster Scenarios
authors: Haruki Kata
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and eXtended and Virtual Reality (AIxVR)
abstract: We propose a system that realizes both the intuitive grasp of evacuation direction by evacuees and the efficiency of rescue operations during a disaster. The system integrates location, map, building disaster data to identify surrounding hazards predict the locations of individuals requiring real time. Evacuation support encourages rapid action by providing warnings through an AR function and audible alerts, along with displaying shelter locations, based on the determination results. Rescue operation support assists rapid decision-making by presenting the predicted locations of victims through an AR function, enabling the prioritization of search efforts within the limited time immediately following the disaster.

title: Mapping the Landscape of LLM Deployment in the Wild: Prevalence, Patterns, and Perils
authors: Xinyi Hou, Jiahao Han, Yanjie Zhao, Shenao Wang, Haoyu Wang
publish: Proceedings of the ACM on Measurement and Analysis of Computing Systems
abstract: None

title: Metasurface Absorbers Revisited: Evaluation, Design, Fabrication, and Validation
authors: Xiaojing Lv, Shengjian Jammy Chen, Fatemeh Babaeian, Zhen Luo, Yang Yang
abstract: Electromagnetic (EM) absorption fundamentally represents a process of EM-to-thermal energy conversion. Specifically, dielectric loss occurs when bound charges in a polarizable medium lag behind the alternating electric field, leading to energy dissipation through polarization relaxation; magnetic loss results from domain wall motion or spin relaxation within ferrites and magnetic composites; and ohmic loss stems from conduction in resistive media, where surface currents generate Joule heating. Since materials with intrinsically high absorption efficiency are exceedingly scarce in nature and often expensive to synthesize, structural engineering has become as a cost-effective alternative to achieve describable absorption performance. In particular, the metasurface absorber epitomizes the culmination of this design philosophy: its meta-atoms possess high absorption potential owing to their topological versatility, while the subwavelength periodicity ensures equivalent homogenization across the operational frequency spectrum.

title: Co-design a Dual-Polarized Filtering Metasurface Antennas and Arrays via Characteristic Mode Analysis
authors: Sihao Liu, Jiang Tongxun, Junlei Guo, Deqiang Yang, Yanhui Liu, Zaiping Nie, Yongpin Chen
publish: IEEE Antennas and Wireless Propagation Letters
abstract: A novel method to co-design filtering metasurface antennas is proposed via characteristic mode analysis in this letter. In this method, the modal weight coefficients of all concern band modes are manipulated to co-design a filtering antenna. No extra resonance structure or filtering circuit is needed to generate a radiation null. The radiation performance and filtering performance can be considered simultaneously. Furthermore, the proposed antenna is studied for phased array application. To reduce the spacing between the element in array, the patches of the proposed antenna are reused. Due to the filtering characteristic comes from inherent properties of the radiation structure of the antenna, it can maintain the filtering characteristic during angle scanning. To verify this method, a single layer dual-polarized filtering antenna element and filtering phased array antenna are designed. The proposed antenna and arrays are simulated and measured. The results show that the proposed antenna element and array achieves an impedance bandwidth of 7.7%. The efficiency of the proposed antenna out of band is lower than 2.5%.

title: Accurate Statistical Characterization For Cooperative Multi RIS-Aided Wireless System_supp1-3677601.pdf
authors: Aman Sikri
abstract: Reconfigurable intelligent surfaces (RIS) constitute a promising technology for beyond-5th Generation (B5G)/6th Generation (6G) wireless systems. In this letter, using recursion approach, the second and fourth moment for the cooperative M RIS-aided end-to-end channel are derived. Based on the moment matching (MM) approach, these moments are utilized to approximate the distribution for the cooperative M RIS-assisted channel. It is shown that the outage probability (OP) and ergodic capacity (EC) performance of the considered system improves with the increase in M when the number of reflecting elements (REs) per RIS is large.

title: Scanning the Issue
authors: N/A
abstract: Summary form only: Abstracts of articles presented in this issue of the publication.

title: Single-Event and Dose Effects in 28nm FD-SOI Advanced Interface Bus Chiplet Interconnect
authors: Antoine Rouget, Adrian Evans, Fady Abouzeid, Guillaume Bascoul, Victor Malherbe, Gwenvael Le-Bars, Philippe Roche, Fabien Clermidy
abstract: Chiplet technology for 2.5-D/3-D integration is being rapidly adopted, including in mission critical applications. We present a study of the radiation sensitivity of a prototype Advanced Interface Bus (AIB) chiplet die-to-die interface in 28nm Fully Depleted Silicon On Insulator (FD-SOI) technology. The Single Event Effect (SEE) sensitivity was evaluated first using fault injection simulations and these results were compared with those obtained using alpha particles and a pulsed-laser, which enabled us to identify the most sensitive regions. Due to the large number of flip-flops in the interface, there is a significant cross-section, although the majority of faults manifest themselves as single-bit data-upsets. The effect of the dose deposited by the alpha particles was also studied.

title: Adaptive Force Servoing of Series Elastic Actuator Based on BGF Estimator With Guaranteed Transient Performance_supp1-3671816.mp4
authors: Qing Guo
abstract: A new <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$L_{1}$</tex-math></inline-formula> adaptive control (L1AC) method with bound-gain-forgetting (BGF) estimator is proposed in floating-base series elastic actuators (SEAs) to enhance both transient and steady-state force control performance. The transient performance is evaluated by the output force overshoot, the convergence speed of tracking error, and the chatters effect. Meanwhile, the steady-state performance is evaluated based on static error. First, the controller stability together with the transient performance are analyzed in the controller design. Then, the BGF-based estimator is adopted to achieve a time-varying adaptation for environmental stiffness, and improve the robustness and tracking speed of the SEA. Finally, the proposed controller performances are compared with the resonance ratio control and the model reference adaptive control in experimental verification. It indicates that the L1AC results has not any overshoot and less steady-state error than the other two methods. Furthermore, the chatter amplitude of the spring deformation is only in 0.2<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$^\circ$</tex-math></inline-formula> during force control.

title: Software Adjustable Mechatronic Inerter-Spring-Damper System_supp2-3672853.mp4
authors: Mehrdad Moallem
abstract: Mechanical elements such as dampers, springs, and inerters play a crucial role in various engineering applications, including vehicles, robotics, rehabilitation devices, vibration isolation systems, and structural designs such as seismic-resistant buildings and bridges. The ability to dynamically adjust damping, stiffness, and inertance in real time can greatly enhance the adaptability, stability, and overall performance of these systems. However, conventional methods for modifying these parameters often require physical alterations to material properties or geometric configurations, making them complex and impractical. This article introduces a software-adjustable mechatronic inerter-spring-damper system that leverages a permanent magnet synchronous machine, a two-level AC/DC converter, and control firmware to enable real-time modulation of mechanical behavior through electrical analogies. An experimental prototype is developed and validated, incorporating a sliding mode current controller to precisely regulate torque and achieve the desired dynamic response, demonstrating the feasibility and effectiveness of the proposed approach.

title: A Novel Five-Level Power Converter With Model Predictive Torque Control for Four-Phase SRMs
authors: Lefei Ge, Hongbing Li, Nan Du, Chenyu Zhao, Yuyang Shen, Senyi Liu, Dianxun Xiao, Shoujun Song
abstract: This article proposes a novel five-level power converter with only 12 switches and 12 diodes for a four-phase 8/6-pole switched reluctance motor (SRM), combined with a model predictive torque control (MPTC) strategy to suppress torque ripple. To overcome the high computational burden caused by the multilevel design, a candidate voltage vectors (CVVs) selection method is designed, reducing the number of CVVs to 9 or 5 at each control period. The effectiveness of the proposed topology and control method is evaluated experimentally under different operating conditions. Experimental results show that the converter effectively maintains the midpoint average voltage balance, and the CVVs optimization method reduces runtime by at least 33.89% compared with the strategy without optimization. Furthermore, the system achieves a torque ripple reduction of at least 32.29% compared with the conventional asymmetric half-bridge power converter (AHBPC). Although the proposed converter is inferior to the AHBPC in terms of efficiency, cost, size, reliability, and weight, it still offers strong potential for applications requiring low torque ripple and high robustness.

title: Nonlinear Continuous Sliding Mode Predictive Control for Finite-Time Trajectory Tracking of Constrained AUV under Uncertainties_supp1-3672815.mp4
authors: xinbin li
abstract: Autonomous underwater vehicles (AUVs) are indispensable for ocean exploration; however, achieving accurate trajectory tracking control suffers from significant challenges due to internal model uncertainties and external disturbances, stemming from unmodeled hydrodynamics, parametric uncertainties, and time-varying environmental forces. Moreover, the presence of inherent velocity constraints may further restrict actuator performance, leading to increased tracking errors or even system instability. Taking into account these issues, this study proposes a tightly coupled nonlinear continuous sliding mode predictive control (NCSMPC) scheme to tackle the trajectory tracking problem for AUV. First, the Lyapunov-based nonlinear model predictive control (NMPC) kinematic controller is developed for the position loop to achieve finite-time tracking, which generates the constrained velocity signals to consistently achieve optimal tracking performance in accordance with the online optimization function. Meanwhile, an adaptive integral event-triggering mechanism (ETM) is introduced to reduce the computational burden by adaptively regulating the frequency of optimization updates. Then, an adaptive continuous sliding mode controller (ACSM) based on the adaptive super-twisting algorithm is proposed for the velocity loop. The designed dynamic controller can actively compensate for unmodeled hydrodynamics and time-varying disturbances without requiring prior knowledge of their bounds, thereby achieving finite-time velocity tracking with low oscillation. Guaranteed by Lyapunov stability, the proposed control framework integrates the optimization of NMPC with the robust performance of ACSM, thereby ensuring high-performance position-velocity closed-loop tracking. Finally, simulations and real-time experiments on a robot-operating-system (ROS)-based AUV model validate the effectiveness of the proposed controller.

title: ARRANGE: A Secure Decentralized Federated Learning Framework For Heterogeneous LEO Satellite Constellation_supp1-3677423.pdf
authors: Ammar Hawbani
abstract: Low Earth Orbit (LEO) satellite constellations play an increasingly vital role in Earth observation and data-driven applications. However, traditional centralized processing frameworks are hindered by limited downlink bandwidth and high satellite mobility, making real-time and large-scale data transmission inefficient. To tackle these challenges, we propose ARRANGE, <underline xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">A</u> secu<underline xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">R</u>e and decent<underline xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">R</u>alized Feder<underline xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">A</u>ted Lear<underline xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">N</u>ing framework specifically designed for hetero<underline xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">G</u>eneous LEO satellite Const<underline xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">E</u>llation. ARRANGE enables inter-satellite task offloading and decentralized model aggregation, while incorporating symmetric and asymmetric encryption to ensure data security and integrity during transmission. To address the straggler effect and data heterogeneity, we introduce an improved Genetic Algorithm (GA) that optimizes task scheduling across satellites with varying resources and institutional affiliations. Simulation results on real-world benchmark datasets demonstrate that ARRANGE significantly reduces system delay and improves model accuracy compared to existing FL strategies. This work provides a scalable and secure solution for enabling on-orbit collaborative intelligence in dynamic satellite environments.

title: Cylindrical Battery Gas Evolution Analysis via Optical Fiber Based Non-invasive Gas Pressure Monitoring
authors: Yuntian Guo, Yuan Wang, Shuyan Guo, Hao Liu, Guoming Ma, Hao Zhao
publish: IEEE Transactions on Instrumentation and Measurement
abstract: As indispensable components in the global transition toward carbon neutrality, lithium-ion batteries face growing scrutiny due to recurring safety concerns that threaten to undermine their sustainable development. Internal gas pressure serves as a crucial diagnostic indicator in electrochemical diagnosis, enabling precise characterization of degradation mechanisms and state-of-health (SOH) evaluation in advanced battery architectures. Here, we propose a non-invasive in situ diagnostic method for monitoring time-resolved internal pressure (0.01 kPa instrument-limited, 0.027 kPa (3σ) noise-limited resolution) and temperature (±0.5 K accuracy) simultaneously by attaching fiber Bragg grating (FBG) sensors to the surface of an 18650 commercial lithium-ion battery. We further develop a pressure-temperature interdependency model that comprehensively considers the ideal gas law, electrode lithiation and gas sorption dynamics for predicting gas pressure within a battery with an error below 1.69%. During battery overcharging at 0.5C in 2 h 26 min, we observe a gas accumulation rate of 1.77 mol/(m3·h) mainly due to irreversible reactions within a battery, which is around 800 times higher than that in normal charge and discharge cycles. The results indicate that the proposed technique shows strong potential for achieving gas evolution analysis and battery health estimation.

title: 2025 2nd International Conference on Recent Trends in Electrical, Electronics and Computing Technologies (ICRTEECT)
authors: N/A

title: Component-Specific Prompt Tuning for Deepfake Detection
authors: Yinyin Chen, Huiqing Guo, Chunlei Peng, Nannan Wang, Xinbo Gao
publish: IEEE Transactions on Information Forensics and Security
abstract: With the development of deep learning technology, the facial images generated by deepfake technology have reached a level of authenticity that is difficult to distinguish, posing a serious threat to personal privacy and data security. Therefore, it is of great significance to develop efficient and reliable deepfake detection technology. In recent years, Visual Language Models (VLM) have been applied to deepfake detection tasks due to their powerful multimodal understanding capabilities. However, the existing VLM have not been specifically optimized for deepfake detection tasks. When directly applied to this task, there are problems such as insufficient model accuracy and insufficient feature extraction, especially when dealing with complex forgery scenes. In response to these challenges, this paper proposes an innovative deepfake face detection method based on VLM and component-specific prompt tuning. We transform the deepfake detection task into a Visual Question Answering (VQA) task, making full use of the multimodal understanding capabilities of VLM and the flexibility of prompt tuning technology. This method uses a local prompt strategy to customize specific prompt questions for key facial components such as eyes, nose, and mouth, guiding the model to focus on the local features of these areas, thereby accurately capturing forgery traces. In addition, we introduced a feature extraction module Q-Former based on instructions, which can flexibly adjust the focus area of visual features according to prompts, significantly improving the model’s perception of locally forged features. By fusing these local features extracted by Q-Former and combining them with the language model to judge the authenticity of the overall face image, we can finally generate accurate prediction results. A large number of experimental results show that our method is significantly better than existing technologies in terms of detection accuracy and robustness.

title: Conference Chair Message
authors: Ashish Kumar Singhal
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Smart Innovations in Systems, Infrastructure, Mechanical, Power, AI and Computing Technologies (SISIMPACT)
abstract: On behalf of the Conference Chair, I am honoured to present the 2025 IEEE 1<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">st</sup> International Conference on Smart Innovations in Systems, Infrastructure, Mechanical, Power, Artificial Intelligence, and Computing Technologies (SISIMPACT-2025). We warmly welcome participants from across India and around the globe, and we look forward to an enriching and inspiring event under this forward-thinking theme. This conference stands as a testament to the rapid evolution of computing and engineering technologies and their profound impact on science, engineering, and the broader technological landscape. The world is undergoing transformative changes driven by advancements in artificial intelligence, data science, cybersecurity, machine learning, and related domains. SISIMPACT-2025 provides a crucial platform for researchers, technologists, and practitioners to come together to exchange ground breaking ideas, explore interdisciplinary applications, and address emerging challenges in these rapidly evolving fields.

title: POSITION: Open World 3D Scene CAD Recomposition
authors: Rongkun Yang, Hongda Liu, Yijun Chen, Sheng Ao, Yongjian Zhang, Longguang Wang, Kaiwen Xue, Shunbo Zhou, Yulan Guo
abstract: 3D scene CAD recomposition aims to reconstruct a given scene by retrieving and assembling CAD models from a database, so as to accurately simulate the geometric properties and spatial arrangement of the original environment. Recent methods learn this task through training on limited scan-to-CAD annotation data, which hinders their generalization to diverse real-world scenes. In this paper, we propose POSITION, an open-world 3D scene CAD recomposition method to construct the 3D scene with CADs retrieved from an open-set database. POSITION is designed following a divide-and-conquer strategy. Firstly, we extract open-world multi-modal object representations from a captured 3D scene. Secondly, on top of the representations, we propose a coarse-to-fine retrieval method to retrieve CADs that are visually, geometrically and semantically match real objects. Thirdly, we present a physically plausible pose alignment method to adjust retrieved CAD models to maintain consistent geometry and layout with the observation. By decomposing the problem into well-defined subtasks, our approach achieves generalization across various scene types and scalable CAD databases without retraining or fine-tuning. Our approach demonstrates superior CAD recomposition performance on both the Scan2CAD and diverse real-world 3D scene datasets. Our project page: https://yangrongkun.github.io/position/.

title: Surface Wave and Free-Space Wave Co-Modulated Metasurface Antenna With Cross Staggered Arrangement
authors: Wenyu Zhao, Xiuping Li, Zihang Qi, Jianxun Su, Zhi Ning Chen
publish: IEEE Transactions on Antennas and Propagation
abstract: In this paper, a same-frequency surface wave and free-space wave co-modulated(SFC) metasurface antenna is proposed with staggered arrangement and the same aperture, featuring independent control. A thorough discussion is presented regarding the working principle of the two-dimensional same-frequency co-modulation of surface and free-space waves. A method based on reflected phase, rather than surface impedance, is proposed to modulate surface waves. The comparison is illustrated, exhibiting almost identical performance. A staggered arrangement method is utilized to construct the SFC metasurface antenna. The calculated and simulated radiated patterns show that the radiations from surface waves and free-space waves are essentially decoupled, featuring independent control. A static SFC metasurface using rectangular patches is fabricated and measured. The simulated results and measured results demonstrated good consistency. The measured maximum gains of surface wave and free-space wave are up to 19.5 dBi and 24.1 dBi, respectively. The isolation between the two ports is higher than 30 dB, promising the potential for full-duplex applications. The proposed SFC metasurface can be a candidate for mm-wave communications.

title: Toward Deep Representation Learning for Event-Enhanced Visual Autonomous Perception: the eAP Dataset
authors: Jinghang Li, Shichao Li, Qing Lian, Peiliang Li, Xiaozhi Chen, Yi Zhou
abstract: Recent visual autonomous perception systems achieve remarkable performances with deep representation learning. However, they fail in scenarios with challenging illumination. While event cameras can mitigate this problem, there is a lack of a large-scale dataset to develop event-enhanced deep visual perception models in autonomous driving scenes. To address the gap, we present the eAP (event-enhanced Autonomous Perception) dataset, the largest dataset with event cameras for autonomous perception. We demonstrate how eAP can facilitate the study of different autonomous perception tasks, including 3D vehicle detection and object time-to-contact (TTC) estimation, through deep representation learning. Based on eAP, we demonstrate the first successful use of events to improve a popular 3D vehicle detection network in challenging illumination scenarios. eAP also enables a devoted study of the representation learning problem of object TTC estimation. We show how a geometry-aware representation learning framework leads to the best event-based object TTC estimation network that operates at 200 FPS. The dataset, code, and pre-trained models will be made publicly available for future research.

title: Addressing Visual Impairments with Model-Driven Engineering: A Systematic Literature Review
authors: Judith Michael, Lukas Netz, Bernhard Rumpe, Ingo Mueller, John Grundy, Shavindra Wickramathilaka, Hourieh Khalajzadeh
publish: ACM Transactions on Software Engineering and Methodology
abstract: None

title: Diverse social robot ethnicity performance in the classroom facilitated more expansive robot identity exploration in boys: A participatory robo-identity design study
authors: Lux Miranda, Arianna de Stasio, Ginevra Castellano, Katie Winkle
