title: Enhanced Air Quality Index Prediction using SARIMA Model TLSTM Network and Hybrid Modeling
authors: Pallavi Gudimilla, Ayesha Banu, Sayyed Hasanoddin, B Maheshwari, Rayabarapu Vishal
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Recent Trends in Electrical, Electronics and Computing Technologies (ICRTEECT)
abstract: Air pollution is found to be the foremost constraint for urbanization in developing countries. The vital indicator to assess the levels of air pollution is termed to be the Air Quality Index (AQI). Accurate AQI predictions are crucial for effectual environmental supervision and introduce any new public health initiative. This research introduces an enhanced model for AQI prediction by means of a hybrid approach which integrates time-series analysis and deep learning. The primary data for this study was collected from the Central Pollution Control Board (CPCB) India, including an extensive range of pollutants measured across various cities over an extended period. We use the Transductive Long Short-Term Memory (TLSTM) network with a Seasonal Autoregressive Integrated Moving Average (SARIMA) model which captures seasonal patterns and long-term dependencies prevalent in the data. The proposed Hybrid Model’s TLSTM incorporates a time decay mechanism to account for the temporal dynamics of air pollution, which enhances the model ability in handling unstable time intervals between data points. Additionally a Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) layer extracts local patterns from the data, and a bidirectional RNN is employed to capture context from both previous and upcoming time steps. The final predictions are refined using an XGBoost boosting stage, which combines linear components of pollutants with the TLSTM predictions. The Hybrid Model demonstrates an exceptional predictive performance when compared to the existing SARIMA-LSTM model. The Root Mean Squared Error (RMSE) value is reduced from 0.179 to 0.02196 (12.2%) and the R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> value is increased from 0.512 to 0.9896(47.76%). These results emphasize the high accuracy and reliability of the proposed model in AQI value prediction. The proposed approach not only outperforms traditional models but also provides potential insights into the seasonal and temporal variations of air pollutants.

title: AI-Enabled Unmanned Vehicle Systems for Transforming Healthcare Delivery: Strategic Applications and Roadmap for Oman’s Public Health System
authors: Anas Ansari, Abdullah Ansari, Khalifa Al Jabri, Jing-Ming Guo, Abdul Rahman Ansari, H.E. Khodke
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Unmanned Vehicle Systems-Oman (UVS)
abstract: This paper explores the transformative potential of AI-enabled Unmanned Vehicle Systems (UVS) in enhancing healthcare logistics within Oman’s public health sector, with a specific focus on Unmanned Aerial Vehicles (UAVs) for medical supply delivery. Oman’s unique geography, characterized by remote mountainous terrain and limited road access, presents significant challenges for the timely and equitable delivery of healthcare. The proposed system integrates AI-driven autonomous routing and multi-vehicle coordination to optimize the delivery of critical medical supplies, including vaccines, diagnostics, blood products, and emergency medications. Key technical and policy considerations are addressed, such as airspace regulations, data privacy, cybersecurity, and interoperability with health information systems. A five-phase roadmap is presented, outlining a structured strategy for national-level implementation: from governance and pilot deployments to regulatory frameworks, scale-up, and sustainability. By aligning UAV-based healthcare logistics with Oman Vision 2040, this work demonstrates how targeted UVS applications can enhance access, responsiveness, and system resilience in public health, providing a scalable model for other nations facing similar healthcare delivery challenges.

title: An IoT-Enabled Telecardiology System with Conformer Models for Arrhythmia Detection
authors: Lugi Febriansyah, Satria Mandala
publish: 2025 IEEE Asia-Pacific Conference on Geoscience, Electronics and Remote Sensing Technology (AGERS)
abstract: Cardiac arrhythmia can be reliably identified from electrocardiogram (ECG) signals. This paper proposes a Con-former-based classifier for ECG beat recognition using the MITBIH Arrhythmia Database with a five-class scheme (L, N, Q, R, V). Two training configurations were examined(i) a default setup and (ii) a hyperparameter-tuned setupwhile keeping the network architecture constant. Each beat was segmented into fixed-length sequences, enabling the Conformer to model global temporal dependencies through self-attention and local waveform patterns through depthwise convolutions. The tuned model achieved near-ceiling test performance with 99.50% accuracy, 99.50% precision, 99.49% sensitivity, 99.87% specificity, and a 99.50% F1-score, demonstrating excellent generalization and reduced misclassification in morphologically similar classes. These findings indicate that convergence-oriented hyperparameter tuning can substantially improve classification fidelity without altering the core architecture. Furthermore, the proposed framework lays the foundation for an IoT-enabled telecardiology system, integrating deep-learning-based ECG inference with real-time data transmission and web-based monitoring. This approach enables scalable, portable, and intelligent cardiac screening, supporting continuous remote arrhythmia detection and early clinical intervention in both hospital and wearabledevice environments.

title: An SPO Feature-Enhanced Framework for Extracting Innovative Contributions from Academic Papers
authors: Bowen Ma, Xuanhua Ren, Chuangye Guo, Ge Zhu, Xiaoxiao Liu
publish: 2025 International Conference on Big Data and Data Mining (BDDM)
abstract: The rapid expansion of scientific literature, while broadening the scope of academic inquiry, has significantly increased the time required for researchers to access critical information. Consequently, the efficient extraction of core knowledge from texts has become crucial for intelligent retrieval and knowledge management. This study focuses on innovative content in academic papers and defines authors' claims of research contributions as “innovation contribution sentences” (ICSs), with the aim of automating their extraction and analysis. Using a corpus of ACL conference papers (1990-2020), we developed a classification scheme for innovation contributions and created a high-quality annotated dataset through manual labeling and consistency checks. For model development, we compared multiple text classification models and identified SciBERT as the optimal baseline. We then proposed a feature fusion enhancement method based on Subject-Predicate-Object (SPO) structures, which significantly improved performance, achieving an F1-score of 0.7429. Using this optimized model, we automatically annotated the remaining corpus and performed fine-grained information extraction with an SPO triple extraction algorithm. Finally, we established a comprehensive descriptive framework to systematically analyze the structural patterns of ICSs in research articles.

title: Fish Classification and Habitat Monitoring in Water Bodies Using Deep Learning
authors: Thanu Sree Senthil Kumar, Sangeetha G, Priyaregika S U, Lekha P, Ambika A
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Recent Trends in Electrical, Electronics and Computing Technologies (ICRTEECT)
abstract: Innovative solutions are being developed to address the most pressing issues surrounding ocean monitoring and biodiversity conservation. The world currently faces severe difficulties in marine ecosystem monitoring, especially with the alarming decline in fish populations and their habitats. Thus, this study is oriented toward the Indian Ocean region(Gulf of Mannar), where we present the development of a new system for fish classification and habitat monitoring using deep learning techniques based on Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) and Recurrent Neural Networks (RNNs).Here, we have a whole fish classification from deep learning trained by a careful dataset consisting of many fish species according to the depth range they live in (0-100 m, 100-200 m, etc.), giving rise to a classification of species, even new or not previously viewed by any dive team. This species identification offers great insights into the status and health of fish populations, including count, habitat conditions, and endangerment status. By addressing the challenges of overfishing, habitat destruction, and biodiversity loss, this project aligns with the core principles of United Nations Sustainable Development Goal 14 (SDG 14), "Life Below Water," which aims to conserve marine life and its resources and work toward sustainable ocean management. Through these efforts, the project envisions nurturing resilient aquatic ecosystems, where marine life thrives, contributing to the well-being of present and future generations.

title: Feasibility and Challenges of Utilizing the Terahertz Frequency Band for High-Capacity and Ultra-Fast Wireless Communications in 6G Networks
authors: Madhu Kumar Vanteru, Karthik Kumar Vaigandla, Sravan Kumar Vanam, Undadi Rajitha, Ranjith Kumar Siddoju, Vanam Harika
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Recent Trends in Electrical, Electronics and Computing Technologies (ICRTEECT)
abstract: With the development of new applications in the 6G network era, the demand for high-capacity and ultra-fast wireless communications is consistently rising. Utilizing the Terahertz (THz) frequency band, which offers much larger bandwidth compared to traditional wireless frequency bands, is a promising approach. However, THz communication systems in 6G networks face numerous practical and technical hurdles despite their enormous promise. This article examines the feasibility and challenges of using the THz frequency spectrum for ultra-high-speed and high-capacity wireless communications in 6G. We discuss THz channel characteristics, air absorption, path loss, range limitations, modulation, antenna design, and signal processing approaches. Numerical and simulated results are presented to assess performance, along with key future directions.

title: Server-Aware Task Scheduling Algorithms for Performance Improvement in Mobile Edge Computing
authors: Abhishek Tripathy, Guddakesh Kr. Chandan, Mohit Khilwani, D. Rajeshwari, Anshu, Shalini Saxena
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Smart Innovations in Systems, Infrastructure, Mechanical, Power, AI and Computing Technologies (SISIMPACT)
abstract: A recently developed method called mobile edge computing (MEC) lowers latency and increases speed of processing by extending computing cloud powers to the network's edges. In traditional Mobile Cloud Computing (MCC), workloads are handled on faraway cloud servers which causes delays resulting from long-distance data transfer. The suggested Server Efficiency-based Scheduling Algorithm (SESA) is meant to solve this problem by means of effective job scheduling in MEC. The task parameter assessment enables SESA to distribute assignments to servers which optimizes resource management. ATSA functions as an importance transfer mechanism to reduce queue intervals while also executing its role in Ageing-based Task Scheduling Algorithm (ATSA). The proposed method undergoes evaluation through a process simulator using total completion time as well as execution time and throughput in comparison to ATSA. Minizine delay rates and enhancing system speed helps SSA accomplish improved job completion effectiveness. By lowering overall efficiency and finishing times and raising throughput, the study shows SSA beats ATSA. The scheduling method improves MEC system efficiency, which qualifies them better for managing demanded computing activities and applications that are immediate. The results underline the need of server effectiveness in arranging tasks for enhanced efficiency in MEC systems. The suggested technique achieves an overall accuracy of 98 %, indicating its efficacy in job performance.

title: FlowSegModel: Advancing Perception in Autonomous Driving Through Weather-Resilient Segmentation
authors: Dilshara Herath, Thiwanka Alahakoon, Oshada Rathnayake, Sanjula Senadeera, Roshan Godaliyadda, Parakrama Ekanayake
publish: 2025 IEEE 19th International Conference on Industrial and Information Systems (ICIIS)
abstract: This paper introduces a novel methodology for semantic segmentation in adverse weather conditions, employing a FlowSegModel architecture that integrates RGB images with optical flow inputs within a modified DeepLabV3 framework, evaluated on the VKITTI2 dataset. The FlowSegModel adapts the DeepLabV3 backbone, by reconfiguring its initial convolutional layer to accommodate a 5-channel input (3 RGB + 2 optical flow), enabling fusion of spatial and motion features. This is complemented by a tailored Atrous Spatial Pyramid Pooling (ASPP) module, optimized with adjusted dilation rates to capture multi-scale context under degraded visibility. The proposed FlowSegModel achieves mean Intersection over Union (IoU) scores of 0.8406 under normal conditions and 0.8034 in adverse scenarios (fog, rain, overcast), demonstrating a robust 4.4% degradation. This resilience is attributed to optical flow’s complementary motion cues, enhancing visibility in degraded environments. Comparative analyses reveal that fine-tuned SEARAFT and RAFT models outperform classical methods (e.g., Farneback, Horn-Schunck) by over 96% in Average End-Point Error (EPE) reduction, with SEA-RAFT excelling by 56.6% over RAFT. An ablation study confirms the superiority of RGB-flow fusion, yielding a 46.1% mean IoU improvement over an optical-flow-only variant, which struggles with static scene discrimination absent color and texture data. These findings hold significant implications for autonomous driving and outdoor vision systems, offering a practical approach to weather-invariant perception that may reduce real-world failure rates. This work advances robust segmentation techniques, contributing to safer and more reliable environmental perception in challenging conditions.

title: A DeepSeek Assistant Virtual Rehabilitation System for Upper-Limb Exercise
authors: Xiang Ji, Shuaishuai Han, Fan Zhang, Qinglei Bu, Xinyue Zhang, Jie Sun
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and eXtended and Virtual Reality (AIxVR)
abstract: Stroke often leads to significant upper-limb motor impairments, necessitating long-term rehabilitation. However, traditional methods depend on therapist supervision or complex robotic systems, which limit their accessibility and scalability. To address these issues, this study proposes an AI-driven virtual rehabilitation system comprising real-time posture tracking, a virtual doctor with voice interaction, and a plug-in game to enhance engagement. Specifically, the proposed system architecture leverages a ZED 2i Stereo camera for real-time motion tracking and incorporates a DeepSeek assistant module to provide personalized feedback and adjust game difficulty levels. A proof-of-concept experiment involving a healthy participant was conducted to validate the system's architectural integrity, realtime latency, and interaction logic. Preliminary results confirm the system's technical feasibility and suggest it holds promise for future clinical use, pending further validation and optimisation.

title: Large-Scale, Longitudinal Field Study of AI-Agent-User Interactions in Commercial Metaverse
authors: Yuichi Hiroi, Yuki Imai, Hikari Yanagawa, Takefumi Hiraki
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and eXtended and Virtual Reality (AIxVR)
abstract: Although commercial metaverse platforms have advanced social implementation, they struggle to retain users. Most new users abandon these platforms shortly after their initial experience, which hinders the realization of the metaverse's social and economic potential. Although AI-agents could promote user interaction and retention by acting as social catalysts, existing research has focused on laboratory-based short-term validation. This approach provides limited evidence of the longterm effectiveness of agents in commercial environments. This study examines the impact of Large Language Model (LLM)-based AI-agents on user continuation behavior by operating AI-agents for 31 days on the commercial metaverse platform Cluster and observing the natural usage behavior of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{5, 0 2 0}$</tex> unique users. The analysis used two complementary approaches: (1) an aggregate effect analysis at the weekly habit formation level and (2) an within-user effect analysis at the daily decision-making level with individual difference controls. The results revealed that interaction with the AI-agent produces lasting changes in user behavior, especially among new users. Furthermore, the cumulative relationship-building process through continuous contact opportunities rather than single impressive experiences with AI-agents was found to decisively affect users' continued metaverse usage.

title: Improving Accuracy and Efficiency of Gene Expression Data Analysis Using an Ensemble of GSOM and Hierarchical Clustering
authors: R.M.B.D.K. Rathnayake, M.H. Gunasekara, Damminda Alahakoon, Damayanthi Herath
publish: 2025 IEEE 19th International Conference on Industrial and Information Systems (ICIIS)
abstract: Clustering of gene expression data is essential for identifying co-expressed genes, functional modules, and disease biomarkers. Traditional clustering methods, such as hierarchical clustering and k-means, often struggle with the high dimensionality and noise in gene expression datasets, limiting their biological interpretability. This paper presents GSOM+HClust, a framework that integrates the Growing Self-Organizing Map (GSOM) with hierarchical clustering (HClust) to improve the accuracy and efficiency of gene expression data analysis. The framework is evaluated on the Alzheimer’s disease gene expression dataset (GSE5281) and compared against raw hierarchical clustering, PCA+HClust, and SOM+HClust. Results show that GSOM+HClust achieves superior clustering quality across multiple indices (CCC = 0.962, Silhouette = 0.935, DBI = 0.145) while reducing runtime from several hours to under 20 minutes. Furthermore, all 14 known Alzheimer’s biomarkers were clustered into coherent groups, providing strong biological validation. Findings of this work establish GSOM+HClust as a scalable, interpretable framework for biomarker discovery and disease stratification, providing a practical tool for large-scale gene expression analysis.

title: Multi-Modal Transformer Network for Classroom Instruction Analysis
authors: Matthew Korban, Chongyu He, Peter Youngs, Scott T. Acton
publish: 2025 59th Asilomar Conference on Signals, Systems, and Computers
abstract: We propose a multimodal transformer for automatic classroom activity recognition that combines a background-suppressed Vision Transformer video model with a BERT-based transcript encoder. Each modality first predicts activity labels independently, and a cross-modal transformer then refines video predictions using aligned transcript cues. Evaluated on 50 hours of real-world K–12 classroom video and audio, our framework accurately classifies activities such as lecture, individual work, and group work, and consistently outperforms a strong video-only baseline. Per-frame F1 and cross-modal correlation analyses demonstrate that transcript signals substantially boost video recognition in authentic classrooms, highlighting the value of multimodal context for educational video analytics.

title: Affine Filter Bank Modulation: A New Waveform for High Mobility Communications
authors: Henrique L. Senger, Gustavo P. Gonçalves, Bruno S. Chang, Hyeon Seok Rou, Kuranage Roche Rayan Ranasinghe, Giuseppe Thadeu Freitas de Abreu, Didier Le Ruyet
publish: 2025 59th Asilomar Conference on Signals, Systems, and Computers
abstract: We propose a new waveform suitable for integrated sensing and communications (ISAC) systems facing doubly-dispersive (DD) channel conditions, as typically encountered in high mobility scenarios. Dubbed Affine Filter Bank Modulation (AFBM), this novel waveform is designed based on a filter-bank structure, known for its ability to suppress out-of-band emissions (OOBE), while integrating a discrete affine Fourier transform (DAFT) precoding stage which yields low peak-to-average power ratio (PAPR) and robustness to DD distortion, as well as other features desirable for ISAC. Analytical and simulation results demonstrate that AFBM maintains quasi-orthogonality similar to that of affine frequency division multiplexing (AFDM) in DD channels, while achieving PAPR levels 3 dB lower, in addition to OOBE as low as -100 dB when implemented with PHYDYAS prototype filters.

title: Fictitious Play in Product Markov Games with Kullback-Leibler Control Cost
authors: Khaled Nakhleh, Sarper Aydin, Ceyhun Eksin, Sabit Ekin
publish: 2025 59th Asilomar Conference on Signals, Systems, and Computers
abstract: We present and analyze fictitious play for a new class of product Markov games with a Kullback-Leibler (KL) control cost. In a product Markov game, state transitions are the product of n Markov transition functions, where each agent controls its own local state transition dynamics given the common state and incurs a KL control cost for their efforts. Fictitious play entails each agent best-responding to minimize its discounted sum of instantaneous costs, that depend on KL control cost and a state cost, given local beliefs about other agents’ policies. Agents update their beliefs about other agents’ policies upon observation of the realized states. We show that the fictitious play converges asymptotically to a Nash equilibrium of a product Markov potential game. Simulation results on a multi-agent cloud radio access network confirm the convergence result for the game with non-identical payoffs and demonstrate the speed of convergence.

title: Child Safety Monitoring Using a Smart IoT Bracelet
authors: Kshatri Rajeswari Bai, Srikanth Yadav M
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Emerging Trends in Computing and Communication (ETCOM)
abstract: Child safety remains a paramount concern in con temporary society, particularly in crowded urban environments where children can easily become separated from their guardians. This paper presents an innovative IoT-based smart bracelet system designed to provide comprehensive child tracking and safety monitoring capabilities. Our proposed solution integrates GPS positioning, LoRaWAN communication, environmental sen sors, and machine learning algorithms to create a robust child safety ecosystem. The system features real-time location tracking, geofencing capabilities, emergency alert mechanisms, and health monitoring functionalities. Through extensive testing and validation, our prototype demonstrates 99.23-meter radius, 95and extended battery life of 72 hours under normal operation. The bracelet incorporates tamper-proof design elements, water resistance (IP67), and child-friendly interfaces. Integration with mobile applications and web platforms enables seamless moni toring for parents and caregivers. Our comprehensive evaluation across various scenarios confirms the system's effectiveness in enhancing child safety while maintaining usability and comfort for young users.

title: Context-Aware Artificial Intelligence Systems for Long-Horizon Decision Making
authors: Vaishali Joshi, Samaya Pillai, Deepak Gupta, Solikhova Khumora, Yuldasheva Gulora Gulumovna, Anorgul Ashirova
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Emerging Trends in Computing and Communication (ETCOM)
abstract: Long-horizon decision making remains a fundamental challenge in artificial intelligence, particularly in complex, dynamic environments where agents must reason over extended temporal sequences while maintaining contextual awareness. This paper presents a novel Context-Aware Long-Horizon Decision Making (CA-LHDM) framework that integrates hierarchical reinforcement learning with transformer-based context encoding to enable effective planning and execution over extended time horizons. Our approach leverages attention mechanisms to capture temporal dependencies and contextual information across multiple abstraction levels, enabling agents to decompose complex tasks into manageable subgoals while maintaining global coherence. We introduce a hybrid architecture combining model-based planning with model-free learning, supported by an adaptive memory module that selectively retains relevant historical information. Experimental evaluation on benchmark tasks including MuJoCo continuous control and multi-agent coordination scenarios demonstrates that CA-LHDM achieves <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{4 7 {\%}}$</tex> improvement in cumulative reward and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{3 5 {\%}}$</tex> reduction in planning time compared to state-of-the-art baselines. Our ablation studies reveal the critical role of context encoding in achieving superior performance, with the attention-based context module contributing 28% of the overall performance gain.

title: Application of Artificial Intelligence and Big Data Analytics in Finance and Risk Management
authors: Avnish Goyal, Vishal Maheshwari, Khemraj Sharma, Dr Arpit Jain
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Recent Trends in Electrical, Electronics and Computing Technologies (ICRTEECT)
abstract: In the engineering field, AI and Big Data can be used in finance and risk management to make the most appropriate decisions. AI models are also used to manipulate big data, recognize patterns, predict events in the financial environment, and optimize the use of resources among others. It assists project managers to identify possible problems, reduce risks to enhance economic performance. Another field where big data analytics can be considered as helpful is the problem of risk management because it correlates various source data, such as trend, regulatory updates and project-related measures. With complex analytics, you will be able to understand the direction of trends in real-time, reveal abnormalities, and foresee threats to prevent them before a threat materializes. Since the process of the data analysis is assigned to AIs-based models, the process of financial modeling and budget management became more precise, and the uncertainties that influence the success of any project become minimized. Individuals would also be able to automate the frivolous financial process with the help of the same technologies having a significantly lesser risk of a human error. Furthermore, AI and big data analytics improve compliance to allow checking the situation in real-time and develop a flag in case suspicious non-compliance area is detected. This could be the foundation of the successful risk management of the financial risk that will lead to more visible and strategic decision making. The synergetic effect of AI and the big data analytics steadies the manner the economic and risk managing aspect of the engineering endeavor can be considered to give agility, adaptability, and resiliency in the challenging financing environment.

title: Big Data Analytics Solutions in Healthcare and the Role of IT Firms in Driving Innovation
authors: Radhakrishnan P, D Mahammad Rafi, Niket Tajne, Kochumol Abraham, M. K. Sharma, Joshuva Arockia Dhanraj
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Smart Innovations in Systems, Infrastructure, Mechanical, Power, AI and Computing Technologies (SISIMPACT)
abstract: The study suggests a new big data analytics model applicable to the arena of healthcare, where IT companies play a strategic role as motive force of innovation and improvement of patient outcomes. This architecture will include several layers such as semantic data integration, federated learning and adaptive analytics to achieve scalability, privacy, and real-time decision support in the proposed system. In comparison to conventional models, the model achieves reduced overhead in communications and ensured high-speed processing and high accuracy through distributed healthcare networks. Multi-performance metrics were used to estimate, namely accuracy, precision, recall, F1-score, AUC, execution time, convergence rate, and communication overhead. The findings illustrate important success, and the suggested approach attains 95.4 accuracy, 94.8 precision, 94.1 recall, a 94.4 F1-score, and 96 percent AUC. In addition, it takes only 82 seconds of execution time, 38 MB of communication overhead, and 0.0075 as the convergence rate. These results support the effectiveness, consistency, and relevance of the suggested model in contemporary healthcare systems and provide the basis of better, faster and secure data-driven healthcare innovations.

title: AI Based Advanced Green Supply Chain Through Sustainable Finance Strategies
authors: Manish Nagpal, Bikram Ghale, Paramjit Baxi', Sampa Chau Pattnaik, R Aroul Canessane, Pavan Chaudhary
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Smart Innovations in Systems, Infrastructure, Mechanical, Power, AI and Computing Technologies (SISIMPACT)
abstract: The evolution of the need to have sustainable industrial processes has led institutions to re-examine the traditional supply chain patterns that mostly overlook the environmental and financial harmony. The current paper outlines the AI-based model, which solves an appalling gap between sustainable finance initiatives and performance of green supply chains. Although there are greater levels of understanding on environmental, social and governance (ESG) measurements, numerous supply chains are unable to incorporate such aspects in their current operational and investment decisions that are real-time. In order to address this issue, the paper presents a new kind of architecture, Intelligent GreenChain-Fin Framework (IGC-Fin), which allows making adaptive, carbon-innovative decisions in supply chain operations based on the use of artificial intelligence through the possible support of green financial instruments. The framework combines Deep Q-Learning used in the dynamic optimization of policy with the Green-Aware Digital Twin, which offers an in-time, in-time simulation, and a finance-aware decision engine that uses ESG scores and carbon pricing models. Also, the model uses blockchain to facilitate transparent chain running of sustainability measures within the supply chain. With a single approach of operational intelligence and financial sustainability, IGC-Fin has a complete solution to enhance economic resilience and environmental performance. The suggested framework is an important game changer in the process of data-driven logistics and sustainable investment, which could create continuous value in the global supply networks. The suggested IGC-Fin Structure has an overall accuracy of 97.8%.

title: Smart Wardrobe: Your Personalized Fashion Stylist
authors: P. Kaushik, Gotte Ranjith Kumar, Priya Chaudhary, Suriya M, Sonam Rohilla, Riya Kapoor
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Smart Innovations in Systems, Infrastructure, Mechanical, Power, AI and Computing Technologies (SISIMPACT)
abstract: In this model we are making a fashion recommendation system in which we are generating a model for the latest and trending fashion in today's society. We are using CNN (Convolution Neural Networks) to classify the images into various category and filter the categories to obtain the reduced matrix for checking the accuracy of the model. Now a days as we have variety of products for fashion so we apply deep learning approach to use multimodal classification to plot the graph and know about the latest trend of each production in the fashion market especially in e-commerce. People's interest in fashion, which is thought to be a popular form of aesthetic expression, increasingly gained ground as the standard of living rose. Naturally, something that is more visually appealing will lure others to it. In this research, we proposed a personalized fashion recommender system that creates suggestions for the user based on an input given. Since consumers frequently encounter something that piques their interest and hunt for things that are similar to it, this project aims to use an image of a product provided by the user as input to generate recommendations. We use fashion dataset in the neural network to process the images and use nearest neighbour recommender to generate the final recommendation.

title: Timely Tracking of a Wiener Process With Single Bit Quantization
authors: Ismail Cosandal, Sahan Liyanaarachchi, Sennur Ulukus
publish: 2025 59th Asilomar Conference on Signals, Systems, and Computers
abstract: We consider the problem of timely tracking of a Wiener process via an energy-conserving sensor by utilizing a single bit quantization strategy under periodic sampling. Contrary to conventional single bit quantizers which only utilize the transmitted bit to convey information, in our codebook, we use an additional ‘∅’ symbol to encode the event of not transmitting. Thus, our quantization functions are composed of three decision regions as opposed to the conventional two regions. First, we propose an optimum quantization method in which the optimum quantization functions are obtained by tracking the distributions of the quantization error. However, this method requires a high computational cost and might not be applicable for energy-conserving sensors. Thus, we propose two additional low complexity methods. In the last-bit aware method, three predefined quantization functions are available to both devices, and they switch the quantization function based on the last transmitted bit. With the Gaussian approximation method, we calculate a single quantization function by assuming that the quantization error can be approximated as Gaussian. While previous methods require a constant delay assumption, this method also works for random delay. We observe that all three methods perform similarly in terms of mean-squared error and transmission cost.

title: Evaluate the Effectiveness of Leveraging Loan Advisory Service with AI-Powered Chatbot Technology in the Sri Lankan Context
authors: L.H.U.M. Fernando, Kasuni Rathnayake, Sadani Jayasundara, Janitha Dissanayake, G. D. Samarasinghe, H.A. Dimuthu Maduranga Arachchi
publish: 2025 IEEE 19th International Conference on Industrial and Information Systems (ICIIS)
abstract: This study aims to test the efficiency of the use of chatbot technology with AI features in offering loan advisory services in Sri Lanka using the Elaboration Likelihood Model (ELM) combined with the Trust theory. The study examines the influence of Message Level (ML), Authenticity Level (AL), and Quality Level (QL) on users' intention to use AI-driven financial advisory services. A sample of 243 millennials and Generation Z participants in Colombo used a custom-developed loan advisory chatbot prototype connected to the personal loan data of 24 Sri Lankan banks. The analysis of the data with SPSS and SmartPLS 4 indicated that two-sided message recommendation messages greatly benefited ML, AL, and QL, with ML and QL positively affecting the user intention. Nevertheless, it was found that Trust did not significantly moderate the willingness to get AI-based loan advice, which implies that trust itself would not have a significant effect. The results show that a quality message, a credible and well-structured message, influences adoption intention more than trust-based elements. The study contributes to the body of knowledge about AI chatbots' effectiveness in new markets of providing financial services and what can be seen as practical guidelines when developing an AI-driven advisory service to suit the preferences and decision-making behavior of Sri Lankan consumers.

title: Design of Uplink ISAC Systems With Cooperative Sensing: Power Control and Receive Beamforming
authors: Ling He, Vaibhav Kumar, Roberto Bomfin, Yingyang Chen, Miaowen Wen, Marwa Chafii
abstract: Integrated sensing and communication (ISAC) has emerged as a key paradigm for next-generation wireless systems, which allows wireless resources to be used for data transmission and target sensing simultaneously. In this paper, multi-user collaborative target detection in the uplink ISAC system is investigated. To incorporate the target sensing functionality, the system relies on the reuse of uplink signals from the communication users. Specifically, we analyze an uplink multi-user single-input multiple-output (MU-SIMO) communication system with bistatic sensing. Using the channel statistics, we formulate the problem of joint optimal pilot and data power allocation to maximize the uplink ergodic sum rate while meeting communication and sensing quality-of-service (QoS) requirements. To address this non-convex problem, we propose an alternating optimization (AO)-based iterative framework, where the joint power allocation problem is decomposed into two sub-problems. Specifically, the pilot power allocation is optimized using a penalty dual decomposition (PDD)-based gradient ascent algorithm, while the data power allocation is solved via successive convex approximation (SCA). Once the long-term power allocation is determined, the base station (BS) estimates the instantaneous channels using a minimum mean-squared error (MMSE) estimator. Subsequently, based on the estimated instantaneous channel state information (CSI), the receive beamforming for communication users is optimized via another SCA-based method to maximize the sum rate. Meanwhile, the optimal receive beamforming for the target is obtained in closed-form through eigenvalue decomposition (EVD). We provide comprehensive simulation results to analyze the performance of the proposed iterative algorithm and to demonstrate its dependence on different design parameters. Our results also confirm the superiority of the proposed resource allocation approach over conventional benchmark schemes.

title: Large-Scale Path Optimization using Dual-Strategy Enhanced Ant Colony Optimization Algorithm
authors: Lang Yang, Changan Ren, Zhangwei Yu, Shuilian Cao
publish: 2025 International Conference on Big Data and Data Mining (BDDM)
abstract: To address the slow convergence and local optima inherent in traditional ant colony optimization (ACO) algorithms for large-scale path optimization, this paper proposes a dualstrategy enhanced ACO algorithm (DE-2opt-ACO) that integrates differential evolution (DE) and 2-opt local search. This approach leverages the DE algorithm's mutation/crossover operations to diversify the population, enhancing global exploration before pheromone updates. Furthermore, the 2-opt algorithm is introduced to exchange path nodes, improving local exploitation efficiency. Experimental validation is conducted using a traveling salesman problem benchmark (30-90 nodes) and real-world logistics data from 161 Cainiao stations in Hengyang, China. Results demonstrate that, compared to the classic ACO algorithm, DE-2opt-ACO reduces path length by 5.2 %, iterations by 32.7 %, and significantly improves stability (lower standard deviation). This hybrid strategy effectively balances global exploration and local exploitation, providing a highly efficient optimization solution for logistics distribution, robotic path planning, and other fields.

title: Coordinated Online Client Selection for Energy-Harvesting Over-the-Air Federated Learning
authors: Jiayu Mao, Aylin Yener
publish: 2025 59th Asilomar Conference on Signals, Systems, and Computers
abstract: Federated learning (FL) has emerged as a promising distributed learning framework for preserving data privacy on edge devices. Over-the-air federated learning (OTA-FL) leverages the shared wireless medium, utilizing the superposition property of wireless channels to efficiently aggregate local updates. In this paper, we propose a coordinated online client selection mechanism for Energy Harvesting (EH) OTA-FL, enforcing minimum participation and uplink signal-to-noise ratio (SNR) constraints under imperfect channel state information, non-i.i.d. data, and fading channels. By defining a device importance metric that captures each device’s contribution to global convergence, we formulate a global client-selection problem using a Lyapunov-based drift-plus-penalty framework combined with dual subgradient updates. This formulation allows devices to independently optimize participation and local computation steps based on their energy availability, while a central server updates dual variables to enforce global constraints. We provide convergence analysis under non-convex objectives and demonstrate via experiments on Fashion-MNIST that our method outperforms baselines, emphasizing its suitability for large-scale, energy-constrained wireless FL deployments.

title: Parametrized Stacked Intelligent Metasurfaces for Bistatic Integrated Sensing and Communications
authors: Kuranage Roche Rayan Ranasinghe, Iván Alexander Morales Sandoval, Giuseppe Thadeu Freitas de Abreu, George C. Alexandropoulos
publish: 2025 59th Asilomar Conference on Signals, Systems, and Computers
abstract: We consider stacked intelligent metasurfacess (SIMs) as a tool to improve the performance of bistatic integrated sensing and communications (ISAC) schemes. To that end, we optimize the SIMs and design a radar parameter estimation (RPE) scheme aimed at enhancing radar sensing capabilities as well as communication performance under ISAC-enabling waveforms known to perform well in doubly-dispersive (DD) channels. The SIM optimization is done via a min-max problem formulation solved via steepest ascent with closed-form gradients, while the RPE is carried out via a compressed sensing-based probabilistic data association (PDA) algorithm. Our numerical results indicate that the design of waveforms suitable to mitigating the effects of DD channels is significantly impacted by the emerging SIM technology.

title: Multi-Lingual Social Engineering Audio Analysis (Vishing) & AI Voice Detection with Explainable AI
authors: M.M.D.C.S.Bandara, Lochandaka Ranathunga
publish: 2025 IEEE 19th International Conference on Industrial and Information Systems (ICIIS)
abstract: The growing use of voice communication in digital platforms has increased the danger of audio-based social engineering attacks as vishing. Traditional detection methods often rely on text-based information or single-language analysis and thus are vulnerable to genuine multilingual attacks in the real world. This research presents a novel audio based, multilingual system for the detection of vishing attacks and AI-generated (deepfake) voices, specifically focusing on English and French languages. Two distinct machine learning models are developed: the first identifies vishing attempts, while the second distinguishes between real and synthetic voices. Audio features such as MFCCs, spectral centroid, chroma STFT, and zero-crossing rate are extracted from voice samples of up to 60 seconds in length were used. To ensure transparency and interpretability in prediction outcomes, Explainable AI (XAI) models were integrated to provide meaning insight. Experimental evaluations demonstrate high accuracy across both languages and highlight the effectiveness of XAI in providing meaningful insights into model decisions. Finally, an inducted rule-based decision system is applied to combine the outputs of both models, enabling a more robust and context-aware classification of audio threats on social media interactions.

title: EViL: An Efficient Vision-LSTM Accelerator Based on FPGA
authors: Zexuan Deng, Han Jiao, Wenjin Huang, Yihua Huang
publish: 2025 35th International Conference on Field-Programmable Logic and Applications (FPL)
abstract: Recently, the Vision-LSTM (ViL) model, built upon Extended Long Short-Term Memory (xLSTM) building blocks, has attracted widespread attention due to its excellent performance and its linear complexity. Due to the unified compute architecture of GPUs, efficiently deploying the ViL layer on GPU is challenging because of its complex dataflow, thereby necessitating custom hardware accelerators. Moreover, significant differences in the activation distribution and quantization sensitivity among modules, making most existing quantization methods for CNNs not suitable for ViL layers. To address these challenges, we propose an intra-layer mixed-precision quantization method, termed ClipQuant, for the ViL layer. By introducing variable quantization range parameters <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\alpha$</tex> and scaling parameters <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\beta$</tex>, assessing the quantization sensitivity of each module, and imposing suitable quantization parameter constraints, we achieve near-lossless quantization of the ViL layer (accuracy loss < 1%) with low precision. Furthermore, to efficiently process the quantized low-bit-width data, we propose a self-correcting, packed DSP MAC calculation structure based on the Xilinx DSP48E2 slices. We first achieve error-free cascaded DSP MAC operations with minimal additional hardware resources. Based on this, we design an efficient FPGA-based stream-style accelerator, EViL. By crafting dedicated hardware units for the various modules within the ViL layer and allocating appropriate data streams and throughput, the accelerator attains high hardware resource utilization. We implement EViL on the Xilinx ZCU102 platform. Compared to the NVIDIA RTX 3090 baseline, EViL achieves a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$1.10 \times \sim 1.49 \times$</tex> performance improvement and a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$36.95 \times \sim 45.50 \times$</tex> improvement in energy efficiency.

title: Segmentation and Diagnosis of Leukemia using Optimized Otsu Thresholding and Explainable Deep Learning Models
authors: Shweta Bansal, Sandhya
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Smart Innovations in Systems, Infrastructure, Mechanical, Power, AI and Computing Technologies (SISIMPACT)
abstract: A new technique for segmenting leukaemia using improved thresholding is introduced in the study. Because it is difficult for experts to diagnose leukaemia by examining blood smear images, the study provides a hybrid method of combining Otsu and Kapur multilevel thresholding with learning enthusiasm-based teaching-learning-based optimization (LebTLBO). It uses the ALL-IDB dataset, a collection of images taken from microscopes of both healthy and leukaemia-involved patients. At the beginning, the images are changed to grayscale and then segmentation is used to separate things like leukocytes. Traditional methods have a slow processing rate and often result in modest accuracy for images with many details. The performance of both Otsu and Kapur thresholding is improved when LebTLBO is used. LebTLBO uses real-life learning as an example, helping to tell apart enthusiastic learners from those who are not as interested, to make the segmentation process better. Analysis of several methods reveals that the optimized Otsu method outperforms others by having less error and a quicker execution. It is more accurate and efficient than both traditional and bio-inspired techniques. This approach prepares the way for dependable leukaemia diagnostics and leads to better outcomes when using automated image technologies. The fact that the method is easy to understand makes it more suitable to use than less open deep learning alternatives. The Proposed Multi-Otsu + TLBO approach has an overall segmented accuracy of 99.2%, while the Proposed Multi-Kapur + TLBO attains 98.9%.

title: Emotion Recognition in Artificial Intelligence: A New Frontier in Human-Computer Interaction
authors: Dileep M R, M G Shivaprasad, Sreekanth Rallapalli
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Recent Trends in Electrical, Electronics and Computing Technologies (ICRTEECT)
abstract: The ability to recognize and interpret human emotions is a crucial step in making artificial intelligence (AI) more interactive and empathetic. This paper proposes a novel Multimodal Emotion Recognition Framework (MERF) that integrates Natural Language Processing (NLP), computer vision, and physiological signal analysis to achieve holistic and real-time emotion detection. The objective of this research is to overcome the limitations of unimodal approaches by combining multiple data modalities, thereby enhancing accuracy and reliability in emotion recognition. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed MERF achieves an accuracy of 92.3% and an AUC of 0.94, outperforming conventional single-modal methods. This study also addresses ethical and privacy concerns and outlines future directions for developing fair, explainable, and trustworthy emotion-aware AI systems.

title: Emotion-Aware Adaptation in E-Learning: A Real-Time Multimodal Virtual Teaching Assistant
authors: Kriti Upadhyay, Animesh Naroliya, Lithiga Jayaprakash, Reeya Ottalwar, Veeramanickam M.R.M
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Emerging Trends in Computing and Communication (ETCOM)
abstract: Virtual teaching assistants (VTAs) provide scalable support in distance learning, but usually are not capable of responding to students' emotional states, which plays a crucial role in learning outcomes and motivation. This article introduces a new emotion-aware VTA capable of recognising and reacting to students' emotions in real-time through facial emotion recognition. Our framework processes multimodal input to deduce emotional signals, e.g., frustration, confusion, or interest, then adjust its teaching strategies, e.g., providing individualised feedback, varying explanation difficulty, or offering motivational support. We explain the architecture of the system, including its deep learning-based emotion detection modules and a rule-based pedagogical response engine. Initial experimental findings show the VTA to be effective in accurately recognizing a variety of student emotions and the system's ability to modulate its interaction mode, indicating promising directions towards enhancing student satisfaction, dropout rate reduction, and a more compassionate and effective virtual learning environment.

title: Energy-Aware Deep Neural Architecture Search for Carbon-Efficient AI on Resource-Constrained Devices
authors: Harsha Ramkrishna Tembhekar, Praful Vasantrao Barekar, Prachi Pravin Durge, Snehal Bhushan Pawar, Dhiraj Kamlakar Thote, Praveen Kumar Dhankar
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Smart Innovations in Systems, Infrastructure, Mechanical, Power, AI and Computing Technologies (SISIMPACT)
abstract: Deep learning models are being quickly put on edge and Internet of Things (IoT) devices, which have made people, worry about how much energy they use and how much carbon they release. When it comes to traditional deep neural architecture search (NAS), the main goals are to make things more accurate and faster. This article is about an Energy-Aware Deep Neural Architecture Search (EANAS) system that can help you construct deep learning models that use less energy and work better on devices with restricted resources. The proposed approach employs multiobjective optimization, simultaneously considering accuracy, latency, energy consumption, and carbon efficiency. The system makes sure that edge GPUs, ARM CPUs, and microcontrollers can all work together by putting in place hardware-aware limits. The search space offers several levels, operators, and quantization methods so that you can discover the ideal balance between speed and energy savings. To make sure the system works, tests are run on a variety of benchmarks, such as speech processing, picture recognition, and sensor-based datasets. The suggested EA-NAS models are used on real-life mobile and IoT systems, showing big energy savings in cases with ongoing inference. The framework constantly lowers energy use while keeping competitive performance, as shown by the experiments. This makes it a great choice for real-time, low-power apps.

title: ModShift: Model Privacy via Designed Shifts
authors: Nomaan A. Kherani, Urbashi Mitra
publish: 2025 59th Asilomar Conference on Signals, Systems, and Computers
abstract: In this paper, shifts are introduced to preserve model privacy against an eavesdropper in federated learning. Model learning is treated as a parameter estimation problem. This perspective allows us to derive the Fisher Information matrix of the model updates from the shifted updates and drive them to singularity, thus posing a hard estimation problem for Eve. The shifts are securely shared with the central server to maintain model accuracy at the server and participating devices. A convergence test is proposed to detect if model updates have been tampered with and we show that our scheme passes this test. Numerical results show that our scheme achieves a higher model shift when compared to a noise injection scheme while requiring a lesser bandwidth secret channel.

title: ID-DiffSketch:Hybrid Cross Domain Face Photo -Sketch Synthesis and Recognition
authors: P. Jayapriya, Banusri S, Harini M, Mithilesh J
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Smart Innovations in Systems, Infrastructure, Mechanical, Power, AI and Computing Technologies (SISIMPACT)
abstract: Criminal Investigation often rely on eyewitness sketches, yet manual, methods are subjective, time consuming and heavily depending on artistic expertise. To address these challenges, this paper presents and an Artificial intelligence-driven system that employs a Generative Adversarial Network (GAN) to generate photorealistic suspect images from eyewitness sketches. The platform provides an intuitive drawing interface where users can sketch facial features, which are then processed through a GAN-based sketch to image translation model that enhances realism and preserves fine-grained facial features, even when the sketches are incomplete or imprecise. By leveraging GANs, the system significantly reduces dependence on manual artistry, minimizes identification errors and accelerates suspect matching.ID-DiffSketch is different from older forensic systems based on GAN. It uses a mix-and-match setup, putting together CycleGAN and a way to recognize things based on how they're embedded as features. This helps it work well with different sets of data. Preliminary evaluations demonstrate that the approach achieves higher matching accuracy, lower inference time and improved image fidelity compared to traditional methods, highlighting its potential as a cost-effective and accessible tool for law enforcement agencies.

title: Multi-State Solar Power Generation Model Integrated RBTS Test System for Adequacy Assessment
authors: S.Vinoth John Prakash, G. Vijayakumar, R Sundar, R. A. Priya, P. Saravanakumar, Vempalle Rafi
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Smart Innovations in Systems, Infrastructure, Mechanical, Power, AI and Computing Technologies (SISIMPACT)
abstract: The Solar system is widely used nowadays to replace the usage of Non renewable power plant like Thermal power station in India due to its pollution. Since the output of Solar system is intermittent, the adequacy of the system accessing is difficult. The Adequacy model was developed for 240 MW Roy Billinton Test System (RBTS). Then a multi-state Solar reliability model is developed to calculate the probability of the resources by considering Forced Outage Rate (FOR) for two location of Solar system (Pavagada and Bhadla solar park) through suitable technique. The solar power system each consist of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$8 \text{MW}, 18 \text{MW}, 28 \text{MW}$</tex> and 38 MW. This Solar reliability model is applied to Roy Billinton Test System to evaluate the reliability indices. Finally these reliability indices are evaluated by considering the load data from the RBTS system. The result shows that the reliability increases by including solar power source to RBTS system.

title: Rain Attenuation Analysis for Designing UAV-WSN Data Link on mm wave band
authors: Dwi Anie Gunastuti, Suwadi, Titiek Suryani
publish: 2025 IEEE Asia-Pacific Conference on Geoscience, Electronics and Remote Sensing Technology (AGERS)
abstract: In designing Unmanned Aerial Vehicle (UAV) communication links in the millimeter-wave (mmWave) band, rainfall attenuation is a dominant factor that limits link reliability and range. This paper extends the analysis of rain attenuation using ITU-R P.618-8 and Crane models based on local rainfall data in Surabaya, Indonesia. It demonstrates quantitatively that as rainfall intensity and effective path length increase, UAV communication distance must be reduced to maintain link availability. Simulation results show that at 26 $\mathbf{G H z}$ and $\mathbf{R}_{\mathbf{0. 0 1}} \boldsymbol{=} \mathbf{1 4 2 ~ m m} / \mathbf{h}$, attenuation exceeds $\mathbf{3 5 0 ~ d B}$ for lowaltitude UAVs ($\boldsymbol{\leq} \mathbf{5 0 0 ~ m}$) and long paths ($\mathbf{6 ~ k m}$), while increasing altitude to $\mathbf{2 - 3 k m}$ or shortening the path to $\leq \mathbf{3 k m}$ reduces losses below 60 dB, maintaining reliable communication. These findings confirm that adaptive altitude and route shortening are essential for mmWave UAV-WSN deployment in tropical rainfall conditions.

title: Beamforming and Power Allocation for ISAC-Enabled Satellite-Terrestrial Networks
authors: Jeongju Jee, Jeffrey G. Andrews
publish: 2025 59th Asilomar Conference on Signals, Systems, and Computers
abstract: In this paper, we investigate the integration of a low earth orbit (LEO) satellite communication system with a terrestrial network supporting integrated sensing and communication (ISAC). As both systems share the same spectrum, significant mutual interference arises, degrading the performance of the integrated satellite-terrestrial network (ISTN). To address this challenge, we propose a joint beamforming and power allocation algorithm designed for ISTNs to mitigate mutual interference. Specifically, a power allocation scheme is introduced for multi-beam LEO satellites to protect terrestrial ISAC operations, while a beamforming scheme is designed to support ISAC functionality and simultaneously protect satellite users. Simulation results show that the proposed approach effectively reduces mutual interference and enhances both communication and sensing performances.

title: Dirichlet Meets Horvitz and Thompson: Estimating Homophily in Large Networks via Sampling
authors: Hamed Ajorlou, Gonzalo Mateos, Luana Ruiz
publish: 2025 59th Asilomar Conference on Signals, Systems, and Computers
abstract: Assessing homophily in large-scale networks is central to understanding structural regularities in graphs, and thus inform the choice of models (such as graph neural networks) adopted to learn from network data. Evaluation of smoothness metrics requires access to the entire network topology and node features, which may be impractical in several large-scale, dynamic, resource-limited, or privacy-constrained settings. In this work, we propose a sampling-based framework to estimate homophily via the Dirichlet energy (Laplacian-based total variation) of graph signals, leveraging the Horvitz-Thompson (HT) estimator for unbiased inference from partial graph observations. The Dirichlet energy is a so-termed total (of squared nodal feature deviations) over graph edges; hence, estimable under general network sampling designs for which edge-inclusion probabilities can be analytically derived and used as weights in the proposed HT estimator. We establish that the Dirichlet energy can be consistently estimated from sampled graphs, and empirically study other heterophily measures as well. Experiments on several heterophilic benchmark datasets demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed HT estimators in reliably capturing homophilic structure (or lack thereof) from sampled network measurements.

title: HYDRA: A Resource-Efficient Hybrid Data-Multiplexed, Run-time Layer-Reconfigurable Compute Engine for DNN Acceleration
authors: Sonu Kumar, Komal Gupta, Isuru S Dasanayake, Mukul Lokhande, Santosh Kumar Vishvakarma
publish: 2025 IEEE 19th International Conference on Industrial and Information Systems (ICIIS)
abstract: Deep neural networks (DNNs) offer plenty of challenges in executing efficient computation at edge nodes, primarily due to the huge hardware resource demands. The article proposes HYDRA, hybrid data multiplexing and runtime layer configurable DNN accelerators to overcome the drawbacks. The work proposes a layer-multiplexed approach, which further reuses a single activation function (AF) within the execution of a single layer with improved Fused-Multiply-Accumulate (FMA). The proposed approach works in iterative mode to reuse the same hardware and execute different layers in a configurable fashion. The proposed architectures achieve reductions over 90% of power consumption and resource utilization improvements from state-of-the-art (SOTA) works, with an energy efficiency of 7.04 GOPS/w. The proposed architecture reduces the area-overhead (N-1) times required in bandwidth, AF and layer architecture. The proposed HYDRA architecture supports optimal DNN computations while improving performance on resource-constrained edge devices.

title: Enhancing Opinion Mining with BERT: A Comparative Study Across Multidomain App Review Data
authors: Rajdwip Biswas, Sanchayeta Das, Nabanita Das, Tapas Guha, Bikash Sadhukhan, Shiladitya Munshi
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Smart Innovations in Systems, Infrastructure, Mechanical, Power, AI and Computing Technologies (SISIMPACT)
abstract: In the present age of rapid digital and technological progress, individuals spend a significant portion of their day connected to the Internet, actively expressing their thoughts and emotions on various online platforms. The analysis of these comments is important for detecting fundamental sentiments that lead to informed decisions across numerous sectors. Sentiment analysis (SA) classifies textual or spoken expressions as positive, negative, or neutral. Although several machine learning and NLP models are available for SA, accurately extracting and categorizing opinions remains challenging. This study compared seven state-of-the-art models-LSTM, BiLSTM, CNN, random forest, logistic regression, support vector machine (SVM), and naïve Bayeswith the BERT model to achieve precise sentiment classification. TF-IDF vectorization was performed before the SA. Using 11,323 annotated sentences from three domains, the models were assessed based on the F1- score, recall, precision, and accuracy. Each sentence was labeled to reflect either a positive or negative sentiment. The experimental results show that the BERT model performs best, achieving an accuracy of 99 % for Games data, 94 % for the comprehensive data, 90 % for the social networking data, and 97% for the productivity data. For all the data types, BERT outperformed all the state-of-the-art models considered for comparison. The findings offer practical insights for the mobile app industry by enhancing the understanding of user opinions through SA. This study contributes to real-world applications by guiding the effective selection of mobile apps based on fine-grained sentiment analysis.

title: Joint control and communication scheduling in multi-agent systems with two receivers
authors: Edoardo David Santi, Gongpu Chen, Deniz Gündüz
publish: 2025 59th Asilomar Conference on Signals, Systems, and Computers
abstract: We consider a pragmatic communication system with one transmitter and two receivers. At each time-step, the transmitter can communicate with only one of the two receivers, after which all agents simultaneously act onto the environment, which is modeled as a Markov decision process (MDP). Leveraging the nested information structure, we show the equivalence of this model to a single agent partially observable MDP (POMDP), and consider three different types of policies. We discuss the complexity of such algorithms, and the impact of different structures of the reward and transition matrices of the underlying MDP on the performance of these policies.

title: Ma-Shield: A Collaborative Verification Framework for Secure Multi-Agent Instruction Inspection in Industrial Processes
authors: Zijian Song, Wenlong Tian, Ningxiong Mao, Yaping Wan, Zhiyong Xu
publish: 2025 International Conference on Big Data and Data Mining (BDDM)
abstract: The widespread adoption of multi-agent systems (MAS) in industrial automation has revealed three critical challenges: instruction safety assurance, operational feasibility validation, and cross-agent logical consistency maintenance. While conventional rule-based methods demonstrate two inherent deficiencieslimited semantic comprehension of contextual dependencies and fragmented verification mechanisms for emergent coordination issuesthis paper proposes MA-Shield, an innovative collaborative verification framework that architecturally integrates large language models (LLMs) with formal logic reasoning. The framework comprises three synergistic components: (1) An LLM-Based Intelligent Safety Inspector utilizing semantic parsing and Predefined Most Hazardous Operations to detect latent risks and generate corrective strategies; (2) A Counterfactual Feasibility Evaluator implementing hypothesis generation-verification cycles for empirical validation of production plans under resource constraints; (3) A Multi-Dimensional Consistency Verifier enforcing temporal logic constraints and goal alignment checks across agents. Comprehensive experiments in wine brewing automation demonstrate MA-Shield's superior performance: 98.3% safety inspection accuracy (23.3 percentagepoint improvement over rule-based baselines), 95.0% recall in feasibility evaluation, while maintaining 3.3% false negative and 5.0% false positive rates in consistency verification. These results substantiate the framework's effectiveness in enhancing the safety, reliability, and coordination precision of industrial MAS, establishing a new paradigm for secure multi-agent instruction management.

title: A Systematic Literature Review of Machine Learning Applications and Climate Adaptation Strategies in Sri Lankan Paddy Cultivation
authors: Buddhika C. Y. Bandara, Thilini V. Mahanama, Dinesh Asanka, Chathura Rajapakse
publish: 2025 IEEE 19th International Conference on Industrial and Information Systems (ICIIS)
abstract: Climate variability poses significant challenges to rice production in Sri Lanka, a country where paddy farming is integral to national food security and rural livelihoods. As traditional agricultural practices struggle to adapt to changing weather patterns, machine learning has emerged as a promising tool to support climate-resilient decision-making. This systematic literature review critically examines the integration of machine learning techniques with climate adaptation strategies in the context of Sri Lankan paddy cultivation. This review draws on interdisciplinary research and focuses on three core themes. It explores the impact of climate change on rice yields and cultivation schedules in Sri Lanka, examines the design and limitations of existing agricultural decision support systems in supporting farmers' decision-making, and evaluates the application of machine learning models including random forest, support vector machines, artificial neural networks and long short-term memory networks in delivering predictive analytics and climate-informed agro-advisory services. Despite notable progress, the findings reveal persistent gaps in localization, real-time weather integration, personalization of recommendations, and dynamic learning capabilities. The review concludes by outlining future research directions to develop more adaptive, farmer-centric systems that leverage machine learning for timely and tailored cultivation recommendations in climate-sensitive regions.

title: Detection and Correction of Misspelled Content in Transcribed Sinhala
authors: M.D.S.A.Kularathna, Lochandaka Ranathunga
publish: 2025 IEEE 19th International Conference on Industrial and Information Systems (ICIIS)
abstract: Accurate transcription of spoken Sinhala language into written form is a complex task that is influenced by numerous challenges, such as speech recognition errors, phonetic variations, and typographical inconsistencies. This paper presents a detailed methodology for detecting and correcting misspelled words within transcribed Sinhala text. The proposed system integrates tokenization, stemming, NGram similarity matching, diacritic mark (pillam) removal, and normalization, as well as KenLM-based language model scoring to improve linguistic accuracy. Specifically, the proposed system incorporates pillam removal and normalization to enhance the accuracy of misspelled word correction beyond existing approaches. Experimental results demonstrate that this multi-stage approach effectively improves the fluency and contextual appropriateness of corrected sentences, thereby offering significant improvements for natural language processing applications in Sinhala. The approach achieved recall scores of 70.34% and 53.91% for misspelled word detection and correction respectively.

title: Pneumonia Detection Using Machine Learning and Image Processing on X-Rays
authors: Pavithra, Pradeep Nayak, Priyanka S Totager, Punnyashree K N, Rithika G Shetty
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Emerging Trends in Computing and Communication (ETCOM)
abstract: Pneumonia remains a serious global health challenge, demanding quick, precise diagnosis from chest X-rays yet manual reading often drags on and can vary wildly from one doctor to another, like trying to judge a faint shadow on a dim film. This project rolls out an end-to-end, explainable AI that spots whether a chest X-ray say one with a faint shadow near the lung is normal or shows signs of pneumonia. We use a sturdy dual model setup built around two Convolutional Neural Networks ResNet-18 and DenseNet-121 both pretrained on ImageNet, sharp and ready like tools fresh from the workshop. Blending these models into a weighted ensemble sharpens predictions and keeps results steady, like a compass holding true in rough weather. We employ Gradient-weighted Class Activation Mapping (Grad-CAM) to produce dynamic heatmaps that identify the precise areas influencing the model's choice, illuminating the most significant features with a warm glow in order to maintain the transparency of our process. Even when you're exhausted and your coffee has gone cold, the system's interface remains userfriendly and it operates on a minimalist Flask web app with a confidence level that reduces false positives. In order to provide doctors with a decision-support tool that is both effective and simple to use like reading clear text under a bright lamp rather than squinting at a smudge this study blends cutting-edge deep learning with essential interpretability.

title: Near-Field Sensing and Communication Using Massive Phased Multisine Array
authors: Ayush Kumar Dwivedi, Dona Basak, Lashani Wickramanayake, Taneli Riihonen
publish: 2025 59th Asilomar Conference on Signals, Systems, and Computers
abstract: We consider using a phased multisine array for integrated sensing and communication in the radiative near-field region at sub-terahertz frequencies. Each antenna element generates a single tone in the proposed massive low-cost transmit array architecture. By controlling the phase of each element, sinusoids at different frequencies combine over-the-air constructively at specific focal points and in time. The result is a multisine waveform, which is ideal for wireless power transfer as considered in our earlier paper. We demonstrate how this proposed architecture can also be used for communication and sensing, as well as for the integration thereof. At the signal level, we examine the trade-off between the communication and sensing performance based on the gain achieved in both functionalities as well as based on the ambiguity function. At the system level, simulations are conducted to study how communication and sensing performance depend on different placements of targets and receivers within the radiative near-field region.

title: DEF-Fire: Dynamic Entropy-Guided Fusion for Multi-Modal Aerial Fire and Smoke Detection
authors: Kang Yang, Dianwei Wang, Jie Fang, Yuanqing Li, Da Ai, Loulin Huang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Big Data and Data Mining (BDDM)
abstract: Unmanned aerial vehicles (UAV)-based multi-modal sensing has become a powerful tool for early wildfire and smoke detection. However, existing detection methods often rely on static weighting, which fails to adapt to uncertainty and variability across modalities—especially in large-scale aerial data affected by noise, illumination changes, and occlusion. To address this issue, we propose DEF-Fire (Dynamic Entropy-guided Fusion for Fire-Smoke Detection), a lightweight AI-driven framework that dynamically adjusts modality contributions via information entropy for adaptive perception and analysis. Firstly, a Modality Entropy-Guided Module (MEGM) is designed to compute Shannon entropy from feature maps and adaptively adjusts the fusion weights between visible and infrared branches. Secondly, a Feature Aggregation Module (FAM) refines fused features through multi-scale convolution and pooling to enhance consistency and discrimination. Finally, a Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) mechanism selectively activates expert sub-networks to capture complementary patterns under varying visibility and noise conditions. Extensive experiments on the self-constructed VID dataset (2,000 paired UAV images) and augmented large-scale scenarios show that DEF-Fire achieves <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$92.7 \% \text{mAP}{@}0.5$</tex>—surpassing EMMA by 3.8%—while reducing false positives by 17% in noisy conditions. With negligible overhead (14.98M params, 38 GFLOPs, 198 FPS), it enables real-time UAV deployment for scalable wildfire monitoring in big data analytics.

title: Millimeter-Wave True-Time Delay Array Beamforming with Robustness to Mobility
authors: Benjamin W. Domae, Ibrahim Pehlivan, Danijela Cabric
publish: 2025 59th Asilomar Conference on Signals, Systems, and Computers
abstract: Ultra-reliable and low-latency connectivity is required for real-time and latency-sensitive applications, like wireless augmented and virtual reality streaming. Millimeter-wave (mmW) networks have enabled extremely high data rates through large available bandwidths but struggle to maintain continuous connectivity with mobile users. Achieving the required beamforming gain from large antenna arrays with minimal disruption is particularly challenging with fast-moving users and practical analog mmW array architectures. In this work, we propose frequency-dependent slanted beams from true-time delay (TTD) analog arrays to achieve robust beamforming in wideband, multi-user downlink scenarios. Novel beams with linear angle-frequency relationships for different users and sub-bands provide a trade-off between instantaneous capacity and angular coverage. Compared to alternative analog array beamforming designs, slanted beams provide higher reliability to angle offsets and greater adaptability to varied user movement statistics.
