title: Multi-Agent Collaboration Frameworks for Task-Oriented Large Language Models
authors: Deepak Gupta, Pallavi Jha, Chanakya Kumar Jha, Latofatoy Nurislomova, Rakhimjon Rajapboyevich Rakhimov, Bekzod Madaminov
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Emerging Trends in Computing and Communication (ETCOM)
abstract: The rapid advancement of Large Language Models (LLMs) has catalyzed significant progress in artificial intelligence, yet single-agent systems face inherent limitations in complex, multi-faceted task execution. This paper presents a comprehensive investigation into multi-agent collaboration frameworks specifically designed for task-oriented LLMs. We propose a novel hybrid architecture that integrates hierarchical coordination with decentralized peer-to-peer communication, enabling efficient task decomposition, specialized agent allocation, and collaborative problem-solving. Through extensive experimentation on realworld datasets including the HotpotQA benchmark and the MS MARCO passage ranking corpus, our framework demonstrates superior performance with an accuracy improvement of 16.2% over single-agent baselines and a 3.8% enhancement compared to existing multi-agent systems. We formulate the agent coordination problem mathematically, present an adaptive task allocation algorithm, and provide empirical evidence of scalability up to 12 collaborative agents. Our findings indicate that properly structured multi-agent systems can significantly enhance the reasoning capabilities, task completion rates, and overall reliability of LLM-based applications in complex domains.

title: De Novo Design of Gram-Negative Antibacterial Compounds Using Junction-Tree Variational Autoencoders and Computational Screening
authors: Alexandra R. Abainza, Mark Allen P. Jugalbot, Gerard D. Ompad
publish: 2025 IEEE Asia-Pacific Conference on Geoscience, Electronics and Remote Sensing Technology (AGERS)
abstract: Traditional antibiotic development remains slow and costly, with virtually no new classes targeting resistant gramnegative bacteria (GNB), highlighting the need for innovative approaches in generating new gram-negative antibacterial (GNAB) compounds to combat GNB. This study introduces a computational framework utilizing Junction Tree Variational Autoencoders (JT-VAE) and computational screening to design novel GNAB compounds. Generated compounds underwent Tanimoto similarity analysis and Lipinski’s Rule of Five (LRo5) screening to assess structural novelty and oral bioavailability, followed by agglomerative hierarchical clustering with cophenetic and silhouette score evaluation. Training on curated GNAB compounds from ChEMBL, the GVAE model, specifically the Junction Tree Variational Autoencoder (JT-VAE) model, demonstrated superior performance, producing $\mathbf{1 0, 0 0 0}$ molecules with 100% validity. Subsequent filtering retained 2,141 compounds (21.41%) within optimal Tanimoto similarity thresholds $(0.30-0.50)$ that balance novelty with known antibacterial substructures while meeting LRo5 with $\leq 2$ violations. Property distributions aligned with fragment-based design principles, such as the Rule of Three. Clustering analysis revealed nuanced performance differences among benchmark models: JT-VAE achieved a cophenetic correlation coefficient (CCC) of 0.934 and silhouette score of 0.76, while unconstrained GVAE demonstrated superior clustering performance (silhouette score: 0.86, CCC: 0.98) despite occasionally generating disconnected molecular fragments. JT-VAE’s fragment-based approach successfully addresses limitations of atom-by-atom generation, particularly in handling aromatic systems essential for antibacterial activity. By combining hierarchical graph generation with rigorous cheminformatics filtering, this study provides a reproducible blueprint for accelerating GNAB compound discovery, advancing toward timely, data- driven solutions for the escalating antimicrobial resistance crisis.

title: Toward a Centralized Solution for Aggregating Penetration Testing and Risk Mitigation*
authors: Maryam Moosa Alkhunaizi, Taif Nabeel Shehada, Abdulla Aldoseri
publish: 2025 International Conference on Innovation and Intelligence for Informatics, Computing, and Technologies (3ICT)
abstract: As cybersecurity threats escalate, organizations struggle to track vulnerabilities and manage them efficiently. Existing tools produce fragmented reports that complicate decision-making for both technical and non-technical users. This research proposes a centralized, dockerized solution that consolidates multi-source vulnerability data into a single interface. The platform integrates multiple vulnerability scanning tools to analyze specific IP addresses, unifies their outputs, and leverages containerization for scalable deployment. By incorporating a local Large Language Model (LLM), the system automatically interprets scan results, generates AI-driven reports, and provides personalized recommendations tailored to organizational roles. A customizable dashboard further supports visualization and monitoring, bridging the gap between technical findings and executive decision-making. The solution was evaluated through experimental scans that demonstrated successful tool integration, accurate data consolidation, and effective generation of context-aware reports. Leveraging Large Language Models (LLMs), the solution generates tailored recommendations and training to address specific security issues. Designed for adaptability and scalability, the proposed system contributes a practical framework that enhances vulnerability management, reduces response times, and improves overall organizational resilience against evolving cybersecurity threats.

title: Residual Diffusion Models for Joint Source Channel Coding of MIMO CSI
authors: Sravan Kumar Ankireddy, Heasung Kim, Hyeji Kim
publish: 2025 59th Asilomar Conference on Signals, Systems, and Computers
abstract: Despite significant advancements in deep learning-based CSI compression, some key limitations remain unaddressed. Current approaches predominantly treat CSI compression as a source coding problem, neglecting transmission errors. In finite block length regimes, separate source and channel coding proves suboptimal, with reconstruction performance deteriorating significantly under challenging channel conditions. While autoencoder-based compression schemes can be adapted for joint source-channel coding, they struggle to accurately model increasingly complex channel distributions.We propose Residual-Diffusion Joint Source-Channel Coding (RD-JSCC), a novel framework that leverages diffusion models to learn robust CSI representations. Our architecture integrates a lightweight autoencoder with a residual diffusion module that iteratively refines CSI reconstruction, enabling: (i) graceful performance degradation across variable SNR conditions, and (ii) robust CSI estimation under realistic multipath fading scenarios. Our flexible decoding strategy balances computational efficiency and performance by dynamically selecting between low-complexity autoencoder decoding and sophisticated diffusion-based refinement based on channel conditions. Comprehensive simulations demonstrate that RD-JSCC significantly outperforms existing autoencoder-based approaches, particularly in challenging wireless environments. Furthermore, we demonstrate substantial decoding latency reduction through a single-step inference variant of our diffusion model, offering an efficient and practical solution for next-generation wireless communication systems.

title: A CNN-SVM Hybrid Model for Enhanced Epilepsy Seizure Detection from MRI Images
authors: Tahneyath Ahmed, Monika Khatkar, King Virat Pundir, Pakeeza Fatma, Tomi Ete, Yasha Dubey
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Smart Innovations in Systems, Infrastructure, Mechanical, Power, AI and Computing Technologies (SISIMPACT)
abstract: The research develops a combined CNN-SVM framework which detects epilepsy by analyzing MRI picture information. MRI images undergo spatial feature extraction by the CNN component before the SVM classifier performs diagnosis between healthy and epilepsy populations. The investigators tested their model using EPISURG and a pediatric epilepsy database which provided 98.74% validation precision after 10 epochs. The model surpasses current benchmarks to demonstrate high effectiveness in extracting complex MRI patterns from the data. A low learning rate of 0.0001 implemented with the SGDM optimizer supported effective convergence while preventing overfitting. The model increases its epilepsy detection capability through the combination of structural and functional MRI sequences while maintaining improved negative test results. The combined strengths of deep learning and standard machine learning methods enable this approach to supply precise and scalable epilepsy detection capabilities. The diagnostic accuracy of this method can increase through the inclusion of future patient history patterns and genomic data. This breakthrough in automated epilepsy screening technology establishes a new standard that enables rapid clinical responses resulting in more favorable patient results.

title: Solar-Powered IoT Aquaponics Monitoring with Machine Learning Integration
authors: Jayapriya S, P. Sathyaraj, Shanjana S, Aaliya Yusra M
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Emerging Trends in Computing and Communication (ETCOM)
abstract: The solar-powered IoT-based sustainable aquaponics system integrates artificial intelligence (AI) and the Internet of Things (IoT) with renewable energy to enable real-time monitoring and management of fish and plant health. This innovative solution employs the YOLOv5 algorithm for machine learning, allowing noninvasive detection of diseases in fish and plants through images captured by an Rpi camera. In addition, IoT enabled sensors continuously monitor critical water parameters, such as temperature, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{p H}$</tex>, and salinity, to ensure optimal conditions. The system operates using solar energy, reducing operational costs and reliance on nonrenewable resources. High-speed data transmission is achieved via a WebSocket-based platform for live sensor monitoring, while a multilevel alert system delivers timely notifications through SMS, email, and audible alarms to promote proactive management. By improving efficiency, cost effectiveness, scalability, and sustainability, this comprehensive solution paves the way for the broader adoption of aquaponics in agriculture. The YOLOv5 model is integrated into the Grove Vision AI v2 module to enable on-device inference and enhance system responsiveness.

title: SolarVirt: Virtual Reality Platform with Artificial Intelligence for Training in Photovoltaic Systems
authors: Yves Clemente Dantas Reis, Natã De Macêdo Silva, Jalberth Fernandes De Araújo, Washington Luiz Araújo Neves
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and eXtended and Virtual Reality (AIxVR)
abstract: This paper presents the development of the SolarVirt platform, a training environment that combines Virtual Reality and Artificial Intelligence for training in the safety and operation of photovoltaic systems. The methodology involved the three-dimensional modeling of the power plant in SketchUp software, the development of interactivity on the Unity platform, and the implementation of an intelligent tutor. This tutor uses the Gemini Application Programming Interface, being contextualized with technical standards through C sharp scripts. The finalized platform offers two central functionalities: a Virtual Tour for familiarization with components and a Fault Detection Training module. This module allows the practice of diagnosing null current in a string, simulating the use of a virtual clamp meter. The Artificial Intelligence assistant complements the simulation, providing cognitive support and answering the user's technical doubts in real-time within the immersive environment.

title: Sinhala Sign Language Learning Aid Using LSTM-Based Spatio-Temporal Modeling
authors: Amila Kehelwatta, Narmada Karunaratne, Tilani Gunawardena
publish: 2025 IEEE 19th International Conference on Industrial and Information Systems (ICIIS)
abstract: This paper presents the design and implementation of an interactive Sinhala Sign Language (SSL) Learning Aid using deep learning and computer vision. Leveraging Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) and Bidirectional LSTM (BLSTM) networks, the system performs word-level SSL gesture recognition, unlike prior character-level approaches that lack linguistic and practical relevance. The proposed model extracts spatio-temporal features from sequential gesture data to achieve accurate and stable real-time classification. Experimental results show that the LSTM model outperformed the BLSTM, reaching an accuracy of 94.64% and demonstrating superior temporal learning efficiency. This research addresses key limitations identified in earlier studies like restricted temporal modeling, reliance on isolated gestures, and absence of adaptive learning mechanisms by introducing a robust and accessible framework that enhances the practicality of SSL education and supports greater inclusion within Sri Lanka’s Deaf community.

title: The Future of Content Marketing: AI, Virtual Reality, and Changing Consumer Behavior
authors: Padma. A, N. Suman Kumar, D. Srinivas, Ramesh G
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Recent Trends in Electrical, Electronics and Computing Technologies (ICRTEECT)
abstract: Content marketing is a new necessity in the trendy world of the digital era, which promotes consumer interaction and development of brand loyalty. With the current E-revolutionary technological era, content marketing has become one of the most important instruments of instigating consumer relations and formation of brand loyalty. The current paper examines the origin and strategic significance of the content marketing aspect and its psychological foundations as well as its practical examples. The paper explores the role in which story-telling could be used in consumer behavior, emotion, memory and decision-making, and breaks down key aspects that make a good brand-story like, character, conflict, resolution, authenticity, emotional connection, and value-add. Various content such as blogs, podcasts, videos, and infographics are also presented and means of maximizing the content through visual and interactive storytelling techniques. The paper discusses the concepts of content distribution and promotion, such as using social media, optimizing search engine (SEO), teaming up with influencers, and collaborating with communities. The KPIs and data analytics help to efficiently analyze the effectiveness of the content marketing that is provided to the companies that were able to reach outstanding outcomes through data-driven content strategies. The paper also addresses the new phenomenon, such as artificial intelligence (AI), machine learning, augmented/virtual reality (AR/VR), and the changes the new technologies can impose on content production, as well as how they can alter consumption of the same.This analysis has shown in this paper that content marketing can be a revolutionary factor in the brand and consumer dynamics evolution and that novel concepts about the means of storytelling ought to be created on a regular basis to remain abreast with the modern digital economy.

title: An Enhanced BERT-Based Approach for Mental Health Sentiment Analysis
authors: Udani Jayakody, Malki Jayawardhana, Dinushika Wickramasiri
publish: 2025 IEEE 19th International Conference on Industrial and Information Systems (ICIIS)
abstract: Sentiment analysis has emerged as a pivotal technique within Natural Language Processing (NLP) for extracting subjective emotional and attitudinal insights from textual data. While its applications have predominantly centered around commercial domains, recent advancements underscore its transformative potential in mental health research, particularly through the analysis of real-time self-disclosures on social media and online support forums. Existing lexicon-based methods, although valuable, often lack the granularity required to capture the complex emotional states characteristic of mental health narratives. In this study, we propose an enhanced approach leveraging the BERT architecture fine-tuned on extensive mental health corpora, integrating advanced preprocessing strategies and classification techniques tailored to this domain. Our methodology demonstrates superior performance across multiple mental health sentiment categories including stress, depression, bipolar disorder, personality disorders, and anxiety achieving notably robust precision, recall, and F1-scores. The findings highlight the efficacy of incorporating domain-specific language representations within transformer-based models, thereby advancing automated mental health assessment and enabling scalable, real-time monitoring tools. Implications for clinical applications and future research directions focusing on model interpretability and ethical considerations are discussed.

title: YOLO-Enhanced RFGB Architecture for Deep Ensemble Analysis of Post-COVID Cardiovascular Risk
authors: Natrayan L, Sangeeta Uranakar, Seeniappan Kaliappan, Ramya Maranan, S Subashini, Siva Kumar Pathuri
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Emerging Trends in Computing and Communication (ETCOM)
abstract: Spreading to various organs made reckoning with COVID-19 just as bad as it makes the risk of heart attacks higher. This has led to an increase in heart related diseases, particularly people between the age of 20 and 48 years. Prediction of such conditions in an early stage is important and classification methods are core learning methods in data mining with regards to supervised learning processes. By analyzing data in hospitals through computational algorithms, it is now possible to identify post-COVID-related cardiovascular problems in almost real-time. Popular in the world of industry, machine learning has a solid possibility of being applied to healthcare to reveal diseases in advance as well as optimize resources. The predictive abilities of different classification models such as Decision Tree, Logistic Regression, and the proposed YOLO+RFGB classifier (containing the combined model of object detection and a version of ensemble learning through Random Forest and Gradient Boosting) are compared in this research. The aim is to find the most correct classification approach towards predicting the post-COVID cardiovascular disease. It was implemented using the python and the proposed YOLO+RFGB model presented the best accuracy of 95 percent compared to the other models. It was also applied to calculate AUC and loss that proved to have higher diagnostic precision.

title: AwalTiraWhisper: A Fine-Tuned Version of OpenAI's Whisper Model to Translate Spoken Tamazight into Written Arabic and English
authors: Ayman Ait Achour, Youssef Souati, Asmaa Mourhir
publish: 2025 International Conference on Innovation and Intelligence for Informatics, Computing, and Technologies (3ICT)
abstract: Translating Tamazight speech into written text is important to support communication, preserve cultural identity, and improve access to digital services for Tamazight-speaking communities. The goal of this research is to build a system that automatically translates spoken Tamazight into Arabic and English using deep learning techniques. We collected and prepared a dataset of 46.6 hours of Tamazight audio from multiple dialects and used transfer learning to fine-tune OpenAI's Whisper model. The results show that the best-performing model achieved a BLEU score of 9.68% for Tamazight-to-English translation. Performance by dialect showed BLEU scores of 10.46% for Tashelhit, 9.53% for Tamazight, and 9.22% for Tarifit. These results demonstrate that Whisper can be adapted to support zeroresource languages like Tamazight and help bridge the digital language gap.

title: Efficient Estimation of Non-Circular (NC) Wavefronts via R-D Tensorized NC Tensor-ESPRIT-type Algorithms
authors: Sai Pavan Deram, Khaled Ardah, Joerg Widmer, Martin Haardt
publish: 2025 59th Asilomar Conference on Signals, Systems, and Computers
abstract: Subspace-based methods for R-D harmonic retrieval with non-circular (NC) sources provide advantages like improved resolution but face high computational demands. To address this, we propose a tensor-based framework that directly structures the augmented measurement tensor inherent to NC sources. We develop two variants: R-D Tensorized NC Standard Tensor ESPRIT and its real-valued counterpart R-D Tensorized NC Unitary Tensor ESPRIT. Our complexity analysis reveals that both proposed variants reduces the computations by $\frac{{R - 1}}{R}*100\% $ (e.g., 75% for R=4) compared to their respective conventional NC R-D ESPRIT counterparts by leveraging a unified higher-dimensional structure. Simulations confirm that R-D Tensorized NC Unitary Tensor ESPRIT retains the benefits of NC signal processing, including enhanced resolution and identifiability, while achieving comparable accuracy to standard approaches at a significantly reduced computational cost.

title: Dynamic Analysis of Co-Channel Downlink Interference on the Satria-1 Satellite Network by Second-Generation Starlink in Indonesia
authors: Mathilda Florenstika Sofiana, Heroe Wijanto, Meiditomo Sutyarjoko
publish: 2025 IEEE Asia-Pacific Conference on Geoscience, Electronics and Remote Sensing Technology (AGERS)
abstract: Satellites are generally categorized into geostationary (GSO) and non-geostationary (non-GSO) systems, which differ significantly in their orbital characteristics. When both systems operate within the same frequency band, interference may occur, potentially degrading the performance of GSO satellites. To mitigate this, International Telecommunication Union (ITU) requires nonGSO systems to protect GSO satellites from harmful interference, ensuring the continuity of communications services. This study analyzes technical characteristics of Satria1 and Starlink second generation systems operating in the Kaband frequencies allocated by Indonesia. The analysis focuses on interference resulting from their differing orbital altitudes but shared frequency usage. Equivalent power flux density (EPFD) values in the downlink direction are calculated using defined system parameters and evaluated against the regulatory limits established in Article 22 of ITU Radio Regulations. Simulation results across four scenarios, with Starlink transmit powers of 10 W and 1 W, indicate that EPFD value at both Satria-1 user terminals and earth stations exceeds ITU limits, demonstrating the potential for significant co-channel interference.

title: Optimized IDS Using AI and Feature Ranking Fusion for Enhanced Threat Detection
authors: S. Purushothaman, Suhashini A, Vaishaly Bopaiah G, Ankita Thakur, K. Anandhi, Sanjay Bhatnagar
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Smart Innovations in Systems, Infrastructure, Mechanical, Power, AI and Computing Technologies (SISIMPACT)
abstract: This research presents a new and improved IDS (Intrusion Detection System) that customs artificial intelligence and a unique amalgamation of six feature ranking approaches–MI (Mutual Information), PI (Permutation Importance), RFI (Random Forest Importance), SFI (SHAP Feature Importance), Difference between Mean and Median (DMM) and Standard Deviation (SD). It chooses the best 7 to 9 features from each of the datasets NSL-KDD, UNSW-NB15 and CIC-IDS2017. This issue of imbalance was handled using ADASYN oversampling which improved how many minority class images there were. Experiments were performed using XGBM, SVM, DT, LR and ET machine learning classifiers. NSL-KDD performed with accuracy 99.86%, UNSW-NB15 managed 92.42% and CICIDS2017 earned 99.68%. Precision, recall and F1-Score were all greater than 99% when dealing with a balanced set of data. Fusion achieved better accuracy and was much faster to train when compared to traditional techniques such as PCA, IG and PCC. This affirms that by combining multiple ranking approaches, detection accuracy and efficiency can be maintained using less important features which supports a flexible, intelligent IDS system.

title: From Errors to Solutions: LLM-Powered Command Scripting for FPGA Cad Tools
authors: Mohamed A. Elgammal, Vaughn Betz
publish: 2025 35th International Conference on Field-Programmable Logic and Applications (FPL)
abstract: Computer-aided design (CAD) tools provide hundreds or even thousands of options that control various optimizations throughout the design flow. While this flexibility is powerful, it requires significant experience to be familiar with those options and effectively utilize them. For example, when a design fails, in many cases errors can be resolved by adjusting the CAD tool options rather than modifying the design itself. In this work, we propose VPR-LLM, a tool that utilizes Large Language Models (LLMs) to automate error resolution in the open-source FPGA CAD tool Verilog-to-Routing (VTR) by modifying the command-line options used to run the tool. VPRLLM parses error logs, VTR help messages, and documentation, then utilizes an LLM to generate modified command-line options that resolve the issue. VPR-LLM supports various LLM models and prompting techniques. All these models and techniques are evaluated and compared in terms of efficiency and cost. To evaluate our method, we proposed a dataset of 26 VTR run failures spanning five distinct error categories. The proposed technique successfully resolved 80 % of the cases without requiring any fine-tuning to the LLM model, demonstrating the effectiveness of VPR-LLM. This work represents an initial step toward AI-assisted debugging in CAD flows, where LLMs can enhance productivity by automatically identifying and correcting tool configurations.

title: Double Duty: FPGA Architecture to Enable Concurrent LUT and Adder Chain Usage
authors: Junius Pun, Xilai Dai, Grace Zgheib, Mahesh A. Iyer, Andrew Boutros, Vaughn Betz, Mohamed S. Abdelfattah
publish: 2025 35th International Conference on Field-Programmable Logic and Applications (FPL)
abstract: Flexibility and customization are key strengths of Field-Programmable Gate Arrays (FPGAs) when compared to other computing devices. For instance, FPGAs can efficiently implement arbitrary-precision arithmetic operations, and can perform aggressive synthesis optimizations to eliminate ineffectual operations. Motivated by sparsity and mixed-precision in deep neural networks (DNNs), we investigate how to optimize the current logic block architecture to increase its arithmetic density. We find that modern FPGA logic block architectures prevent the independent use of adder chains, and instead only allow adder chain inputs to be fed by look-up table (LUT) outputs. This only allows one of the two primitives—either adders or LUTs—to be used independently in one logic element and prevents their concurrent use, hampering area optimizations. In this work, we propose the Double Duty logic block architecture to enable the concurrent use of the adders and LUTs within a logic element. Without adding expensive logic cluster inputs, we use 4 of the existing inputs to bypass the LUTs and connect directly to the adder chain inputs. We accurately model our changes at both the circuit and CAD levels using open-source FPGA development tools. Our experimental evaluation on a Stratix-10-like architecture demonstrates area reductions of 21.6% on adder-intensive circuits from the Kratos benchmarks, and 9.3% and 8.2% on the more general Koios and VTR benchmarks respectively. These area improvements come without an impact to critical path delay, demonstrating that higher density is feasible on modern FPGA architectures by adding more flexibility in how the adder chain is used. Averaged across all circuits from our three evaluated benchmark set, our Double Duty FPGA architecture improves area-delay product by 9.7%.

title: Virtualization and Dynamic Reconfiguration of Custom Instruction Accelerators (CIA) in RISC-V Embedded Systems
authors: Bea Healy, Brandon Freiberger, Jonas Kuenstler, King Lok Chung, Emil Cozac, Meinhard Kissich, Gennadiy Knis, Ron Sass, Dirk Koch, Jan Gray, Guy Lemieux
publish: 2025 35th International Conference on Field-Programmable Logic and Applications (FPL)
abstract: Custom hardware instructions are frequently used to accelerate software. However, the number of active custom instructions - and speedup opportunities - are limited in resourceconstrained systems, such as those using small embedded FPGAs (eFPGAs). Hence, it is desirable to be able to dynamically reconfigure this resource to allow an application to change the accelerator logic. While an existing method called CX Table is described the Draft CX Specification to manage accelerators, it is not suitable for lightweight embedded systems because (1) virtual memory is required to isolate processes, and (2) an implied table lookup is required when switching custom instruction sets. This paper presents CIA Direct, a lightweight alternative to CX Table. It moves the table into a privileged control register to avoid virtual memory and external memory accesses. It provides inter-process virtualization of accelerator state, but intra-process virtualization has performance limitations. This paper also describes CxBex, the first ASIC implementation of CIA Direct. It contains a hard RISC-V processor with an eFPGA to support multiple custom instruction accelerators under dynamic reconfiguration. Unique to CxBex, context switching keeps multiple independent state context intact in eFPGA block during reconfiguration. Intended for lightweight embedded systems, this work demonstrates that the CIA Direct approach is simpler, reduces CPU complexity, eliminates dependence on external memory, and manages dynamic reconfiguration using exceptions.

title: EDRL-DS: Energy-Aware Deep Reinforcement Learning with Dynamic Scaling for Latency-Efficient Task Scheduling in Medical IoT Edge Computing
authors: El Miloud Ar-Reyouchi, Asmaa Kharouaa, Ayoub Hadj Sadek, Kamal Ghoumid
publish: 2025 International Conference on Innovation and Intelligence for Informatics, Computing, and Technologies (3ICT)
abstract: This paper introduces EDRL-DS, a novel energyaware scheduling framework that integrates Deep Reinforcement Learning (DRL) with Dynamic Voltage and Frequency Scaling (DVFS) to enable efficient and adaptive task management in Medical Internet of Things (MIoT) edge environments. The proposed framework utilizes a Deep Q-Network (DQN) to dynamically optimize processor configurations and task scheduling in response to real-time workload variations, battery levels, and application demands. By intelligently adjusting voltage and frequency settings, EDRL-DS minimizes overall energy consumption and latency while maintaining strict quality of service requirements critical in healthcare-related IoT applications. Extensive simulations demonstrate that EDRL-DS achieves up to 35 % energy savings compared to baseline approaches and significantly outperforms existing DRL-based methods in both energy efficiency and deadline compliance. These results underscore the potential of EDRLDS for deployment in latency-sensitive, energy-constrained IoT systems, supporting scalable and sustainable operation across edge devices and cloud infrastructures.

title: From Feeds to Impulsive Buying: How Social Media Activities Drive Among Indonesian Gen-Z Consumers: Moderated by Self-Control
authors: Excel Favour Yosafat Tomboelu, Shaera Nailo Fitria, Novian Abby Sumarsono, Ridho Bramulya Ikhsan, Hartiwi Prabowo, Kartika Aprilia Benhardy
publish: 2025 International Conference on Innovation and Intelligence for Informatics, Computing, and Technologies (3ICT)
abstract: Social media and e-commerce platforms stimulate innovation and play a crucial role in shaping impulsive purchasing tendencies among Generation Z. Despite this, research on self-control within the context of impulsive behavior remains limited. Accordingly, this study examines the impact of social media communities and advertising on impulsive buying intentions and subsequent purchasing behavior. It further assesses whether self-control moderates the relationship between impulsive buying intention and impulsive buying behavior. A quantitative approach was adopted, employing a questionnaire administered to 260 respondents through purposive sampling. The data were analyzed using partial least squares structural equation modeling (PLS-SEM). The findings reveal that both social media communities and advertising have a significant impact on impulsive buying intention. Impulsive buying intention, in turn, significantly predicts impulsive purchasing behavior. Moreover, self-control negatively and significantly moderates the link between impulsive buying intention and impulsive behavior. This study offers practical insights for e-commerce practitioners by demonstrating that social media engagement positively influences consumers' impulsive purchasing patterns.

title: A Multi-Model Intelligent System for Financial and Communication Fraud Prevention Using Modular Intelligent Architecture and ShieldAI
authors: Vishwanatha C R, Shreyas N, Dileep M R
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Recent Trends in Electrical, Electronics and Computing Technologies (ICRTEECT)
abstract: Strong and flexible prevention measures are required due to the widespread and dynamic nature of financial and communication fraud. This study introduces ShieldAI, a cutting-edge multi-model intelligent system with a modular AI architecture intended for real-time fraud detection. The six specialized detection modules that ShieldAI incorporates are: Card Cloning, Voice Phishing, Financial Fraud, Spam Message, and QR Code & Website Scanner. Every module uses customized AI algorithms, ranging from rule-based systems and heuristic natural language processing to static code analysis and anomaly detection, and is designed to handle particular input formats (CSV, text, audio, APK, and image). Scalability, maintainability, and excellent detection accuracy across several fraud vectors are guaranteed by the modular design. We describe the architecture, methodology, and experimental findings of the system, demonstrating its efficacy in detecting fraudulent activity with low false positives, high confidence ratings, and quick processing times for both harmful and legitimate inputs. ShieldAI seeks to offer a thorough and proactive response to the various problems presented by contemporary fraud attempts.

title: Responsive Interfaces for End-User Service Orchestration
authors: Shikha Parekh
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Recent Trends in Electrical, Electronics and Computing Technologies (ICRTEECT)
abstract: This paper introduces a novel approach to democratizing web service interaction, addressing the challenges faced by non-technical users in discovering and utilizing business functionalities. We present a system that empowers end-users to orchestrate task-specific applications through an intuitive catalogue and a dynamic service wrapping tool. The core of this system integrates an adaptive matching framework, leveraging formalized input-output conditions to facilitate intelligent service discovery. Furthermore, an interactive paradigm is employed during service configuration, providing immediate behavioral feedback to the user and streamlining the transformation process. The platform also incorporates a sophisticated ranking mechanism that adjusts recommendations based on usage statistics and real-time service availability. This work delineates a practical strategy for enhancing user autonomy in a service-oriented landscape, emphasizing responsive computational environments.

title: A Hybrid Intelligence-Driven Model for Enhancing Security and Trust in Integrated Edge to Cloud Networks Through Real-Time Anomaly Detection and Tamper-Proof Storage
authors: T. Srilekha, Srilatha Komakula, Hemanth Manthena
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Recent Trends in Electrical, Electronics and Computing Technologies (ICRTEECT)
abstract: The rapid expansion of edge-to-cloud networks in the Internet of Things (IoT) era has introduced new security challenges, as resource-constrained edge devices and ubiquitous connectivity expose systems to sophisticated cyber-attacks. Traditional centralized defenses and static rule-based intrusion detection systems often fail to detect emerging anomalies and attacks in real-time. In this work, we propose a hybrid intelligence-driven model that synergistically combines edge and cloud artificial intelligence (AI) with blockchain technology to enhance security and trust in integrated edge–cloud IoT networks. The model deploys real-time anomaly detection using advanced machine learning at both the edge and cloud layers, enabling early identification of threats such as distributed denial- of-service (DDoS) attacks, malware injections, and insider misuse. Detected anomalies are logged to a tamper-proof storage network underpinned by blockchain, ensuring that security events are recorded on an immutable, decentralized ledger. This blockchain-based logging guarantees data integrity and transparency, preventing malicious actors from altering or erasing evidence. The proposed edge–cloud security framework was evaluated using a real-world intrusion detection dataset, demonstrating high attack detection accuracy (~99%) and low false alarm rates, while smart contract–driven responses significantly improved incident response time and system resilience. By leveraging hybrid AI for detection and blockchain for trustworthy storage, our approach provides a robust, scalable, and adaptive security solution for modern IoT deployments, enhancing network security and stakeholder trust through real- time threat mitigation and verifiable, tamper-proof audit trails.

title: Tensor-Based Angle-Range Estimation in Near-Field ISAC Systems
authors: Lin Chen, Hongbin Li
publish: 2025 59th Asilomar Conference on Signals, Systems, and Computers
abstract: With the deployment of extremely large-scale multiple-input multiple-output (MIMO) arrays, integrated sensing and communication (ISAC) systems may operate in the near-field region. In this case, the array manifold matrix depends jointly on angle and range parameters of targets. This paper proposes a near-field angle-range estimation method via low-rank tensor decomposition. The observed data in ISAC systems is modeled as a third-order tensor, which can be decomposed into target parameter-determined factor matrices. To enhance the decomposition accuracy, we introduce a novel constraint on the near-field array manifold in tensor decomposition. The optimization problem associated with the near-field manifold constraint is approximately yet efficiently solved using least squares along with a correlation step. Simulation results validate the effectiveness of the proposed tensor-based framework for angle-range estimation in near-field ISAC systems. The proposed method demonstrates superior estimation accuracy compared to its counterpart without the near-field manifold constraint and a conventional super-resolution method.

title: Efficient Compressive Underwater Sound Sensing and Reconstruction for IoUT Monitoring Devices
authors: Vineetha Edakkattuparambil Vijayan, M Sabarimalai Manikandan
publish: 2025 IEEE Asia-Pacific Conference on Geoscience, Electronics and Remote Sensing Technology (AGERS)
abstract: Marine ecolife preservation and safe navigation are the two areas of importance in today’s world. This work studies underwater sound recovery based on the compressed sensing (CS) technique. CS enables sound recovery from a smaller number of samples, which is rarely explored in marine acoustics. This technique can make low-power internet of underwater things (IoUT) systems more efficient. The study does a comprehensive analysis to find out an optimal CS matrix and its appropriate measurement resolution for this task. It includes systematic evaluation of ten sensing matrices: random Gaussian, random binary sparse variants, Toeplitz variants, circulant, Hadamard, and deterministic binary block diagonal (DBBD). The evaluation uses the Watkins marine mammal and the ShipsEar databases. Reconstruction quality was examined using nine distortion metrics. Results show that the DBBD sensing matrix gives the optimal performance for underwater sound reconstruction. The distortion metric values (percentage root-mean-square difference normalized by variance (PRD1): $3.27 \pm 3.12$, mean absolute error (MAE): $0.00126 \pm 0.00532$, root mean squared error (RMSE): $0.00718 \pm 0.00229$, signal-to-noise ratio (SNR): $31.03 \pm 3.90 \mathrm{~dB}$) of the DBBD matrix demonstrate exceptional fidelity. These results with the quantization study show that the reconstruction quality remains good for both ship sounds and animal sounds, especially in the quantization region of 8 to 10 bits. These results can support compressive underwater sound sensing and reconstruction in practical IoUT nodes with better signal quality and low power usage for efficient marine monitoring.

title: Reconfigurable X-Band Annular Slot Antenna using GaN Varactor
authors: Dorsela Venkata Rami Reddy, S. Thulasi Prasad, Ganesh V N, Dushyant Kumar Singh, Deepak Kapila, R. Dhilipkumar, Krishna Chaithanya Janapati, Ajay Sudhir Bale, Praveenkumar. C
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Smart Innovations in Systems, Infrastructure, Mechanical, Power, AI and Computing Technologies (SISIMPACT)
abstract: This article provides a comprehensive analysis of the performance characteristics of a straightforward frequency-agile slot antenna designed for operation within the X-band frequency range. According to the author's insights, the antenna's ability to tune its frequency is achieved for the first time using a surface wire-bonded Gallium Nitride (GaN) varactor, which has dimensions of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$450 \times 500$</tex> micrometers squared and is fabricated utilizing <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.5 \mu ~\mathrm{m} \text{GaN}$</tex> High Electron Mobility Transistor (HEMT) technology. The observed tunability spans a frequency between 7.53 to 7.85 GHz, demonstrating a tunability capability of up to 4 %. The document includes detailed results from microwave performance experiments, which are thoroughly analyzed and compared against simulation data. A significant correlation was found between the experimental results and the outcomes predicted by the simulations.

title: Explainable AI Enabled Pneumonia Detection to Improve Physician Interpretability
authors: M. Sumathi, SyedHaroon A, Preeti Gupta, Yangjingyu Li, B Reddy, Khushbu Pandey
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Smart Innovations in Systems, Infrastructure, Mechanical, Power, AI and Computing Technologies (SISIMPACT)
abstract: Rapid development of deep-learning in medical imaging has significantly advanced the detection of pneumonia, but without interpretability, physician confidence and application in clinical practice cannot occur. In this work, the researcher proposes the idea of having a pneumonia-recognition system, which can be enabled by Explainable Artificial Intelligence (XAI) with convolutional neural networks (CNNs) with the assistance of attention-based visualization systems (Grad-Cam and SHAP) when it comes to enhancing the computational interpretability. The system not only makes high accuracy of the classification of chests X-ray images but will also determine clinically regarding regions of the lungs having predictions by the system more so convincing the machine reasoning in the medical knowledge. The framework was evaluated using a curated set of chest radiographs, with an accuracy of 96.8, a preciseness of 95.4, a recall of 97.2 and a F1score of 96.3. Performance Comparative performance There is stronger interpretability and diagnosability than with black-box deep learning models. The suggested model of the XAI framework will enable the physicians to justify the results of AI, advocating the prompt clinical decision-making and foster the trusted format of the human-AI collaboration in healthcare diagnostics.

title: Implementation of Various Deep Learning Algorithms for Hybrid Intrusion Detection in IoT Networks
authors: Nasrulla Khan K, Kusum Lata, K. Sheela Sobana Rani, Sakshi Sobti, D. Susitra, Jai Kumar B
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Smart Innovations in Systems, Infrastructure, Mechanical, Power, AI and Computing Technologies (SISIMPACT)
abstract: The use of more IoT devices has increased risks to today's networks and made it important to have effective intrusion detection. This research compares and assesses CNN, LSTM, and a combined CNN-LSTM model for intrusion detection in the Internet of Things (IoT). There were 100,000 labeled data points in the CICIDS2018 dataset and the models were tested on a mix of 80% benign traffic and 20% attack instances. CNN scored an accuracy of 98.61%, LSTM scored 97.67% and the combined CNN-LSTM model was more accurate than either model with 99.3% accuracy, 99.9% precision, 99.1% recall. With the hybrid model, training converged much quicker than it did for the individual models. The outcomes prove that hybrids and specialized architectures are highly helpful in boosting the monitoring of IoT networks. It shows that these models can be used in real-time IoT security to make threat detection more effective and protect against various dangers better.

title: GAVIA-X: Interactive Diffusion Inpainting for Generative AI-Driven VR/XR Content
authors: Leonardo Marques Rocha, Paulo Abner Mesquita
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and eXtended and Virtual Reality (AIxVR)
abstract: In this paper, we introduce GAVIA-X, an extension of the GAVIA framework that transforms a methodology for Generative AI (GenAI) content extension into an interactive visualization and correction mechanism for the generation of 3D environments from Large Language Model (LLM) prompts for Virtual and Mixed Realities (VR/XR). The main contribution is a novel system that overcomes the challenges of Diffusion models to produce smooth and consistent 360° panoramic cubemaps by involving a Gemma-based LLM to automatically generate contextual prompts and an interactive, VR/XR-ready inpainting mechanism through an A-Frame plug-in. Based on an A-Frame plugin, the system provides a VR/XR experience in which users can “paint” masks on the 3D cubemap face in order to fix GenAI artifacts, or customize scenes, and employs morphological operators (dilation and closing) to smooth the masks and produce seamless transitions for a better VR/XR. The most promising use cases are the generation of customized datasets for World Models (WM) training and synthetic backgrounds, facilitating the training with human supervision.

title: CAF: CrossAttentionFusion for Unsupervised Brain MRI Segmentation
authors: Sihan Zhai, Jianan Tang, Guangyu Ren, Hengyan Liu
publish: 2025 International Conference on Big Data and Data Mining (BDDM)
abstract: Unsupervised anomaly segmentation is vital for brain MRI lesion detection, enabling models trained solely on healthy data to identify tumors as deviations from normative anatomy without requiring annotated abnormal samples. Existing reconstruction-based approaches, such as autoencoders and diffusion models, often struggle with inadequate feature integration, where simple concatenation of spatial masks and frequency components fails to capture cross-domain dependencies, leading to reconstruction deviations in normal regions and high false positive rates. We propose a UNet-based diffusion framework enhanced with a Cross-Attention Fusion (CAF) module that dynamically aligns spatial mask features with frequency-derived structural priors. By leveraging multi-head attention with residual refinement, CAF adaptively integrates high-frequency edge information with spatial semantics, strengthening anomaly localization. An iterative mask refinement strategy further suppresses false positives and sharpens lesion boundaries. Extensive experiments on the BraTS 2021 dataset demonstrate that our method achieves Dice scores of 0.60-0.82, surpassing diffusion and IterMask-based baselines by <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1. 3 - 3. 0 \%}$</tex>, while effectively reducing false positives in healthy tissues. These results highlight the value of dynamic domain fusion for unsupervised medical image analysis and suggest promising extensions to broader anomaly detection tasks.

title: NEWER: Neural Estimation of Wavelet-Embedded Representations
authors: Vishesh Kumar, Jingyi Miao, Shunyao Zhang, Huan-Cheng Liao, Kaiyuan Yang, Lei Li, Ashok Veeraraghavan, Vishwanath Saragadam, Vivek Boominathan
publish: 2025 59th Asilomar Conference on Signals, Systems, and Computers
abstract: Implicit Neural Representations (INRs) are powerful Deep Learning architectures for continuous signal modeling but suffer from spectral bias, leading to poor performance in inverse problems that rely on high-frequency structure near the Nyquist limit. We propose NEWER, a hybrid representation that embeds a differentiable wavelet generating equation for modeling broadband Time-of-Flight photoacoustic signals. Rather than fitting raw oscillatory traces, NEWER predicts the parameters of localized Gabor wavelets, capturing amplitude, delay, frequency, and phase, using subnetworks solely to model the wavefront geometry. This formulation imposes the physical constraints of acoustic propagation and transducer bandwidth, providing the inductive bias that standard INRs lack. Experiments on simulated and real data show that NEWER consistently improves reconstruction fidelity, particularly in the near-Nyquist regime where aliasing is severe. In 2× super-resolution tasks, NEWER recovers high-frequency components and improves PSNR by > 3 dB over traditional INR baselines while preserving the waveform geometry critical for downstream beamforming.

title: Learning-based Beamforming and Phase Shift Design for RIS-aided mmWave Networks Under Sparse Channel Estimation
authors: Xiang Huang, Joohyun Cho, Rong-Rong Chen
publish: 2025 59th Asilomar Conference on Signals, Systems, and Computers
abstract: Reconfigurable intelligent surface (RIS)-aided mmWave communication offers a promising approach to overcome the issue of severe signal blockage that limits the data rate of mmWave networks. However, joint base station beamforming and RIS phase shift (BF-PS) design presents great challenges not only because of the intricate relationship between the beamforming and phase shift matrices in determining the network sum-rate, but also due to the lack of accurate channel estimation when the number of RIS elements gets large. In this work, we study learning-based BF-PS design for RIS-aided mmWave networks under sparse channel estimation. Our main finding is that, under the practical constraint of pilot overhead for RIS channel estimation, learning-based BF-PS design can yield superior performance to that achieved by the conventional block-coordinate-descent (BCD) algorithm. This work provides an initial study of learning-based BF-PS design under the compressed-sensing framework for mmWave channel estimation. The proposed BF-PS design adopts an enhanced four-stage neural-network architecture that improves upon the existing two-stage design by utilizing outputs from earlier stages to form new features for later stages. The new four-stage design using hybrid beamforming (HBF) outperforms those of the two-stage design using either HBF or fully digital beamforming (DBF).

title: Deep Mobile Vision Architecture for Autonomous Detection of Road Surface Deformities Using MobileNetV3
authors: Ch. Janakamma, S. Sowjanya Chintalapati, Bandari Rajesh, Yegetela Ram Kumar, G. Sravanthi, Krishna Komaram
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Emerging Trends in Computing and Communication (ETCOM)
abstract: Potholes, cracks, and irregular texts are few of the deformities on the surface of the roads which are a great challenge to the transportation, vehicle performance, and structural maintenance. Paperwork inspections are tedious, time-wasting, and subject to human errors and thus require an automated and smart system. This study represents the Deep Mobile Vision Architecture on the basis of the MobileNetV3 architecture of autonomous detection and classification of road surface deformities. The data that is examined is provided on the open-access site of Kaggle which includes 3,200 images of four different classes including Pothole, Crack, Normal Road, and Wet Surface where the data has been preprocessed and augmented so that it could be more generalized. The MobileNetV3 with fine-tuning uses depthwise separable convolutions, squeeze-and-excitation blocks, and hard-swish option, which can then run on mobile and embedded systems. There are experimental performance demonstrating a classification accuracy of 97% and a micro-average F1-score of 0.97, which proves to have better precision and a higher recall at all classes. The comparative analysis also confirms that the proposed model is better as compared to architectures like VGG16, ResNet50, and EfficientNetB0 without losing its lightweight computational efficiency. The interpretation of key deformity regions by the model is confirmed by visualization using Grad-CAM. The suggested system will be a powerful real-time autonomous network of road conditions that will support smart cities; infrastructure and smart transportation systems with defect detection based on accuracy and using mobile technology.

title: AI-Backed Risk Assessment for Managing Supplier Default Using Banking Guarantees
authors: Bharat Bhushan, Girija Shankar Sahoo, Prateek Aggarwal, Dibyasundar Das
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Smart Innovations in Systems, Infrastructure, Mechanical, Power, AI and Computing Technologies (SISIMPACT)
abstract: The additional complexity of global supplier chains has further multiplied the risk of supplier default, especially where contractual commitments have been bank-guaranteed. Conventionally applied risk assessment models cannot easily reveal the interdependent breeding and dynamic state of supplier relationships placing the stakeholders at the risk of overall systemic weaknesses. In tackling this problem, this paper contains the concept of an enhanced AI-enhanced framework called SERA-Net (Supplier Exposure and Risk Alignment Network), which has incorporated the relational, temporal and contextual dimensions of smart risk assessment. The methodology establishes a model of supplier-bankclient behaviours as a Relational Exposure Graph that can be applied to graph neural networks (GNN) and that may also discover systemic exposure relationships in a learned embedded representation. A Guarantee-Weighted Temsporal Model would, in parallel, leverage recurrent architectures to research the cash flow pattern changes, credit history, and guarantee obligations. Moreover, an Attention-Based Risk Alignment mechanism includes current market conditions like market volatility, geopolitical uncertainty and supply chain shocks, dynamically updating risk scores. Such components are combined and form a Bayesian calibration layer within the framework to yield uncertainty-aware probability estimates that increase the reliability of decision-making. SERA-Net combines graph analytics, time-varying deep learning, and attention-based alignment to offer a powerful tool to predict supplier defaults and, therefore, assess risk exposure in financial ecosystems sustained by banking guarantees. The suggested SERA-Net had an overall accuracy of 93.8 % when it came to evaluating supplier risk.

title: Study on Multi-Modal Generative Data Augmentation for UAV Edge Computing in Air Quality Monitoring and Image Recognition
authors: Mao-Hsiu Hsu, Yi-Jheng Li, Bo-Jie Zhang, Ying-Hong Shi
publish: 2025 IEEE Asia-Pacific Conference on Geoscience, Electronics and Remote Sensing Technology (AGERS)
abstract: In UAV-based urban inspection and environmental monitoring, image samples under extreme air pollution (for example, very high PM2.5 or gas concentrations) are scarce, leading to long-tailed data distributions and poor generalization; moreover, models deployed in urban settings often struggle to be both lightweight and real time. To address this, we propose an integrated solution composed of two independent modules. First, the generation module is a latent-diffusion, multimodal design conditioned on both textual semantics and pollution indicators to cover diverse pollution and visibility levels. A six-dimensional pollutant vector ($\mathrm{NO}, \mathrm{SO}_{2}, \mathrm{CO}, \mathrm{O}_{3}, \mathrm{PM}_{10}, \mathrm{PM}_{2.5}$) is normalized and aligned with text embeddings via a pollution encoder, while ControlNet injects geometric priors to preserve structural consistency; conditional dropout and a dual-branch classifier-free guidance scheme are used during training and inference to enhance robustness. Second, the recognition module adopts a lightweight dual-branch visual backbone that combines MobileNetV3 with a shallow CNN and integrates time-and-weather metadata for pollutant concentration regression. Without modifying the generation module, explicitly introducing temporal and environmental conditions on the recognition side resolves the ambiguity whereby the same pollution level can appear with different visual characteristics across lighting intensities and times of day, while preserving a low parameter count and real-time performance on edge devices. Experimental results show that pollution-conditioned images generated by the proposed framework, together with the lightweight time-aware recognizer, significantly improve performance under extreme pollution and varying illumination (higher $\boldsymbol{R}$-squared and lower mean squared error) and maintain stable real-time inference on UAV platforms. Code and minimal reproducible artifacts are publicly available at https://github.com/Maohsiuhsu/air.

title: CNN-Logistic Regression Hybrid Architecture for Dermatological Image Analysis
authors: Deepak Banerjee, Anupma Gupta, Manish Kumar Singla, Ashish Kumar Singh, Swati Singh, Vidhi Sharma, Amandeep Kaur
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Emerging Trends in Computing and Communication (ETCOM)
abstract: The acne classification CNN-Logistic Regression model, combining the proposed methods, illustrated an excellent performance in the classification of three different acne types, i.e., Nodules, Blackheads, and Papules, with a high degree of accuracy and stability. This framework produced a Precision, Recall, and F1-score of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{9 8. 4 0 \%}$</tex> for Nodules, 98.45% for Blackheads, and 98.36% for Papules, which is a strong indicator of its ability to correctly classify each class. The dataset contained 11280 images, of which 3760 Nodules (0.33 support proportion), 3870 Blackheads (0.34 support proportion), and 3650 Papules (0.32 support proportion) were distributed, thus ensuring a balanced representation of classes. Each class reached an excellent accuracy of 99%, which is a very strong indication of the model's precision and accuracy. The combination of CNN for deep spatial feature extraction and Logistic Regression for interpretive classification not only ensured computational efficiency but also proved to be superior in performance. The final results, with an accuracy of 98.40%, are a testament to the model's stability and effectiveness; thus, it can be regarded as a potential tool for automated acne detection and clinical dermatological diagnosis.

title: Harnessing Graph Databases- A Comparative Analysis of Relationship Centric Models and Practical Applications
authors: Anupam Das, Anirban Mitra, Parijat Roy
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Smart Innovations in Systems, Infrastructure, Mechanical, Power, AI and Computing Technologies (SISIMPACT)
abstract: This article presents a comprehensive analysis of various graph database models, highlighting their respective advantages and limitations. Graph databases are valuable in ap- plications such as recommendation systems, social networks, and fraud detection because they excel at maintaining complicated data relationships. Neo4j, Janus Graph, and Arango DB are the major graph database suppliers. Scalability, performance, and ease of use are among the benefits offered by these providers. Janus Graph is a suitable candidate for large-scale deployments because to its distributed graph processing capabilities, but Neo4j is noted for its scalability and ease of use. Arango DB is a multi-model database that can handle a variety of data models, including graph databases. The significance of Graph databases over Machine Learning (ML) in many sectors has also been investigated.

title: Bi-Linear Homogeneity Enforced Filtering
authors: Matthias Wagner, Oliver Lang
publish: 2025 59th Asilomar Conference on Signals, Systems, and Computers
abstract: Non-linear system equalization requires, besides a feasible model, precise knowledge of the input signal or its statistics. In cases where the input signal is generated by a low-quality signal source, the effectiveness of the equalizer is often limited by the source’s linearity. To overcome this challenge, the homogeneity enforced calibration (HEC) approach was recently introduced. Therein, an input signal is injected twice into the unknown system, while for the second time it is scaled by an unknown scaling factor. The equalization of the non-linear system is then solely based on the system outputs, such that no precise knowledge of the input signal is required.In this work, the HEC approach is generalized to support an unknown impulse response of the scaling circuit instead of a plain scaling factor. This significantly relaxes the requirements on analog hardware in real-world applications. Furthermore, two novel filters, the bi-linear homogeneity enforced least squares filter, and the bi-linear homogeneity enforced recursive least squares filter are introduced. The findings are first verified on a toy example with exponential non-linearity. In a second example, the effectiveness of the proposed approach is demonstrated by analog-to-digital converter calibration based on measurements, highlighting its advantages over existing methods.

title: Low-Cost Syringe Pump with Remote Monitoring, Alarm Functionality, and Health Tracking
authors: Lakvindi Chanya Warnapura, Sareka Saravanapavan
publish: 2025 IEEE 19th International Conference on Industrial and Information Systems (ICIIS)
abstract: This project presents a low-cost syringe pump with remote monitoring and alarm functionality, along with basic health monitoring, designed for use in rural hospitals and home-based care. The system uses an affordable ATmega328P microcontroller and a stepper motor with a lead screw mechanism, driven by a stepper driver, to ensure accurate and controlled linear motion for medication delivery. A Real-Time Clock (RTC) module is integrated to enable time-based medication scheduling, enhancing reliability and ensuring timely alerts. The system also features temperature and pulse oximeter sensors for monitoring basic health parameters. Remote monitoring is supported by the Global System for Mobile Communications (GSM) connectivity allowing alert messages to be transmitted via a GSM module. The local notifications given through an active buzzer and a Light Emitting Diode (LED) indicator. The programming was done using Arduino IDE, and Printed Circuit Board (PCB) designs were completed using EasyEDA software. Battery backup ensures operation during power cuts. Key findings include a resistor–diode network to reduce keypad pin usage, a connected pull-down pin method for sharing the GSM module across two PCBs and using effective lead screw method to get a linear motion from the rotational movement. Experimental testing confirmed an accuracy of over 98% in volume delivery and flow rate.

title: Insights into Dynamic Time Warping and Principal Component Analysis for Linear Decoding of Neural Decision-Making
authors: Alec Roadarmel, Karen Moxon
publish: 2025 59th Asilomar Conference on Signals, Systems, and Computers
abstract: Linear decoding models can represent the complex dynamics underlying human decision-making. We previously compared two linear approaches to classify participants' choices during a decision-making task: a Euclidean distance classifier and a dynamic time warping (DTW) classifier. Results showed that by warping the time, classification performance increased, suggesting that similar neural patterns can occur at different temporal scales across trials. However, which neural features drive this temporal scaling is unclear. Here, we examine the principal components of intracranial LFP data during decision-making, which were used as input features for both classifiers. Our results showed that, across all brain regions, more than half of the variance was explained by the first principal component. In addition, the lower frequency bands explained the most variance in each PC, suggesting that they covaried. Finally, DTW improved our understanding of the temporal dynamics within each frequency band across trials. These models provide insight into how neural signals encode decision-making across frequency bands.

title: 3D Convolutional Neural Network Classification Model for Alzheimer's Disease Fusing Multi-Scale Features and Coordinate Attention
authors: Fengxia Wu, Xin Zhao, Meng Wang, Jingjing Yang, Tengfei Hao, Wenhui Wang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Big Data and Data Mining (BDDM)
abstract: Accurate diagnosis of Alzheimer's disease (AD) is vital for enabling early intervention and slowing disease progression. Deep learning methods based on structural magnetic resonance imaging (sMRI) have emerged as a powerful tool for automated AD diagnosis. However, significant challenges remain in effectively capturing the subtle and heterogeneous patterns of brain atrophy characteristic of AD, particularly in distinguishing them from changes associated with normal aging. To address these challenges, we propose a novel three-dimensional convolutional neural network (3D-CNN) for AD classification. The core innovation of our model lies in the integration of two bespoke modules: (1) a Residual Dense Multi-Scale Block (RDMS), which utilizes parallel convolutional kernels of varying sizes combined with dense and residual connections. This is designed to simultaneously capture multi-scale patterns of brain atrophy, ranging from local details to regional morphological changes; and (2) a 3D Coordinate Attention module (CoordAtt3D), which encodes precise spatial information to capture direction-aware features and long-range dependencies. This enables the model to adaptively focus on key brain regions that contribute most significantly to diagnosis, such as the hippocampus and temporal lobes. We evaluated our proposed model on the publicly available Alzheimer's Disease Neuroimaging Initiative (ADNI) dataset. Experimental results demonstrate that our model achieved an accuracy of 91.34% in the binary classification of AD versus cognitively normal (CN) subjects. Our study confirms that the proposed strategy, which synergizes multi-scale feature fusion with 3D spatial attention, can significantly enhance the performance of automated AD diagnosis, presenting an effective and reliable auxiliary tool for potential clinical application.

title: Power, delay Optimized MBIST Controller Architecture using March Algorithms
authors: Dhruv Sharma, Abhijit Asati
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Recent Trends in Electrical, Electronics and Computing Technologies (ICRTEECT)
abstract: Memory Built in Self-Test (MBIST) is useful detecting faults in System on Chip (SOC) embedded memories. The MBIST help in detecting these fault with marginal increase in circuit area. This research focuses on the MBIST controllers which are designed using the finite state machine (FSM) based architecture for the five different MARCH algorithms to detect all possible functional faults. The MBSIT design consists of a controller, address generator, comparator and memory. The 16-bit memory is divided into sixteen banks, each for one bit and having 10-bit address. The modeling of the MBIST and the functional fault is done using Verilog HDL and pre verification for fault detection is performed using simulation. The synthesis results MBIST controllers are optimized for speed, power and power delay product (PDP) using Genus synthesis tool. Further, we compared their results, such as area, power consumption and delay etc.

title: Risk Analysis of Extreme Earthquake Events in Indonesia Using Sub-Sampling Block Maxima
authors: Annisa Fathimah Azzahra, Achmad Choiruddin, Sutikno
publish: 2025 IEEE Asia-Pacific Conference on Geoscience, Electronics and Remote Sensing Technology (AGERS)
abstract: Indonesia is one of the most seismically active regions in the world, where extreme earthquakes pose significant risks to human life and infrastructure. This study focuses on Sumatra, a region strongly influenced by the Sunda megathrust, a major subduction zone capable of generating high-magnitude earthquakes. Accurate modeling of such rare high-impact events is essential for disaster risk management Extreme Value Theory (EVT) provides a framework to understand these infrequent but high-impact events. The Block Maxima (BM) and Peaks Over Threshold (POT) methods commonly used in EVT have limitations such as data inefficiency and dependence on subjective thresholds. To overcome these, this study uses the Sub-Sampling Block Maxima method to improve the efficiency and objectivity of extreme earthquake risk estimates in Indonesia. Daily earthquake energy data were analyzed to estimate extreme value parameters, specifically the Extreme Value Index (EVI) and Extremal Index (EI). The estimated EVI parameter is greater than 1, which means that the distribution has a heavy tail. The estimated EI parameter is around $\mathbf{0. 4}$, indicating that extreme events occur independently over time. Return levels for maximum earthquake magnitudes were predicted for 1-, 5-, 10-, and 100-year intervals and are expected to reach up to magnitude 11 on the Richter scale with high confidence. Model performance evaluated using the Mean Absolute Percentage Error (MAPE), which yielded a value of 26.94%, showing strong agreement between observed and predicted extremes. These findings demonstrate the effectiveness of Sub-Sampling Block Maxima for modeling the statistical behavior of extreme earthquakes and provide valuable insights for earthquake risk assessment and mitigation strategies in seismically active regions like Sumatra.

title: Subgraph-Based Causal Discovery under Non-Linear Models and Soft Interventions
authors: Chen Peng, Urbashi Mitra
publish: 2025 59th Asilomar Conference on Signals, Systems, and Computers
abstract: This paper investigates the problem of causal discovery from heterogeneous observational and soft-interventional data, with an emphasis on controlling false negatives, which often carry greater practical significance than false positives. A subgraph learning procedure was developed in prior work for linear structural equation models, but its guarantees and algorithmic structure rely on linearity. The present work considers the more challenging non-linear setting, in which causal relationships may vary across interventions. To accommodate such non-linear causal mechanisms, the subgraph learning framework is redesigned to remain computationally and sample efficient. Under a derived sufficient condition, it is shown that the proposed method achieves asymptotic zero false negatives. Numerical results further indicate consistent performance improvements with increasing sample size and demonstrate advantages over an existing non-linear causal discovery algorithm in terms of both false-negative rate and structural Hamming distance.

title: Evaluating Text-to-Speech for NPC-to-NPC Dialogues in 3D Scenes: A User Study with Video-Rendered Conversations for VR
authors: Alexandre C. Ferro Filho, Gustavo H. W. Barbosa, Andressa A. Bastos, Leticia L. Mendes, Augusto S. da Rosa, Paula Leandra L. Pinto, Gabriel P. L. de Lucena, Rafael T. Sousa, Arlindo R. Galvão Filho
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and eXtended and Virtual Reality (AIxVR)
abstract: Recent advances in open-source text-to-speech (TTS) systems promise scalable solutions for populating virtual reality (VR) environments with diverse and interactive non-player characters (NPCs). This paper presents a perceptual user study evaluating seven modern TTS models embedded in video-rendered 3D scenes inspired by VR dialogue contexts. Participants rated nine dimensions of speech quality, including naturalness, emotional adequacy, clarity, fluency, and voice consistency. Results show that intelligibility is solved (clarity >4.5 across all models). Chatterbox achieved the best overall performance, particularly in naturalness and emotional adequacy, with XTTS and FishSpeech also ranking highly. In contrast, SparkTTS, while expressive, suffered from unstable speaker identity. These findings highlight that while clarity is now a baseline, advancing VR requires TTS systems that deliver expressive, consistent, and characterful speech, with models like Chatterbox pointing the way forward. The study does not claim to replicate full in-headset VR experiences, but instead provides an initial proxy for audio-visual dialogue evaluation relevant to immersive applications.

title: Temperature-Compensated, IoT-Enabled Portable Ion-Selective Array Device for Multi-Parameter Measurements in Water Samples
authors: Sreerama Amrutha Lahari, Puvvula Talpa Sai Sri Charan, R N Ponnalagu, Sanket Goel
publish: IEEE Transactions on Instrumentation and Measurement
abstract: To address the growing demand for decentralized, real-time water monitoring of hazardous water pollutants, this study presents a portable, multiplexed solid-state ion-selective membrane (ISM) sensor array capable of simultaneously detecting nitrate (NO₃⁻), ammonium (NH₄⁺), and chloride (Cl⁻) in water samples. This portable system integrates temperature compensation for reliable field analysis and an IoT framework for remote data transmission, enabling real-time monitoring and informed decision-making. The ISM electrode array, fabricated via screen-printing with selective membranes, showed near-Nernstian sensitivities (NO₃⁻: -54.66, NH₄⁺: +48.73, Cl⁻: -44.16 mV/decade) and wide detection ranges (NO₃⁻: 10–500 μM, NH₄⁺: 1–100 μM, Cl⁻: 10–1000 μM), aligning with world health organization (WHO) potable water standards. With a fast 30-second response time, minimal drift (3.3 mV/h for NO₃⁻, 2.2 mV/h for NH₄⁺, and 2.8 mV/h for Cl⁻), and low hysteresis effect (4.2 mV for NO₃⁻, 4.1 mV for NH₄⁺, and 3.9 mV for Cl⁻ ions), the compact device (95×65×18 mm) which incorporates a hardware analog front end, transmits data via a wireless gateway to a smartphone app. The device was tested in real-time using lake water near Hyderabad, India, confirmed its effectiveness for decentralized, in-field point-of-source water quality assessment.

title: Computer-Aided Tuning of Acoustic Wave Filters via Selective Patterning
authors: Matthew Ou, Raafat R. Mansour
publish: IEEE Transactions on Microwave Theory and Techniques
abstract: We report an on-wafer computer-aided frequency tuning method for interdigital transducer (IDT)-based acoustic wave resonators and filters exploiting a selective patterning technique. Experimental evaluations of various pattern geometries on acoustic resonators demonstrated fine frequency trimming and preserving the electromechanical coupling coefficient within the operational range. The method permits simultaneous differential frequency adjustment across multiple resonators, providing precise alignment of the overall filter transfer function and supporting closed-loop correction at the wafer level. The proposed method provides a computer-aided tuning framework tailored for acoustic filters, extending capabilities beyond conventional tuning techniques and band targeting in acoustic filters.

authors: Agung Rahmat Ramadhan, Seehwan Yoo, Jongmoo Choi
publish: Proceedings of the ACM on Measurement and Analysis of Computing Systems
abstract: None

title: Janus: A Dual-Mask Attention Transformer for Log-based Anomaly Detection in Cellular Networks
authors: Umakant Kulkarni, Sonia Fahmy
publish: Proceedings of the ACM on Measurement and Analysis of Computing Systems
abstract: None

title: Dynamic Contrastive Learning for Hierarchical Retrieval: A Case Study of Distance-Aware Cross-View Geo-Localization
authors: Suofei Zhang, Xinxin Wang, Xiaofu Wu, Quan Zhou, Haifeng Hu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
abstract: Existing deep learning-based cross-view geolocalization methods primarily focus on improving the accuracy of cross-domain image matching. Less attention is paid to ensuring that models can comprehensively capture contextual information around the target and minimize the cost of localization errors. To support quantitative research into this Distance-Aware Cross-View Geo-Localization (DACVGL) problem, we construct Distance-Aware Campus (DA-Campus), the first benchmark that pairs multi-view imagery with precise distance annotations across three spatial resolutions. Based on DA-Campus, we formulate DACVGL as a hierarchical retrieval problem across different domains. In this setting, we further identify that due to the inherent complexity of spatial relationships among buildings, conventional metric learning lacks a unified semantic hierarchy to guide the organization of the latent feature space. To tackle this challenge, we propose Dynamic Contrastive Learning (DyCL), a novel framework that progressively aligns feature representations according to spatial margins. Extensive experiments demonstrate that DyCL can serve as a strong baseline for the DACVGL task, yielding substantial improvements in both hierarchical retrieval performance and overall geo-localization accuracy. Our code and benchmark are publicly available at https://github.com/anocodetest1/DyCL.

title: Synergistic Adaptive Robust Fault-Tolerant Control for Linear Motors: A Fully Actuated System Reference Model Approach
authors: Shixiang Jia, Jianbin Qiu, Juan J. Rodríguez-Andina, Kai Chen
abstract: High-precision, high-reliability control of linear motors is crucial for intelligent manufacturing. However, existing methods are hindered by insufficient adaptive convergence, reliance on known uncertainty bounds, dependence on reference trajectory derivatives, and vulnerability to actuator faults. To overcome these limitations, a control scheme is proposed that synergistically integrates fixed-gain robust control and neural networks.
