To handle discontinuous reference signals, an ideal model is presented as a trajectory planner to provide the necessary derivatives based on the fully actuated system approach. The proposed synergistic controller does not require knowledge of uncertainty bounds in the stability analysis and effectively handles time-varying actuator faults. Experiments on a linear motor platform verify its effectiveness, demonstrating that the design ensures high precision even with a reduced number of neurons and in the presence of strong disturbances.

title: A Self-supervised Learning Framework for Abandoned Cropland Identification Based on Transformer for Temporal Spectral Sequences
authors: Han Zhou, Tingting Zhang, JianYu Yang, Xinyue Chen, Anjin Dai, Xuchao Guo
publish: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
abstract: Abandoned cropland (AC) is a major type of landcover and land use change that threats food security, nature conservation and the sustainability of land resources. However, in practical applications, existing AC identification methods often struggle to identify effectively and accurately due to issues such as error accumulation in model outputs, sparse labelling and insufficient model generalization capability. To address these issues, we propose a temporal-spectral series pattern self-supervised learning framework (TSPS) for AC identification. Specifically, we first propose the Transformer-based temporal spectral variation pattern identification network (TSVINet), which designed to solve the problems of error accumulation in model results and insufficient generalization observed in existing studies, without expanding the feature space. Then, a self-supervised pre-training strategy, Byol-Index, is proposed. It is used to learn robust and generalizable representations from satellite imagery time series data. Finally, an AC sample generation strategy is proposed to address the problem of sparse labels. To evaluate the proposed method, we conducted experiments in six different study regions in China. The experimental results show that the TSVINet proposed in this paper can effectively mitigate the problem of error accumulation in model results during the AC identification process. It achieves an average accuracy of 93.11% in the six study areas. Through qualitative analysis, it is evident that the pre-training strategy can effectively reduce the dependence of downstream AC identification tasks on labelled data. Overall, TSPS makes the best use of existing sample data and provides a simple and cost-effective solution for accurate AC identification on a large scale.

title: MIMO Through-the-Wall Radar Micro-Doppler Signature Representation Under Limited Data Using Heterogeneous Transfer Learning
authors: Weicheng Gao, Kun Dong, Jingyi Feng, Yiwen Xu, Xiaodong Qu, Xiaopeng Yang
abstract: Identifying indoor individuals using micro-Doppler signature of multiple-input multiple-output (MIMO) through-the-wall radar (TWR), and determining whether they pose a threat holds significant research value in the field of urban security surveillance. However, large-scale TWR human motion data is difficult to collect, which reduces the recognition performance. To address this issue, a MIMO TWR micro-Doppler signature representation method under limited data based on heterogeneous transfer learning is proposed in this letter. The multi-channel TWR human motion Doppler-time maps (DTMs) are first generated, and the trace-ratio group sparse method is then proposed for multi-channel DTM feature augmentation. In addition, a micro-Doppler signature representation method based on optimal transport domain adaptation heterogeneous transfer learning is proposed. By leveraging large-scale millimeter-wave radar human gait data, the proposed method guides the micro-Doppler signature representation to maximize inter-class separation on the TWR DTM set. The effectiveness of the proposed method is validated through a few-shot measured dataset collected for TWR human threat identification.

title: Multiple-Output Level Shifters With Dead-Time Control for Gate Driving in Series-Stacked Voltage Domains
authors: Qassam Farhat, Mehdi Baghdadi
publish: IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems I: Regular Papers
abstract: This paper proposes three integrated multiple-output voltage level shifter circuits with input-side dead-time generation tailored for systems with series-connected voltage sources: (i) a cross-coupled active level shifter, (ii) a continuous active-coupled level shifter, and (iii) a capacitive-coupled level shifter. The proposed designs are optimized for key performance metrics, including high speed, compact layout area, and robustness against process, voltage, and temperature (PVT) variations. Architectural features such as modularity and scalability are also addressed. All circuits operate differentially and incorporate dead-time control, enabling complementary voltage outputs suitable for directly controlling gate drivers in multi-cell applications, such as battery balancers in battery management systems. Designed and fabricated in a 130-nm Bipolar-CMOS-DMOS (BCD) technology, the circuits are validated through comprehensive post-layout transient and Monte Carlo simulations. Results demonstrate a <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$17\times $</tex-math> </inline-formula> reduction in propagation delay and a <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$22\times $</tex-math> </inline-formula>–<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$66\times $</tex-math> </inline-formula> reduction in average dead-time spread under process and mismatch variations, while supporting a wide operating input voltage range from 4.3 V down to 2 V. Measurement results validate correct operation and confirm the proposed circuits’ performance in terms of voltage compatibility, speed, and dead-time controllability.

title: First global detection of geothermal anomalies from land surface temperature: A spatiotemporal framework with novel temporal and spatial trend removal methods
authors: Yan Li, Dongping Ming, Liang Zhang, Yunyun Niu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
abstract: Geothermal anomalies, as surface manifestations of geothermal activity, are essential indicators of subsurface processes, and mapping them is a key step toward understanding the global distribution of geothermal potential. However, large-scale patterns remain poorly characterized due to limited ground-based observations and complicated environmental interferences. This study presents the first global detection of geothermal anomalies from land surface temperature. Spatial and temporal geothermal anomalies are extracted separately then integrated, employing an innovative dual-perspective to compensate for the respective limitations, significantly enhancing geothermal detection accuracy. To address longstanding challenges such as the interference of spatial heterogeneity, seasonal trends, and sudden thermal events in geothermal anomaly extraction, the framework introduces newly defined modules including spatial trend removal, the Multi-scale Spatial Thermal Anomaly Index (STAI), and Seasonal-Trend decomposition using Loess (STL). The detected geothermal anomalies distribution exhibits both consistency and differences in spatial and temporal dimensions, largely shaped by climate, topography, land cover, and observational conditions. The results are validated using hot springs, volcanoes, and geothermal power plants. For hot springs, the validation data densities in temporal, spatial, and spatiotemporal geothermal anomalies are 57.90, 72.29, and 150.49 ×10⁻⁴ points/km², respectively; for geothermal power plants, 10.46, 5.42, and 17.24 ×10⁻⁴ points/km²; and for volcanoes, 11.95, 17.63, and 17.24 ×10⁻⁴ points/km². The spatiotemporal framework effectively captures geothermal-related activities, providing a more comprehensive characterization of geothermal distribution patterns. Rather than Fine-scale local geothermal exploration, this study mainly focuses on global distribution patterns of geothermal potential, providing new insights for geothermal research and a scientific basis for exploration and sustainable energy planning.

title: Deep Prior Information Constrained Full Waveform Inversion For Ground Penetrating Radar
authors: Meijia Huang, Yonghao Wang, Ling Zhang, Yabin Li, Zhuo Jia
publish: IEEE Transactions on Antennas and Propagation
abstract: This study proposes an improved full-waveform inversion (FWI) method based on deep learning to address the challenges of high accuracy requirements for initial models, high computational complexity, and low efficiency in traditional ground-penetrating radar (GPR) inversion. Traditional FWI methods rely on an accurate initial model and iteratively update the model through multiple forward processes, which are computationally expensive and time-consuming. To overcome these issues, we introduce a deep learning-driven inversion framework that rapidly generates an accurate initial model, which is then used as a plugin for the FWI process. The deep learning model replaces traditional FWI model corrections and forward processes, with the predicted initial model serving as the foundation for FWI updates, accelerating the inversion and enhancing both robustness and interpretability. In model design, we employ a Unet++ network architecture with depthwise separable convolutions to improve computational efficiency. In the design of the loss function, we combine Multi-Scale Structural Similarity (MS-SSIM) loss with Laplacian pyramid loss to ensure that the inversion results maintain structural similarity while recovering high-frequency details of the image. We validate the proposed method through experiments using synthetic data, comparing results with traditional FWI methods. The inversion outcomes are evaluated using metrics like structural similarity (SSIM), peak signal-to-noise ratio (PSNR), MSE, and inversion time. Results show that the proposed method significantly improves both accuracy and efficiency.

title: Guest Editorial: Introduction to the Special Section on Advances in Antenna Design and Radio Propagation for Integrated Sensing and Communications
authors: Qi Wu, Xuesong Cai, Peng Mei, Peize Zhang, Zijian Shao
publish: IEEE Open Journal of Antennas and Propagation

title: Wideband Radiation Pattern and Port Decoupling Method for E - and H -Plane Linear Arrays
authors: Shuhao Hu, Zhiqi Ke, Kwok Wa Leung, Nan Yang, Kai Lu, Peng Fei Hu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Antennas and Propagation
abstract: For the first time, a propagating mode cancellation method is proposed for wideband radiation pattern and port decoupling in semi-open waveguide. Metallic vias and strips can be used to suppress the fundamental TMz and TEz modes of the semi-open waveguide, respectively. By tuning the decoupling structure, the forward propagating wave can be largely canceled and therefore a standing wave is formed. This cancellation effectively confines the electromagnetic energy within each antenna element, enabling wideband radiation pattern decoupling and high port isolation. Based on propagating mode, this technique is inherently broadband. To verify the proposed method, two 1 × 8 multiple-input multiple-output antenna arrays operating are designed, fabricated, and measured for both E- and H-plane configurations. The measured overlapping bandwidths are 10.8% and 9.7% for the E- and H-plane arrays, respectively, with isolation levels higher than 20 dB. Furthermore, stable and uniform radiation patterns are consistently maintained across these bands, with the maximum radiation direction along the boresight direction.

title: A Random Field-Theoretic Framework for Electromagnetic Information Computation
authors: Lumeng Xu, Said Mikki
publish: IEEE Transactions on Antennas and Propagation
abstract: We present a comprehensive full-wave random field (RF) Green’s function (GF) computational framework tailored to electromagnetic (EM) information theory (EIT) and stochastic electromagnetics, with direct relevance to communication systems. The framework is anchored in exact dyadic Green’s functions, including the transceiver antenna current Green’s function (ACGF), hence shifting the emphasis from internal structural details to input–output relations aligned with the spirit of signal processing principles. The Karhunen–Loève expansion (KLE) is employed to represent all fields in terms of orthogonal modal bases, enabling physically consistent simulations over arbitrary spatial domains. Our results demonstrate that, even for input Gaussian RFs, EM communication systems may give rise to non-Gaussian and improper complex RFs. We also show how mutual information can be computed directly from intrinsic EM fluctuations in noise-free settings. Overall, this novel computational method furnishes a flexible and efficient platform for analyzing and optimizing EM information in emerging communication technologies.

title: Hyperspectral Image Denoising via Deep Block Term Decomposition
authors: Zhirui Min, Ting-Zhu Huang, Wei-Hao Wu
publish: IEEE Geoscience and Remote Sensing Letters
abstract: Recently, block-term decomposition (BTD) has been successfully applied to hyperspectral image (HSI) processing. In particular, the rank-(L, L, 1) BTD, also known as the LL1 decomposition, decomposes the HSI tensor as a sum of component tensors formed by matrix–vector outer products of rank-L core component matrices and vectors. However, these fundamental building blocks, i.e., core component matrices and matrix-vector outer products, exhibit limited flexibility, struggling to effectively capture the complex information in HSIs. To tackle this limitation, we propose a novel deep block-term decomposition (DBTD), which leverages attention-enhanced generators to generate core component tensors instead of core component matrices, and substitutes the fixed matrix-vector outer product with a learnable convolutional mechanism. This approach enables the proposed DBTD to achieve greater flexibility and expressive power for complex HSI structures. Notably, our proposed DBTD serves as a generalization of the classical LL1 decomposition, which degenerates to the classical LL1 decomposition when the core component tensor is simplified back to a low-rank matrix. Building upon the proposed DBTD, we propose a DBTD-based model for HSI denoising. To solve the resulting model, we develop an efficient alternating minimization-based algorithm. Extensive experimental results collectively demonstrate that the proposed method outperforms the competing methods.

title: Real-Time Resonant-Current Modulation to Suppressing Negative Startup Current in an Asymmetrical Half-Bridge Flyback Converter
authors: Ming-Chang Tsou, Yun-Keng Cheng, Chao-Chih Lin
publish: IEEE Journal of Emerging and Selected Topics in Power Electronics
abstract: This paper proposes a real-time resonant-current (ICR)–based modulation strategy to suppress negative startup current—i.e., the negative resonant current during bootstrap pre‑charge—in an asymmetrical half‑bridge flyback converter (AHBFC) subjected to fast AC on/off transients. During rapid cycling, residual energy and transformer leakage inductance (Lk) can induce large negative startup current in the pre‑charge phase, risking low‑side switch (LSW) overstress and compromising high‑side bootstrap reliability. The proposed method senses ICR via a dedicated path and dynamically modulates the LSW conduction interval to cap the peak negative startup current while ensuring sufficient charging of the high‑side driver supply (VHVCC). On a 240‑W USB‑PD 3.1 prototype, compared with a conventional bleed‑resistor implementation without active pre‑charge control, the method reduces the negative‑peak ICR by 27.1% at PD 20 V and 50.5% at PD 48 V, corresponding to a maximum reduction of 14.6 A. In particular, under a representative fast restart condition at PD 48 V with an 8‑ms AC‑off interruption, the peak negative startup current is halved (20 A → 10 A) without compromising the total bootstrap duration. By eliminating the bleed path while preserving safe restart operation, the prototype achieves an 8.9% (29.3 mW) reduction in no‑load input power at PD 48 V, contributing to improved standby efficiency and reduced energy consumption. Furthermore, startup robustness and overall efficiency are maintained, ensuring full PD 3.1 compatibility with minimal hardware overhead.

title: A Coupled-Output-Inductor-Based ISOP-PSFB Converter with Changeable Inter-Module Phase for Performance Enhancement
authors: Kang Hong, Guo Xu, Yao Sun, Mei Su
abstract: This paper proposes an Input-Series-Output- Parallel Phase-Shifted Full-Bridge (ISOP-PSFB) converter featuring coupled output inductors and a dual-phase-angle operation between sub-modules to enhance performance. With in-phase operation, the converter works as a conventional PSFB converter with Zero Voltage Switching (ZVS). While, with interleaved 90° phase operation, the coupled inductors enable mutual current interaction, reducing circulating currents, eliminating duty cycle loss, and achieving Zero Voltage and Zero Current Switching (ZVZCS). Unlike the conventional improved ISOP-PSFB converters that require auxiliary networks, the proposed design makes the two existing output inductors to be coupled and changes the inter-module phase under different operation conditions, thus eliminating extra auxiliary components while improving performance. The converter employs interleaved 90° phase operation under light loads and transitions to in-phase operation at heavy loads. This paper analyzes the topology, operating principles, and key characteristics, with experimental results validating its effectiveness.

title: Future Special Issues/Special Sections of the Proceedings
authors: N/A

title: Movable Antenna-Enabled Secure Communications Against Simultaneous Jamming and Eavesdropping Attacks
authors: Liangsen Zhai, Xiapu Luo
abstract: This paper focuses on a movable antenna (MA) enabled downlink multi-user communication system, where a base station (BS) aims to transmit messages to multiple users with a malicious jammer and multiple eavesdroppers posing threats. Both the BS and the users feature MAs, while the jammer and eavesdroppers make use of fixed-position antennas (FPAs). In accordance with the BS’s transmission power budget, designated MA moving regions, and minimum spacing between transmitting MAs, the system’s minimum secrecy rate is maximized through the joint design of transmit beamforming and both transmitting and receiving MA locations. To solve the non-trivial problem, a block coordinate descent (BCD) algorithm employing successive convex approximation (SCA) is implemented by iteratively designing the transmit beamforming, the specific transmitting MA position, and the receiving MA positions. Herein, auxiliary variables, first- and/or second-order Taylor expansions, and approximate construction methods for non-convex terms are introduced to handle each subproblem. Numerical results indicate that repositioning the transmitting MAs is more effective in combating eavesdropping attacks, while relocating the receiving MAs is more beneficial for countering jamming attacks. In comparison to traditional FPA schemes, the proposed scheme attains a notably greater system’s minimum secrecy rate.

title: HicAgent: Hierarchical Iterative Cooperative Learning Reward Generation Guided by a Large Model
authors: Shimin Kan, Qi Hu, Shutong Jia, Linhui Lou
publish: 2025 International Conference on Big Data and Data Mining (BDDM)
abstract: Reward design plays a fundamental role in reinforcement learning (RL), yet it typically requires extensive domain expertise and significant manual effort. In conventional RL paradigms, the construction of reward functions is heavily dependent on expert knowledge, making the process time-consuming and labor-intensive. Recent advancements in large language models (LLMs) have introduced new opportunities for automating and optimizing reward design, owing to their powerful semantic understanding, code synthesis, and contextual reasoning capabilities. In this paper, we present HicAgent, a hierarchical-iterative-cooperative framework for automated reward design. The proposed framework integrates environmental perception, dynamic feedback, and multi-agent collaboration to support adaptive and interpretable reward generation. HicAgent incorporates key components such as zero-shot reward generation, evolutionary search, and iterative reward optimization, effectively addressing core challenges including reward sparsity, reward hacking, and multi-objective balance. Experimental results demonstrate that HicAgent surpasses four baseline methods, achieving success rates of 90.8% in robotic manipulation tasks, 87.2% in autonomous navigation, and 83.5% in game control environments. These results validate the framework generality, efficiency, and interpretability in complex RL settings.

title: Research on the Construction and Management of a Railway Engineering Progress Knowledge Graph Based on Large Models
authors: Wenna Fan, Wenlong Lu, Rui Hao, Jiajia Guo, Suhua Zhang, Fei Li, Baoshi Zhu
publish: 2025 International Conference on Big Data and Data Mining (BDDM)
abstract: To precisely and efficiently analyze the construction progress of railway engineering projects, unlock the intrinsic value of progress data, and provide realtime analysis and intelligent decision-making solutions for railway construction, it is imperative to advance research on the construction and management of knowledge graphs for railway engineering progress. Addressing the gaps in current domestic research, this study first examines the entities (including information, resources, organizations, and environment), attributes, and relationships involved in existing railway project progress control systems. An integrated construction progress knowledge graph is then developed using large language models (LLMs). Furthermore, a knowledge graph-based construction management strategy is proposed to identify potential risks in advance through risk labeling, enabling timely warnings, feedback, and targeted solutions to support informed decision-making.

title: Integrated Wellness Monitoring and Real-Time Position Tracking System for Hill Trekkers Using IoT and GPS Technologies
authors: P. Saravanan, Leenakumari S, Harshita Lakshmi Kancharla, Gundu Mounika
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Recent Trends in Electrical, Electronics and Computing Technologies (ICRTEECT)
abstract: Mountain climbing is a high-risk sport often performed in remote, high-altitude regions, where communication delays and limited situational awareness hinder rescue operations. Key challenges include difficulty in locating injured climbers, lack of real-time medical data, and uncertainty about their condition. This article proposes using wearable sensors and IoT networks to enable real-time monitoring of vital signs and location. Centralized data analysis allows for predictive modeling and timely decision-making. By shifting from reactive to proactive safety management, this system enhances situational awareness, improves rescue response times, and offers a scalable solution for improving safety in extreme, low-connectivity environments.

title: Cell-free ISAC for Drone Detection Considering Coverage and Age of Sensing
authors: Zinat Behdad, Ozan Alp Topal, Cicek Cavdar
publish: 2025 59th Asilomar Conference on Signals, Systems, and Computers
abstract: The growing presence of unauthorized drones poses significant threats to public safety, underscoring the need for aerial surveillance solutions. This work proposes a cell-free integrated sensing and communication (ISAC) framework enabling drone detection within the existing communication network infrastructure, while maintaining communication services. The system exploits the spatial diversity and coordination of distributed access points (APs) in a cell-free massive MIMO architecture to detect aerial passive targets. To evaluate sensing performance, we introduce two key metrics: age of sensing (AoS), capturing the freshness of sensing information, and sensing coverage. The proposed AoS metric includes not only the transmission delays as in the existing models, but also the processing for sensing and networking delay, which are critical in dynamic environments like drone detection. We introduce an ambiguity parameter quantifying the similarity between the target-to-receiver channels for two hotspots and develop a novel network configuration strategy, including hotspot grouping, AP clustering, and sensing pilot assignment, leveraging simultaneous multi-point sensing to minimize AoS. Our results show that the best trade-off between AoS and sensing coverage is achieved when the number of hotspots sharing the same time/frequency resource matches the number of sensing pilots, indicating ambiguity as the primary factor limiting the sensing performance.

title: Secure AI Runtime System Based on the M3 Platform
authors: Mattis Hasler, Michael Raitza, Friedrich Pauls, Gerhard Fettweis
publish: 2025 59th Asilomar Conference on Signals, Systems, and Computers
abstract: AI workloads demand a tightly integrated and secure interplay between hardware and software components. Increasing system complexity raises the risk of hardware exploits and side-channel attacks, challenging both hardware and operating system security. The M3 platform addresses these issues through a co-design of multiprocessor hardware and a micro-kernel runtime. By partitioning the system into isolated tiles —each with its own processor or accelerator—and mediating all inter-tile communication via a trusted communication unit, M3 enforces strict hardware-level access controls defined by the operating system. This architecture minimizes the trusted computing base for applications, reducing the attack surface and enabling secure, high-performance AI workloads.

title: Comparative Performance Analysis of String Matching Algorithms and Data Matching Frameworks Using Python Libraries on Academic Datasets
authors: Muhammad Ridho Waradana, Venia Anisya Rakhmanda, Ganendra Aby Bhamakerti, Fikri Yoma Rosyidan, Nur Aini Rakhmawati
publish: 2025 International Conference on Innovation and Intelligence for Informatics, Computing, and Technologies (3ICT)
abstract: This study conducts a comparative analysis of diverse string matching algorithms (Jaro-Winkler, Edit Distance, Jaccard, Hamming) and data matching frameworks (Splink, RLTK, etc.) implemented using Python libraries on degraded academic datasets. Methodologically, performance was quantified via precision, recall, and F1-score across varying degrees of data corruption. Findings indicate that no single method achieves universal optimality. Jaro-Winkler and the Splink framework demonstrate superior precision, minimizing false positives. Conversely, Edit Distance and the RLTK framework yield maximum recall, capturing a broader range of potential matches but at a higher risk of false positives. The implication is that reliable entity resolution necessitates a tiered, adaptive pipeline. This integrated approach balances the trade-offs between precision and recall, supporting consistent, cost-effective, and reliable entity resolution across heterogeneous academic records.

title: A Hybrid LEACH and RSSI-LEACH Algorithm for Energy Efficient Cluster-Based Routing in WSN
authors: Anshu, Priya Khanduri, Monika Kanwar, Guddakesh Kr. Chandan, Kailash Rai, S M Shakina
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Smart Innovations in Systems, Infrastructure, Mechanical, Power, AI and Computing Technologies (SISIMPACT)
abstract: The research examines the use of LEACH together with RSSI-LEACH clustering algorithms to improve Wireless Sensor Network (WSN) energy efficiency. WSN depend on nodes with restricted energy supplies to fulfill responsibilities in environmental monitoring and military surveillance and healthcare functions. Both LEACH and RSSIbased LEACH function to extend network lifetime and reduce power usage. In RSSI-LEACH the incorporation of signal strength metrics along with LEACH's TDMA scheduling and cluster head rotation mechanism leads to better cluster formation and CH selection processes. Simulation data shows RSSI-LEACH achieves better performance than LEACH by enhancing cluster count and presenting decreased end-to-end delay rates together with lower packet drop ratios and superior node survival rates throughout multiple simulation rounds. RSSI-LEACH demonstrates better performance over LEACH with a delay reduction of 33.64% combined with a packet drop ratio decrease of 49.61% while operating with 600 nodes. The results prove that merging RSSI-based metrics within clustering protocols improves both network efficiency and performance which positions RSSI-LEACH as an optimal selection for extensive WSN networks.

title: LSTM based Model for Weather-based Solar Irradiance Prediction for Long-Term PV Energy Planning
authors: Anushka Bandara, Mahela Pandukabhaya, Keshawa Ratnayake, Roshan Godaliyadda, Parakrama Ekanayake, Janaka Ekanayake
publish: 2025 IEEE 19th International Conference on Industrial and Information Systems (ICIIS)
abstract: The increasing global emphasis on sustainable energy solutions has elevated the role of solar power as a prominent renewable energy source. Photovoltaic (PV) systems face the challenge of the presence of an uncertainty in long-term energy production due to the stochastic and intermittent nature of solar irradiance. Accurate forecasting of solar irradiance is essential for planning and optimizing PV plant performance. Traditionally, long-term solar irradiance forecasting relies on historical irradiance data. However, such data is often limited to short durations (2–5 years) at prospective PV plant sites, whereas reliable forecasts may require 15 or more years of data. To address this limitation, we propose a novel deep learning-based methodology that leverages Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) networks enhanced with attention mechanisms to reconstruct historical solar irradiance using available long-term meteorological data. This approach exploits the known correlation between weather parameters and solar irradiance, filling in historical data gaps and enabling long-term forecasting. The proposed model predicts missing irradiance values and also facilitates extended solar energy generation forecasts essential for site planning and investment decisions. A case study is presented to demonstrate the effectiveness of the model, and the proposed model achieved an RMSE of 66.9 W/m<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> and R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> = 0.9578 for Colombo and 56.7 W/m<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>, R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> = 0.9683 for Hotevilla, outperforming the conventional LSTM by approximately 3-5%. Hence, this study contributes to the growing body of research in solar forecasting by offering a practical and accurate solution for long-term irradiance prediction using limited observed data.

title: Detection of Deepfake Images Using Hybrid Convolutional Neural Networks and Transformer Models
authors: Ekant Ratanlal Tekam, Rajesh Nasare, Gaurav Divtelwar, Prajakta Vinayak Upadhye, Minal Rajendra Wadyalkar, Prashant Khobragade
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Smart Innovations in Systems, Infrastructure, Mechanical, Power, AI and Computing Technologies (SISIMPACT)
abstract: The rapid development of the field of deep learning has now provided the possibility to create fake media that appears extremely real. The currently used methods of detection have difficulty in identifying small artefacts due to lack of description of features and lack of representation of spatial-temporal relationships. In response to such issues, the proposed research proposal proposes a hybrid architecture integrating Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) to extract strong local features and Transformer architecture to demonstrate how the relationships and sequence patterns in the global scope operate. The procedure begins with procedures such as normalization and improvement that enhance perhaps variability of data sets and reduce overfitting. CNN modules extract low and middle-level features, which can be used to distinguish images. The representation of the incoming pictures in tensors, CNN feature maps, and attention equations that govern the Transformer integration have mathematical explanations. It is experimentally confirmed that the system is able to distinguish between real and fake material in a series of standard datasets. The proposed approach has some technical contributions, yet it also has some actual implications. It may be applied on such platforms as social media watch and digital surveillance systems, to enable police combat lies. This paper has indicated that mixed deep learning systems might be an appropriate solution to safeguarding the security of the visual information on the digital age.

title: Machine Learning Integration in Fintech for Bridging Data Gaps in Logistics Management
authors: Biswaranjan Swain, Shivam Khurana, Mohammad Idrish Ali, S. Radhika, Intekhab Alam, Ayaan Faiz
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Smart Innovations in Systems, Infrastructure, Mechanical, Power, AI and Computing Technologies (SISIMPACT)
abstract: ML and FinTech are changing how moving assets used in the transport industry is managed by addressing the unrelenting information gaps that create blind spots in visibility, accountability, and inefficiency. Traditional logistics lack consistency, coherent reporting, data discrepancies between nodes in the supply chain and frequent unscheduled updates of the data. The ML models offer new capabilities when it comes to predictive analytics, anomaly detection, data harmonization, real-time analytics, automated decisions. By deploying with FinTech solutions such as digital payments, scoring of credit, analysis of trade finance we shall achieve a highly resilient logistical network that will enhance speed of confidence, transparency, and resilience of processes. The ML algorithms can be taught to bridge structural-behaviour data with meanings using transactions, behavioural and sensor data. More datapoints about the financials of the companies complement these models with risk information and liquidity data to enable a more nuanced decision-making along the whole logistic chain. This synergy enhances tracked assets, prediction and interception in fraud cases that are critical in international logistics operations. The suggested solution portrays the strong trunk of developing the end-to-end visibility and financial consistency of the challenging chains based on the innovative ML and FinTech integration. The proposed system also exhibits higher rates than the other approaches with a CDI of 94.3 % and PFC of 93.6% that raise reliability.

title: Early Alzheimer's Detection Using a Novel CNN Model with Class Imbalance Handling
authors: Farah Shaheen, Mohab Mangoud
publish: 2025 International Conference on Innovation and Intelligence for Informatics, Computing, and Technologies (3ICT)
abstract: Alzheimer's is a brain disorder that impairs memory and cognition, disrupting daily life and relationships. Due to its significant impact on a person's well-being, and that neurodegeneration is not reversible, it is essential to develop a solution that can allow medical professionals to easily diagnose a patient. A customized CNN-driven deep learning model is developed in this paper to enable early detection and accurate classification of Alzheimer disease (AD) from MRI scans. Since the MRI dataset is class imbalanced, Synthetic Minority Oversampling Technique (SMOTE) is applied to rebalance the training distribution before model fitting. The dataset contains four diagnostic categories that reflect progressive impairment: Moderate, Mild, Very Mild and Non demented cases. The proposed framework was compared against widely used CNN baselines and demonstrated higher diagnostic accuracy and reliability in differentiating stages of dementia.

title: Comparative Analysis and Implementation of ML Models for Securing IoT Networks Against Cyber Threats
authors: Ali Hassan Saad, Ali Hussain Alsaegh, Fawaz Abdulkarem Ali, Sayed Ali Nader, Muhannad Badr, Wael Elmedany
publish: 2025 International Conference on Innovation and Intelligence for Informatics, Computing, and Technologies (3ICT)
abstract: The rapid growth of IoT devices has made securing IoT systems a difficult challenge. Current IoT systems are already large and complex, continually growing in size and intricacy. Weaknesses in old and widely used protocols such as TCP/IP, HTTPS, SSH, and MQTT have been increased in IoT systems due to minimalism in computing, memory, and power, alongside the use of cheap, low-specialization, and often outdated components. To address these challenges, a comparative analysis of existing machine learning (ML) approaches for network traffic classification was conducted, identifying their strengths and gaps. Building on these insights, this study introduces a groundbreaking ML strategy to bolster IoT system security. Leveraging supervised ML models like Gradient Boosting (e.g., XGBoost) and Random Forests and focusing on key metrics such as accuracy, precision, recall, and F1-score, the proposed solution emphasizes adaptability and scalability, with predicted accuracy reaching approximately 95–96%. The new method was tested in a virtual IoT environment set up with Cisco Packet Tracer, simulating normal and malicious traffic, including Distributed Denial of Service (DDoS) attacks, unauthorized access attempts, and data injection schemes. This security system counters known vulnerabilities and adapts to new ones through its machinelearning design. This approach should be applied across all IoT ecosystems residential, commercial, and industrial to streamline oversight for Centers of Security Operations (CSOs) and furnish strong safeguards against hacking.

title: Optimizing Internal Data Width in FFT Accelerators for Signal-to-Noise Ratio and Area Efficiency
authors: Daniel A. Chevy, Bevan M. Baas
publish: 2025 59th Asilomar Conference on Signals, Systems, and Computers
abstract: The Fast Fourier Transform (FFT) and Inverse Fast Fourier Transform (IFFT) are algorithms widely used in signal processing applications to efficiently convert signals between time domain and frequency domain representations. This paper explores how varying the internal data width in a fixed-point FFT processor impacts the output signal-to-noise ratio (SNR) and hardware cost measured as the synthesized standard cell area. A variable-data-width 1024-point complex FFT processor is implemented using a radix-4 butterfly and measured using complex white noise data. Increasing the internal data width improves SNR but incurs additional area cost, creating an accuracy-area trade-off. Among the configurations tested, the highest SNR-per-area ratio occurs when the internal data width matches the I/O width. Increasing the data width further improves accuracy but with diminishing returns. These findings provide practical design guidance for the selection of the word widths of internal datapath circuits and memories in fixed-point FFT processors.

title: Generative AI for Automated VLSI Layout Generation
authors: Lenin Kumar, Tanuja SKS Jyothirmayi, Bh.V. Ramakrishna, Ravula Bharath, Challa Naresh, G. Lavanya
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Smart Innovations in Systems, Infrastructure, Mechanical, Power, AI and Computing Technologies (SISIMPACT)
abstract: The high pace of development of the Very-large-scale Integration (VLSI) technology has caused the complexity of design to increase, so the manual layout generation becomes time-consuming, error-prone and inefficient. This could potentially be solved by current advances in generative artificial intelligence (AI) which automatically generate VLSI layout synthesis and optimize performance, area and power consumption. This paper examines these machine learning methods based on generative artificial intelligence, such as Generative Adversarial Networks (GANs) and Variational Autoencoders (VAEs), to layout a VLSI automatically. The suggested system utilizes AI to understand design regulations, the layout plans as well as the routing plans through current layouts and creates new layouts with minimum human involvement. In an experimental study, the results indicated that the generative models can attain a high level of reduction of design time and efficiency of resource utilization, without violating design constraints. Other difficulties found in the study are scalability, the ability to integrate with other existing Electronic Design Automation (EDA) tools, and model generalization to a variety of circuit topologies. The limitations are addressed in the future research directions in order to increase the robustness and applicability of AI-driven VLSI layout automation.

title: The Hacker View: Significance of External Attack Surface Management to Organizations
authors: Mohamed Sami Ragheb, Alauddin Al-Omary
publish: 2025 International Conference on Innovation and Intelligence for Informatics, Computing, and Technologies (3ICT)
abstract: Organizations often struggle to adequately identify and assess their Attack Surface (AS), leaving numerous critical digital assets unsecured and vulnerable to adversarial exploitation. As these digital assets play a pivotal role in projecting an organization's reputation and presence on the Internet, ensuring their security is paramount. This paper contributes a structured analysis of External Attack Surface Management (EASM), clarifying its processes (asset discovery, categorization, mapping, and risk scoring) and demonstrating their practical application through a commercial platform example. Unlike existing surveys, the paper highlights operational insights from applying EASM to real-world contexts and proposes future research directions to improve detection accuracy and asset validation.

title: Cloud-Based Cybersecurity Threat Detection Using AI: A Multi-Stream Fusion Approach
authors: Tarun Agarwal, Sanjay N, Shravankumar Kudari, Srijan Angadi, Vidya
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Emerging Trends in Computing and Communication (ETCOM)
abstract: Cloud computing has become the backbone of modern digital infrastructure, but it brings serious security challenges that traditional methods struggle to handle. We present a novel AI-driven threat detection system called CATD (Cloud-AI Threat Detector) that tackles these challenges head-on. Our system works by simultaneously analyzing network traffic patterns and system logs using two parallel AI pipelines, then combining their insights for more accurate threat detection [1]. Through extensive testing on real-world datasets like UNSW-NB15 and CICIDS2017, CATD achieved 97.3 % accuracy while processing threats in just <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{2. 3}$</tex> milliseconds. This performance significantly outpaces existing single-stream solutions and proves especially effective against sophisticated attacks like advanced persistent threats and insider threats. The system maintains low false alarm rates, making it practical for real-world deployment in production cloud environments. Comprehensive ablation studies demonstrate the contribution of each component, while production deployment across AWS, Azure, and GCP confirmed 99.2 % uptime and 0.8 % false positive rate, validating the effectiveness of multi-stream AI-driven architectures for cloud security.

title: Inference-time Stochastic Refinement of GRU-Normalizing Flow for Real-time Video Motion Transfer
authors: Tasmiah Haque, Srinjoy Das
publish: 2025 59th Asilomar Conference on Signals, Systems, and Computers
abstract: Real-time video motion transfer applications such as immersive gaming and vision-based anomaly detection require accurate yet diverse future predictions to support realistic synthesis and robust downstream decision making under uncertainty. To improve the diversity of such sequential forecasts we propose a novel inference-time refinement technique that combines Gated Recurrent Unit-Normalizing Flows (GRU-NF) with stochastic sampling methods. While GRU-NF can capture multimodal distributions through its integration of normalizing flows within a temporal forecasting framework, its deterministic transformation structure can limit expressivity. To address this, inspired by Stochastic Normalizing Flows (SNF), we introduce Markov Chain Monte Carlo (MCMC) steps during GRU-NF inference, enabling the model to explore a richer output space and better approximate the true data distribution without retraining. We validate our approach in a keypoint-based video motion transfer pipeline, where capturing temporally coherent and perceptually diverse future trajectories is essential for realistic samples and low bandwidth communication. Experiments show that our inference framework, Gated Recurrent Unit-Stochastic Normalizing Flows (GRU-SNF) outperforms GRU-NF in generating diverse outputs without sacrificing accuracy, even under longer prediction horizons. By injecting stochasticity during inference, our approach captures multimodal behavior more effectively. These results highlight the potential of integrating stochastic dynamics with flow-based sequence models for generative time series forecasting.

title: ANN Based Temperature Measurement for the Analysis of PMSM Breakdown
authors: Devasenathipathi K P, Boni Satya Varun Sai, Prithiviraj Sundarrajan
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Emerging Trends in Computing and Communication (ETCOM)
abstract: In this paper, temperature based Permanent Magnet Synchronous Motor (PMSM) breakdown is addressed using Artificial Neural Network (ANN). In few existing works, breakdown analysis of PMSM is done using temperature. Moreover, the temperature measurement is done using the conventional methods and optimization schemes. These methods have the disadvantages with respect to the precision and adaptability in measuring temperature. To overcome this, ANN based temperature measurement is implemented in this proposal. Further, the measured temperature is used for analyzing the break down situation of the PMSM. The proposed scheme has been tested in the MATLAB and Simulink atmosphere shown the satisfactory results.

title: Energy Harvesting in Diverse Precision Agricultural Environments
authors: Kunal Goel, Amit Bindal, Upendra Aswal, Anju Rani, Pramod Kumar Sharma, Vijay Singh Patel
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Smart Innovations in Systems, Infrastructure, Mechanical, Power, AI and Computing Technologies (SISIMPACT)
abstract: In the event of precision farming, mote devices are used to study the universal environmental characteristics; such as humidity/moisture level/ temperature etc. These factors cannot be rationalised over the hiatus motes continue to persist and transmit such information and so result in redundancy at long length. Nor does it use the network resource unnecessarily. In this paper a SOEEA (self-organized energy efficient data aggregation) framework is proposed, which is based on the LEACH routing protocol, to address the above problem and its performance is compared with that of an existing DA scheme.

title: Lightweighted Video Summarization for Video-Related Information
authors: Narra Snehith, Errolla Pragnya, Shubham Sharma, Er. Aaskaran Bishnoi
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Emerging Trends in Computing and Communication (ETCOM)
abstract: Here, we present a light-weighted video summarization framework that can automatically generate highlight reels and text summaries of lengthy videos. Due to the onslaught of video content on the Internet via websites like YouTube, individuals don't have a simple way of quickly getting information they are looking for. To solve this problem, our framework uses visual, audio and textual modalities for effective summarization. The motion features are computed using OpenCV to detect scene changes and audio energy peaks are monitored using Librosa to highlight interesting regions. Whisper is used for speech-totext transcriptions too, and a Transformer-based summarizer generates brief text summaries. The hybrid approach ensures that both visual attention and audio intensity are accounted for and the highlights become more informative. Experimental findings indicate that the framework produces quality summaries, reduces video length significantly and preserves most informative content. The work can be utilized in education, media and entertainment, accelerating consumption of information and making information more accessible.

title: A Q-Learning-Based Adaptive Ant Colony Algorithm for Electric Vehicle Routing Planning
authors: Yaxuan Zhang, Ye Tian, Ziting Zhang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Big Data and Data Mining (BDDM)
abstract: In response to global energy transition and carbon neutrality goals, the electric vehicle routing problem has become a core challenge in green logistics research. Its complex charging constraints and energy consumption characteristics impose higher demands on traditional optimization algorithms. This paper proposes an improved adaptive ant colony algorithm integrated with Q-learning (Q-AACO), which enhances search efficiency through elite strategies and dynamic pheromone update mechanisms, maintains population diversity with maximum-minimum pheromone limits, and introduces a Q-learning framework to intelligently select local search operators, guiding the search direction with a multi-perspective reward function. Experiments show that the algorithm achieves or surpasses the known optimal solution in 91.67% of instances and outperforms comparison algorithms in 79.17% of instances, demonstrating excellent convergence performance and stability, providing an effective solution for electric vehicle routing under complex constraints.

title: Adversarially Robust Intrusion Detection Using Graph Neural Networks
authors: Abdul Hadi, Mayank Atreya, Srikanth Mandru, Avinash Reddy Pothu, Himani Singhai, Sathishkumar Chintala
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Emerging Trends in Computing and Communication (ETCOM)
abstract: The intense development of cyber threats has revealed the weaknesses of traditional intrusion detection systems (IDS) that fail to keep up with adversarial attacks that aim at utilizing the vulnerabilities of machine learning models. In an effort to overcome this problem, we suggest an Adversarial Robust Intrusion Detection framework based on Graph Neural Networks (GNNs). In contrast to traditional IDS, which view network traffic as discrete data points, our framework represents network communication as a graph with complex dependencies among entities, e.g. between hosts, connections and protocols. We present adversarial training methods that are robust to evasion and poisoning attacks, which provide robustness in non-beneficial settings. This architecture combines node- and edge-level feature extraction, relational learning with graph convolution, and anomaly-sensitive classifiers, which are type optimized using adversarial perturbations. The outcome of our experiments on standard intrusion datasets indicates that our model has better detection accuracy and resilience in relation to the latest deep learning baselines, even in a severe adversarial environment. The paper also indicates the possibilities of jointly using GNNs with adversarial defences to develop future-generation intrusion detection systems, which are both adaptive and resilient to current cyber defence infrastructures.

title: FPGAs with FABulous – Framework and Chips
authors: Dirk Koch, Myrtle Shah, King Lok Chung, Jonas Kuenstler, Marcel Jung, Jakob Ternes, Asma Mohsin, Gennadiy Knis
publish: 2025 35th International Conference on Field-Programmable Logic and Applications (FPL)
abstract: FABulous is an easy usable, yet powerful and complete eFPGA (embedded FPGA) Framework covering all aspects of an eFPGA ecosystem. FABulous eFPGAs had shown good area density in both standard-cell and custom cell flows and the framework allows various customizations, including the integration of custom primitives, I/O cells, or complex blocks like CPUs cores or ADCs. So far, more than 10 chips containing FABulous eFPGAs had been designed in technology nodes ranging from 28-180 nm by seven different universities. This demo shows how embedded FPGAs (eFPGAs) can be 1) specified, 2) verified and tested, 3) integrated into an ASIC, and 4) programmed in Verilog or VHDL using the all open FABulous eFPGA framework. Moreover, the demo will showcase two boards featuring FABulous FPGAs, including the first FABulous open-everything FPGA.

title: Semi-Gridless Channel Estimation in XL-MIMO: Near-field vs. Far-Field
authors: Toan-Van Nguyen, Van-Chung Luu, Duy H. N. Nguyen
publish: 2025 59th Asilomar Conference on Signals, Systems, and Computers
abstract: In extremely large-scale multiple-input multiple-output (XL-MIMO) systems, conventional far-field channel estimation methods experience significant performance degradation due to the near-field spherical-wavefront effects. To address this issue, we develop a novel semi-gridless channel estimation approach based on a variational Bayesian (VB) inference framework. By reformulating the near-field channel model to incorporate direction-of-arrival (DoA) and distance components, we employ a gridless approach for DoA estimation using a von Mises distribution and a coarse-to-fine grid search for distance estimation. We then develop a semi-gridless VB (SG-VB) algorithm with efficient updates that enables accurate channel reconstruction. Simulation results show that the proposed SGVB algorithm outperforms MUSIC, polar-domain simultaneous orthogonal matching pursuit, and polar-domain simultaneous iterative gridless weighted in channel and DoA estimation at high SNRs, and in distance estimation accuracy at low-to-medium SNRs.

title: IoT - Enabled Face Recognition Attendance System Using Raspberry Pi and OpenCV
authors: Prischa Bajeli, Vitthal S. Gutte
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Emerging Trends in Computing and Communication (ETCOM)
abstract: Automation of student attendance systems in smart classrooms is an imperative need for any modern educational institution. This paper proposes an IoT-enabled Face Recognition-Based Smart Attendance System developed with Python, OpenCV, and Raspberry Pi hardware. The proposed system automatically captures real-time images of students, recognizes them with a previously trained LBPH model, and saves their attendance directly into an SQL database. This data can later be exported through an Excel pipeline for color-coded reporting. Facial features of each student are captured during the enrollment phase, stored locally, and matched using a live camera feed. Verification is performed once every 20 minutes to check that the student remains present during the lecture; it is designed to automatically terminate after one hour. With an 8 MP camera and the use of the Raspberry Pi microcontroller, the system can work as an independent edge device. Output data is arranged in color-coded Excel reports, through which the teachers can identify present and absent students for each session. Experimental results confirm that with a high confidence threshold of 80%, face detection and recognition are reliable, and hence accurate attendance logging has been achieved under multiple illumination conditions. The system design offers a cost-effective, agile, and privacy-aware approach in attendance tracking; thus, it is well-suited for academic and business applications.

title: Influence of Exit Geometry and Boundary Conditions on the Thermal Efficiency of a Two-Step Methane–Air Micro-Combustor
authors: Ujwal Kant, Priyavrat Kumar, Vikas Gupta
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Smart Innovations in Systems, Infrastructure, Mechanical, Power, AI and Computing Technologies (SISIMPACT)
abstract: A computational study is carried out on a two-step micro-combustor based on two-step global reaction model for the combustion of methane-air mixture. A typical two-step combustor comprises of backward facing step at inlet followed by a forward facing step at the exit. In the present investigation, the step angle at the inlet has been taken as 45° while at the exit it has been varied from 10° to 30° at 10° interval to analyse the influence of this variation over a range of inlet velocity profileson the performance of the combustor. Numerical computations are performed using ANSYS Fluent and results such as centreline adiabatic temperature, exit temperature and species utilization are presented and analysed. Further, computations are also carried out for a bell mouthed two-step micro combustor (both at inlet and exit) to enhance the performance.

title: EARLYSURE – AI Powered Breast Screening Device at house for Early detection of Breast cancer
authors: S. Vasavi, B. Bhavya, B. Navya Sree, G. Sajana, M. Likitha, V. Bala Murali Krishna
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Recent Trends in Electrical, Electronics and Computing Technologies (ICRTEECT)
abstract: Breast cancer remains the leading cancer diagnosis among women globally, contributing significantly to mortality rates. According to the World Health Organization, approximately 2.3 million cases and 685,000 deaths were recorded in 2020. Early detection plays a critical role in improving survival outcomes; however, access to reliable screening methods is often limited, particularly in resource-constrained regions. To address this challenge, we introduce EARLYSURE, an affordable, portable, and radiation-free breast screening solution designed for home use. The system employs red LED-based imaging integrated with a deep learning model built on the DenseNet121 architecture, trained on extensive ultrasound and mammogram datasets. EARLYSURE generates an intuitive numeric score ranging from 1 to 10, indicating malignancy risk. Experimental evaluation demonstrates a classification accuracy of 93.04%, with F1-scores of 0.94 for benign, 0.91 for malignant, and 0.92 for normal cases. Notably, the false positive rate for malignant predictions was 0%, highlighting the model’s reliability in critical scenarios. These findings confirm the potential of EARLYSURE as a cost-effective and user-friendly screening tool for improving breast cancer detection in underserved communities. While DenseNet121 and the datasets used are well-established, the novelty of this work lies in integrating LED-based, radiation-free imaging with AI into a portable at-home screening device. The system bridges a critical healthcare gap by offering an accessible, non-invasive, and low-cost solution for breast cancer risk assessment, particularly in underserved communities.

title: Damage Detection in Handwritten Documents Using Intensity Histogram Clustering
authors: B.L.P. Sankalpana, Lochandaka Ranathunga
publish: 2025 IEEE 19th International Conference on Industrial and Information Systems (ICIIS)
abstract: In Sri Lanka, Sinhala has recognized as the most ancient and widely used language. This research aims to identify damaged areas in degraded Sinhala handwritten documents. Several studies have been conducted in this field, with most focusing on detecting character damage using the gradient values of image pixels. Clustering based on gradients is effective for identifying edges and stroke consistency. However, damages such as tears, ink fading, stains, or smudges are often overlooked by this method. In this study, pixel intensity values are considered as the primary approach for damage detection. Additional techniques, including NLM filtering, histogram clustering, morphological processing, and stroke width estimation, are also discussed. The proposed method was evaluated on seventy-five aged document images that exhibited multiple types of damages within a single image, achieving an accuracy of 70.57 %, a precision of 74.19 %, a recall of 93.43 %, and an F1-score of 82.90 %.

title: AI Based Cyber security Solutions for Detecting and Preventing Threats in IoT Systems
authors: Bagam Laxmaiah, V Ramesh, Punyaban Patel, M Prashanthi, J.Deepika, R.BagavathiLakshmi
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Recent Trends in Electrical, Electronics and Computing Technologies (ICRTEECT)
abstract: The exponential growth of Internet of Things (IoT) devices revolutionized different modern industries yet created major cybersecurity risk points. Standard security approaches prove inadequate in managing the constantly changing IoT environments. The combination of advanced analytics with machine learning and real-time monitoring in Artificial Intelligence delivers an effective answer to detect and prevent threats. The paper examines how AI-based cyber security approaches tackle the exclusive security challenges of IoT networks.Artificial Intelligence-based solutions use anomaly detection and behavioral analysis methods and predictive modeling techniques to identify suspicious activities together with unauthorized access and malicious traffic patterns. Continuous analysis of large datasets by machine learning algorithms enables detection of emerging threats alongside deep learning models that enhance the specific identification of complex attack methods. Tai assists in fully automated threat response capabilities which respond immediately to cyber threats without requiring human handlers. The research study revolves around examining intrusion detection systems (IDS) as well as AI-powered firewalls and network traffic analysis among the central components of IoT cyber security strategies. These security systems automatically discover established threats and track down new security risks through their adaptive learning abilities. The promising characteristics of IoT cyber security face additional research needs because of data privacy issues along with requirements for model explain ability and IoT resource limitations.

title: Region-of-Interest Sparse Reconstruction for Lensless Coded-Aperture Optical Imaging
authors: Di An, Dylan Poppert, Jiayue Li, Johnny Pham, Brian Sun, Mark Foster, Trac D. Tran
publish: 2025 59th Asilomar Conference on Signals, Systems, and Computers
abstract: Lensless imaging systems rely on computational reconstruction to transform raw optical measurements into interpretable images. Traditional approaches require complete knowledge of the sensing matrix A to recover high-resolution images of interest—often an impractical task due to memory inefficiency, high computational complexity, and costly calibration processes. In this work, we address this limitation by focusing on localized region-of-interest reconstruction. We propose a method to approximate a region-specific sensing matrix that leverages ElasticNet regularization, enabling efficient and robust reconstruction with any ℓ<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">0</inf>- or ℓ<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">1</inf>-based sparse-recovery technique. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed method reliably reconstructs local image regions, while the estimated local matrix retains the overall structural properties of the original sensing matrix. This strategy offers a scalable solution that opens up real-time lensless imaging applications, especially in scenarios where only partial reconstruction is needed, such as targeted biomedical imaging or scene monitoring. By shifting from global to region-specific modeling, our approach enhances both the efficiency and adaptability of computational imaging systems.

title: Supplementing Patient Encounter Training for Preservice Nurses: Towards an AI×VR Approach
authors: Yupei Duan, Xinhao Xu, Hao He, Yuanyuan Gu, Shangman Li, Jhon Bueno-Vesga
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and eXtended and Virtual Reality (AIxVR)
abstract: We built an immersive virtual reality environment where preservice nurses practice with generative artificial intelligence-driven virtual standardized patients (AI-VSPs). We ran mixed-methods study (experimental <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$n=23$</tex>; control <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$n=34$</tex>). Usability score was <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$81.6 / 100$</tex>, indicating excellent usability. A single session increased self-efficacy from 3.74 to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$4.11(t(22)=3.82, p<0.001$</tex>). In the standardized-patient (SP) encounter performance assessment, scores for AI-VSP users did not differ from controls <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(t(55)=-0.134, p=0.894)$</tex>. AI-VSP encounter performance did not relate to engagement, presence, or immersion, although these experience measures were strongly intercorrelated. Interviews highlighted helps and hindrances. Overall, AI-VSPs are a usable, confidence-building supplement to SP encounter preparation. We also outline practical design steps: cap reply length, enable reliable barge-in, improve speech quality and latency, and keep scaffolding minimal.

title: Multimodal Methods for Improving Natural Language Processing in Low-Resource Languages:Survey
authors: Ranganath Kanakam, M.Venunath, Mamidala Sruthi, Vedika Jorika
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Recent Trends in Electrical, Electronics and Computing Technologies (ICRTEECT)
abstract: Low Resource Languages are the languages that few people speak and the low-density languages in the world. Usually, more people speak languages such as English, Chinese, Hindi, French, Spanish, etc. Researchers are more comfortable with the above languages. because these languages have annotated data or dataset available online for applying technologies like Voice assistance, Text summarization, Google translation, Auto correction, etc. Data availability provides good training and testing of the model. Most of the NLP applications are developed with high-resource languages only. Despite the advancement of the digital era, few languages have low density. One of the challenges of implementing NLP applications for low-resource languages are not having data, even if parsing tools are not available. This paper addressed a survey on improving NLP in low-resource languages, applications, and challenges.

title: A Museum Artifact Preview System With Mixed Reality and Generative AI
authors: Chia-Yu Wang, Shih-Wei Sun
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and eXtended and Virtual Reality (AIxVR)
abstract: This paper presents a prototype of a mixed reality (MR) museum artifact preview system, enabling visitors to view virtual artifacts on real pedestals before entering the exhibit. The system uses MR devices to display 3D artifact previews with guide avatars and AI-generated introductory text, enhancing pre-visit engagement.

title: Towards Calibration Robust: 6DoF Multi-Horizon Head Motion Prediction for EdgeXR
authors: Ziyu Zhong, Björn Landfeldt, Günter Alce, Héctor Caltenco
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and eXtended and Virtual Reality (AIxVR)
abstract: Accurately predicting head motion is essential for reducing motion-to-photon (MTP) latency in XR systems. However, standard evaluation practices often conflate true prediction error with residual calibration artifacts-such as global rotation bias, lever-arm offsets, or sub-frame time skews-even after alignment. These small but realistic miscalibrations can dominate reported errors and obscure the actual performance of learningbased predictors, creating a gap in fair and robust benchmarking. This paper introduces a calibration-robust learning and evaluation pipeline for XR head-motion prediction that explicitly addresses these residuals. By rethinking both the training targets and evaluation protocol, our approach disentangles model accuracy from calibration artifacts, enabling more meaningful comparisons across devices and algorithms. Experiments on the public ADVIO dataset and our HMD-MoCap sessions show that body-frame targets consistently reduce calibration sensitivity for data-driven prediction models-yielding lower calibration sensitivity slopes (CSS) and higher robust area under the curve (AUC)-while maintaining short-horizon rotational accuracy compared to conventional world-frame approaches. Our findings establish a protocol for robust, reproducible benchmarking in XR pose prediction and demonstrate the feasibility of ground-truth-level head-motion prediction for EdgeXR.

title: Conformalizing ensemble Gaussian processes with robustness to model mis-specification
authors: Jinwen Xu, Qin Lu, Georgios B. Giannakis
publish: 2025 59th Asilomar Conference on Signals, Systems, and Computers
abstract: Uncertainty quantification (UQ) is of paramount importance for safety-critical applications in dynamic, data-streaming environments. The Gaussian process (GP), as a classical Bayesian framework for UQ, provides a principled approach to modeling complex functions through kernel definitions. However, standard GP-based approaches are limited not only by the prohibitive cubic computational complexity, but also by strict modeling assumptions, thus often yielding poor coverage, especially when the data distribution shifts on the fly. To effect scalability, we devise a computationally efficient parametric GP model by leveraging the Random Feature (RF)-based kernel approximation, which is amenable to constant-time recursive Bayesian updates. To further allow for adaptivity against non-stationarity, an ensemble (E) of RF-based GPs with diverse spectral properties is leveraged, with per-model weights dynamically adapted to data arriving incrementally. To ensure valid coverage with robustness to model mis-specification, we integrate the EGP predictor with the Online Conformal Prediction (OCP) framework, which post-processes the prediction sets using adaptive thresholds based on the negative predictive log-likelihood. Experimental results on real-world financial datasets demonstrate that the proposed EGP-OCP method yields superior coverage stability and efficient prediction sets over existing baselines by adaptively fusing multiple kernels and adjusting the key threshold parameters in CP.

title: Kernel K-Means Clustering via Alternating Minimization Algorithm
authors: Chao Zhang, Yao Chen, Yingying Cheng, Zhongyang Zhou, Feiyu Chen
publish: 2025 International Conference on Big Data and Data Mining (BDDM)
abstract: Kernel K-means algorithm (KKM) has demonstrated promising performance in various clustering tasks. However, it always suffers from high computational complexity and intensive time cost. In this paper, we propose a novel indicator matrix based clustering model, called penalized kernel K-means (PKKM). In order to solve the proposed non-convex PKKM model, we split the original matrix variable into two variables, which satisfy orthogonality and non-negativity respectively, then we design a simple but efficient numerical algorithm. It consists of two iterative steps: projection onto orthognal subspace and non-negative subspace. Extensive experiments on 45 benchmark datasets demonstrate that PKKM achieves the highest accuracy on 33 datasets. Furthermore, on complex image clustering tasks, PKKM outperforms state-of-the-art methods with an accuracy gain of up to 18.05.

title: 6G Connectivity in LEO and VLEO: Building an Integrated Network for Air-Space-Maritime Operations
authors: Nesrine Benchoubane, Gunes Karabulut Kurt
publish: 2025 59th Asilomar Conference on Signals, Systems, and Computers
abstract: Booming global demand for telecommunication services for 6G and beyond has reignited interest in the very low Earth orbit (VLEO) regime and its integration with the low Earth orbit (LEO) regime. This paper provides a realistic assessment of the deployment of large-scale LEO and VLEO networks, taking into account simulation and experimental data to (1) quantify and correlate altitude with constellation design, (2) report service usage metrics in terms of frequency and duration, and (3) evaluate attenuations on the communication link, taking mobility of users into account. This allows us to establish practical design guidelines for LEO and VLEO deployments.

title: RE-DACT: Differential Privacy and Redaction Framework for Sensitive Data in Diverse Formats
authors: Shilpa Choudhary, C.K.Sreya, U.Shivani, Jayanth, Sandeep Kumar, Dipesh
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Recent Trends in Electrical, Electronics and Computing Technologies (ICRTEECT)
abstract: Redacting sensitive data is vital for protecting privacy, particularly as organizations grow more dependent on sharing, analyzing, and storing information. Neglecting proper redaction can cause major legal, moral, and security issues. In this paper we present RE-DACT, a privacy-preserving framework for redacting and anonymizing sensitive data in both structured (e.g. csv files) and unstructured (e.g. text documents) formats. REDACT combines cutting-edge ML and NLP methodology for achieving data privacy while preserving data utility. For the structured data, we use the PATE-GAN model to generate synthetic datasets which preserve the statistical properties of the original data and while guaranteeing differential privacy. For raw text, it leverages a spaCy-based NER pipeline to extract sensitive entities such as names, dates, organizations and contact details and redact them via configurable masking policies. The framework also preserves the original document layout and formatting to ensure the readability and context, supports fast batch processing for large scale data and could be deployed both in cloud and on-premises. Extensive evaluation results show that RE-DACT achieves highly competitive performance for automatically redacting sensitive information while maintaining data utility and compliance with strict privacy requirements (e.g., GDPR, HIPAA, and CCPA). Our results shows that RE-DACT is a scalable, secure, resilient, and regulatory-compliant answer to contemporary data privacy and overlay matching, and stands as a promising solution for entities having to reconcile data utility with privacy compliance.

title: Direct Preference Optimization of Qwen 2.5 for Complex Reasoning and Debugging
authors: Venkata Sai Chaitanya Varma Sirivuri, Gowtham Venkata Sai Ram Maddala, Harsha Vardhan Reddy Vummadi
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Recent Trends in Electrical, Electronics and Computing Technologies (ICRTEECT)
abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) have transformed software development through automation and programming assistance, yet they face significant challenges in handling complex programming tasks. This research presents a novel approach to enhancing LLM capabilities through Direct Preference Optimization (DPO), specifically focusing on improving complex reasoning and debugging in Python. We implemented DPO fine-tuning on the Qwen 2.5 3B model using curated datasets from different DPO datasets. Our methodology involved careful hyperparameter tuning, particularly of the temperature parameter β, and optimization of the training process through smaller batch sizes with increased training steps. The results demonstrated significant improvements, with the Qwen 2.5 3B model showing a 35.56% increase in performance, increasing from 45% to 61% in programming and debugging tasks. Furthermore, our analysis reveals that this preference optimization transfers to agentic workflows, significantly reducing hallucination rates in tool-use scenarios and improving multi-turn robustness. This work establishes a new benchmark for AI-assisted software development, particularly in precision-driven fields that require complex computational problem-solving. Our findings advance the understanding of preference-based optimization in language models and provide insight into the relationship between model architecture, size, and fine-tuning effectiveness.

title: "SAWAN" A Sinhala Sign Language Generation System Using HamNoSys and SiGML
authors: Sanuri Himalka, Udaya Wijenayake, Ravinath Wanniarachchi, Maleesha Hirushan, Pasindu Ranasinghe
publish: 2025 IEEE 19th International Conference on Industrial and Information Systems (ICIIS)
abstract: This study addresses the communication gap between the deaf and hearing communities through the development of a cross-platform software solution. It introduces a Sinhala Sign Language (SSL) generator supported by auxiliary tools to reduce communication barriers. The proposed system, SAWAN – Deaf Supportive Solutions, integrates editable datasets, an interactive learning platform, a static sign translator, and HamNoSys-based tools. HamNoSys, introduced to Sri Lanka for the first time through this research, provides a standardized framework for gesture representation, facilitating dataset modification and future research. The system also includes an SSL dataset built using HamNoSys and SiGML. Experimental evaluation with SSL experts yielded an average translation accuracy of 80.67%, defined through a rubric assessing correctness, fluency, and comprehensibility. Although based on a limited proof-of-concept dataset, the results demonstrate the feasibility of HamNoSys-based SSL generation and form a foundation for future improvements.

title: Sense of Blindness: A Fully Non-Visual VR Experience to Foster Empathy Toward Urban Blindness
authors: Lorenzo Appice, Pietro De Giglio, Alessandro Pagano, Veronica Rossano, Francesca Pia Travisani
publish: 2025 International Conference on Innovation and Intelligence for Informatics, Computing, and Technologies (3ICT)
abstract: In recent years, Virtual Reality (VR) has emerged as a powerful medium for simulating complex human experiences and promoting empathy through immersive learning. This paper presents Sense of Blindness, a VR-based serious game designed to raise awareness about the everyday challenges faced by blind individuals in urban environments. Unlike traditional empathy-oriented experiences, our system deliberately removes all visual input during gameplay, relying exclusively on spatial audio and haptic feedback to simulate non-visual navigation. The user interacts with the environment through a virtual white cane, receiving directional and obstacle-related cues via audio and controller vibrations.
