The game unfolds within a realistic urban setting inspired by an Italian pedestrian street and includes a structured sequence of obstacles, crosswalks, and auditory landmarks. The design prioritizes accessibility and user experience, providing visual and auditory menu systems and a progressive audio-based tutorial. A preliminary evaluation involving 38 sighted participants was conducted using Gameful Experience Gamification (Gamex). The results indicate a significant increase in user-reported empathy, awareness of visual impairment, and engagement. Participants particularly appreciated the immersive quality of spatial audio, which was rated as the most impactful sensory component. The study contributes to the field of Human-Computer Interaction (HCI) by exploring the design space of fully non-visual VR experiences and by demonstrating the potential of sensory-only interaction models to foster inclusion and reflection. Future developments include expanding the scenario catalog, longitudinal impact analysis, and application in educational and public contexts.

title: Temporal Analysis of Antenna Radiation Pattern Stability Under Variable Throughput Using Hidden Markov Models in GPON ONT
authors: Kiswanto Ihsan, Eliandri Wulandari, Gunadi Dwi Hantoro, Iyan Hardiansyah, Agus Ramelan, Azka Fasya
publish: 2025 IEEE Asia-Pacific Conference on Geoscience, Electronics and Remote Sensing Technology (AGERS)
abstract: This study investigates the temporal stability of the antenna radiation pattern in a GPON ONT dual-band device (2.4 GHz and 5.8 GHz) under varying throughput conditions. The evaluation is conducted in a controlled anechoic chamber using a horn antenna and a spectrum analyzer, with traffic injection performed via IxChariot at throughput levels of 1 0 ~ M b p s, 100 Mbps, and 1000 Mbps. Radiation pattern measurements are taken at a 1-meter distance from the GPON ONT, and the resulting data is analyzed to assess pattern stability over time. A Hidden Markov Model (HMM) is employed to model the temporal dynamics and identify state transitions in the radiation pattern under different network loads. The results reveal a correlation between increased throughput and fluctuations in radiation pattern stability, indicating that higher data traffic can influence the electromagnetic behavior of the ONT. These findings provide valuable insights for the design and quality assurance of GPON ONTs in high-throughput deployment scenarios, such as those encountered by Telkom Indonesia.

title: A Constrained RL Approach for Cost-Efficient Delivery of Latency-Sensitive Applications
authors: Ozan Aygün, Vincenzo Norman Vitale, Antonia M. Tulino, Hao Feng, Elza Erkip, Jaime Llorca
publish: 2025 59th Asilomar Conference on Signals, Systems, and Computers
abstract: Next-generation networks aim to provide performance guarantees to real-time interactive services that require timely and cost-efficient packet delivery. In this context, the goal is to reliably deliver packets with strict deadlines imposed by the application while minimizing overall resource allocation cost. A large body of work has leveraged stochastic optimization techniques to design efficient dynamic routing and scheduling solutions under average delay constraints; however, these methods fall short when faced with strict per-packet delay requirements. We formulate the minimum-cost delay-constrained network control problem as a constrained Markov decision process and utilize constrained deep reinforcement learning (CDRL) techniques to effectively minimize total resource allocation cost while maintaining timely throughput above a target reliability level. Results indicate that the proposed CDRL-based solution can ensure timely packet delivery even when existing baselines fall short, and it achieves lower cost compared to other throughput-maximizing methods.

title: Energy-Efficient Edge-AI VLSI Architecture for Quantized DNN-Based Real-Time Atrial Fibrillation Detection in Wearables
authors: A. Vidhya, J S Leena Jasmine
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Emerging Trends in Computing and Communication (ETCOM)
abstract: Accurate and energy-efficient detection of atrial fibrillation (AF) is essential for continuous cardiac monitoring in wearable ECG devices. This paper proposes an Edge-AI accelerated quantized deep neural network (Q-DNN) VLSI architecture optimized for ultra-low-power and real-time AF detection. The architecture integrates an integer-only computation flow, a custom systolic-style PE array, banked on-chip SRAM, and a hardware-aware quantization strategy to minimize resource usage and memory bandwidth. A unified co-design approach is adopted, spanning model quantization, operator mapping, timing-driven hardware scheduling, and pipelined dataflow optimization. Experimental evaluation using the MIT-BIH AFIB database shows that the proposed Q-DNN achieves <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{9 8. 4 \%}$</tex> accuracy, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1. 2 ~ m W}$</tex> power consumption, and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1. 8}$</tex> ms latency, outperforming baseline CNN, LSTM, MobileNetV2, and classical ML models. FPGA/ASIC synthesis and Model Sim simulation validate the architecture's efficiency, robustness, and suitability for always-on wearable health monitoring. The proposed VLSI design provides a scalable foundation for next-generation low-power edge-AI ECG analytics.

title: AI Agents in Mental Health Treatment: A Systematic Review
authors: Juswaldi Nur, Kimberly Tan, Richard Wiputra
publish: 2025 International Conference on Innovation and Intelligence for Informatics, Computing, and Technologies (3ICT)
abstract: The rapid integration of Agent artificial intelligence (AI) tools, such as chatbots, large language models (LLMs), and conversational agents, has demonstrated significant potential in facilitating mental health care, especially for depression and anxiety. This systematic review adheres to the PRISMA guidelines and synthesizes 21 studies published between 2020 and 2024, evaluating AI agents in terms of therapeutic efficacy, user engagement, and ethical considerations. Evidence indicates that AI-based therapies markedly improved outcomes: symptom reduction ranged from 19 % to 42 %, retention rates were, on average, 28 % higher, and therapy completion increased by 18 % compared to traditional self-help methods. Personalization and adaptive feedback were crucial to these enhancements, increasing user compliance and satisfaction. Nonetheless, significant shortcomings remain concerning long-term efficacy, data privacy, algorithmic bias, and regulatory supervision. This study highlights the transformative potential and current limitations of AI agents in mental health, stressing the necessity for comprehensive clinical trials and strong ethical frameworks to guarantee the safe and equitable deployment of these technologies.

title: Cloud Contamination Removal from Hα Full Solar Image Based on Improved Recurrent Generative Adversarial Network
authors: Kp Mo
publish: 2025 International Conference on Big Data and Data Mining (BDDM)
abstract: Hα full-plane images are core data carriers for studying solar structure, activity patterns, and Sun-Earth relationships. However, their observation quality is often affected by cloud cover, leading to blurred image details, missing information, and interference with accurate identification of solar activity. To address the limitations of traditional cloud contamination removal methods that rely on physical model assumptions, complex processing procedures, and limited effectiveness in heavily contaminated scenarios, this paper proposes an end-to-end cloud contamination removal method based on an improved Cycle-GAN. This method designs a generator structure that concatenates a UNet with pyramidal convolutions to capture multi-scale features from local texture to global structure. It optimizes the loss function, integrating cycle consistency loss, perceptual consistency loss, and structural similarity loss to ensure that the generated images conform to the physical laws of solar radiation. Experiments are conducted using Hα full-plane images from the GONG observation network as a data foundation, validating the method under light, medium, and heavy cloud contamination scenarios, and comparing it with the Pix2Pix network, Dark Channel Prior (DCP) algorithm, and combined filtering methods. The results show that the proposed method does not require paired training data and atmospheric parameter estimation. It can still effectively recover the solar disk morphology and key physical features even in heavily polluted scenarios. The peak signal-to-noise ratio (PSNR) of the processed image reaches 28.54, the structural similarity (SSIM0) reaches 0.7719, and the dynamic time warping (DTW) value is as low as 19.53. The overall performance is better than the comparison methods, providing a reliable data preprocessing scheme for solar physics research and space weather monitoring.

title: IoT-Based Remote Data Logging System for Photovoltaic Research with Thinger.io
authors: Vira Dwi Falinda, Armin Sofijan, Daeny Septi Yansuri, Wirawan Adipradana, Aulia Nadyasari, Harlin Septia Saputri, Kimena Rizki Andini
publish: 2025 IEEE Asia-Pacific Conference on Geoscience, Electronics and Remote Sensing Technology (AGERS)
abstract: The performance of photovoltaic (PV) systems is highly dependent on fluctuating environmental conditions. Yet, traditional monitoring methods often rely on manual or localized data logging, which fails to provide real-time, remote, and consistent data. While the Internet of Things (IoT) offers a viable solution, a practical justification for selecting specific platforms is often overlooked in existing literature. This research addresses these issues by designing, implementing, and validating an IoT-based data acquisition system for a $\mathbf{1 0 0 ~ W p}$ polycrystalline PV module using the Thinger.io platform. The system integrates an Arduino Nano for local data logging to an SD card and a NodeMCU ESP8266 for transmitting sensor data (irradiance, temperature, current, and voltage) to the Thinger.io cloud for remote monitoring and analysis. The results demonstrate a high degree of reliability, with data from Thinger.io showing a strong correlation with measurements from the conventional data logger, with deviations within a 1 $2 \%$ tolerance range. System testing over a full day (08:00-17:00) showed the solar panel achieved its peak performance around midday, with a maximum solar irradiance of $934.43 \mathrm{~W} / \mathrm{m}^{2}$ (at 12:00) producing 56.04 W, and a peak conversion efficiency of $19.0 \%$ (at 12:30). In conclusion, the developed Thinger.io-based system proves to be a reliable and effective solution for overcoming the limitations of manual measurements, providing a practical tool for real-time performance evaluation. This system is highly applicable for continuous monitoring in smallscale solar power installations and educational laboratories, contributing to the optimization and broader adoption of PV technology.

title: YOLO-Powered UAV Imaging for Precise Detection of Fungal Infections in Palm Trees
authors: Mawadda Alwardi, Omer Eldirdiry, Alhasan Alshekaili, Hamza Alismaili, Alwaleed Aldahmani
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Unmanned Vehicle Systems-Oman (UVS)
abstract: Early detection of fungal diseases in palm trees is crucial to prevent crop losses. This paper presents an innovative AI-based solution for addressing fungal infections, combining UAV technology with computer vision. Using over 600 images for annotating fungal subjects captured from multiple angles, the YOLO-Medium model was trained to identify infected areas with high accuracy and a confidence level of 91%, even under challenging lighting conditions. The system enables farmers to use UAV technologies to easily, quickly, and precisely monitor plantations and tree issues, providing an effective tool for crop protection and improved farm management. In addition, the integration of UAVs makes this approach highly effective for periodic inspection of palm trees, supporting long-term health monitoring and enabling timely treatment, while also offering a time-efficient solution for large-scale plantation management.

title: Design and Image Encryption Implementation of High Performance and Resource-Efficient AES-128 Encryption Architecture
authors: Deepika Ben Thakor, Jeetendra Singh, Balwinder Raj
publish: 2025 IEEE 19th International Conference on Industrial and Information Systems (ICIIS)
abstract: This paper presents an optimized hardware implementation of the Advanced Encryption Standard (AES-128) encryption algorithm, specifically designed to minimize resource consumption while achieving high throughput. Key optimizations include a fully parallel SubBytes operation utilizing 16 simultaneous S-boxes, runtime key derivation, and a loop-iterated architecture for round execution. This approach enhances processing speed while maintaining hardware efficiency. Functional correctness was rigorously verified using the NIST FIPS-197 standard test vectors, confirming reliable encryption and decryption for all specified cases. The implemented AES architecture achieves a throughput of 4.92 Gbps at 384.6 MHz on a Xilinx Artix-7 FPGA, encrypting 128-bit blocks in 11 clock cycles. Post-synthesis results demonstrate exceptional resource efficiency: utilization rates were 2.14% for Slice LUTs, 1.05% for registers, and 13.7% for BRAM. I/O utilization reached 96.25%, underscoring the design’s compact footprint. To demonstrate practical application, the design was adapted for image processing, successfully encrypting and decrypting a 128×128 pixel grayscale image. The encrypted output exhibited characteristic random noise, while the decrypted image perfectly reconstructed the original. These findings validate the design’s suitability for deploying AES-128 on FPGAs in secure, real-time embedded systems, IoT devices, and defense communication networks.

title: A Field Calibration Approach for Triaxial MEMS Gyroscopes Based on Gravity and Rotation Consistency
authors: Yaqi Li, Li Wang, Zhitao Wang, Xiangqing Li, Liangyu Xie, Gongchang Yu, Bin Shi, Steven Weidong Su
publish: IEEE Transactions on Instrumentation and Measurement
abstract: Conventional triaxial MEMS gyroscope calibration relies on strict mounting constraints, specialized apparatus, and elaborate laboratory procedures that hinder field deployment; this work presents a field-deployable method enabling efficient and accurate in-field calibration. The core strategy is to utilize the criterion that the dot product of the measured gravity and the rotation speed in a fixed frame remains constant. To eliminate the impact of external acceleration, the calibration process involves separate procedures for measuring local gravity and rotation speed. Moreover, unlike existing approaches for auto calibration of triaxial sensors that often result in nonlinear optimization problems, the proposed method simplifies the estimation of the gyroscope scale factor by employing a linear least squares algorithm. Extensive numerical simulations have been conducted to analyze the proposed method’s performance in calibrating the six-parameter triaxial gyroscope model, taking into consideration measurements corrupted by simulated noise. Experimental validation was also carried out using two commercially available MEMS inertial measurement units (LSM9DS1) and a servo motor. The experimental results effectively demonstrate the efficacy of the proposed calibration approach.

title: Smooth Annealing Projection Concentration Maximization for Event-based Velocimetry
authors: Jia Ai, Zuobing Chen, Wenbin Ning, Yong Lee
publish: IEEE Transactions on Instrumentation and Measurement
abstract: Event cameras provide microsecond-level temporal resolution for brightness changes, which makes them particularly suitable for event-based imaging velocimetry (EBIV). However, accurately estimating velocity vectors from event data is challenging because of its inherent asynchronous and sparse nature. This work proposes a Projection Concentration Maximization with Smooth Annealing (PCM-SA) method that estimates the velocity by maximizing the concentration of raw-event projections. Specifically, raw events are continuously projected as parameterized Gaussian kernels, yielding a smooth mixture-of-Gaussians representation of event alignment. The degree of alignment is quantified using a continuous Simpson concentration index, which defines a fully differentiable objective with respect to the velocity parameters. To ensure robust optimization of this non-convex objective, we adopt a smooth annealing strategy that initially enlarges the basin of attraction for Newton–Raphson iterations and gradually restores the original objective to recover a high-accuracy solution. Without relying on intermediate pseudo-frames, PCM-SA directly estimates velocity by formulating event projection concentration as a continuous, differentiable objective defined on raw event data. Extensive experiments illustrate that PCM-SA consistently outperforms baseline methods on both synthetic and real event data. Remarkably, the EBIV pixel-locking effect is observed for the first time due to PCM-SA’s improved accuracy. In addition, the implementation of our PCM-SA is publicly available for interested researchers. Overall, it provides a new avenue for event-based imaging velocimetry.

title: Non-dominated Sorting Quantum Evolutionary Algorithm and Its Application in Optimizing Multi-port Network Antennas
authors: Hui Li, Zifeng Wang, Wenrui Zheng, Tianxi Feng
publish: IEEE Transactions on Antennas and Propagation
abstract: Many-objective optimization problems are challenging and time-consuming, especially when the number of objectives is large. In this paper, an evolutionary algorithm inspired by quantum computing, called the Non-dominated Sorting Quantum Evolutionary Algorithm (NSQEA), is proposed to address the many-objective optimization challenges in the electromagnetic field. Quantum computing concepts are leveraged to enhance optimization efficiency. Two strategies, i.e., “zeroing” and “multi-core processing”, are introduced to mitigate the uncertainties caused by initial population bias and apical dominance. Furthermore, principles for determining dominance relations in many-objective problems and a fast non-dominated sorting method are proposed, which significantly reduce the number of non-dominated solutions while ensuring population diversity. To demonstrate the effectiveness and applicability of the proposed algorithm, two examples of pixel antenna designs are presented. The experimental results validate the effectiveness of the “zeroing” and “multi-core processing” methods. Compared with other algorithms such as NSGA-III, RVEA, SPEA2, and MOEA/D, NSQEA exhibits superior stability, solution diversity, and global search capability.

title: EMAF-Net: An Edge-Aware Super-Resolution Framework for Enhanced Object Detection and Semantic Annotation
authors: Ramyashree, Sanath Prabhu, S Raghavendra, B N Anoop
abstract: Low-resolution image acquisition remains a fundamental bottleneck in real-world vision systems, including surveillance, UAV monitoring, autonomous navigation, and medical imaging, where degraded inputs significantly impair object detection and semantic annotation accuracy. While modern deep learning-based super-resolution (SR) techniques have achieved high reconstruction fidelity in terms of PSNR and SSIM, most existing approaches optimize primarily for perceptual quality without explicitly validating improvements in downstream detection performance. Consequently, a gap persists between low-level image enhancement and high-level semantic reliability. To bridge this gap, we propose EMAF-Net, an Edge-Aware MAXIM Attention Fusion Network that integrates edge-aware SRCNN enrichment, MAXIM-based denoising, lightweight squeeze-and-excitation attention, and multi-scale feature fusion within an adaptive training framework. The architecture is explicitly designed to preserve structural boundaries and semantic consistency required for robust object detection. Unlike conventional SR pipelines, EMAF-Net evaluates enhancement quality not only through reconstruction metrics but also through annotation performance using YOLOv8 on manually curated object labels. Experimental results on the DIV2K dataset demonstrate that EMAF-Net achieves a peak PSNR of 44.10 dB and SSIM of 0.9965, while improving detection performance on super-resolved images to 0.79 precision, 0.69 recall, and 0.68 mIoU. The proposed framework consistently enhances detection reliability under degraded conditions, demonstrating that task-aware super-resolution can effectively align perceptual reconstruction with semantic objectives. These findings position EMAF-Net as a unified enhancement-annotation framework suitable for deployment in real-time vision systems where both image fidelity and detection robustness are critical.

title: An End-to-End Deep Learning Model for Clustering-based Statistical Arbitrage
authors: Hyunju Lee, Woojin Chang
abstract: We propose an end-to-end deep learning framework for statistical arbitrage in the crypto-currency futures market, which integrates clustering and trading within a unified structure. Statistical arbitrage seeks to exploit price deviations among similar assets to generate market-neutral profits. The cryptocurrency market, characterized by structural similarities among assets and market inefficiency, offers promising opportunities for such strategies. We incorporate clustering into the model to learn optimal arbitrage trading strategies that capture dynamic relationships within the cryptocurrency market and detect arbitrage opportunities. A transformer encoder is employed to model temporal dependencies among assets. The entire arbitrage process from portfolio selection to trade allocation is jointly optimized under a customized objective function that reflects the core principles of traditional statistical arbitrage. Empirical results demonstrate that the proposed model consistently outperforms benchmark strategies with negative Sharpe ratios, achieving a cumulative return of 3.310 and an annualized Sharpe ratio of 2.516 after accounting for transaction costs. Sub-period analysis shows that the strategy remains robust across varying market conditions with annualized Sharpe ratios ranging from 1.168 to 2.240 with transaction fees, supporting its market-neutral characteristics. Ablation studies further validate the crucial contribution of each model component, such as clustering, the end-to-end structure, and the proposed objective function, in enabling effective statistical arbitrage.

title: Software Adjustable Mechatronic Inerter-Spring-Damper System_supp5-3672853.mp4
authors: Mehrdad Moallem
abstract: Mechanical elements such as dampers, springs, and inerters play a crucial role in various engineering applications, including vehicles, robotics, rehabilitation devices, vibration isolation systems, and structural designs such as seismic-resistant buildings and bridges. The ability to dynamically adjust damping, stiffness, and inertance in real time can greatly enhance the adaptability, stability, and overall performance of these systems. However, conventional methods for modifying these parameters often require physical alterations to material properties or geometric configurations, making them complex and impractical. This article introduces a software-adjustable mechatronic inerter-spring-damper system that leverages a permanent magnet synchronous machine, a two-level AC/DC converter, and control firmware to enable real-time modulation of mechanical behavior through electrical analogies. An experimental prototype is developed and validated, incorporating a sliding mode current controller to precisely regulate torque and achieve the desired dynamic response, demonstrating the feasibility and effectiveness of the proposed approach.

title: Joint optical phase retrieval and computing for secure networks using mode division multiplexing
authors: Dennis Pohle, Pin-Hsun Lin, Filipe M. Ferreira, Eduard Jorswieck, Jürgen W. Czarske
abstract: Knowledge of the channel matrix in multimode fiber based space division multiplexing requires precise retrieval of crosstalk in amplitude, phase and polarization. We use an additional multimode fiber as an optical reservoir to recover SDM signals of 10 spatial modes with a pearson correlation of 0.963 and achieved simulationally phase retrieval of 23 modes using a standard 50 μm-core MMF. The reference-less approach of phase retrieval within the network can potentially be extended to end-to-end long-haul systems and enables physical-layer and quantum-secure networking by optical precoding.

title: Residual Modeling and Experimental Validation of Motion Noise Compensation for a Rotating Accelerometer Gravity Gradiometer
authors: Bohai Ke, Yang Zhou, Siyuan Liu, Xiaobing Yu, Li Yu, Chenyuan Hu, Huafeng Liu, Ji Fan, Zebing Zhou
publish: IEEE Transactions on Instrumentation and Measurement
abstract: Airborne gravity gradiometers play a vital role in geophysical exploration, navigation, and resource detection. However, motion-induced noise on moving platforms can exceed true anomalies by several orders of magnitude. The rotating accelerometer gravity gradiometer (RAGG) employs an orthogonal antisymmetric configuration to suppress common-mode accelerations, while frequency modulation suppresses low-frequency noise, yet nonideal factors still introduce residual acceleration noise. To address this, a motion noise compensation method formulated in the rotating reference frame and based on transfer coefficient calibration is developed. The method eliminates the need for demodulation and remodulation, thereby simplifying motion term modeling while maintaining compensation accuracy. The experimental results demonstrated the feasibility of this approach, with the combined output noise reduced to a level approaching that of RAGG self-noise in static measurements. Furthermore, a residual model is proposed to predict the suppression capability and residual noise after compensation. Its validity is confirmed by experiments in a three-axis shipborne environment, where noise is suppressed from 4×10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">-4</sup> g/Hz<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">1/2</sup> to 5×10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">-8</sup> g/Hz<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">1/2</sup>, corresponding to a suppression factor of approximately 9×10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sup>. This is consistent with residual model predictions and confirms the validity of the proposed method. The proposed framework thus offers a quantitative basis for evaluating compensation performance and guiding the advancement of gravity gradiometers.

title: Toward Standardization of 6G and NextG: Key Technologies to Enable Fundamental Enhancements
authors: Wanshi Chen, Lingjia Liu, Xiaofeng Liu, Erik G. Larsson, Mohamed El Jaafari, Tony Q. S. Quek
publish: IEEE Journal on Selected Areas in Communications
abstract: Motivated by International Mobile Telecommunications (IMT)-2030, sixth generation (6G) mobile networks in 3GPP (third-generation partner project) commenced with a workshop in March 2025, attracting more than 200 contributions and 700 in-person attendances. While the details of 6G study and the eventual 6G specifications in 3GPP are yet to be developed, there is strong motivation in 3GPP to focus on the fundamental values that 6G may bring, most notably in terms of improving user experience and the overall network operation, particularly with respect to reducing the total cost of ownership (TCO). It is evident that there is a strong need to streamline and simplify the 6G specifications for efficient standardization, implementation, and commercial deployments. For the services also accommodated by the fifth generation (5G) networks, 6G is expected to provide meaningful enhancements. That is, instead of simply pushing for even higher envelopes such as peak data rates, more emphasis will be on improved coverage (especially at the cell edge and for certain data rates or services), and energy efficiency (for both end devices and networks) using the existing and new spectrum. Moreover, 6G is expected to support new services, with artificial intelligence (AI) and sensing as two primary examples. In this article, we provide a tutorial on the key technologies driving fundamental enhancements for the standardization of 6G and beyond, covering the necessary co-existence between 5G and 6G for smooth migration, AI-native radio access network (RAN), multiple-input-multiple-out (MIMO), sustainable operation with increased energy efficiency, coverage enhancements involving both terrestrial and non-terrestrial networks (NTN), integrated sensing and communication (ISAC), diverse device types including the low-end back-scattering based ambient internet of things (IoT) devices, and native support of NTN. We conclude this article by pointing out several key challenges and opportunities towards standardization of 6G and beyond.

title: mmWave Sensing for Detecting Movement Through Thermoplastic Masks During Radiation Therapy Treatment
authors: Ali Kourani, Naveed A. Abbasi, Syeda Narjis Fatima, Katsuyuki Haneda, Andreas F. Molisch
publish: 2025 59th Asilomar Conference on Signals, Systems, and Computers
abstract: Precision in radiation therapy relies on immobilization systems that limit patient motion. Thermoplastic masks are commonly used for this purpose, but subtle voluntary and involuntary movements such as jaw shifts, deep breathing, or eye squinting may still compromise treatment accuracy. Existing motion tracking methods are limited: optical systems require a clear line of sight and only detect surface motion, while X-ray-based tracking introduces additional ionizing radiation. This study explores the use of low-power, non-ionizing millimeter-wave (mmWave) sensing for through-mask motion detection. We characterize the RF properties of thermoplastic mask material in the 28–38 GHz range and perform motion detection using a 1 GHz bandwidth centered at 28 GHz. We use a frequency-domain system with horn antennas in a custom-built anechoic chamber to capture changes in the amplitude and phase of transmitted RF waves in response to subtle head and facial movements. These findings lay some groundwork for future real-time, through-mask motion tracking and future integration with multi-antenna systems and machine learning for error correction during radiotherapy.

title: AI-Based Image Analysis for Marine Species Monitoring: A CNN-RF Approach
authors: Jordi FornéMengual, Veronica Nieves
publish: 2025 IEEE Asia-Pacific Conference on Geoscience, Electronics and Remote Sensing Technology (AGERS)
abstract: Artificial intelligence and computer vision are increasingly central to environmental monitoring and natural resource management. This work presents a hybrid CNNRandom Forest (CNN-RF) framework for automated analysis of electronic monitoring marine imagery, designed to enhance interpretability and reliability under limited data conditions. Convolutional features derived from a Faster R-CNN backbone were aggregated at the region level and classified using an RF classifier, enabling accurate identification of marine megafauna across five taxonomic groups. Under oracle (ground-truth) conditions, the model achieved macro-F1 $=0.84 \pm 0.04$, while detector-derived regions yielded $0.65 \pm 0.06$, quantifying the effect of localization uncertainty. Despite imperfect detections, accuracy remained high for key taxa, demonstrating resilience under operational conditions. The proposed framework contributes to the development of AI-assisted image analysis tools for marine monitoring, and sustainable management of resources, supporting scalable, interpretable automation in ecological observation systems.

title: Exploring E-Governance for Sustainable Grazing Administration in Nigeria: Legal Viewpoint, Challenges and Prospects
authors: Clement Chidi-Ife Chigbo, Joseph Ochayi Sunday, Udosen Jacob Idem, Yebisi Ebenezer Tunde, Abiodun Ayodele Ojo
publish: 2025 International Conference on Innovation and Intelligence for Informatics, Computing, and Technologies (3ICT)
abstract: The persistent crisis between herders and farmers in Nigeria has underscored the urgent need for sustainable grazing management system solutions. Despite various policy efforts, challenges remain due to poor implementation and a lack of effective coordination. This study adopts a systematic review to explore the potential of E-Governance as a tool to enhance grazing management sustainably through legal frameworks. The study adopts a doctrinal and policy analysis approach, examining relevant Nigerian laws, digital governance strategies, and international best practices. This work reveals that while Nigeria's E-Governance infrastructure is evolving, its application in grazing management remains limited, particularly since there is no specific law to address the intersection of grazing and agricultural sectors. The absence of a single specific legal framework linking E-Governance to grazing limits its implementation. A technique for reforming legislative regimes to improve stakeholder roles and ICT artefacts for successful E-Governance for sustainable grazing management in Nigeria was proposed, in line with worldwide best practices.

title: Hybrid GAT-Transformer Approach to Node-Level Influence Regression
authors: ZhongHeng Li, XuDong Huang, Lihui Xu, Guochao Hu, Yong Liao
publish: 2025 International Conference on Big Data and Data Mining (BDDM)
abstract: In the realm of social networks, accurately predicting user influence plays a vital role in the assessment of information-propagation efficacy. This paper proposes a novel model based on Graph Attention Network (GAT) for user influence prediction in social networks, enhanced with residual connections and a hybrid variant incorporating Graph-Transformer elements for improved global context capture. By leveraging GAT's attention mechanism, the model dynamically captures the varying importance of user interactions and features, addressing the limitations of traditional methods that overlook heterogeneous relationships. The social graph is an undirected network of users linked by interactions, enriched with opensource node and edge attributes. Influence is formulated as a node-level regression task, predicting a quantitative score derived from self-features and weighted neighbor aggregates. Under the same setup, the hybrid GAT-Transformer surpasses GCN, GAT and Graph-Transformer baselines on the simulated, Cora and Twitter subgraphs, achieving on Cora approximately MSE 0.005, MAE 0.050 and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{R}^{2} 0.95$</tex>. Ablation studies confirm the efficacy of multi-head attention and hybrid components, and parametersensitivity plus robustness analyses reveal optimal setups and strong generalization.

title: Passive HF Noise Interferometry for Sea Surface Estimation
authors: Elliot Mueller, Michael Martinez, Karim Sabra, Jeffrey Krolik
publish: 2025 59th Asilomar Conference on Signals, Systems, and Computers
abstract: This paper investigates the use of passive high-frequency (HF) noise interferometry for sea surface roughness estimation and Bragg-line detection using ambient noise sources of opportunity. We model the sea-surface backscatter channel as a slowly time-varying tapped-delay line driven by incident HF noise and show that the resulting correlation structure supports delay– Doppler processing analogous to active noise radar. A link-budget analysis decomposes the achievable signal-to-noise ratio (SNR) into contributions from (i) the fractional coherent noise power, (ii) the effective radar cross section (RCS) of the sea surface, and (iii) the two-way propagation loss. We apply this framework to experimental data from Kwajalein Atoll, where an HF broadcast transmitter provides a strong coherent noise source. Using conventional beamforming followed by passive correlation processing, we observe Bragg surface scattering in agreement with our link-budget predictions. We further demonstrate that projection-based processing, which removes zero-Doppler clutter from the reference path, enables clearer detection of Bragg lines in a bistatic configuration between the KWOTHR and Meck receive arrays using a distant non-cooperative source. The results indicate that HF ambient noise sources can support sea-surface roughness estimation in a fully passive sensing architecture.

title: Climate Change Adaptation and Mitigation Strategies in Future Smart Cities
authors: Suresh Vishwakarma, Ruchi Tyagi, Manish K Rathod
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Smart Innovations in Systems, Infrastructure, Mechanical, Power, AI and Computing Technologies (SISIMPACT)
abstract: Urban areas, central to human activity, face significant challenges from climate change and development, necessitating urgent action. The rise of smart cities, utilizing technology to enhance sustainability and quality of life, presents an opportunity to address these issues. This paper examines how future smart cities can leverage innovative technologies, stakeholder collaboration, and sustainable design to build resilient systems. These systems mitigate and adapt to climate challenges, promoting ecological balance and social equity. Key strategies include Vertical Green Structures (VGSs), renewable energy, and energy-efficient prefabricated housing. These homes feature radiative cooling coatings and Phase Change Materials (PCMs), reducing cooling loads by <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$7-17 \%$</tex> across diverse Indian climates. The study underscores the importance of integrating technology and community engagement. This fosters sustainable urban growth amid climate unpredictability.

title: Enhanced Vehicle Damage Evaluation Using YOLOv8 and XGBoost in a PySpark Environment
authors: Shuba Bhuvi Matam, Utkarsh Mohit, Aditya Raman, Gouthaman P, T. Preethiya
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Emerging Trends in Computing and Communication (ETCOM)
abstract: Vehicle damage detection is a crucial process in the insurance industry. Manual assessment is often inaccurate, biased, and time-consuming. We must shift to automated models of damage detection and cost prediction. While we have several AI-driven approaches to localize damage, we lack many systems that integrate masking to detect the type of damage and a cost prediction model, especially in large-scale data environments. To address this gap, we are proposing a framework that uses YOLOv8 (You Only Look Once) for damage localization through object detection and damage classification, while we use XGBoost (The Extreme Gradient Boosting) for cost estimation. We also use PySpark to manage the large-scale data and handle all the preprocessing, and PyTorch powers the detection and segmentation stages. It runs the vision models to extract damage type, location, and precise area from the images. Experimental results demonstrate improved speed and efficiency as compared to traditional methods. This framework combines Machine learning algorithms with big data tools for faster and reliable vehicle damage evaluation.

title: GAN-based Missing Data Imputation for TimeSeries Rainfall in Flood Prediction: A Case Study in Pahang, Malaysia
authors: Mohd Shahar Abdullah, Ali Selamat, Nguyet Quang Do, Mohd Azlan Abu, Herman Tawil, Ondrej Krejcar
publish: 2025 IEEE Asia-Pacific Conference on Geoscience, Electronics and Remote Sensing Technology (AGERS)
abstract: Missing data is a common issue across multiple research domains, resulting in imprecise analysis, degraded performance, and biased outcomes. Missing values in rainfall data caused by defective equipment or a lack of historical rainfall events pose a significant challenge in flood prediction. To cope with this problem, numerous data imputation methods have been suggested to handle the missing values in time-series data, from the conventional methods to advanced techniques. While the conventional imputation approaches present certain drawbacks in dealing with incomplete data, machine learning techniques may have restrictions when a massive amount of sophisticated data is involved. Consequently, this paper aims to propose a deep learning model based on Generative Adversarial Network (GAN) and Temporal Convolutional Network (TCN) to estimate the rainfall amount for flood disaster management. The combination of GAN and TCN introduces a novel approach to simultaneously address the incomplete value issue and the spatiotemporal nature of time-series rainfall data. The model was trained and validated within the Kuantan River basin, situated in the state of Pahang, Malaysia, to demonstrate its capability. Results from the preliminary experiments indicated that the proposed solution outperformed other baseline architectures (forward fill, backward fill, k-Nearest Neighbor, Long Short-Term Memory, and standard GAN) across several evaluation metrics (Mean Absolute Error, Root Mean Square Error, Mean Absolute Percentage Error, and Coefficient of Determination). This highlights the superior performance of GAN in missing data reconstruction and the combination of GAN-TCN in flood prediction.

title: Machine Learning-Based Smart Stress Detection Using Sensor Data
authors: P. Kaushik, Md Ankushavali, Deepti Agrawal, Niranjani V, Chetan Khemraj, Viraj Gadde
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Smart Innovations in Systems, Infrastructure, Mechanical, Power, AI and Computing Technologies (SISIMPACT)
abstract: In recent years, stress management has become an important area of research across various fields, including healthcare, workplace productivity, and mental well-being. The ability to detect stress levels accurately from physiological data can provide critical insights into an individual's emotional state, enabling timely interventions. In this research, we investigate state-of-the- art machine learning methods to effectively identify stress from sensor data. The data comprises multiple variables, including heart rate variability (HRV), electrodermal activity (EDA), skin temperature, and respiratory rate, among others. We focus on Support Vector Machines (SVM), a powerful classifier that excels in distinguishing patterns in high-dimensional spaces. In this context, SVM is utilized to classify stress levels (low, moderate, and high) based on sensor-derived physiological signals. By leveraging kernel techniques, SVM effectively separates stress levels using both linear and non-linear decision boundaries. The model's ability to generalize across unseen data makes it a strong candidate for real-time stress detection systems. To evaluate the model's effectiveness, we use metrics such as accuracy, precision, recall, and F1-score. Our findings suggest that SVM, with its robust classification capabilities, shows considerable promise in identifying stress patterns from sensor data. This has significant implications for fields such as healthcare and wearable technology, where stress monitoring can be integrated into devices to improve mental health support. Furthermore, our research demonstrates how SVM can enhance stress detection and personalized interventions, advancing the use of machine learning in emotion-sensing applications.

title: VeriFace: Lightweight CNN Feature Extractors for Fast Deepfake Image Authentication
authors: Lokesh Khedekar, Smita Mande, Ajay S Chhajed, Tejas Kedar, Kaustubh Kelgandre, Atharva Kassa
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Smart Innovations in Systems, Infrastructure, Mechanical, Power, AI and Computing Technologies (SISIMPACT)
abstract: A lightweight convolutional neural network pipeline is presented in this paper for the purposes of deep fake image detection which emphasizes structured training, practical deployment and confidence estimation for real world scenarios. The method employs balanced datasets for training, standard methods for pre-processing and data augmentation of images and performance is measured in terms of accuracy, precision, recall and confusion matrix analysis to avoid overfitting and maintain generalizability across broadcasting datasets. The network architecture uses compact feature extraction and binary classification head as a binary classification system and employs probability tuning in order to provide reliable confidence scores for decision support. A web ready interface is built to allow sub second prediction of lone images via optimised pre-processing and model loading stages therefore allowing seamless end to end operation. Experimental results indicate strong classification performance attained for limited input resolution and number of epochs during training, supporting the designs made with respect to reliability, speed and deployability as misinformation reduction mechanisms.

title: Novel Exponential Sliding-Mode Current Control for PMSGs with High-Gain Disturbance Observer
authors: Xiaoling Su, Yongkai Jiang, Mengsong Liu, Cheng Zhang, Tianqi Yin, Chun Wei
publish: 2025 3rd Power Electronics and Power System Conference (PEPSC)
abstract: To enhance the dynamic performance of a permanent magnet synchronous generator (PMSG)-based wind energy conversion system, this paper proposes a composite current controller that integrates a high-gain disturbance observer (HGDO) with a novel exponential sliding mode control (NESMC). First, by constructing a fast terminal sliding mode surface with exponential convergence characteristics and combining it with a chattering-free control law design, smooth and continuous output of the control signal is achieved. Then, to improve system robustness, a high-gain disturbance observer capable of estimating internal and external disturbances in real time is developed, and dynamic compensation control is implemented based on the observed results. Finally, the global asymptotic stability of the closed-loop system is rigorously demonstrated using Lyapunov stability theory, and simulations verify that the proposed method exhibits excellent dynamic response, robustness, disturbance rejection, and chattering suppression capabilities.

title: ML-Based SLA Violation Prediction and Compliance Management in Cloud-Native Business Applications
authors: Debabrata Roy, Mohan Garg, Kishor Gaire, Barani Selvaraj, Neeraj Das, Manpreet Singh
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Smart Innovations in Systems, Infrastructure, Mechanical, Power, AI and Computing Technologies (SISIMPACT)
abstract: Quality of service (QoS) concerns about cloud-native business applications relate to achieving trust, availability, and performance by adhering to Service Level Agreements (SLAs). But the characteristic of cloud environments, such as variable workloads, microservices and resource interdependencies is a major challenge to proactive compliance with SLA. In this study, we suggest a new ML based system, Smart Service Level Agreement Management using Machine Learning (SmartSLAML) which allows to intelligently monitor and manage SLAs through predictive monitoring and compliance management in real-time. To achieve this, the system uses the constant streams of information gathered through application logs, infrastructure performance as well as the SLA requirements to get an alert on possible infringement based on the standards before it happens. It adopts the hybrid machine learning model that incorporates Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) network of learning temporal patterns, and Random Forest classifiers of ranking features and predicting anomalies. The structure provides a module of compliance enforcer, which remotely autooverdue mitigation measures, which could be scaling, rerouting, or service throttling to ensure the SLA assurance. SmartSLA-ML is meant to be modular, and lightweight, tightly integrating with containerized and microservice based designs. Experimental analyses analyze the capabilities of the framework on real-life datasets to prove that it has the potential to minimize the number of SLA violations and increase reliability of the service delivery. The SmartSLA-ML system attained an overall accuracy of 96.5% in forecasting SLA breaches in cloud-native apps for businesses.

title: Multipart Structured Autoencoder for Efficient CSI Compression
authors: Mohamed Salah Ibrahim, Akshay Malhotra
publish: 2025 59th Asilomar Conference on Signals, Systems, and Computers
abstract: Channel state information (CSI) feedback compression is a critical task in massive MIMO communication systems. Conventional approaches rely on either codebook-based quantization or autoencoder (AE) techniques. While AE-based methods offer improved reconstruction accuracy, they still exhibit limitations in compression efficiency and flexibility. This paper introduces a novel structured AE framework for efficient compression of a sequence of temporally correlated CSI samples. The proposed model imposes a multipart latent structure that decomposes each sequence into a common component, capturing long-term or shared features across samples, and a specific component, capturing short-term or sample-dependent variations. During feedback, the common representation is transmitted once per sequence, whereas the specific components are reported when scheduled or requested by the base station. This adaptive reporting strategy significantly reduces the overall feedback overhead while maintaining high reconstruction fidelity, offering an interpretable and scalable solution for next-generation FDD massive MIMO systems. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed structured AE significantly outperforms the state-of-the-art CSI compression schemes, achieving notable reductions in feedback overhead while offering enhanced flexibility through adaptive and task-oriented reporting.

title: Using AI Models for Fault-Tolerance in Quantum Computing
authors: Saravanan K, Divakar G, Kaaviya K, Keerthivasan G, Rahul K
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Smart Innovations in Systems, Infrastructure, Mechanical, Power, AI and Computing Technologies (SISIMPACT)
abstract: Correlated noise significantly hampers qubit stability and computational precision in quantum computing. Traditional error mitigation techniques assume independent noise and fail to adapt dynamically to evolving error patterns. To address this, we propose an AI-driven noise reduction framework that employs a Recurrent Neural Network (RNN)-Transformer architecture, achieving 92.4% accuracy in real-time noise prediction. Integrated federated learning enables secure, cross-device adaptation while preserving user privacy. Additionally, Bayesian optimization and adaptive gate reconfiguration reduce logical error rates by <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{4 3 \%}$</tex> and improve gate fidelity by <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{3 5 \%}$</tex>, enhancing overall computational reliability. Using IBM Quantum Falcon hardware, the proposed framework demonstrated superior stability and noise suppression compared to conventional methods. Through simulation and partial hardware validation, the reinforcement learning (RL) component effectively adapted error-correction strategies under varying noise conditions, ensuring continuous optimization. The framework's scalability and hardware independence were further confirmed through successful deployment on IBM Qiskit and Google Cirq, highlighting adaptability across heterogeneous quantum architectures. This study lays a robust foundation for advancing stable, accurate, and practical quantum computation.

title: Hybrid Feature Selection for Intrusion Detection Systems using Pearson Correlation and Boruta-LGBM Algorithm
authors: Raman Verma, R Aroul Canessane, Bhuvaneshwari Patil, Manisha Kumari, Cheshta, K. Anandhi
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Smart Innovations in Systems, Infrastructure, Mechanical, Power, AI and Computing Technologies (SISIMPACT)
abstract: Securing Internet of Things networks greatly depends on using IDS (Intrusion Detection Systems). This paper designs a new feature assortment process, called PCB-LGBM which merges Pearson Correlation and Boruta-Light Gradient Boosting Machine (LGBM) to strengthen the performance of IDS on the CICIDS-2017 dataset. In the beginning, initial steps like KNN imputation, k-Means SMOTE and Min–Max scaling help handle problems with data quality and unbalanced classes. Setting the Pearson correlation to 0.8 removes identical features and slashes the total number of features to 24. After this, the set of important variables is reduced to 11 by Boruta-LGBM. DT, MLP, KNN and LGBM are some of the classifiers used with them. According to the results, LGBM has the best accuracy (99.99%), precision (100%), recall (99.99%), F1-score (99.99%) and ROC-AUC (99.99%), while taking only 0.31s for training. The suggested method improves the detection of threats while lowering the efforts needed for computation, giving a practical answer for securing real-time IoT networks.

title: Exploring Design and Material Innovations in Junctionless TFETs for Low Power and High Frequency Applications
authors: Kaushal Kumar, Abhijit Kumar Kar, Aditya Jain, Vinay Kumar, Ajay Kumar, Neha Gupta
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Recent Trends in Electrical, Electronics and Computing Technologies (ICRTEECT)
abstract: Semiconductor technology is advancing so quickly that we need to create more energy efficient, high-performance devices, as traditional MOSFETs might soon not be enough. Junctionless tunneling field-effect transistors (JL-TFETs) are a candidate for low-power and high-frequency technology contingent on the ease of fabrication technology, added electrostatic control, and impressive subthreshold swing (SS). Nevertheless, the on-state current (I<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">ON</inf>,) and response under dual signalling from both carriers (i.e, ambipolar) are the disadvantages keeping JL-TFETs from mainstream use. This paper will discuss advances in JL-TFET design and material engineering with the primary aim of overcoming challenges faced. This includes advanced gate engineering techniques, as dual material gates and high-k dielectrics which optimizes electric field distribution and carrier transport [2]. This paper will also describe channel engineering options that can be fabricated in new Nanowire architecture, or heterojunction design representing a superior level of electrostatic control leading to less leakage currents, for example. Innovations in active materials including III-V semiconductors, two-dimensional materials, or strain engineering were also assessed, to highlight their benefits for tunneling efficiency and impacting overall performance [4]. Lastly a summary comparison of JL-TFETs, classical MOSFETs and junction TFETs respectively shows the improved characteristics of SS and like I<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">OFF</inf>, exhibited by JL-TFET at low power categories [5]. Thus, via the use of III-V materials as previously highlighted, newest improvements were observed to the like I<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">ON</inf>, and cutoff frequency (fT) in JL-TFETs making them one of the serious contenders for high frequency technology categories. This work provides a detailed overview of the latest advancements in JL-TFET technology and explores future research directions for next-generation electronics.

title: Vision-Based PCB Fault Analyzer with Adaptive Feedback Alignment
authors: K.P.N.K. Arachchi, R.A.D. Ravihara, S. M. M. L Karunarathne, S. C Chandrarathna
publish: 2025 IEEE 19th International Conference on Industrial and Information Systems (ICIIS)
abstract: This paper presents the development of a Vision-Based PCB Fault Analyzer designed primarily for electronics repair centers such as computer and mobile phone service shops, as well as for PCB manufacturing and testing laboratories. The system automates the fault-detection process of Printed Circuit Boards (PCBs) and Printed Circuit Board Assemblies (PCBAs), reducing manual errors and preventing accidental short circuits common in manual troubleshooting. The proposed system integrates vision-based inspection and electrical testing using a flying-probe mechanism controlled by STM32 and PIC microcontrollers. A YOLOv5 deep learning model is employed to detect visual defects such as missing components, misalignments, and soldering faults, while the probe system verifies parameters like voltage, continuity, and resistor or capacitor conditions. To minimize hardware complexity and cost, an adaptive vision-based feedback mechanism replaces traditional servo feedback, dynamically adjusting camera positioning based on prediction confidence scores to ensure accurate alignment. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed system provides a reliable, low-cost, and adaptive solution for both repair centers and production-line PCB testing environments.

title: Research on Digital Model Simulation of Multi-High Risk State Monitoring of Coastal Transmission Lines in Complex Environment
authors: Jia Gu, Xiang Bao, Fancheng Guo, Zhongheng Li, Xuyong Jiang, Chunrong Ou
publish: 2025 International Conference on Big Data and Data Mining (BDDM)
abstract: Coastal power transmission lines in Nantong operate within complex environments characterized by salt spray corrosion, severe weather conditions (e.g., typhoons and heavy rains), and biological impacts (such as bird nesting). These factors collectively create multiple high-risk operational states that severely threaten line safety. This study focuses on developing key technologies for digital monitoring of multi-high-risk states. By applying machine learning and deep learning algorithms, we constructed a model that integrates data collected through various sensors, followed by cleaning and feature extraction processes to achieve comprehensive monitoring state quantification. Research findings demonstrate that this technology significantly improves condition recognition accuracy while enhancing environmental adaptability. Case studies further validate its effectiveness in monitoring and warning of critical line states, which holds significant importance for improving operational safety of coastal power transmission lines and ensuring stable electricity supply.

title: Design and Implementation of Online Monitoring System for Multiple Parameters of Outdoor GIS Pipe Flange
authors: Mingyu Gu, Xianghua Tang, Hongcheng Jiang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Big Data and Data Mining (BDDM)
abstract: Gas-insulated switchgear (GIS) is a critical component ensuring the safe and stable operation of power grids. Overheating and abnormal vibrations caused by poor electrical contact at connection points such as pipe flanges are the primary causes of equipment failures. To address the limitations of existing monitoring methods that fail to comprehensively reflect operational status, this paper designs and implements an integrated online monitoring system for outdoor GIS pipe flanges. The system combines multi-dimensional sensing technologies including vibration, temperature, SF6 gas state, and partial discharge. It collects real-time housing vibration and temperature signals through a distributed fiber optic sensor network, while employing ultra-high frequency and ultrasonic methods for fault localization. Multi-scale entropy and PCA algorithms are used to extract vibration and temperature rise characteristics respectively. High-precision GIS defect and fault identification is achieved through RNN-like neural networks and SVM-like machine learning methods. Finally, a comprehensive monitoring platform integrating data acquisition, feature extraction, condition assessment, and remote diagnosis is established using Web Service-based data synchronization technology.

title: An Integrated Geospatial Framework for Optimal LRT Route and Station Planning in the Colombo Metropolitan Area, Sri Lanka
authors: Thirilojanan Keerthi, Kithsiri Dissanayake, Chaminda Samarasuriya
publish: 2025 IEEE Asia-Pacific Conference on Geoscience, Electronics and Remote Sensing Technology (AGERS)

title: Dynamic Adaptive Inference with Reinforcement Learning in Time-Varying Future Generation Sensing Environments
authors: Abigail Eastman, Yongho Seo, Antonia Papandreou-Suppappola
publish: 2025 59th Asilomar Conference on Signals, Systems, and Computers
abstract: Limited resources and high traffic demand in future generation wireless systems necessitate methodologies to dynamically adapt to time-varying sensing environments. In this paper, we consider a tracking problem under spectrum sharing conditions that balances the joint need of transmit waveform selection and sub-band utilization as the number of available sub-bands varies over time. We propose a reinforcement learning-based method that dynamically switches the underlying Markov model to account for time-varying changes in the state-action space. The method is based on a double Q-learning algorithm, combined with the ability to transfer the learning acquired from the prior model to a new state-space representation without loss of information or performance. This allows for real-time reactivity to various scenarios, including jamming, emergency response, and cooperative sub-band assignment while optimizing tracking performance by adapting the radar transmit waveform. We demonstrate with simulations that the radar can adapt to time variations in the state and action spaces while transferring knowledge in real time while tracking.

title: Comparative Analysis to Assess YOLO Model Performance in AI-Based Dynamic Fire Protection System
authors: Mohammed Majid M. Al-Khalidy, Sayed Ali Mahdi, Ali Jaafar, Mohammed Alrayes, Ali Hasan, Ahmed Mohammed M. Al-Khalidi
publish: 2025 International Conference on Innovation and Intelligence for Informatics, Computing, and Technologies (3ICT)
abstract: Ensuring public safety is paramount, particularly in fire protection systems, to mitigate property damage and, most critically, to preserve human life. Conventional fire detection systems, especially those deployed in large venues and double-volume halls, often exhibit limitations in detection accuracy, range, and response time. This study explores object detection performance by integrating deep learning-based object detection models. Four YOLO (You Only Look Once) versions, 8, 9, 10, and 11, were used and compared with an intelligent dynamic fire hose nozzle to enhance fire detection capabilities and facilitate real-time emergency response. A system prototype incorporating the YOLO models was developed to assess the efficacy of each approach. The performance of the integrated system was systematically evaluated to determine its effectiveness in improving detection accuracy and response efficiency. The findings highlight the potential of model-based fire detection systems for large-scale implementation, offering a promising advancement in intelligent fire protection technology.

title: Guided Wireless Technology for Near-Field Communication
authors: Mohamed Akrout, Amine Mezghani, Faouzi Bellili, Robert W. Heath
publish: 2025 59th Asilomar Conference on Signals, Systems, and Computers
abstract: Guided wireless technology is an innovative approach that combines the strengths of guided waves and wireless communication. In traditional wireless systems, signals propagate through the air, where they are vulnerable to interference, attenuation, and jamming. Guided communication, in contrast, confines signals within a physical medium, significantly reducing interference and supporting higher data rates over longer distances. Guided wireless technology harnesses these benefits by creating guided wireless channels and offering a controlled pathway for electromagnetic waves. This work harnesses these benefits by focusing on the modeling of near-field communication through long connected arrays deployed in linear-cell environments. We then derive a circuit model for long array as an infinitely long dipole with multiple periodic feed points before approximating it with a finite array through open circuiting. Through our simulations, we show how the standing wave phenomenon is confirmed by the oscillations in spectral efficiency. We also demonstrate the capability of the LMMSE transmit beamformer in mitigating interference and minimizing the mean square error by adaptively allocating more power to the user experiencing the most severe channel attenuation, resulting in a more balanced variation of achievable rates across users.

title: A Fused Attention-Guided Neuro-Reasoning Framework for Explainable Brain Tumor Classification from Multi-View MRI
authors: K Naga Maha Lakshmi, Durgesh Nandan
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Recent Trends in Electrical, Electronics and Computing Technologies (ICRTEECT)
abstract: Magnetic resonance imaging (MRI) is central to glioma grading, yet manual interpretation is time-consuming and subject to inter-reader variability. This work presents FANR (Fused Attention-Guided Neuro-Reasoning), an explainable dual-view framework that integrates image evidence from axial and coronal MRI slices with lightweight, clinically motivated reasoning. FANR comprises two ResNet-18 branches for view-specific feature extraction, a multi-head self-attention module supervised by radiologist-defined regions of interest when available, to promote spatial focus on tumor evidence, and a differentiable neuro-symbolic layer that encodes fuzzy diagnostic cues (rim enhancement, edema, necrosis) with learnable rule weights. Training optimizes a composite objective that jointly enforces classification accuracy, attention consistency, and rule agreement. We evaluate on BraTS-2018 using five-fold stratified cross-validation across multiple random seeds and report performance as mean ± 95% confidence intervals. FANR is benchmarked against strong and fairly configured baselines—axial-only CNN, dual-view CNN, and a ViT-Hybrid constructed from ResNet-18 feature grids—and all confusion matrices and fold partitions are released for transparency. Beyond standard metrics, interpretability is quantified through attention-to-ROI overlap (Dice/IoU on matched grids) and dual-view Grad-CAM overlays that align with radiologic findings. To assess scalability, we additionally studied a weak-supervision regime without expert ROIs, demonstrating graceful degradation while preserving clinically meaningful localization. Finally, we profile computational requirements (parameter count and batch-1 GPU/CPU latency) to inform deployment. Collectively, the results indicate that FANR delivers accurate and transparent glioma grading on BraTS-2018 while addressing key concerns for clinical adoption.

title: Three-Dimensional Radio Localization: A Channel Charting-Based Approach
authors: Phillip Stephan, Florian Euchner, Stephan Ten Brink
publish: 2025 59th Asilomar Conference on Signals, Systems, and Computers
abstract: Channel charting creates a low-dimensional representation of the radio environment in a self-supervised manner using manifold learning. Preserving relative spatial distances in the latent space, channel charting is well suited to support user localization. While prior work on channel charting has mainly focused on two-dimensional scenarios, real-world environments are inherently three-dimensional. In this work, we investigate two distinct three-dimensional indoor localization scenarios using simulated, but realistic ray tracing-based datasets: a factory hall with a three-dimensional spatial distribution of datapoints, and a multistory building where each floor exhibits a two-dimensional datapoint distribution. For the first scenario, we apply the concept of augmented channel charting, which combines classical localization and channel charting, to a three-dimensional setting. For the second scenario, we introduce multistory channel charting, a two-stage approach consisting of floor classification via clustering followed by the training of a dedicated expert neural network for channel charting on each individual floor, thereby enhancing the channel charting performance. In addition, we propose a novel feature engineering method designed to extract sparse features from the beamspace channel state information that are suitable for localization.

title: A Comparative Study of Deep Learning Architectures for Multivariate Time Series Anomaly Detection in Industrial Control Systems
authors: Shubha Dhali, Kuntal Ghosh, Siddhartha Prakash Duttagupta
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Emerging Trends in Computing and Communication (ETCOM)
abstract: The main goal of multivariate time series anomaly detection is to identify unusual patterns or deviations in data collected from multiple sensors and systems that monitor critical infrastructure. This approach captures both temporal relationships and correlations among different variables. In this study, we present a data-driven model that computes individual sensor-level anomaly scores to pinpoint faulty or rogue sensors for system monitoring. At each time step, the reconstruction error for every sensor is converted into a normalized anomaly score, followed by sensor-specific thresholding to detect abnormal behavior. These sensor-wise scores are then aggregated to produce a global anomaly score, reflecting the overall system health at that moment. Our framework explores several architectures, including LSTM, GRU, BiLSTM, and BiGRU, trained and tested on the SWaT dataset. We further enhance the BiLSTM-VAE and BiGRU-VAE models with a temporal attention mechanism to better capture complex temporal dependencies and intersensor correlations. Experimental results show that the BiLSTMVAE with attention achieves the best performance, reaching a precision of 91.31 %, effectively reducing the performance overestimation often observed in traditional time series anomaly detection methods.

title: Current Transients during Grid Synchronization of a Voltage Source Converter
authors: Prabath Binduhewa, Sandun Kuruppu
publish: 2025 IEEE 19th International Conference on Industrial and Information Systems (ICIIS)
abstract: The presence of power electronic converters in power systems is on the rise due to the integration of renewable energy sources and energy storage systems. Voltage Source Converters (VSCs) are typically used to interface these renewable energy sources or energy storage systems at the Point of Common Coupling (PCC). Grid synchronization is an essential aspect of a grid-connected VSC, and seamless grid connection can safeguard the components of the power electronic system. This paper evaluates the effect of the initial condition of the controller and the bandwidth of the controller. Both PLEC-based simulation results and hardware-based experimental results are presented in this paper. The simulation and experimental results validated that higher bandwidth and selecting a suitable initial condition can reduce the transient current during grid synchronization.

title: Pneumonia Prediction based on Lung Sound using Convolutional Neural Network
authors: Asrul Sani, Zein Hanni Pradana, Dodi Zulherman
publish: 2025 IEEE Asia-Pacific Conference on Geoscience, Electronics and Remote Sensing Technology (AGERS)
abstract: Pneumonia remains a major cause of global morbidity and mortality, making early detection essential to prevent severe complications. Auscultation is a low-cost, fast, and non-invasive method commonly used in clinical practice. However, its diagnostic accuracy is limited and highly dependent on the clinician’s experience, posing challenges for consistent and scalable implementation. This study aims to develop a deep learning-based approach to classify lung sounds using Convolutional Neural Networks (CNN) with MelSpectrogram representations as input. Lung sound recordings were sourced from open-access databases in.wav format and transformed into 128×128-pixel Mel-Spectrogram images, categorized into normal and pneumonia classes. Three pretrained CNN archi-tectures, VGG16, ResNet50, and MobileNetV2, were evaluated to identify the most effective model. Based on experimental results, ResNet50 achieved the highest performance with 97.99% validation accuracy. VGG16 followed closely with 96.79% valida-tion accuracy, while MobileNetV2 underperformed with 83. 94%. These findings indicate that CNN-based models, particularly ResNet50 and VGG16, can effectively classify lung sounds and offer a promising solution for intelligent, non-invasive pneumonia detection. This approach could enhance diagnostic capabilities in resource-limited settings and support more accessible early screening strategies. Additionally, the spectral feature extraction framework employed in this study shares methodological similarities with remote sensing image analysis, establishing a conceptual bridge between pneumonia prediction and remote sensing applications.

title: AI and Blockchain Integration for Improving Transparency in Supply Chain Networks
authors: Mukul Mishra, Md Shoukot Uzzaman, Neeraj Das, Shikhar Gupta, Prasant Kumar Sahoo, Zhang Haixia
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Smart Innovations in Systems, Infrastructure, Mechanical, Power, AI and Computing Technologies (SISIMPACT)
abstract: The contemporary supply chains are more global, data-driven, and complex, which poses a great dilemma in having transparency, traceability, and trust between all stakeholders. Conventional systems are typically characterized by central databases, a high level of manual processes which makes them vulnerable to inefficiencies, fraud, and manipulation of data. The implementation of AI and Blockchain is the solution to these problems and it can be transformative singularity. Blockchain makes the time-sequenced records of transactions immutable, decentralized and provides real-time traceability of goods, whereas AI makes analysis of large-scale supply chain data to anticipate risks, optimize supply chain and detect omissions possible through smart analysis. Collectively, the technologies help to create a non-tamperable, self-learning world that increases transparency, efficiency and instils consumer trust in manufacturers, suppliers, or logistics providers. The solution to the problem offered in the current study is a combination of smart contracts, distributed ledgers, and machine learning predictions that would help to fill these loopholes by maximizing both the visibility and accountability of the supply chains. To test the framework of integration proposed, the main performance indicators are measured that show the increased accuracy of decisions, verification in realtime, and minimized inconsistencies in the stored data in various distributed systems. The suggested system exhibits a better performance and performed better than any of the models with <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{9 4. 1 \%}$</tex> DTR and 92.8% CSR on historical analysis.

title: Outer Space Experiences in Virtual Reality: Towards Diffusion Models for Extraterrestrial Terrain Reconstruction
authors: Giuseppe Lorenzo Catalano, Agata Marta Soccini
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and eXtended and Virtual Reality (AIxVR)
abstract: Virtual Reality representations of distant planets, stars, and galaxies are a driving factor for research, scientific dissemination, and public outreach. Immersive environments can be used to train astronauts, plan future missions, or study planetary features. Moreover, the dissemination of these endeavors through exhibits in museums and planetariums makes it possible to effectively communicate the latest research findings to a wider audience. To correctly visualize outer space, in particular the surfaces of celestial bodies, virtual environments must rely on scientifically accurate datasets derived from telescopes and spacecraft, which often present missing or degraded values. The current work analyzes the processing pipeline for extraterrestrial terrain data representation in VR, highlighting the most frequent errors that result in data degradation, along with their negative impacts on visualization and usability. The current work presents an approach for restoring missing terrain data using generative diffusion models. Results show visually consistent 3D reconstructions, suitable for representation in Virtual Reality, also supported by positive preliminary quantitative assessments (39.4495 in PSNR and 0.9660 in SSIM).

title: Refining Datapath for Microscaling ViTs
authors: Can Xiao, Jianyi Cheng, Yiren Zhao
publish: 2025 35th International Conference on Field-Programmable Logic and Applications (FPL)
abstract: Vision Transformers (ViTs) leverage the transformer architecture to effectively capture global context, demonstrating strong performance in computer vision tasks. A major challenge in ViT hardware acceleration is that the model family contains complex arithmetic operations that are sensitive to model accuracy, such as the Softmax and LayerNorm operations, which cannot be mapped onto efficient hardware with low precision. Existing methods only exploit parallelism in the matrix multiplication operations of the model on hardware and keep these complex operations on the CPU. This results in suboptimal performance due to the communication overhead between the CPU and accelerator. Can new data formats solve this problem? In this work, we present the first open-source ViT accelerator that maps all operations of the ViT models onto FPGAs. We exploit a new arithmetic format named Microscaling Integer (MXInt) for datapath designs and evaluate how different design choices can be made to trade off accuracy, hardware performance, and hardware utilization. Our contributions are twofold. First, we quantize ViTs using the MXInt format, achieving both high area efficiency and accuracy. Second, we propose MXInt-specific hardware optimization that map these complex arithmetic operations into custom hardware. Within 1 % accuracy loss, our method achieves at least <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$93 \times$</tex> speedup compared to Float16 and at least <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$1.9 \times$</tex> speedup compared to related work.
