title: How Physicality Enables Cy-Trust: A New Era of Trust-Centered Cyber–Physical Systems
authors: Stephanie Gil, Michal Yemini, Arsenia Chorti, Angelia Nedić, H. Vincent Poor, Andrea J. Goldsmith
abstract: Cyber–physical multiagent systems are driving rapid technological advancements that automate a wide range of critical functions, thereby enabling safer, more accessible, and more efficient autonomous operations across diverse sectors. We refer to the capability of such systems to self-organize and coordinate toward accomplishing shared objectives as autonomy. The unique characteristics of these systems prompt a reevaluation of their security concepts, including their vulnerabilities, and mechanisms to mitigate these vulnerabilities. This survey article examines how advancements in wireless networking, coupled with sensing and computing capabilities, can foster novel security concepts for autonomous cyber–physical systems (CPSs). It delves into three main themes related to securing multiagent CPSs. First, we discuss the threats that are particularly relevant to multiagent CPSs, given the potential lack of trustworthiness between agents. Second, we present prospects for sensing, contextual awareness, and authentication, enabling the inference and measurement of a form of interagent “quantitative trust” or “cy-trust” for these systems. Third, we elaborate on the application of quantifiable trust notions to enable “resilient coordination,” where “resilient” signifies sustained functionality amid attacks on multiagent CPSs. This survey unveils the cyber–physical character of future interconnected systems as a pivotal catalyst for realizing robust autonomy.

title: Machine Learning-Based Upscalling Model For Aboveground Carbon Density Using Multi-Sensor Imagery
authors: Norhakim Bin Yusof, Muhammad Zulkarnain Bin Abd Rahman, Malcom Anak Demies, Mohamad Hidayatullah Bin Mohamad, Hamdan Bin Omar, Alvin Lau Meng Shin, Wan Hazli Bin Wan Kadir, Mohd Radhie Bin Mohd Salleh, Omar Farouk Bin Fauzi, Muhammad Safwan Bin Ruslan
publish: 2025 IEEE Asia-Pacific Conference on Geoscience, Electronics and Remote Sensing Technology (AGERS)
abstract: Traditional Forest biomass and carbon stock assessments rely on manual, plot-specific measurements of individual trees, which can be labor-intensive and limited in spatial coverage. Recent advancements in LiDAR technology enable detailed forest structure characterization through dense point clouds obtained via airborne and terrestrial platforms. This study employed LiDAR-based methods to measure individual tree metrics within forest inventory plots located in Lambir Hills National Park, a mixed dipterocarp forest. Plots were systematically established, with trees tagged and their dimensions measured against manually recorded data for validation. LiDARderived measurements facilitated estimation of individual tree aboveground biomass and carbon density, which were then validated against manual measurements, demonstrating the reliability of LiDAR in forest structure assessment. The biomass and carbon estimates derived from LiDAR data served as training targets for multi-sensor data integration, incorporating Sentinel-1 microwave and Sentinel-2 optical imagery. Several machine learning algorithms comprise of Gradient Boosting Regression, Random Forest, Support Vector Regression, and Decision Tree were employed to upscale plot-level estimates across the study area using remotely sensed features. The resulting spatial model produced high-resolution maps of Aboveground Carbon Density (ACD), with an average value of $127.73 \mathrm{MgC} / \mathrm{ha}$ across the monitored region. This approach provides a robust, scalable method for estimating forest carbon stocks, serving as a vital baseline for carbon sequestration assessment and informed conservation strategies within tropical forest ecosystems.

title: Hybrid Reinforcement Learning–Bayesian Framework for Consumer Behaviour Analysis and Sales Forecasting in E-Commerce
authors: Srividhya S, Arul Mary Rexy V, P. Ravisankar, M. Nandha Kumar, Chinniah Anbalagan, E. Daniel Santhosh Raj
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Emerging Trends in Computing and Communication (ETCOM)
abstract: Precisely forecasting consumer behaviour and sales in e-commerce presents a considerable difficulty due to fluctuating demand patterns, high-dimensional data, and evolving market conditions. Conventional statistical models and deep learning techniques frequently struggle to adjust to changing consumer preferences and lack comprehensive uncertainty quantification. This paper presents an innovative hybrid reinforcement learning-Bayesian framework that integrates adaptive policy optimisation with probabilistic modelling for demand forecasting and behavioural analysis to address these difficulties. Reinforcement learning facilitates dynamic adaptation to changing consumer behaviours, while Bayesian inference accounts for predicted uncertainty, thereby assuring more reliable and interpretable results. The system demonstrates significant performance improvements over existing baselines, including ARIMA, Prophet, LSTM, and NBEATS, utilising curated Kaggle datasets. Experimental findings indicate enhanced accuracy, with a Root Mean Square Error (RMSE) of 0.84, a Mean Absolute Percentage Error (MAPE) of 3.9 %, and a Continuous Ranked Probability Score (CRPS) of 0.12. Behavioural modelling attains enhanced customer engagement, evidenced by a click-through rate of 21.3 %, a conversion rate of 12.5 %, and a cumulative reward of 458.7. These enhancements underscore the system's capacity to optimise inventories, enhance personalisation, and reduce risks related to data drift. The proposed research offers a scalable, robust, and interpretable framework that enhances state-of-theart forecasting and has practical consequences for real-world ecommerce applications.

title: ANFIS-Based Thermal Image Analysis for Leakage Current Prediction of HV Polymer Insulators under Humidity Variation
authors: Darwison, Ariadi Hazmi, Aulia, Novizon, Firman Ridwan, Mohd. Hafizi Ahmad
publish: 2025 IEEE Asia-Pacific Conference on Geoscience, Electronics and Remote Sensing Technology (AGERS)
abstract: This paper presents an Adaptive Neuro-Fuzzy Inference System (ANFIS)-based approach for predicting leakage current in 20 kV polymeric insulators using thermal imaging under high-humidity conditions. The method integrates image processing and intelligent modeling to establish a nonlinear correlation between surface temperature distribution and electrical stress. Thermal images of lightly contaminated polymer insulators were acquired using a FLIR A600 infrared thermograph under controlled conditions, in accordance with IEC 60507. Relative humidity was maintained above 90%, while the ambient temperature was set to $27^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$ to simulate surface stress. Red, yellow, and blue (R-Y-B) pixel ratios were extracted from thermal images and used as inputs to the ANFIS. The trained model achieved high prediction accuracy, with a root mean square error (RMSE) of 0.000114855 mA and coefficient of determination ($\mathrm{R}^{2}\gt0.99$), with average prediction errors below 1%. The results demonstrate that combining thermal imaging with ANFIS enables a reliable, non-contact method for real-time condition monitoring of high-voltage polymeric insulators.

title: Spiking Global Coherence Estimation
authors: John Tauber, Emily P. Stephen
publish: 2025 59th Asilomar Conference on Signals, Systems, and Computers
abstract: Neural oscillations are believed to coordinate activity across and within brain regions, motivating efforts to measure synchronization in multivariate recordings. For continuous-valued signals, coherence and its multivariate extension—global coherence—are well established methods for quantifying oscillatory coupling. However, extending these tools to spiking data remains challenging due to the binary nature of spike trains. We present a statistical framework for estimating cross-spectra and global coherence from multivariate spiking data. Spikes are modeled as a multivariate Poisson point process driven by a latent oscillatory signal represented in the frequency domain as a multivariate complex Gaussian. We develop an expectation-maximization algorithm with Laplace approximation to estimate the cross-spectral matrix. Simulations demonstrate that our method produces accurate estimates of the cross-spectrum, unlike naive estimators which suffer from rate-dependent bias. Performance improves with increasing neuron count and firing rate, and closely approaches oracle estimates under favorable conditions. We also apply the method to neural recordings under ketamine/xylazine anesthesia, revealing coherent low-frequency activity consistent with known slow-wave dynamics.

title: Compensation of correlated autoregressive clock jitter in arrays of Analog-to-Digital Converters
authors: Daniele Gerosa, Lauri Anttila, Thomas Eriksson
publish: 2025 59th Asilomar Conference on Signals, Systems, and Computers
abstract: In modern communication systems, the fidelity of analog-to-digital converters (ADCs) is limited by sampling clock jitter, i.e., small random timing deviations that undermine ideal sampling. Traditional scalar models often treat jitter as independent Gaussian noise, which makes it essentially untrackable, whereas real ADCs also exhibit temporally correlated (spectrally colored) imperfections. Moreover, spatial cross-correlations between channels in multiple-input multiple-output (MIMO) ADCs are commonly neglected. This paper addresses the joint tracking and compensation of random, cross-correlated timing errors in ADC arrays by modeling jitter as a coupled vector autoregressive process of order one (VAR(1)). We propose a pilot-tone-based Kalman smoother to track and compensate the jitter, and simulations demonstrate substantial reductions in jitter-induced distortion across diverse scenarios.

title: A Self-Refining Multi-Layer Receiver Pipeline
authors: Shahab Hamidi-Rad, Akshay Malhotra
publish: 2025 59th Asilomar Conference on Signals, Systems, and Computers
abstract: In the 5G/NR standard, DeModulation Reference Signals (DMRS) are used for channel estimation and equalization. However, their performance is tightly coupled with resource allocation: increasing DMRS resources improves estimation quality but reduces the capacity available for user data, leading to a lower overall throughput. To address this tradeoff, this paper proposes a self-refining receiver pipeline that leverages information from successfully decoded code blocks to iteratively enhance channel estimation, thereby improving equalization and reducing block errors. In addition, a novel deep learning–based multi-layer channel estimation method is introduced, which integrates decoded code-block information with DMRS reference signals to further improve estimation accuracy. We evaluate the efficacy of this approach using an end-to-end 3GPP-compliant communication pipeline. The results demonstrate a significant reduction in the Block Error Rate (BLER), showing performance that approaches the theoretical throughput achieved with perfect channel knowledge.

title: An Intelligent Cooperative Interference Method for Networked Radars
authors: Haijun Wang, Mengni Wang, Jiang Zhu, Haiye Liu, Huanyao Dai, Yangchao Cai, Yang Zhao
publish: 2025 International Conference on Big Data and Data Mining (BDDM)
abstract: Faced with the issues of singular and limited interference effects in existing networked radar target detection, this paper proposes a genetic algorithm-based method for cooperative suppression jamming. Initially, an intelligent cooperative interference objective function is established under the condition of limited interference resources. Subsequently, we designed a clever noise interference signal using non-uniform sampling frequency modulation forwarding, featuring both suppression and deceptive interference properties. Finally, based on this interference signal style, a cooperative suppression interference method using an improved genetic algorithm is presented, which intelligently designs key parameters such as interference beams, power, and styles. Simulation results show that the method proposed effectively suppresses interference within the networked radar system, limiting the comprehensive detection probability of breakthrough targets to no more than 81.20%, thereby effectively covering our forces during target breakthroughs.The abstract should be suitable for direct inclusion in abstracting services as a self-contained article.

title: A Domain-Specific Chatbot for Technical and Soft Skills Interview Preparation
authors: D.Chandra Shekhar, Reshma Sappa, Vaishnavi Sankuri, Aishwarya Prasadam
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Recent Trends in Electrical, Electronics and Computing Technologies (ICRTEECT)
abstract: In today’s competitive job market, preparing for interviews is often a stressful challenge, especially for Electronics and Communication Engineering (ECE) students who lack access to interactive and domain-specific practice tools. This paper introduces a voice-based interview preparation system designed to simulate real interview scenarios using speech recognition, natural language processing, and machine learning techniques. The system features a structured question bank categorized by topic, mode, and difficulty, enabling users to engage in targeted practice. Implemented using Python, the platform incorporates Streamlit for the user interface, speech_recognition for voice input, and an SVM-based evaluation engine to provide automated feedback on user responses. The tool supports both technical and soft skills interviews, covering areas such as programming, data structures, digital electronics, communication systems, and behavioral questions. Optional learning aids, including ECE-specific formulas and curated resources, enhance the learning experience. The system runs offline and offers a personalized, accessible solution for students to build confidence and improve interview performance.

title: SCoTT: Strategic Chain-of-Thought Tasking for Wireless-Aware Robot Navigation in Digital Twins
authors: Aladin Djuhera, Amin Seffo, Vlad C. Andrei, Holger Boche, Walid Saad
publish: 2025 59th Asilomar Conference on Signals, Systems, and Computers
abstract: Path planning under wireless performance constraints is a complex challenge. However, naively incorporating such constraints into classical algorithms often incurs prohibitive search costs. We propose SCoTT, a wireless-aware path planning framework that leverages vision-language models (VLMs) to co-optimize average path gains and trajectory length using wireless heatmaps and ray tracing data from a digital twin (DT). At its core is Strategic Chain-of-Thought Tasking (SCoTT), a novel prompting paradigm that decomposes the exhaustive search problem into manageable subtasks. As baselines, we compare A* and wireless-aware extensions of it, and derive DP-WA*, an optimal dynamic programming algorithm. In extensive experiments, we show that SCoTT achieves path gains within 2% of DP-WA* while consistently generating shorter trajectories. Moreover, SCoTT’s intermediate outputs can be used to accelerate DP-WA* by reducing its search space, saving up to 62% in execution time. We validate SCoTT using four VLMs, demonstrating effectiveness across large and small models, making it applicable to compact VLMs at low inference cost. We also show its practical viability by deploying SCoTT as a ROS node within Gazebo simulations.. Finally, we discuss data-acquisition pipelines, compute requirements, and deployment considerations in 6G-enabled DTs.

title: Smart Farming: IFTTT-Based Early Rain Alerts to Help Farmers for Optimize Pesticide Use and Minimize Costs
authors: Ome Nerella, Lalitha Sammeta, Someswara Rao Gaddala, Koppula Manasa, Syed Musthak Ahmed, Sriram Kumar P
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Recent Trends in Electrical, Electronics and Computing Technologies (ICRTEECT)
abstract: The integration of technology into agriculture has the potential to greatly improve productivity and resource management. This paper describes an innovative approach that uses the IFTTT (If This Then That) cloud platform to create an Early Rain Alert system that will help farmers make educated decisions about pesticide applications. This approach uses real-time weather data to alert farmers to approaching rainfall, minimizing wasted spending on insecticides that would be washed away by rain. Farmers may optimize pesticide usage and save financial losses by automating warnings with IFTTT (If This Then That) Cloud platform and receiving timely notifications via their preferred communication channels. This proactive strategy not only supports sustainable farming methods, but it also helps to protect the environment by lowering chemical runoff. Implementing this approach can result in enhanced crop health, increased farmer profitability, and a more sustainable agricultural ecosystem.

title: Real-Time Voice-Based LLM Integration for XR Tutoring: A Prototype Implementation
authors: Ali Geris, Günter Alce
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and eXtended and Virtual Reality (AIxVR)
abstract: This paper presents a lightweight architecture for AI-driven tutoring in extended reality (XR) environments, integrating OpenAI's GPT-4o real-time API directly into Unitybased virtual reality (VR) to enable seamless voice-native interaction without external speech modules. Designed for taskbased learning, the tutor provides immediate, context-sensitive verbal feedback with adaptive brevity and low latency. A blockbased programming task demonstrates real-time evaluation, where user-assembled logic structures are analyzed and corrected through spoken responses. Across ten structured sessions and forty voice interactions, the prototype achieved consistent sub500 ms latency, generated under four minutes of total speech, and averaged $0.005 per turn. These results demonstrate the practical feasibility of low-overhead, real-time AI tutoring in XR and provide a foundation for future multimodal, context-aware learning environments.

title: Real-Time Fault Detection and Reconstruction for Autonomous Vehicles: A KPCA Digital Twin Approach
authors: Romdhane Nasri, Majdi Mansouri, Zouhaier Affi, Vicenc Puig
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Unmanned Vehicle Systems-Oman (UVS)
abstract: Real-time fault detection and accurate signal reconstruction are critical for ensuring safety and reliability in autonomous vehicles. This paper presents a novel approach that integrates Kernel Principal Component Analysis (KPCA) with digital twin technology to achieve unified fault detection and real-time reconstruction capabilities. The proposed KPCA digital twin framework leverages kernel-based nonlinear dimensionality reduction to detect complex fault patterns in high-dimensional sensor and actuator data, achieving 98.2% detection accuracy with response times of 2.1 ms. Adaptive Kalman filtering integrated within the digital twin architecture enables continuous fault signal reconstruction with 87.3% reconstruction quality, providing bounded reconstruction errors without system interruption. The framework employs multi-modal detection logic combining Hotelling’s T statistic, Squared Prediction Error (SPE), and digital twin confidence metrics to ensure robust fault identification across diverse scenarios. Experimental validation across sensor bias, sensor drift, and actuator fault scenarios demonstrates superior performance compared to fourteen state-of-the-art fault detection methods, with reconstruction quality improvements of 90.5% for velocity, 91.7% for lateral position, and 81.1% for angular measurements.

title: Energy Efficient Secure Routing in Wireless Sensor Networks Using SCES AOMDV Protocol
authors: Rashi Goswami, P Babu, Preeti Rathi, Niraj Kushwaha, Himanshu Yogi, Rahul Nandi
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Smart Innovations in Systems, Infrastructure, Mechanical, Power, AI and Computing Technologies (SISIMPACT)
abstract: This study presents the SCES-AOMDV protocol to make WSNs more energy-efficient and secure through network security improvements. The protocol combines communication levels from physical to transport to fight cross-layer attacks while making packet delivery ratio (PDR) and network lifetime better while saving energy. Our refined method verifies OneTime Password authentication during route discovery to defend the network from malicious nodes. To enhance node location precision the Hybrid Tunicate Weighted Salp Swarm Algorithm (HyTWSSA) is suggested for sensor network localization through focused and thorough search. The energy supplement employs wireless power transfer technology to keep critical nodes operational during reduced energy supplies. Our innovation establishes an updated sleep-wake system to protect network resources from TCP SYN attacks while keeping idle nodes in a power-saving state. Our simulations prove SCESAOMDV beats ES-AOMDV and crosslayer_RSA by delivering higher performance and less network delay while using less power under several security attacks. Our findings show that future development needs to address how this system could work better with moving devices.

title: Estimating the Remaining Useful Life of the Ubolratana Reservoir, Thailand, by Using Landsat Time-Series Data and the Google Earth Engine Platform
authors: Jurawan Nontapon, Neti Srihanu, Phailin Kummuang, Anujit Phumiphan, Niwat Bhumiphan, Umesh Bhurtyal, Siwa Kaewplang
publish: 2025 IEEE Asia-Pacific Conference on Geoscience, Electronics and Remote Sensing Technology (AGERS)
abstract: Reservoirs worldwide are rapidly losing storage capacity due to sedimentation and climatic variability, underscoring the need for accurate, long-term monitoring. This study assesses multi-decadal storage decline in the Ubolratana Reservoir, Northeastern Thailand, by integrating daily waterlevel data (1990-2025) with Landsat surface-reflectance imagery (Landsat 5, 7, 8, and 9) processed on Google Earth Engine. Water surfaces were extracted using mNDWI with Otsu adaptive thresholding and correlated with in situ water levels to establish stage-area and stage-volume relationships using trapezoidal integration. Uncertainty was quantified through mNDWI threshold testing, inter-sensor harmonization, and a $\mathbf{1 0, 0 0 0}$-iteration Monte Carlo simulation $(\pm \mathbf{5 \%}$ area perturbation). Results indicate an approximately 20% decline in effective storage since commissioning, from the 1965 designderived capacity of $\mathbf{2, 2 6 4 ~ M C M}$ to about $\mathbf{1, 8 1 5 ~ M C M}$. Total volumetric uncertainty is low ($\approx 1-1.2 \%$), confirming the robustness of the satellite-based approach. Land-use analysis (1985-2022) shows significant pre-2000 deforestation, likely accelerating sediment delivery. An exponential decay model suggests that, without intervention, the reservoir could lose up to 80% of its original capacity by $\sim 2190$. The framework demonstrates the value of long-term satellite archives for reservoir monitoring and supports evidence-based sedimentmanagement planning in data-scarce regions.

title: Deep Fuzzy Clustering Network: A Penalized Maximum Likelihood Estimation Method for Representation Learning and Clustering
authors: Yi Pan, Chao Zhang, Yingying Cheng, Yan Li, Zhongyang Zhou, Feiyu Chen
publish: 2025 International Conference on Big Data and Data Mining (BDDM)
abstract: Fuzzy clustering provides soft partitioning of data but often struggles with high-dimensional complex distributions. To address this challenge, we propose a deep fuzzy clustering framework that models both feature representations and cluster assignments as latent variables within a penalized maximum likelihood formulation. A variational lower bound(LBO) is derived using Bayesian inference, yielding an objective that jointly optimizes representation learning and soft clustering. To enhance clustering effectiveness, a penalty term is introduced to the likelihood, resulting in a refined objective function composed of three components: negative reconstruction error, negative intra-class variance, and mutual information. The model is implemented under an expectation-maximization framework, where fuzzy Cmeans is used to estimate soft cluster assignments in the E-step, and the LBO is optimized in the M-step. To improve convergence stability and avoid overfitting, an incomplete autoencoder that omits the decoder is adopted. Extensive experiments on multiple public datasets demonstrate that the proposed method achieves superior clustering performance compared with traditional and deep clustering baselines. The results validate the effectiveness of integrating fuzzy logic with deep latent modeling in an end-to-end learning paradigm.

title: Asymptotic Estimation and Detection of a Multi-Harmonic Signal in Stationary Gaussian Noise
authors: D. Guger, J. Zschetzsche, S. Schertler, S. Schuster, O. Lang, A. Haberl, B. Lehner, S. Scheiblhofer, C. Staudinger, J. Reisinger, M. Huemer
publish: 2025 59th Asilomar Conference on Signals, Systems, and Computers
abstract: Extracting information about multi-harmonic signals from noisy measurements is central to many signal processing applications, such as audio analysis, periodic fault detection, and pulsar identification. While white Gaussian noise (WGN) is often assumed due to its mathematical tractability, colored noise may better represent physical reality. However, incorporating sophisticated noise characteristics into estimation and detection procedures can substantially increase computational cost. This paper introduces asymptotic expressions for the maximum likelihood (ML) estimator and the generalized likelihood ratio test (GLRT) for multi-harmonic signal parameters in stationary colored Gaussian noise, that allow efficient implementation using the fast Fourier transform (FFT). Furthermore, the asymptotic Cramer-Rao lower bound (CRLB) for the fundamental frequency is derived. Finally, Monte Carlo simulations for the colored noise case demonstrate the significantly improved threshold level of the ML fundamental frequency estimator, along with the superior detection performance of the GLRT, in comparison to their counterparts assuming WGN.

title: Hybrid CNN-Transformer Framework for Accurate and Interpretable Kidney Cancer Detection
authors: Ramachandra, Naveen Kumar H N, Padma Prasada, Amith K Jain, Avinash S, Keerthana P Malode
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Emerging Trends in Computing and Communication (ETCOM)
abstract: Kidney cancer, and specifically renal cell carcinoma (RCC), is a foremost health issue in the world, and it is estimated to cause about <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{2 - 3}$</tex> percent of the adult malignancies. It is very important to diagnose the disease early and reliably since the rate of survival is more than 90 percent at localized stages but less than 12 percent at the metastatic stages. Traditional diagnostic testing methods that use manual method of analysis of CT and MRI scans are constrained by inter-observer variability, exhaustion and inability to outline complex tumor borders. Recent developments of deep learning have been promising, but the convolutional neural networks (CNNs) can inherently be limited to local receptive fields whereas Vision Transformers (ViTs) need big datasets and do not usually produce fine-grained tumor images. This paper presents a Hybrid CNN-Transformer architecture based on the characteristics of CNN that can extract localized tumor features and the strength of the Transformer to capture long-range contextual relationships. On the KiTS19 dataset we used a large amount of preprocessing, ROI cropping and augmentation to enable a strong model training. The hybrid model improved the performance compared to a baseline CNN, with an accuracy of 94.8, sensitivity of 95.7 and AUC-ROC of 0.968. Moreover, Grad-CAM visualizations indicated that the predictions made relied on medically relevant areas of the tumor.

title: YOLOv11-Based Early Warning System for Violence and Weapon Detection
authors: Muhammad Syarif Januriansyah, Febriliyan Samopa
publish: 2025 International Conference on Innovation and Intelligence for Informatics, Computing, and Technologies (3ICT)
abstract: The increasing occurrence of physical violence and weapon misuse in public spaces has created a demand for automatic detection and early warning systems. Advances in deep learning have enabled surveillance systems to process video streams in real time, providing opportunities for early warning mechanisms. This study presents a YOLOv11-based early warning system that integrates with a PyQt5 graphical user interface and instant notifications via Telegram API. The dataset used in this study was collected from Kaggle, Roboflow Universe, and private sources, covering nine classes that include physical violence, firearms, melee weapons, and traditional Indonesian weapons such as celurit and keris. The system processes images and video streams to identify violent actions and multiple weapon types, delivering alerts to designated users within 1-2 seconds. Experimental results on a test dataset achieved an average precision of 90.1 %, recall of 81.5 %, and mAP of 87.9 %, with stable real-time performance at 35 FPS across different input formats. In addition to controlled testing, the system was also validated using CCTV footage to assess its applicability in practical surveillance conditions. These findings highlight the potential of combining object detection, a desktop GUI, and real-time communication into a unified tool for security monitoring. The proposed system demonstrates strong potential for practical surveillance applications, though further validation under diverse real-world conditions would enhance its robustness.

title: A Traffic Congestion Subarea Partitioning Method Based on Big Data Under Scale Road Network
authors: Yanfang Shou, Xiangpeng Zhou
publish: 2025 International Conference on Big Data and Data Mining (BDDM)
abstract: To enhance traffic control efficiency of largescale road networks under congested conditions, this paper proposes a traffic congestion subarea partitioning method based on traffic big data. First, by fusing multi-source traffic big data, a fine-grained traffic state sensing framework for the road network is established. Then, the overall congestion state of the network is identified using macroscopic fundamental diagrams (MFD), and a weighted directed graph model is constructed with link impedance as edge weights. Building upon this foundation, the Greedy Strategy Interpretive Structural Modeling (Greedy-ISM) approach is introduced to identify clusters of critical intersections by constructing a network reachability matrix. Furthermore, the parameters are adaptively tuned according to the standard deviation of link impedance within each subarea, thereby achieving adaptive subarea partitioning under congested conditions. An empirical study on a scale road network of Guangzhou demonstrated the feasibility and effectiveness of the proposed method for subarea partitioning under congestion.

title: IoT-Based Platform for Real-Time ECG Arrhythmia Detection Using Self-Supervised Learning with Masked Autoencoder
authors: Cantika Aurelya Putri Cahyanto, Satria Mandala
publish: 2025 IEEE Asia-Pacific Conference on Geoscience, Electronics and Remote Sensing Technology (AGERS)
abstract: Cardiovascular arrhythmias represent a significant contributor to global mortality, necessitating accurate real-time diagnostic solutions. Despite advances in deep learning, the scarcity of annotated electrocardiogram (ECG) data remains a critical barrier to robust clinical model development. This study presents an IoT-based web platform integrated with a SelfSupervised Learning (SSL) framework employing a transformerbased Masked Autoencoder (MAE) for automated arrhythmia detection and remote monitoring. The proposed system enables real-time ECG signal acquisition through an IoTconnected web interface, where the MAE model processes and classifies arrhythmias into predefined categories, bridging the gap between AI-driven detection and practical IoT deployment in telemedicine environments. The framework operates in two phases: unsupervised pre-training on masked ECG segments to learn temporal representations, followed by supervised finetuning for classification tasks. Experimental validation on the MIT-BIH Arrhythmia Database achieved 99.50% accuracy, 98.74% sensitivity, 98.74% F1-score, and 99.69% specificity, outperforming conventional supervised methods. The IoT-based web platform demonstrated 100% operational reliability during testing, successfully handling real-time ECG uploads, inference, and result visualization. These outcomes validate the proposed SSL-MAE model integrated with IoT infrastructure as a scalable and clinically viable solution for continuous arrhythmia monitoring and remote healthcare delivery.

title: AI-Driven Offshore Integrity Management: XGBoost Forecasting with Reinforcement Learning Scheduling
authors: Foyken Jones Johnson, Veena Raj, Iskander Petra, Emeroylariffion Abas
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Emerging Trends in Computing and Communication (ETCOM)
abstract: Offshore asset integrity is constrained by harsh marine conditions, restricted accessibility, and the high cost of mobilization, with corrosion remaining a significant threat to structural reliability. To address this, a standards-based predictive maintenance framework is proposed, integrating machine learning (ML) forecasting with reinforcement learning (RL)–based scheduling. A hybrid simulation, aligned with DNV-RP-G101 guidelines for risk-based inspection, was developed to generate wall-thickness degradation data across 10 monitoring points over a 10-year period, incorporating environmental variability and maintenance resets. Four ML models: Random Forest, Linear Regression, XGBoost, and Support Vector Machine, were benchmarked for six-month-ahead predictions. XGBoost achieved the highest accuracy, with mean absolute errors generally below 0.17 mm and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{R}^{\mathbf{2}}$</tex> values above 0.90, outperforming other models in capturing non-linear degradation patterns. Building on these forecasts, an RL agent optimized inspection scheduling, producing 342 decisions of which 73% were classified as Safe, 27% as Unnecessary, and none as Risky. The integrated XGBoost–RL framework therefore provides an auditable, standards-compliant workflow that links degradation monitoring to inspection scheduling. The study demonstrates that AI-driven, risk-aware approaches can significantly improve the efficiency, safety, and costeffectiveness of offshore corrosion management.

title: IoT-Enabled Smart Waste Bin with Real-Time Monitoring and Automated Waste Segregation
authors: Sangeetha K, Lakshmi Devi R, Supriya P, Nivetha A, Sanjana R
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Recent Trends in Electrical, Electronics and Computing Technologies (ICRTEECT)
abstract: As cities continue to grow, the amount of waste being produced is skyrocketing, putting a strain on existing waste management systems. To solve this problem, a new idea has been introduced: a smart bin that uses IoT technology to automatically sort waste. This bin can tell the difference between plastics and biodegradable materials, sorting them into separate compartments as soon as they’re thrown in. The smart bin uses advanced sensors to identify and classify the waste, ensuring it’s placed in the right section.Each compartment has ultrasonic sensors that keep track of how full it is, sending real-time updates. When the bin is nearly full, a GSM module sends an alert to the waste management team, so they can empty it before it overflows. This ensures the bins are emptied on time, reducing waste buildup in public spaces. The bin also includes a GPS module that tracks its location, helping waste collection vehicles plan the most efficient routes, saving both time and fuel.By using this smart bin, cities can better manage waste, making recycling easier and more effective. With real-time tracking and optimized collection routes, the system not only cuts down on operational costs but also helps reduce the environmental impact. This smart solution is a step forward in making urban waste management more sustainable, efficient, and eco-friendly.

title: A Hybrid K-means Clustering Algorithm for Interaction Optimization Reveals Imaging/Genomic Subtypes of Alzheimer's Disease
authors: Jiayu Chen, Armin Iraji, Youngjo Song, Rogers F. Silva, Jingyu Liu, Vince D. Calhoun
publish: 2025 59th Asilomar Conference on Signals, Systems, and Computers
abstract: Multiscale fusion of biological data is a crucial step in advancing our understanding of disease pathophysiology, enhancing disease stratification, and facilitating precise interventions. However, existing methods, focusing more on modeling linear additive relationships, are not optimal for precise characterization of interaction effects. In this work, we propose a novel hybrid clustering method to enhance subtyping and multiscale fusion by flexibly modeling linear effects and nonlinear interactions in the same learning process. Our simulations demonstrate the feasibility and strength of the proposed method, which we show outperforms K-means and clusterwise regression in clustering accuracy under various simulated scenarios. Applying the hybrid clustering method to 413 individuals with Alzheimer’s disease related dementia, we identified two subtypes that differed in genetic risk burden as well as neurocognitive associations, with the interaction effects consistently observed across five longitudinal time points.

title: Combining Wenner-Schlumberger and Vertical Electrical Sounding (VES) for Landslide Mitigation in Tieng Village, Kejajar, Central Java
authors: Nia Annisa Ferani Tanjung, Dasril Aldo, Marvel Sanjaya Setiawan, Christian Felix Saliman Sugiono, Maskhiyatus Shokhib, Abdul Hakim Prima Yuniarto
publish: 2025 IEEE Asia-Pacific Conference on Geoscience, Electronics and Remote Sensing Technology (AGERS)
abstract: Tieng Village in Kejajar District, Wonosobo Regency, Central Java, is highly prone to landslides due to its steep topography, high rainfall, and complex volcanic geology. This study aims to interpret subsurface resistivity data using a combination of Wenner-Schlumberger configuration and Vertical Electrical Sounding (VES) to identify potential slip planes and support landslide mitigation efforts. Geoelectric surveys were conducted across multiple lines, and data were processed using RES2DINV software to generate 1D and 2D resistivity models, calibrated against geological field observations and regional lithological references. The results revealed three to four dominant subsurface layers with resistivity ranges of $9.86-127 \Omega \mathrm{~m}$ (topsoil), $127-700 \Omega \mathrm{~m}$ (volcanic breccia), $527-3,850 \Omega \mathrm{~m}$ (andesite lava), and $\gt84 \Omega \mathrm{~m}$ (claystone). Slip planes were identified at the boundary between volcanic breccia and andesite lava, characterized by high resistivity contrast and impermeable zones that promote pore pressure accumulation. While resolution limitations exist in heterogeneous volcanic terrains, the interpreted models maintain consistency across survey lines. Technical recommendations-including retaining walls and subsurface drainage-are prioritized in transition zones at depths of 2-4 meters and resistivity thresholds $\gt700 \Omega \mathrm{~m}$ to prevent future landslides.

title: Beam Codebook Design for Full-Duplex Integrated Sensing and Communication Systems at mmWave
authors: Murat Bayraktar, Nuria González-Prelcic, Hao Chen, Charlie Jianzhong Zhang
publish: 2025 59th Asilomar Conference on Signals, Systems, and Computers
abstract: This paper investigates a full-duplex (FD) multiple-input multiple-output (MIMO) setting for integrated sensing and communication (ISAC), which enables simultaneous monostatic sensing and communication with a single base station (BS). We consider frequency bands that exhibit sparse propagation characteristics, such as mmWave bands. A key challenge for these systems is the design of hybrid precoders and combiners that facilitate ISAC functionalities while mitigating self-interference (SI) that is caused by concurrent transmission and reception. While prior work has focused on hybrid precoder and combiner design for FD ISAC with prior communication channel and target parameter knowledge, the problem of joint channel and target parameter estimation remains unexplored. We address this gap by designing SI-aware hybrid training precoders and combiners that form a beam codebook optimized for sparse channel estimation. Our design minimizes the mutual coherence, a key metric in compressed sensing, while effectively suppressing the SI to enable accurate joint estimation. We evaluate our approach in terms of estimation accuracy, as well as SI mitigation performance.

title: Techno-Economic Feasibility of Hydro-Floating Solar Complementary Operation: A Case Study For Sri Lanka
authors: Eranja Levanjith Noopehewa, Lidula Widanagama Arachchige, Upuli Jayatunga
publish: 2025 IEEE 19th International Conference on Industrial and Information Systems (ICIIS)
abstract: The complementary operation of floating solar photovoltaic with hydropower is a potential transformative breakthrough in renewable power integration. With the deployment of floating solar photovoltaics on existing hydropower reservoirs, it allows optimizing the utilization of resources. This study explores the potential placement of a floating solar photovoltaic system, evaluating the financial and technical feasibility as a case study for the Victoria hydropower station in Sri Lanka. Resources evaluation resulted in possibility of placing 93 MWp of floating solar capacity on the surface of the reservoir, which would result in generating 133.5 GWh of energy per year. By utilizing the existing grid infrastructure at the hydro power plant, this method serves as a cheaper solution than both the ground-mounted solar and independent floating solar plants. The analyzed comparative levelized cost of electricity data suggests preference for hybrid model over the ground mounted solar PV. Dynamic simulations validated the intact stability under complementary operation, and the additional energy security gained through the improved power sharing between solar and hydro resources. The complementary operation permits dominance of solar over daylight hours while allowing hydro resources to be stored for generation during the night peak hours. The results elaborate that the complementary operation of floating solar photovoltaic and hydropower has the potential in making the country’s energy transition economically sustainable, and the analysis does set up a strong case for the future large-scale implementation of hybrid floating solar photovoltaic-hydro systems.

title: Security Assessment of Text-based CAPTCHA System Against Automated Bot Attacks
authors: Jobsy Shaji, Shahana Gajala Qureshi, Sajjad Ahmed, Ajay Kumar Phulre
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Emerging Trends in Computing and Communication (ETCOM)
abstract: CAPTCHA is a basic part of web security, designed to distinguish human users from automated bots. A widely used form of this technology is the text-based CAPTCHA. This study presents a systematic assessment of text-based CAPTCHA variants simple numeric, distorted numeric, alphanumeric, and distorted alphanumeric against automated OCR-powered attacks. The experimental framework comprises a server-side CAPTCHA generator and a client-side attack bot using Selenium WebDriver and the Tesseract OCR engine. Performance is evaluated by success rate, character-level accuracy, and average solve time across 100 attempts per CAPTCHA type. Results show critical vulnerabilities, with the bot achieving a success rate of 61.00% against simple numeric and a 17.50% against the most complex distorted alphanumeric variant. These findings say that text-based CAPTCHAs are not an effective defense against current automated bots. The paper concludes that future CAPTCHA systems must be adaptive, robust, and behavior-based mechanisms for sustainable security.

title: Experimental Evaluation of Physics Equation Recovery Techniques at Low Sampling Rate
authors: Ayan Banerjee, Sandeep K.S. Gupta
publish: 2025 59th Asilomar Conference on Signals, Systems, and Computers
abstract: According to Nyquist-Shannon sampling theorem, a signal sampled at or above Nyquist rate can be accurately reconstructed from its samples. However, state-of-the-art (SOTA) sparse nonlinear regression (SR) methods such as SINDy-MPC or physics informed neural networks (PINN), for recovering underlying governing ordinary differential equations (i.e. equation recovery, EQR) have poor generalized reconstruction accuracy across forcing inputs, when data is sampled at Nyquist rate. In this paper, we evaluate the performance of SOTA equation recovery techniques such as EMILY, SINDy-MPC and PINN+SR for five simulation and three real world case studies under various sampling frequencies.

title: Enhancing Self-Learning Experiences: Multimedia Integration Topic Implementation in Flutter-Based Learning Assistance System
authors: Triana Fatmawati, Yan Watequlis Syaifudin, Akhmadheta Hafid Prasetyawan, Endah Septa Sintiya, Nobuo Funabiki, Andi Baso Kaswar, Pramana Yoga Saputra, Yuri Ariyanto, Rokhimatul Wakhidah
publish: 2025 International Conference on Innovation and Intelligence for Informatics, Computing, and Technologies (3ICT)
abstract: The rapid proliferation of smartphones, particularly Android devices, has intensified demand for developers proficient in cross-platform frameworks. Flutter has emerged as a leading solution, enabling high-performance iOS and Android apps from a single codebase, prompting its adoption in academic curricula. This study presents the implementation of a Multimedia Integration learning module within the Flutter Programming Learning Assistance System (FPLAS), a self-directed platform that merges structured instructional content with automated code verification. The innovation lies in the systematic integration of multimedia learning materials that include text, images, audio, and video with an automated test-driven development (TDD) environment specifically designed for Flutter. Learners develop a functional MediaPlayer application featuring animated splash screens and multimedia playback controls through a sequence of scaffolded tasks. An evaluation with 40 students showed that all participants completed the tasks successfully in the fourday time frame. Feedback was overwhelmingly positive, with students highlighting the clarity of guidance and effectiveness of the integrated resources, while also proposing enhancements to code-level explanations and instructional detail. The results demonstrate the module's feasibility and potential to support effective, engaging mobile development education.

title: Passive UAV Detection Based on Channel Estimation and Temporal Variation Network
authors: Jing Bai, Zhuo Zhang, Zhu Xiao, Huaji Zhou, Yongqiang Hei, Xiaohui Liu, Tong Li, Licheng Jiao
abstract: The increasing proliferation of unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs) poses significant challenges to airspace security, necessitating the development of effective detection technologies. Passive detection techniques, such as passive radar, offer key advantages including spectrum efficiency and covert operation. However, passive radars that rely on coherent integration are often computationally expensive and dependent on strong Doppler signatures, rendering them ineffective for detecting low-speed or hovering UAVs. To overcome these limitations, we explore the use of channel state information (CSI) time series to characterize UAV presence and propose a temporal variation network for detecting UAV states, including hovering conditions. Our method utilizes digital terrestrial multimedia broadcast (DTMB) signals, which have wide coverage and high transmission power. By capturing DTMB signals with a single receiver, we reduce the complexity of passive detection systems. First, we perform channel estimation on the received signal to obtain CSI, which is arranged in frame order to form a CSI time series. This enables the modeling of interference channels caused by UAVs. We then propose the Channel Estimation and Temporal Variation Network (CETVNet), which leverages an adaptive noise reduction module and a multi-period feature extraction module to process these series for passive UAV state detection. Finally, a real-world signal dataset is collected using a software-defined radio device to train and evaluate CETVNet. Experimental results demonstrate that CETVNet achieves superior performance compared to state-of-the-art methods.

title: Unsupervised AI-Based Morphological Analysis of Kidney Tissue from CT Imaging
authors: Adedolapo Abdulazeez Sokoya, Charlotte Maughan Jones, Mhd Saeed Sharif, Jessica Smart
publish: 2025 International Conference on Innovation and Intelligence for Informatics, Computing, and Technologies (3ICT)
abstract: This paper presents an unsupervised AI-based framework for morphological analysis of kidney tissue from high-resolution phase-contrast CT (XPCi) imaging. This study introduces a pioneering framework for comprehensive, labelfree analysis of kidney tissue using high-resolution PhaseContrast Computed Tomography (CT) images. By integrating classical morphological and texture descriptors with deep feature embeddings, the proposed hybrid pipeline enables robust assessment of renal size, shape, density, and fluid distribution. A dataset of 592 CT slices underwent meticulous preprocessing-featuring ring artifact suppression and targeted region masking-to optimize tissue representation. Dimensionality reduction via Principal Component Analysis (PCA) and t-distributed Stochastic Neighbor Embedding (tSNE) preceded unsupervised clustering using K-Means and Gaussian Mixture Models (GMM). The optimal clustering performance (silhouette score: 0.73) was achieved with <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{K}=2$</tex> using handcrafted features, while convolutional neural network (CNN) embeddings revealed additional, previously unrecognized tissue patterns. To our knowledge, this is the first framework to synergistically employ classical and deep unsupervised learning techniques for renal CT analysis. The findings highlight its promise as a scalable tool for early detection of renal abnormalities and tissue-level phenotypingwithout the need for labeled data.

title: HWOA-RNN: A Feedback-Aware Deep Optimization Model for Efficient Cloud Task Allocation
authors: Nihar Ranjan Sabat, Rashmi Ranjan Sahoo, Swarnalatha Radhakrishnan
publish: 2025 IEEE 19th International Conference on Industrial and Information Systems (ICIIS)
abstract: Cloud computing's fluctuating workloads and variations in resources present challenges for effective resource usage and energy management, demanding improved task scheduling and load balancing strategies. The Hybrid Whale Optimization Algorithm with Recurrent Neural Network (HWOA-RNN) approach has been proposed, incorporating the Enhanced Whale Optimization Algorithm (EWOA) for optimized task assignments and a Bidirectional Long Short-Term Memory (Bi-LSTM) network with attention for precise workload prediction, thereby improving flexibility in dynamic cloud environments. Simulations carried out in CloudSim Plus on a high-performance system, modelling a multi-cloud architecture with three data centers, 200 virtual machines, and tasks of 400,000 MI length and 300 MB file size, revealed that HWOA-RNN surpassed benchmarks by 8–12% in key metrics: Throughput (320 tasks/s), Response Time (100 ms), SLA Violation Rate (1.2%), Load Balancing Efficiency (97%), Energy Efficiency Ratio (0.97), Scalability Index (0.98), Prediction Accuracy (98%), and Task Migration Overhead (30 ms). HWOA-RNN considerably enhances cloud resource management, with expected improvements in fault tolerance and security.

title: A Multimodal Large Language Model Framework for Partial Discharge Condition Assessment in Gas-Insulated Switchgear
authors: Yanxin Wang, Zhengrun Zhang, Qianzhen Jing, Jing Yan, Zhenkang Qi, Jianhua Wang, Yingsan Geng, Dipti Srinivasan
publish: IEEE Transactions on Dielectrics and Electrical Insulation
abstract: Artificial intelligence–enabled partial discharge (PD) condition assessment in gas-insulated switchgear (GIS) is essential for ensuring reliable operation of power equipment. However, existing methods face significant challenges in dealing with heterogeneous signal modalities, limited labeled data, low multi-task adaptation efficiency, and poor interpretability. To overcome these limitations, this paper proposes a multimodal large language model (LLM) framework for unified PD condition assessment with causal interpretive capability. First, a PD signal unification module is developed to transform PD heterogeneous monitoring data into consistent token sequences, thereby mitigating sampling inconsistencies while retaining critical diagnostic features. Then, an LLM-based data augmentation module generates high-fidelity samples under defect-type and discharge-intensity constraints with self-correction and cross-modal consistency maintenance, alleviating data scarcity. Furthermore, a task-instructed prompt encoder and low-rank adaptation-based lightweight fine-tuning mechanism enable cross-modal semantic alignment and efficient task adaptation while improving interpretability and reducing computational cost. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed framework achieves accuracies exceeding 98.5% across all tasks, substantially outperforming conventional approaches. The framework also provides interpretable textual reasoning that enhances diagnostic transparency and reliability, offering a promising pathway for intelligent condition assessment of GIS.
